Surgery + O&T 题库

HK Final MB · MCQ / EMQ · 2001 – 2022 · Compiled by M23 Adrian Lui

1147 MCQ · 151 EMQ · 16 专科 · 点击卡片标题展开题干与选项 · Cmd/Ctrl + K 聚焦搜索 · 答案未包含（原编者标注 SAQ not included） · 适用于 LMCHK / Final MB 选择题训练

SECTION 01

General Principles of Surgery

43 MCQ · 7 EMQ
GEN·MCQ001 2001 MCQ38 MCQ Wound healing is adversely affected by the following EXCEPT:

Wound healing is adversely affected by the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Jaundice
  2. B Congestive heart failure
  3. C Chemotherapy
  4. D Interposition of prolene mesh
  5. E Ventilator support
💡 查看答案
E

Ventilator support does not directly impair wound healing. Jaundice (bile salts inhibit fibroblasts), CHF (tissue hypoxia), chemotherapy (cytotoxic), prolene mesh (foreign body reaction) all impair.

GEN·MCQ002 2002 MCQ5 MCQ Concerning normal water and electrolyte balance in an average 70 kg adult, which of the following statements is CORRECT?

Concerning normal water and electrolyte balance in an average 70 kg adult, which of the following statements is CORRECT?

  1. A Approximately 2000–2500 mL of urine is passed in 24 hours
  2. B The major extracellular cation is potassium
  3. C Approximately 300 mL of fluid is lost in faeces in 24 hours
  4. D Approximately 1200–1500 of fluid is lost as water vapour via the skin and respiratory tract (insensible water loss)
  5. E Aldosterone acts on the kidney to conserve potassium and hydrogen ions
💡 查看答案
D

Insensible water loss via skin + lungs ≈ 800–1500 mL/day. Urine ~1500 mL (not 2000–2500). Major ECF cation = Na⁺ (not K⁺). Faecal loss ~100–200 mL. Aldosterone conserves Na⁺ and excretes K⁺/H⁺.

GEN·MCQ003 2002 MCQ17 MCQ Patients fasted for more than 24 hours will:

Patients fasted for more than 24 hours will:

  1. A Utilize glucose by glycogenolysis
  2. B Utilize glucose by proteolysis
  3. C Utilize ketones as the main cerebral energy source
  4. D Have an increase basal metabolic rate
  5. E Will develop hypotension
💡 查看答案
B

After 24h hepatic glycogen is depleted → glucose obtained via gluconeogenesis from amino acids (proteolysis). Ketones become main cerebral fuel only after 3–4 days of starvation. BMR decreases, not increases.

GEN·MCQ004 2002 MCQ38 MCQ Management of hypovolaemic shock following trauma DOES NOT involve:

Management of hypovolaemic shock following trauma DOES NOT involve:

  1. A Achievement of venous access via peripheral vein catheterization
  2. B Bladder catheterization
  3. C Immediate administration of whole blood or red cell concentrate
  4. D Appropriate analgesic agents
  5. E Oxygen administration
💡 查看答案
C

Initial resuscitation uses crystalloid (NS/RL), not immediate blood. Blood products require cross-matching. All other options (IV access, catheter, analgesia, O₂) are part of initial management.

GEN·MCQ005 2002 MCQ75 MCQ All of the following are factors which predispose to wound infection EXCEPT:

All of the following are factors which predispose to wound infection EXCEPT:

  1. A Impaired blood supply
  2. B Development of a wound haematoma
  3. C The presence of devitalized tissue in the wound
  4. D Antibiotic administration during surgery
  5. E Foreign material in the wound
💡 查看答案
D

Prophylactic antibiotics during surgery PREVENT wound infection. Impaired blood supply, haematoma, devitalized tissue, and foreign material all predispose to wound infection.

GEN·MCQ006 2002 MCQ88 MCQ Which of the following is NOT TRUE regarding systemic inflammatory response syndrome?

Which of the following is NOT TRUE regarding systemic inflammatory response syndrome?

  1. A Is characterized by tachycardia and pyrexia
  2. B Is caused by micro-organisms
  3. C May be seen in severe acute pancreatitis
  4. D May lead to multiple organ dysfunction syndrome
  5. E May be associated with gut mucosal barrier dysfunction
💡 查看答案
B

SIRS is NOT exclusively caused by micro-organisms — it can result from non-infectious causes (pancreatitis, trauma, burns). Sepsis = SIRS + confirmed infection.

GEN·MCQ007 2002# MCQ2 MCQ Preoperative nutritional status can be assessed from:

Preoperative nutritional status can be assessed from:

  1. A Body weight
  2. B Serum potassium concentration
  3. C Blood sugar concentration
  4. D Tricipital skin fat estimation
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
D

Tricipital skinfold thickness directly measures subcutaneous fat stores — a specific anthropometric nutritional parameter. Body weight alone is confounded by fluid status. K⁺ and glucose are not nutritional markers.

GEN·MCQ008 2002# MCQ3 MCQ Wound healing is UNLIKELY to be impaired in patients with:

Wound healing is UNLIKELY to be impaired in patients with:

  1. A Obstructive jaundice
  2. B Diabetes mellitus
  3. C Cushing’s syndrome
  4. D Chronic obstructive airway disease
  5. E Antithrombin III deficiency
💡 查看答案
E

Antithrombin III deficiency causes hypercoagulability, not impaired wound healing. Obstructive jaundice, DM, Cushing's (excess steroids), and COAD (tissue hypoxia) all impair healing.

GEN·MCQ009 2002# MCQ72 MCQ Postoperative pyrexia DOES NOT occur secondary to:

Postoperative pyrexia DOES NOT occur secondary to:

  1. A Pulmonary atelectasis
  2. B Deep venous thrombosis
  3. C Subphrenic abscess
  4. D Pulmonary embolism
  5. E Blood collection
💡 查看答案
D

PE is less commonly listed as a primary cause of post-operative pyrexia. Atelectasis (day 1), DVT (day 5), subphrenic abscess (day 5–10), and haematoma reabsorption all classically cause post-op fever.

GEN·MCQ010 2002# MCQ73 MCQ Ionizing radiation may give rise to the following malignancies EXCEPT:

Ionizing radiation may give rise to the following malignancies EXCEPT:

  1. A Leukaemia
  2. B Thyroid malignancy
  3. C Squamous cell carcinoma of skin
  4. D Osteogenic sarcoma
  5. E Colonic carcinoma
💡 查看答案
E

Colonic carcinoma is not classically associated with ionizing radiation. Radiation-induced malignancies include leukaemia, thyroid CA, skin SCC, and osteosarcoma.

GEN·MCQ011 2003 MCQ13 MCQ All of the following are associated with an increased incidence of abdominal wound dehiscence EXCEPT:

All of the following are associated with an increased incidence of abdominal wound dehiscence EXCEPT:

  1. A Bringing a stoma through the incision
  2. B Closure of the wound with a continuous suture
  3. C Presence of ascites
  4. D Presence of jaundice
  5. E Use of braided sutures
💡 查看答案
B

Continuous mass closure is the standard recommended technique (Jenkins rule) and does NOT increase dehiscence. Stoma through incision, ascites, jaundice, and braided sutures all increase dehiscence risk.

GEN·MCQ012 2003 MCQ29 MCQ The major cause of impaired wound healing is:

The major cause of impaired wound healing is:

  1. A Anemia
  2. B Diabetes mellitus
  3. C Local tissue infection
  4. D Malnutrition
  5. E Steroid use
💡 查看答案
C

Local tissue infection is the single most important cause of impaired wound healing. It directly destroys tissue, prolongs inflammation, and impedes repair.

GEN·MCQ013 2003 MCQ44 MCQ The COMMONEST cause of a transfusion reaction is:

The COMMONEST cause of a transfusion reaction is:

  1. A Air embolism
  2. B Contaminated blood
  3. C Human error
  4. D Outdated blood
  5. E Unusual circulating antibodies
💡 查看答案
C

Human error (ABO incompatibility from clerical/identification mistakes) is the commonest cause of serious transfusion reactions.

GEN·MCQ014 2003 MCQ71 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE in the post-operative period?

Which of the following is TRUE in the post-operative period?

  1. A Fatal pulmonary embolus is usually preceded by evidence of deep vein thrombosis in the legs
  2. B Dehiscence of an abdominal wound is more likely to occur in patients who develop pulmonary collapse
  3. C Thrombus begins to form in the leg veins after the second week
  4. D Pseudomembranous colitis should be treated with a broad spectrum antibiotic
  5. E Less than 1% of wounds become infected
💡 查看答案
B

Pulmonary collapse → coughing → ↑ intra-abdominal pressure → wound dehiscence. Fatal PE is often NOT preceded by DVT symptoms. Thrombus forms intra-operatively. PMC is treated with metronidazole/vancomycin (not broad-spectrum). Wound infection rate 2–5%.

GEN·MCQ015 2004 MCQ10 MCQ A blood transfusion reaction may cause all of the below EXCEPT:

A blood transfusion reaction may cause all of the below EXCEPT:

  1. A Fever and rigors
  2. B Jaundice
  3. C Abdominal pain
  4. D Renal impairment
  5. E Epistaxis
💡 查看答案
E

Epistaxis is not a typical feature of transfusion reactions. Fever/rigors, jaundice (haemolysis), abdominal pain, and renal impairment (haemoglobinuria) are all recognized features.

GEN·MCQ016 2004 MCQ31 MCQ Which of the following is NOT an indication for enteral nutrition through a naso-intestinal tube?

Which of the following is NOT an indication for enteral nutrition through a naso-intestinal tube?

  1. A Obstructive esophageal cancer
  2. B Bulbar palsy
  3. C Inadequate oral intake due to debilitating illness
  4. D High output enterocutaneous fistula
  5. E Severe trismus
💡 查看答案
D

High output enterocutaneous fistula — feeding proximal to fistula increases output. Needs TPN or feeding distal to fistula. Obstructive oesophageal CA, bulbar palsy, debilitation, and trismus are all valid indications.

GEN·MCQ017 2004 MCQ68, 2011 MCQ85 MCQ Complications of massive blood transfusion include all of the following EXCEPT:

Complications of massive blood transfusion include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Depletion of clotting factors
  2. B Hypocalcaemia
  3. C Hypothermia
  4. D Hypokalaemia
  5. E Thrombocytopenia
💡 查看答案
D

Massive transfusion causes HYPERkalaemia (K⁺ leaks from stored RBCs), not hypokalaemia. Other complications: dilutional coagulopathy, hypocalcaemia (citrate chelation), hypothermia, thrombocytopenia.

GEN·MCQ018 2004 MCQ85 MCQ Peri-operative blood transfusion is detrimental in which of the following conditions?

Peri-operative blood transfusion is detrimental in which of the following conditions?

  1. A Aortic surgery
  2. B Colonic carcinoma
  3. C Total hip replacement
  4. D Coronary artery bypass graft
  5. E Pancreatic carcinoma
💡 查看答案
B

Perioperative blood transfusion is immunosuppressive and has been specifically shown to worsen outcomes in colonic carcinoma (increased recurrence rates).

GEN·MCQ019 2004 MCQ86 MCQ The following are absorbable sutures EXCEPT:

The following are absorbable sutures EXCEPT:

  1. A Catgut
  2. B Dexon
  3. C Vicryl
  4. D PDS
  5. E Prolene
💡 查看答案
E

Prolene (polypropylene) is non-absorbable. Catgut (natural), Dexon (polyglycolic acid), Vicryl (polyglactin), PDS (polydioxanone) are all absorbable.

GEN·MCQ020 2005 MCQ28 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a predisposing factor for delayed wound healing?

Which of the following is NOT a predisposing factor for delayed wound healing?

  1. A Hypoalbuminaemia
  2. B Removal of arginine from diet
  3. C Uraemia
  4. D Long term use of hypoglycaemic drugs
  5. E Chronic obstructive airway disease
💡 查看答案
D

Long-term hypoglycaemic drugs control blood sugar → actually help wound healing. Hypoalbuminaemia, arginine deficiency, uraemia, and COAD all impair wound healing.

GEN·MCQ021 2005 MCQ90 MCQ A physician’s moral and legal obligations in an “informed consent” process includes all of the following EXCEPT:

A physician’s moral and legal obligations in an “informed consent” process includes all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Avoid coercive attitude and/or language
  2. B Clear explanation of the proposed intervention in a manner understandable to a reasonable lay person
  3. C Explain all of the proposed intervention
  4. D Indicate the physician’s own professional preference among the available options
  5. E Remind the patient that he/she has the right to change her mind even after he/she has signed the informed consent form
💡 查看答案
C

'Explain ALL' is too absolute — physicians explain material risks and nature of the procedure, not every conceivable detail. Avoiding coercion, clear explanation, indicating preference, and reminding of right to withdraw are all obligations.

GEN·MCQ022 2006 MCQ7 MCQ Which of the following is useful for the treatment of malignant disease but is NOT a chemotherapeutic agent?

Which of the following is useful for the treatment of malignant disease but is NOT a chemotherapeutic agent?

  1. A Xeloda
  2. B Taxol
  3. C Erbitux
  4. D 5-FU
  5. E Pharmorubicin
💡 查看答案
C

Erbitux (cetuximab) is a monoclonal antibody (targeted therapy/biologic), not a traditional chemotherapeutic agent. Xeloda (capecitabine), Taxol (paclitaxel), 5-FU, and Pharmorubicin (epirubicin) are all cytotoxic chemo agents.

GEN·MCQ023 2006 MCQ10 MCQ Normal skin wound will not have:

Normal skin wound will not have:

  1. A Coagulation
  2. B Inflammation
  3. C Granulation
  4. D Fibroplasia
  5. E Remodelling
💡 查看答案
C

In primary intention healing, granulation tissue is clinically insignificant/minimal. It is a hallmark of secondary intention healing. Coagulation, inflammation, fibroplasia, and remodelling all occur in normal primary healing.

GEN·MCQ024 2008 MCQ80 MCQ Total parenteral nutrition in surgical patients:

Total parenteral nutrition in surgical patients:

  1. A Is usually given through peripheral venous route
  2. B Is beneficial for all malnourished patients
  3. C Carries a risk of sepsis
  4. D Can be given via nasogastric tube
  5. E Can be used to treat hypovolaemic shock
💡 查看答案
C

TPN carries risk of catheter-related sepsis. TPN is given via central vein (not peripheral). It's not beneficial for ALL malnourished (enteral preferred when possible). NG = enteral, not parenteral. Cannot treat shock.

GEN·MCQ025 2010 MCQ33, 2015 MCQ33, 2018 MCQ18 MCQ A 50-year-old gentleman is admitted for preoperative assessment. Which of the following is the first criterion to decide on the suitability of the patient for d…

A 50-year-old gentleman is admitted for preoperative assessment. Which of the following is the first criterion to decide on the suitability of the patient for day surgery under general anaesthesia?

  1. A Age
  2. B American Society of Anaesthesiologists (ASA) classification
  3. C Gender
  4. D Serum albumin level
  5. E Surgeon’s preference
💡 查看答案
B

ASA classification is the primary criterion for day surgery suitability. ASA I–II generally suitable; ASA III may be considered with optimization. Age, gender, albumin are secondary.

GEN·MCQ026 2010 MCQ75 MCQ Which of the following antiplatelet drugs works by inhibiting thromboxane A2?

Which of the following antiplatelet drugs works by inhibiting thromboxane A2?

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Clopidogrel
  3. C Low molecular weight heparin
  4. D Unfractionated heparin
  5. E Warfarin
💡 查看答案
A

Aspirin irreversibly inhibits COX-1 → blocks thromboxane A₂ synthesis. Clopidogrel inhibits ADP receptor (P2Y12). LMWH, UFH = anticoagulants (anti-Xa/IIa). Warfarin = vitamin K antagonist.

GEN·MCQ027 2011 MCQ27 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a predisposing factor of delayed abdominal wound healing?

Which of the following is NOT a predisposing factor of delayed abdominal wound healing?

  1. A COPD
  2. B Hypercalcaemia
  3. C Hypoalbuminemia
  4. D Lack of arginine
  5. E Uraemia
💡 查看答案
B

Hypercalcaemia does not impair wound healing. COPD (hypoxia), hypoalbuminaemia (nutritional), arginine deficiency (NO precursor), and uraemia (toxic metabolites) all delay healing.

GEN·MCQ028 2011 MCQ86 MCQ 65/F. Hartmann operation for CA rectum. Post-op day 2 fever 40°C, BP 90/60 mmHg, urine output 10 mL. What is the most likely cause?

65/F. Hartmann operation for CA rectum. Post-op day 2 fever 40°C, BP 90/60 mmHg, urine output 10 mL. What is the most likely cause?

  1. A Cardiogenic shock
  2. B Anaphylactic shock
  3. C Hypovolemic shock
  4. D Neurogenic shock
  5. E Septic shock
💡 查看答案
E

Post-Hartmann day 2: high fever 40°C + hypotension + oliguria = septic shock, most likely from anastomotic leak/peritonitis. Cardiogenic would show pulmonary oedema. Anaphylactic needs allergen exposure.

GEN·MCQ029 2012 MCQ10, 2020 MCQ?? MCQ A 60-year-old gentleman has developed a pyrexia of 38.5°C at 24 hours after a right hemicolectomy. What would be the MOST LIKELY cause of his pyrexia?

A 60-year-old gentleman has developed a pyrexia of 38.5°C at 24 hours after a right hemicolectomy. What would be the MOST LIKELY cause of his pyrexia?

  1. A Atelectasis
  2. B Deep vein thrombosis
  3. C Pneumonia
  4. D Thrombophlebitis
  5. E Urinary tract infection
💡 查看答案
A

Day 1 post-op fever = 'Wind' = atelectasis. Mnemonic: Wind (day 1), Water (UTI day 3), Wound (day 5), Walking (DVT day 5–7), Wonder drugs (day 7+).

GEN·MCQ030 2012 MCQ53 MCQ Which of the following is the best in guiding fluid replacement therapy?

Which of the following is the best in guiding fluid replacement therapy?

  1. A BP
  2. B Haematocrit
  3. C Neck vein status
  4. D Pulse rate
  5. E Urine output
💡 查看答案
E

Urine output (target >0.5 mL/kg/hr) is the most reliable bedside indicator of adequate organ perfusion and fluid resuscitation.

GEN·MCQ031 2013 MCQ17 MCQ Which of the following about tumour marker is true?

Which of the following about tumour marker is true?

  1. A Carcinoembryonic antigen does not raise in relapse of colorectal cancer
  2. B Human chorionic gonadotrophin does not raise in germ cell tumour
  3. C Tumour marker is always used for monitoring treatment response
  4. D Tumour markers are tumour-specific
💡 查看答案
C

Tumour markers are used for monitoring treatment response (serial levels). CEA DOES rise in CRC relapse (A wrong). hCG DOES rise in germ cell tumours (B wrong). Markers are NOT tumour-specific (D wrong).

GEN·MCQ032 2013 MCQ18 MCQ Which of the following is an advantage of laparoscopic surgery?

Which of the following is an advantage of laparoscopic surgery?

  1. A Adhesion formation is less
  2. B Bleeding control is more efficient
  3. C Less chance of iatrogenic injuries
  4. D No loss of stereotactic vision
  5. E Tactile feedback not affected
💡 查看答案
A

Less adhesion formation is a well-established advantage of laparoscopic surgery (less peritoneal trauma). Bleeding control is harder (B), iatrogenic injury risk exists (C), stereotactic vision lost (D), tactile feedback reduced (E).

GEN·MCQ033 2013 MCQ19, 2016 MCQ29 MCQ Which one of the following suture materials is commonly used for skin closure in adult patients undergoing abdominal surgery?

Which one of the following suture materials is commonly used for skin closure in adult patients undergoing abdominal surgery?

  1. A Catgut
  2. B Polyamide (Nylon)
  3. C Polydioxanone (PDS)
  4. D Polyglactin (Vicryl)
  5. E Polypropylene (Prolene)
💡 查看答案
B

Polyamide (Nylon) — non-absorbable monofilament suture, commonly used for skin closure. Catgut outdated. PDS and Vicryl are absorbable (used for fascia). Prolene also possible but nylon is more standard for skin.

GEN·MCQ034 2013 MCQ20 MCQ Radiation therapy has a main role in surgical patient of which malignancy?

Radiation therapy has a main role in surgical patient of which malignancy?

  1. A CA rectum
  2. B CA stomach
  3. C Malignant melanoma
  4. D Nasopharyngeal carcinoma
  5. E CA testis
💡 查看答案
D

NPC is primarily treated with radiotherapy (± chemotherapy), not surgery. RT is the main treatment modality. In rectal CA, RT is adjuvant to surgery. Melanoma and gastric CA are relatively radioresistant.

GEN·MCQ035 2014 MCQ36, 2018 MCQ15 MCQ Regarding wound healing:

Regarding wound healing:

  1. A Can be classified into 3 stages
  2. B Occurs at similar rate at different sites
  3. C Results in worse scarring in infants
  4. D On average takes 3 months to complete
  5. E Scar maturation takes up to 3 years or more
💡 查看答案
E

Scar maturation takes up to 2–3 years or more. 3 stages is arguable (4 phases). Healing rate varies by site. Infants scar better, not worse. Initial wound closure ~3 weeks, not 3 months.

GEN·MCQ036 2015 MCQ24 MCQ The following are wound healing stages with primary intention except:

The following are wound healing stages with primary intention except:

  1. A Haemostasis
  2. B Inflammation
  3. C Proliferation
  4. D Remodelling
  5. E Wound contraction
💡 查看答案
E

Wound contraction is a feature of secondary intention healing, not primary. Primary intention stages: haemostasis → inflammation → proliferation → remodelling.

GEN·MCQ037 2015 MCQ31 MCQ A man has a pyrexia of 38.5°C 24 hours post-operatively. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A man has a pyrexia of 38.5°C 24 hours post-operatively. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Atelectasis
  2. B Deep vein thrombosis
  3. C Urinary tract infection
💡 查看答案
A

24h post-op fever = atelectasis ('Wind'). DVT and UTI occur later.

GEN·MCQ038 2015 MCQ36 MCQ 60/M, massive blood transfusion needed after traumatic injury. Which of the following is a complication?

60/M, massive blood transfusion needed after traumatic injury. Which of the following is a complication?

  1. A Hyperthermia
  2. B Hypercalcaemia
  3. C Hyperkalaemia
  4. D Nutritional deficiency
  5. E Thrombosis
💡 查看答案
C

Hyperkalaemia — stored blood K⁺ leaks out → massive transfusion overloads. Also causes hypothermia (not hyper), hypocalcaemia (not hyper), coagulopathy. Nutritional deficiency and thrombosis are not acute complications.

GEN·MCQ039 2016 MCQ6 MCQ A 70 years old man presenting with newly diagnosed lymphoma requiring central venous catheter for chemotherapy. Insertion via open cutdown is planned. Which of …

A 70 years old man presenting with newly diagnosed lymphoma requiring central venous catheter for chemotherapy. Insertion via open cutdown is planned. Which of the following is the most appropriate site for access?

  1. A Axillary vein at the infraclavicular area
  2. B Cephalic vein at the deltopectoral groove
  3. C Common femoral vein at groin
  4. D Popliteal vein at popliteal fossa
  5. E Subclavian vein at supraclavicular region
💡 查看答案
B

Cephalic vein at the deltopectoral groove is the classic site for open venous cutdown for central access. Axillary/subclavian/femoral are percutaneous Seldinger sites. Popliteal is not used.

GEN·MCQ040 2016 MCQ27, 2018 MCQ17 MCQ 40/M with good past health is admitted for elective surgery for femoral hernia repair. He develops wound infection on the 7th day post-operatively. Which is the…

40/M with good past health is admitted for elective surgery for femoral hernia repair. He develops wound infection on the 7th day post-operatively. Which is the most likely cause of the complication?

  1. A Anaemia
  2. B Atelectasis
  3. C Haematoma formation
  4. D Malnutrition
  5. E Prolene mesh
💡 查看答案
C

Haematoma formation is the most likely local cause of wound infection in a healthy 40/M — provides a nidus for bacterial growth. Anaemia, malnutrition less likely in healthy patient. Atelectasis is pulmonary, not wound.

GEN·MCQ041 2016 MCQ66, 2019 MCQ??, 2021 MCQ?? MCQ 68/F with history mucous diarrhoea due to rectal villous adenoma. She also has a history of congestive heart failure on digoxin and diuretics. What is the MOST …

68/F with history mucous diarrhoea due to rectal villous adenoma. She also has a history of congestive heart failure on digoxin and diuretics. What is the MOST IMPORTANT investigation prior to surgical resection?

  1. A Blood glucose level
  2. B Plasma digoxin
  3. C Plasma calcium
  4. D Plasma chloride
  5. E Plasma potassium
💡 查看答案
E

Villous adenoma secretes mucus rich in K⁺ + diuretics deplete K⁺ → severe hypokalaemia. Patient on digoxin — hypokalaemia potentiates digoxin toxicity → fatal arrhythmias. Must check K⁺ before surgery.

GEN·MCQ042 2017 MCQ19 MCQ An old man develop atelectasis on post-op day 2. Which is the most suggestive?

An old man develop atelectasis on post-op day 2. Which is the most suggestive?

  1. A Temperature > 40°C
  2. B Cough and sputum
  3. C Pulse oximetry show decreased oxygen saturation
  4. D Increased respiratory rate
  5. E Confusion
💡 查看答案
C

Decreased SpO₂ on pulse oximetry is the most direct and objective sign of atelectasis (V/Q mismatch). Fever >40°C, cough, tachypnoea, and confusion are less specific.

GEN·MCQ043 2022 MCQ40 MCQ Chronic smoker. Emergency omental patch repair. Eight hours after surgery SaO2 drop from 97% to 94%. Clinically asymptomatic. What is most likely cause of desat…

Chronic smoker. Emergency omental patch repair. Eight hours after surgery SaO2 drop from 97% to 94%. Clinically asymptomatic. What is most likely cause of desaturation?

  1. A Atelectasis
  2. B Pneumonia
  3. C UTI
  4. D Myocardial infarction
💡 查看答案
A

8h post-op in chronic smoker, asymptomatic, mild desaturation 97→94% = atelectasis. Too early for pneumonia. UTI/MI don't cause this pattern.

GEN·EMQ001 2004 EMQ4 EMQ CAUSES OF SHOCK

Theme: CAUSES OF SHOCK

Options
  1. A Pulmonary embolism
  2. B Myocardial infarction
  3. C Cardiac tamponade
  4. D Trauma
  5. E Burns
  6. F Sepsis
  7. G Acute pancreatitis
  8. H Major surgery
  9. I Ruptured aortic aneurysm
  10. J Ectopic pregnancy

For each patient below, select the MOST likely cause from the list of options above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 50 years old gentleman arrives at Casualty in shock. His blood pressure is 80/50 mmHg. The heart sounds are muffled. The JVP increases with inspiration.
  2. Q2. A 68 years old gentleman presents to Casualty in shock with continuous abdominal pain radiating to his back. The abdomen is rigid with an expansile abdominal mass.
💡 查看答案
Q1. C — Muffled heart sounds + JVP rises with inspiration (Kussmaul's sign) = cardiac tamponade (Beck's triad: hypotension, muffled sounds, ↑JVP).
Q2. I — Abdominal pain radiating to back + rigid abdomen + expansile mass = ruptured abdominal aortic aneurysm.
GEN·EMQ002 2007 EMQ2 EMQ POST-OPERATIVE MANAGEMENT — OLIGURIA / ANURIA

Theme: POST-OPERATIVE MANAGEMENT — OLIGURIA / ANURIA

Options
  1. A Add 2L of 5% dextrose to the daily intravenous fluid regime
  2. B Add a minimum of 2L of normal saline to the daily intravenous fluid regimen
  3. C Urinary catheterisation
  4. D Intravenous frusemide
  5. E Administer desmopressin
  6. F Commence renal dose dopamine infusion
  7. G Administer antibiotics
Questions
  1. Q1. 74/M produces small quantity of urine 24 hours after inguinal hernia repair. Tender suprapubic mass on abdominal examination.
  2. Q2. 70/F day four post-right hemicolectomy. Breathless and bilateral basal crepitation on auscultation. Urine output is less than 1000 mL in last 24 hours. 3L positive fluid balance in 3 days.
  3. Q3. 60/M projectile vomiting. Serum chlorine 85 mmol/L. pH 7.5. Nasogastric aspirate 2 L each 24 hours. 500 mL of urine in last 12 hours.
💡 查看答案
Q1. C — Post-inguinal hernia repair + tender suprapubic mass + oliguria = urinary retention → urinary catheterisation.
Q2. D — Post-hemicolectomy day 4, breathless, bilateral basal creps, 3L positive balance = fluid overload → IV frusemide.
Q3. B — Projectile vomiting, low Cl⁻, alkalosis = hypochloraemic metabolic alkalosis from gastric losses → replace with normal saline (NaCl).
GEN·EMQ003 2009 EMQ6 EMQ LEG SWELLING

Theme: LEG SWELLING

Options
  1. A Cardiac failure
  2. B Cellulitis
  3. C Deep vein thrombosis
  4. D Gout
  5. E Lipoedema
  6. F Liver cirrhosis
  7. G Lymphoedema tarda
  8. H Malabsorption
  9. I Hepatitis
  10. J Venous valvular insufficiency
Questions
  1. Q1. A woman complained of progressively worsening bilateral foot swelling and leg swelling up to mid-thigh for 3 years with minimal diurnal change. On physical examination, the skin is abnormal and thickened.
  2. Q2. A 72 years old woman presented with left leg welling above an area of discoloration and induration around the ankle. She had little discomfort, except for a limp due to a femoral neck fracture 2 years ago. She had hypertension, but other blood tests are normal.
  3. Q3. A 25 years old gentleman presented with chronic diarrhoea, significant weight loss, and bilateral oedema of leg up to mid shin. Sigmoidoscopy revealed no abnormalities. CT showed abnormal lesion in small bowel.
💡 查看答案
Q1. G — Bilateral progressive leg swelling ×3yr, thickened skin, minimal diurnal change = lymphoedema tarda.
Q2. J — Unilateral swelling + ankle discoloration/induration (lipodermatosclerosis) + history of immobility = chronic venous insufficiency.
Q3. H — Chronic diarrhoea + weight loss + bilateral oedema + small bowel lesion = malabsorption (hypoalbuminaemia → oedema).
GEN·EMQ004 2010 EMQ2 EMQ RADIOLOGY

Theme: RADIOLOGY

Options
  1. A Abdominal X-ray
  2. B Angiogram
  3. C Barium enema
  4. D Barium meal and follow through
  5. E CT abdomen
  6. F ERCP
  7. G Erect chest X-ray
  8. H MRI abdomen
  9. I Ultrasound abdomen
  10. J PET abdomen
Questions
GEN·EMQ005 2010 EMQ4 EMQ POST-OPERATION FEVER

Theme: POST-OPERATION FEVER

Options
  1. A Atelectasis
  2. B Transfusion reaction
  3. C Deep vein thrombosis
  4. D Idiopathic
  5. E Pelvic collection
  6. F Phlebitis
  7. G Urinary tract infection
  8. H Wound infection
Questions
  1. Q1. 50/M. Open cholecystectomy performed for acute cholecystitis. Complicated by anastomotic leakage on post-op day 2 requiring peritoneal lavage and wound toilet. Then he developed fever on day 5.
💡 查看答案
Q1. H — Post-op day 5 after complicated cholecystectomy with peritoneal contamination → wound infection (typical timing day 5–7).
GEN·EMQ006 2011 EMQ5 EMQ A. CXR

Theme: A. CXR

Options
  1. B CT scan
  2. C ECG
  3. D Haemoglobin
  4. E Serum Ca
  5. F Serum glucose
  6. G Thyroid function test
  7. H Ultrasound
  8. I Urine examination
  9. J X-ray of the neck
Questions
  1. Q1. A 45 years old lady, after thyroidectomy, noticed paraesthesia on the side of the face.
  2. Q2. 60/M, fluctuating fever 4 days after right hemicolectomy, chest exam is normal.
  3. Q3. 70/M, diabetic patient, after trans-urethral resection of prostate developed fever, chest and abdomen exam is normal, serum glucose level is within normal limits.
  4. Q4. A 37 years old lady, presented with 3 cm sessile polypoid lesion at antrum of stomach. EUS finding: lesion at submucosa of stomach wall.
  5. Q5. A 65 years old man presented with tarry stool for 2 days. Abdominal exam NAD. PR: old melaena. Endoscopy found an 4 cm ulcerated mass in submucosa. EUS found the mass located in muscularis layer.
💡 查看答案
Q1. E — Post-thyroidectomy facial paraesthesia = hypocalcaemia (hypoparathyroidism) → check serum calcium.
Q2. B — Post-hemicolectomy day 4, fluctuating fever, normal chest → CT scan to look for intra-abdominal collection/abscess.
Q3. I — Post-TURP fever, normal chest/abdomen, normal glucose → urine examination for UTI (most common post-TURP complication).
Q4. B — Submucosal gastric lesion (likely GIST/ectopic pancreas) → CT scan for staging and characterization.
Q5. B — 4cm ulcerated submucosal mass in muscularis (likely GIST) → CT scan for staging.
GEN·EMQ007 2014 EMQ6 EMQ POSTOPERATIVE FEVER

Theme: POSTOPERATIVE FEVER

Options
  1. A Atelectasis
  2. B DVT
  3. C UTI
  4. D Idiopathic
  5. E Pelvic collection
  6. F Phlebitis
  7. G Wound infection
Questions
  1. Q1. Woman in her sixties. APR with intermittent fever on post-op day 10. Wound appears normal.
  2. Q2. M/55 with DM. Had open cholecystectomy for cholecystitis. Had bile leakage during operation with NS irrigation of the peritoneal cavity and abdominal wound done. Presented with fever on post-op day 5.
  3. Q3. M/65, smoker, laparotomy + lysis + adhesiolysis for IO. Fever on 2nd day, no blood transfusion received.
💡 查看答案
Q1. E — APR day 10 fever, wound normal → pelvic collection (common after pelvic surgery, especially APR).
Q2. G — Open cholecystectomy with bile leak and peritoneal contamination, day 5 fever → wound infection.
Q3. A — Day 2 post-op fever in smoker, no transfusion → atelectasis.
SECTION 02

Breast Surgery

76 MCQ · 10 EMQ
BR·MCQ001 2001 MCQ7 MCQ The following are risk factors of carcinoma of breast EXCEPT:

The following are risk factors of carcinoma of breast EXCEPT:

  1. A Late menarche
  2. B Family history of carcinoma of breast
  3. C Nulliparous
  4. D Late menopause
  5. E Personal history of carcinoma of breast
💡 查看答案
A

Late menarche is protective (shorter estrogen exposure). Early menarche is the risk factor. Family Hx, nulliparity, late menopause, personal Hx are all risk factors.

BR·MCQ002 2001 MCQ10 MCQ The MOST reliable method of breast screening is:

The MOST reliable method of breast screening is:

  1. A Clinical examination
  2. B Self-examination
  3. C Mammography
  4. D Ultrasonography
  5. E Measurement of tumour marker
💡 查看答案
C

Mammography is the most reliable breast screening method with highest sensitivity for early detection.

BR·MCQ003 2001 MCQ24, 2005 MCQ82, 2007 MCQ1 MCQ A 47 years old lady was found to have a 3 cm ductal carcinoma-in-situ in her left breast at 2 o’clock position. The MOST appropriate treatment option is:

A 47 years old lady was found to have a 3 cm ductal carcinoma-in-situ in her left breast at 2 o’clock position. The MOST appropriate treatment option is:

  1. A Close surveillance
  2. B Excisional biopsy
  3. C Radiation therapy
  4. D Simple mastectomy
  5. E Tamoxifen
💡 查看答案
D

3cm DCIS — too large for simple excision with clear margins in many cases. Simple mastectomy is most appropriate for large DCIS. (Alternatively wide local excision + RT if margins achievable, but for 3cm DCIS mastectomy is safest option listed.)

BR·MCQ004 2001 MCQ30 MCQ Lymph from the breast may drain into the following sites EXCEPT:

Lymph from the breast may drain into the following sites EXCEPT:

  1. A Pectoral lymph node
  2. B Supratrochlear lymph node
  3. C Internal mammary lymph node
  4. D Contralateral breast
  5. E Supraclavicular lymph node
💡 查看答案
B

Supratrochlear lymph node (at elbow) does NOT receive breast lymphatic drainage. Breast drains to pectoral (axillary), internal mammary, supraclavicular, and contralateral breast nodes.

BR·MCQ005 2001 MCQ37 MCQ The LEAST common site for distant spread of carcinoma of breast is:

The LEAST common site for distant spread of carcinoma of breast is:

  1. A Bone
  2. B Liver
  3. C Kidney
  4. D Lungs
  5. E Pleura
💡 查看答案
C

Kidney is the least common site for breast cancer metastasis. Common sites: bone > lung > liver > brain > pleura.

BR·MCQ006 2001 MCQ40 MCQ Modified radical mastectomy removes the following tissues EXCEPT:

Modified radical mastectomy removes the following tissues EXCEPT:

  1. A Pectoralis major
  2. B Axillary lymphatic tissue
  3. C Breast tissue
  4. D Pectoral fascia
  5. E Nipple
💡 查看答案
A

Modified radical mastectomy (Patey) preserves pectoralis major but removes breast, pectoral fascia, nipple, and axillary tissue. Halsted radical mastectomy removes pectoralis major.

BR·MCQ007 2001 MCQ45, 2006 MCQ34 MCQ A 30 years old lady is found to have a mobile, well circumscribed breast lump which she claims to have been present for the last 2 months. Fine needle aspiratio…

A 30 years old lady is found to have a mobile, well circumscribed breast lump which she claims to have been present for the last 2 months. Fine needle aspiration yielded benign cells. The MOST likely diagnosis of this lump is:

  1. A Radial scar
  2. B Gynaecomastia
  3. C Fibroadenoma
  4. D Invasive ductal carcinoma
  5. E Mucinous carcinoma
💡 查看答案
C

30/F, mobile, well-circumscribed, benign FNAC = fibroadenoma ('breast mouse'). Classic presentation in young women.

BR·MCQ008 2001 MCQ55 MCQ The following are contraindications of breast-conserving surgery in a patient with carcinoma of breast EXCEPT:

The following are contraindications of breast-conserving surgery in a patient with carcinoma of breast EXCEPT:

  1. A Tumour size is greater than 4 cm
  2. B Tumour beneath areola
  3. C Old age
  4. D Small breast
  5. E Multi-focal disease
💡 查看答案
C

Old age alone is NOT a contraindication for breast-conserving surgery. Large tumour (>4cm), subareolar location, small breast (poor cosmetic outcome), and multifocal disease are contraindications.

BR·MCQ009 2002 MCQ15 MCQ The management of nipple discharge includes the following EXCEPT:

The management of nipple discharge includes the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Imprint cytology
  2. B Ductogram
  3. C Ultrasonography of breast and nipple
  4. D Fine needle aspiration
  5. E Microdochectomy
💡 查看答案
A

Imprint cytology is not a standard investigation for nipple discharge. Ductogram, USG, FNAC, and microdochectomy are all appropriate.

BR·MCQ010 2002 MCQ30 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST common condition that causes cyclical mastalgia?

Which of the following is the MOST common condition that causes cyclical mastalgia?

  1. A Costochondritis
  2. B Cervical radiculopathy
  3. C Aberrant normal development and involution of breast epithelium
  4. D Fibroadenoma
  5. E Phylloides tumour
💡 查看答案
C

Aberrant normal development and involution (ANDI) of breast epithelium (fibrocystic changes) is the most common cause of cyclical mastalgia.

BR·MCQ011 2002 MCQ64, 2002# MCQ42, 2006 MCQ31 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST appropriate treatment for cyclical mastalgia?

Which of the following is the MOST appropriate treatment for cyclical mastalgia?

  1. A Gamolenic acid
  2. B Vitamin D
  3. C Ranitidine
  4. D Aspirin
  5. E Clarithromycin
💡 查看答案
A

Gamolenic acid (evening primrose oil) is used for cyclical mastalgia. Vitamin D, ranitidine, aspirin, clarithromycin have no role.

BR·MCQ012 2002 MCQ85 MCQ In the management of breast cancer, breast conservation surgery with adjuvant radiotherapy:

In the management of breast cancer, breast conservation surgery with adjuvant radiotherapy:

  1. A Is suitable for all breast cancer patients
  2. B Has no impact on disease free or overall survival
  3. C Has higher local recurrence rates than mastectomy
  4. D Is recommended only for pre-menopausal patients
  5. E Increases disease-released psychological morbidity
💡 查看答案
C

BCS + RT has higher local recurrence rates than mastectomy, but overall survival is equivalent. Not suitable for all patients (A). Not only pre-menopausal (D).

BR·MCQ013 2002# MCQ21 MCQ A 37 years old gentleman complained of a right-sided breast mass. The history started 3 months ago when he first noticed some discomfort on wearing T-shirt. The…

A 37 years old gentleman complained of a right-sided breast mass. The history started 3 months ago when he first noticed some discomfort on wearing T-shirt. The mass became more obvious with progressive increase in intensity of pain. The other breast was unremarkable. Which of the following treatment options should be initiated?

  1. A Vitamin B6
  2. B Primrose oil
  3. C Prednisone
  4. D Tamoxifen
  5. E Stilbesterol
💡 查看答案
D

Tamoxifen is used for gynecomastia treatment (anti-estrogen effect). Male breast mass + pain + progressive = gynaecomastia.

BR·MCQ014 2003 MCQ1 MCQ Which of the following statements is TRUE regarding risk factors for breast cancer?

Which of the following statements is TRUE regarding risk factors for breast cancer?

  1. A Incidence does not appear to be age-related among those over 35 years old
  2. B Family history is a major risk factor for developing breast cancer
  3. C Late first pregnancy does not increase the risk of breast cancer
  4. D Diet and weight have no association with breast cancer risk
  5. E History of breast feeding increases risk of breast cancer
💡 查看答案
B

Family history is a major risk factor for breast cancer. Incidence IS age-related (A wrong). Late first pregnancy DOES increase risk (C wrong). Diet/weight DO affect risk (D wrong). Breastfeeding DECREASES risk (E wrong).

BR·MCQ015 2003 MCQ17 MCQ Which of the following statements is CORRECT regarding the natural history of breast cancer?

Which of the following statements is CORRECT regarding the natural history of breast cancer?

  1. A On average, a 1 cm breast cancer has been subclinically present for approximately 1 year
  2. B Tethering of the skin occurs as a result of glandular fibrosis and shortening of Cooper’s ligaments
  3. C Skin edema in breast cancer results only from direct skin invasion by tumour
  4. D Bone metastases result from arterial dissemination of cancer cells
  5. E Ipsilateral lung involvement occurs most often as a result of direct lung invasion
💡 查看答案
B

Skin tethering results from Cooper's ligament involvement (fibrosis and shortening). 1cm tumour present ~8-10 years subclinically (A wrong). Skin oedema from dermal lymphatic invasion (C wrong). Bone mets via venous (Batson's plexus), not arterial (D wrong).

BR·MCQ016 2003 MCQ33 MCQ Which of the following does NOT belong to the chronic cystic mastitis group?

Which of the following does NOT belong to the chronic cystic mastitis group?

  1. A Papillomatosis
  2. B Blunt duct adenosis
  3. C Sclerosing adenosis
  4. D Apocrine metaplasia
  5. E Mondor’s disease
💡 查看答案
E

Mondor's disease is superficial thrombophlebitis of breast veins — NOT part of fibrocystic disease (chronic cystic mastitis). Papillomatosis, blunt duct adenosis, sclerosing adenosis, apocrine metaplasia are all components.

BR·MCQ017 2003 MCQ50 MCQ Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding phylloides tumour?

Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding phylloides tumour?

  1. A It bears a histologic relationship to fibroadenoma
  2. B 10% are malignant
  3. C As with ductal carcinoma, it has a high incidence of multicentricity within the breast
  4. D Axillary lymph nodal metastases are uncommon
  5. E Wide local excision or total (simple) mastectomy will usually suffice for treatment
💡 查看答案
C

Phyllodes tumour is NOT multicentric (unlike ductal carcinoma). It IS related to fibroadenoma (A true). ~10% malignant (B true). LN mets uncommon (D true). WLE/mastectomy sufficient (E true).

BR·MCQ018 2003 MCQ72, 2004 MCQ52, 2005 MCQ15 MCQ When considering wide local excision (WLE), axillary dissection, and radiotherapy as the definitive treatment for a breast carcinoma, which of the factors below…

When considering wide local excision (WLE), axillary dissection, and radiotherapy as the definitive treatment for a breast carcinoma, which of the factors below would bear FAVOURABLY on that choice?

  1. A A T1 tumour
  2. B Central (subareolar) location within the breast
  3. C An extremely large breast
  4. D A very small breast
  5. E A synchronous second primary cancer within the breast
💡 查看答案
A

T1 tumour (<2cm) favours BCS + RT. Subareolar (B), large breast with difficult surveillance (C), small breast (D), and synchronous second primary (E) are all unfavourable.

BR·MCQ019 2004 MCQ33, 2005 MCQ84 MCQ Bloody nipple discharge is associated with:

Bloody nipple discharge is associated with:

  1. A Fibroadenoma
  2. B Fibrocystic disease of the breast
  3. C Pregnancy
  4. D Intraductal papilloma
  5. E Phylloides
💡 查看答案
D

Intraductal papilloma is the classic cause of bloody/serous nipple discharge from a single duct.

BR·MCQ020 2004 MCQ34, 2005 MCQ72 MCQ Annual screening mammogram is indicated for which of the following situation?

Annual screening mammogram is indicated for which of the following situation?

  1. A Over 18 years old
  2. B From 30 years old and onwards
  3. C From 40 years old and onwards
  4. D With a palpable lump regardless of age
  5. E From menopause
💡 查看答案
C

Annual screening mammography is recommended from age 40 onwards (some guidelines from 50). Not from 18 or 30. A palpable lump needs diagnostic workup, not screening.

BR·MCQ021 2004 MCQ37, 2005 MCQ79 MCQ Contraindication to breast conservative surgery for breast cancer is:

Contraindication to breast conservative surgery for breast cancer is:

  1. A Age beyond 5 years
  2. B Bilateral breast cancer
  3. C Lymph node involvement
  4. D Size > 2 cm
  5. E Multifocal lesions
💡 查看答案
E

Multifocal lesions are a contraindication for breast-conserving surgery. Age >5yr (?=50), bilateral CA, LN involvement, size >2cm are not absolute contraindications.

BR·MCQ022 2004 MCQ87, 2005 MCQ87 MCQ Which of the following is a premalignant condition of breast cancer?

Which of the following is a premalignant condition of breast cancer?

  1. A Fibroadenoma
  2. B Breast abscess
  3. C Fibroadenosis
  4. D Atypical ductal hyperplasia
  5. E Paget’s disease of the nipple
💡 查看答案
D

Atypical ductal hyperplasia is a premalignant condition with 4-5× increased breast cancer risk. Fibroadenoma (benign), abscess (inflammatory), fibroadenosis (benign). Paget's disease IS malignant (not premalignant).

BR·MCQ023 2005 MCQ26 MCQ Which of the following is NOT associated with poor prognosis of breast cancer?

Which of the following is NOT associated with poor prognosis of breast cancer?

  1. A BRCA1 gene mutation
  2. B Lymph node involvement
  3. C High grade tumour
  4. D Post mastectomy breast reconstruction
  5. E Lymphovascular involvement
💡 查看答案
D

Post-mastectomy reconstruction does NOT worsen prognosis. BRCA1, LN involvement, high grade, and lymphovascular invasion are all poor prognostic factors.

BR·MCQ024 2006 MCQ63, 2007 MCQ52 MCQ 45/F. Presents with progressive enlarging mass in right breast over 12 months. The mass is 7 cm at the retro-areolar region. It is firm, not fixed to skin or un…

45/F. Presents with progressive enlarging mass in right breast over 12 months. The mass is 7 cm at the retro-areolar region. It is firm, not fixed to skin or underlying structures. Which is the most likely pathology?

  1. A Abscess
  2. B Cancer
  3. C Radial scar
  4. D Fibroadenoma
  5. E Phyllodes tumour
💡 查看答案
E

7cm progressive retroareolar mass, firm, mobile = phyllodes tumour. Too large for fibroadenoma growth pattern. Abscess would be tender/fluctuant.

BR·MCQ025 2006 MCQ67 MCQ A postmenopausal woman with ER-positive breast cancer measuring 4.2 cm prefers breast conservation. What is the best treatment?

A postmenopausal woman with ER-positive breast cancer measuring 4.2 cm prefers breast conservation. What is the best treatment?

  1. A Mastectomy
  2. B Wide local excision
  3. C Radiation
  4. D Chemotherapy
  5. E Hormonal therapy
💡 查看答案
E

Postmenopausal, ER+, wants BCS but tumour 4.2cm — neoadjuvant hormonal therapy (aromatase inhibitor) to downstage before BCS. This shrinks the tumour to enable conservation.

BR·MCQ026 2007 MCQ42 MCQ A 56 years old lady has left breast microcalcifications on her mammography during screening. There is no mass palpable. The ultrasound of her breast is unremark…

A 56 years old lady has left breast microcalcifications on her mammography during screening. There is no mass palpable. The ultrasound of her breast is unremarkable. What is the significance of this?

  1. A She could be discharged and followed up in 6 months
💡 查看答案
A

Incomplete recall — only option A listed. Microcalcifications without palpable mass need further workup, not simple discharge.

BR·MCQ027 2009 MCQ43 MCQ Which of the following is true regarding ductal carcinoma-in-situ (DCIS)?

Which of the following is true regarding ductal carcinoma-in-situ (DCIS)?

  1. A For non-palpable lesions, hook-wire guided wide local excision is preferred
  2. B Level I and II axillary lymph node dissection is needed
  3. C Mastectomy is necessary
💡 查看答案
A

Non-palpable DCIS → hook-wire guided wide local excision (stereotactic guided). Axillary dissection NOT needed for DCIS (B wrong). Mastectomy not always necessary (C wrong).

BR·MCQ028 2009 MCQ44 MCQ Which of the following is not a risk factor for breast cancer?

Which of the following is not a risk factor for breast cancer?

  1. A Family history of ovarian cancer
  2. B Family history of breast cancer
  3. C Personal history of LCIS
  4. D Personal history of fibroadenoma
  5. E Personal history of contralateral invasive ductal carcinoma
💡 查看答案
D

Personal history of fibroadenoma is NOT a risk factor for breast cancer. Family history of ovarian/breast cancer, personal LCIS, and contralateral invasive ductal carcinoma are all risk factors.

BR·MCQ029 2009 MCQ45 MCQ Which is the most common site of accessory breast tissue?

Which is the most common site of accessory breast tissue?

  1. A Axilla
  2. B Groin
  3. C Just above the breast
  4. D Just below the breast
  5. E Umbilicus
💡 查看答案
A

Axilla is the most common site for accessory breast tissue (along the embryological milk line).

BR·MCQ030 2009 MCQ46, 2018 MCQ3, 2020 MCQ2 MCQ Which of the following can BEST prevent lymphedema?

Which of the following can BEST prevent lymphedema?

  1. A Sentinel lymph node biopsy even axillary lymph nodes are palpable
  2. B Post-operative axillary irradiation
  3. C Regular gentle skin massage and skin protection
  4. D Regular heavy objection lifting after surgery
  5. E Routine level 3 axillary dissection
💡 查看答案
C

Regular gentle skin massage and skin protection (lymphatic drainage techniques) best prevent lymphoedema. SLNB prevents it by avoiding full dissection (but A says 'even with palpable LN' = inappropriate for SLNB).

BR·MCQ031 2009 MCQ47, 2014 MCQ4 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST COMMON pathologic cause of gynaecomastia?

Which of the following is the MOST COMMON pathologic cause of gynaecomastia?

  1. A Cirrhosis
  2. B Drug induce
  3. C Hyperthyroidism
  4. D Primary hypogonadism
  5. E Testicular tumour
💡 查看答案
B

Drug-induced gynaecomastia is the most common pathological cause (spironolactone, cimetidine, digoxin, oestrogens, antiandrogens). Cirrhosis, hyperthyroidism, hypogonadism, testicular tumour are less common.

BR·MCQ032 2009 MCQ48, 2014 MCQ5 MCQ A 49 years old lady was found to have a 2 cm benign phyllodes tumor. What is your management?

A 49 years old lady was found to have a 2 cm benign phyllodes tumor. What is your management?

  1. A Excisional biopsy
  2. B Breast irradiation
  3. C Mastectomy
  4. D Modified radical mastectomy
  5. E Wide local excision
💡 查看答案
E

Benign phyllodes tumour 2cm → wide local excision (≥1cm margin) to prevent local recurrence. Simple excision biopsy has high recurrence rate. Mastectomy is overtreatment.

BR·MCQ033 2010 MCQ1 MCQ Operable invasive breast cancer with one single palpable lymph node. What is the management?

Operable invasive breast cancer with one single palpable lymph node. What is the management?

  1. A Axillary dissection of level I and II
  2. B Axillary dissection of level I
  3. C Sentinel lymph node biopsy
💡 查看答案
A

Palpable axillary LN (clinically positive) → axillary dissection levels I and II. SLNB is for clinically node-negative patients.

BR·MCQ034 2010 MCQ2, 2015 MCQ3 MCQ Which item is the most significant prognostic factor for breast cancer?

Which item is the most significant prognostic factor for breast cancer?

  1. A Age
  2. B Axillary node status
  3. C Tumour size
  4. D C-erb-B2 oncogene over expression
  5. E Oestrogen and progesterone receptor positive
💡 查看答案
B

Axillary lymph node status is the most significant prognostic factor for breast cancer survival.

BR·MCQ035 2010 MCQ4, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following treatment is most recommended for ductal carcinoma in-situ (DCIS)?

Which of the following treatment is most recommended for ductal carcinoma in-situ (DCIS)?

  1. A Chemotherapy after surgery
  2. B Mastectomy if DCIS is larger than 4 cm
  3. C Modified radical mastectomy irrespective of size
  4. D Radiotherapy after mastectomy if DCIS is larger than 4 cm
  5. E Re-excision of margin if the margin is just 2 mm
💡 查看答案
B

Mastectomy is recommended for DCIS >4cm (unable to achieve clear margins with BCS). Chemo not used for DCIS (A). MRM not needed (C). RT not needed after mastectomy for DCIS (D). 2mm margin may be adequate for DCIS (E debatable).

BR·MCQ036 2010 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following is the MOST COMMON site of breast cancer?

Which of the following is the MOST COMMON site of breast cancer?

  1. A Central region
  2. B Lower inner quadrant
  3. C Lower outer quadrant
  4. D Upper inner quadrant
  5. E Upper outer quadrant
💡 查看答案
E

Upper outer quadrant is the most common site for breast cancer (~50% of cases), due to greatest amount of breast tissue.

BR·MCQ037 2011 MCQ20, 2015 MCQ4 MCQ A 40 years old woman complains of swollen erythematous right breast. Which of the below is NOT a differential diagnosis?

A 40 years old woman complains of swollen erythematous right breast. Which of the below is NOT a differential diagnosis?

  1. A Breast abscess
  2. B Fat necrosis
  3. C Inflammatory breast carcinoma
  4. D Lactational mastitis
  5. E Lactational mastitis associated with ductal ectasia
💡 查看答案
B

Fat necrosis presents as a hard lump (can mimic cancer) but NOT typically with diffuse swelling/erythema. Breast abscess, inflammatory carcinoma, and mastitis all present with swollen erythematous breast.

BR·MCQ038 2012 MCQ1 MCQ 48/F. Complains of breast mass. MMG and USG show multiple cysts. Aspiration of the index lesion reveals straw colored fluid and the lesion collapses after aspir…

48/F. Complains of breast mass. MMG and USG show multiple cysts. Aspiration of the index lesion reveals straw colored fluid and the lesion collapses after aspiration. What is the most appropriate management then?

  1. A Aspiration of all the cysts
  2. B Observe and reassessment MMG
  3. C Excisional biopsy
  4. D Tamoxifen
💡 查看答案
B

Straw-coloured aspirate + cyst collapses = simple cyst. Management: observe and reassess with mammography. No need to aspirate all cysts (A). No need for excision (C) or tamoxifen (D).

BR·MCQ039 2012 MCQ2 MCQ 55/F. Complains of left breast mass. On physical examination found a 5.5 cm left breast mass and 1 ipsilateral mobile lymph node. What is the staging for this t…

55/F. Complains of left breast mass. On physical examination found a 5.5 cm left breast mass and 1 ipsilateral mobile lymph node. What is the staging for this tumor?

  1. A T2N2M0
  2. B T3N1M0
  3. C T3N2M0
  4. D T4N1M0
  5. E T4N2M0
💡 查看答案
B

5.5cm mass = T3 (>5cm). 1 mobile ipsilateral axillary LN = N1. No distant mets = M0. → T3N1M0.

BR·MCQ040 2012 MCQ89 MCQ A 24 years old female presented with a tender swelling in the left breast. She has been breast feeding for the past 3 weeks and was well until 4 days prior to t…

A 24 years old female presented with a tender swelling in the left breast. She has been breast feeding for the past 3 weeks and was well until 4 days prior to the painful swelling. On examination, there is a tender erythematous fluctuant mass in the left breast. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Abscess
  2. B Carcinoma
  3. C Cyst
  4. D Duct ectasia
  5. E Fibroadenoma
💡 查看答案
A

24/F breastfeeding ×3wk, 4 days of painful swelling, tender erythematous fluctuant mass = lactational breast abscess.

BR·MCQ041 2013 MCQ56 MCQ 36/F. 3 cm invasive ductal carcinoma. Mammogram shows no axillary LN. Imaging shows no distant metastasis. Patient worry about cosmetic outcome. What is the mos…

36/F. 3 cm invasive ductal carcinoma. Mammogram shows no axillary LN. Imaging shows no distant metastasis. Patient worry about cosmetic outcome. What is the most appropriate treatment?

💡 查看答案
A

Incomplete recall (0 options). For 3cm IDC wanting cosmesis: wide local excision (BCS) + SLNB + adjuvant RT is most appropriate.

BR·MCQ042 2014 MCQ1 MCQ Which of the following is NOT associated with increased incidence of breast cancer?

Which of the following is NOT associated with increased incidence of breast cancer?

  1. A Atypical ductal hyperplasia on previous biopsy
  2. B History of breastfeeding
  3. C Late menopause
  4. D Nulliparity
  5. E Obesity
💡 查看答案
B

History of breastfeeding is NOT associated with increased breast cancer — it is PROTECTIVE. ADH, late menopause, nulliparity, and obesity all increase risk.

BR·MCQ043 2014 MCQ2, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following about tamoxifen is true?

Which of the following about tamoxifen is true?

  1. A As an adjuvant therapy to breast cancer, it has to be used for 10 years
  2. B It is an oestrogen receptor agonist with partial antagonist action
  3. C It is not associated with increased risk of endometrial cancer/ hyperplasia
  4. D It does not cause menopausal symptoms
  5. E Treatment of choice for old frail patient who cannot tolerate surgery
💡 查看答案
E

Tamoxifen can be used as treatment for old/frail patients who cannot tolerate surgery (primary endocrine therapy). It's taken 5yr not 10 (A wrong). It's a partial ANTAGONIST at breast (B has the terminology inverted). It DOES increase endometrial CA risk (C wrong). It CAN cause menopausal symptoms (D wrong).

BR·MCQ044 2014 MCQ3 MCQ Which of the following is true about breast abscess?

Which of the following is true about breast abscess?

  1. A Breast feeding should be discontinued
  2. B Fistula more common in smokers
  3. C Incision and drainage is the only choice of treatment
  4. D Local anaesthesia must be used in pregnancy
  5. E Tetracycline is the antibiotics of choice
💡 查看答案
B

Periductal fistula is more common in smokers (smoking damages subareolar ducts). Breastfeeding should continue (A wrong). Aspiration is an option before I&D (C wrong). GA may be needed (D wrong). Flucloxacillin, not tetracycline (E wrong).

BR·MCQ045 2014 MCQ6, 2020 MCQ34 MCQ What is sentinel lymph node?

What is sentinel lymph node?

  1. A First drainage lymph node
  2. B Lymph node nearest to breast
  3. C Lymph node furthest from breast
  4. D Any lymph node in level I
  5. E Largest lymph node in level I
💡 查看答案
A

Sentinel lymph node = the first lymph node to receive drainage from the primary tumour site.

BR·MCQ046 2015 MCQ1 MCQ Which of the following regarding FNAC is correct?

Which of the following regarding FNAC is correct?

  1. A Should always repeat if acellular
  2. B Should always send cystic fluid for cytology
  3. C Should always be done for breast masses unless a cone (?core) biopsy can be performed
  4. D Should be done before mammography
  5. E Should readily aspirate cellular material
💡 查看答案
C

FNAC should always be done for breast masses unless core biopsy can be performed — part of triple assessment. Acellular FNAC may need repeat or core biopsy (A sometimes). Cystic fluid cytology has low yield unless bloody (B not always).

BR·MCQ047 2015 MCQ2 MCQ For women presenting with palpable breast lumps, which is incorrect?

For women presenting with palpable breast lumps, which is incorrect?

  1. A Thorough history should be taken including family history
  2. B Mammography should be taken with two different views
  3. C USG of the breast should not be done for women >35 years old
  4. D Trucut biopsy or core biopsy should be considered if FNAC is inconclusive
💡 查看答案
C

USG should NOT be avoided for women >35 — USG complements mammography and is useful at all ages. Thorough history (A), two-view mammography (B), and core biopsy for inconclusive FNAC (D) are all correct.

BR·MCQ048 2015 MCQ5, 2018 MCQ5 MCQ Which of the following is true about sentinel lymph node biopsy?

Which of the following is true about sentinel lymph node biopsy?

  1. A If sentinel lymph node biopsy did not reveal metastasis, axillary dissection can be spared
  2. B Patent blue is the only method for locating sentinel lymph nodes
  3. C Intra-op frozen section is 100% reliable in determining whether there are metastasis
  4. D SLN results in more lymphedema than AD
  5. E There is only one sentinel lymph node
💡 查看答案
A

If SLNB is negative, axillary dissection can be spared (key benefit). Patent blue is not the only method (B — can use radioisotope). Frozen section not 100% (C). SLNB causes LESS lymphoedema (D wrong). Multiple SLNs possible (E wrong).

BR·MCQ049 2015 MCQ6, 2018 MCQ6 MCQ An 85-year-old frail and demented lady with a history of congestive heart disease and renal impairment has a 4-cm hard right breast mass with palpable ipsilater…

An 85-year-old frail and demented lady with a history of congestive heart disease and renal impairment has a 4-cm hard right breast mass with palpable ipsilateral axillary lymph nodes. A core biopsy of the mass has confirmed an invasive ductal carcinoma, estrogen receptor (ER) positive. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

  1. A Excisional biopsy of the breast mass
  2. B Modified radical mastectomy
  3. C Radical mastectomy
  4. D Simple mastectomy
  5. E Tamoxifen
💡 查看答案
E

85/F, frail, demented, comorbid, ER+/PR+ IDC → primary endocrine therapy (tamoxifen) is most appropriate. Too frail for surgery.

BR·MCQ050 2016 MCQ52 MCQ Which of the drug is NOT associated with gynaecomastia?

Which of the drug is NOT associated with gynaecomastia?

  1. A Amphetamine
  2. B Spironolactone
  3. C Calcium channel blocker
  4. D Cimetidine
  5. E Anabolic steroid
💡 查看答案
A

Amphetamine is NOT commonly associated with gynaecomastia. Spironolactone, CCBs, cimetidine, and anabolic steroids are all known causes.

BR·MCQ051 2016 MCQ68 MCQ Which of the following arteries does not provide blood supply to the breast?

Which of the following arteries does not provide blood supply to the breast?

  1. A Acromial thoracic artery
  2. B Intercostal artery
  3. C Lateral thoracic artery
  4. D Thoracodorsal artery
  5. E Perforator branches of internal mammary artery
💡 查看答案
D

Thoracodorsal artery (branch of subscapular) does not provide blood supply to the breast. Acromial thoracic (thoracoacromial), intercostal, lateral thoracic, and internal mammary perforators all supply the breast.

BR·MCQ052 2016 MCQ69 MCQ Which of the following is not a mammographic sign of malignancy?

Which of the following is not a mammographic sign of malignancy?

  1. A Cluster microcalcification
  2. B Architectural distortion
  3. C Macrocalcification
  4. D Undefined boarder
  5. E Stellate lesion
💡 查看答案
C

Macrocalcification (coarse, eggshell, popcorn) is typically BENIGN. Clustered microcalcification, architectural distortion, irregular margins, and stellate lesions are mammographic signs of malignancy.

BR·MCQ053 2016 MCQ70 MCQ The following sonographic signs point to suspicious breast lesions EXCEPT:

The following sonographic signs point to suspicious breast lesions EXCEPT:

  1. A Internal calcification
  2. B Intralesional vascularity
  3. C Irregular border
  4. D Overlying skin thickening
  5. E Shorter than height lesion
💡 查看答案
E

Lesion shorter than height (wider than tall) is a BENIGN feature on USG. Taller than wide is suspicious. Internal calcification, vascularity, irregular border, and skin thickening are suspicious features.

BR·MCQ054 2016 MCQ71 MCQ Common sites of breast metastasis except:

Common sites of breast metastasis except:

  1. A Brain
  2. B Bone
  3. C Lung
  4. D Liver
  5. E Stomach
💡 查看答案
E

Stomach is NOT a common site for breast cancer metastasis. Common sites: bone, brain, lung, liver, pleura.

BR·MCQ055 2017 MCQ7 MCQ Which of the following treatment is most recommended for DCIS?

Which of the following treatment is most recommended for DCIS?

  1. A Chemotherapy after surgery
  2. B Mastectomy if DCIS > 3 cm
  3. C Modified radical mastectomy irrespective of size
  4. D RT after mastectomy if DCIS > 3 cm
  5. E Re-excision of margin after mastectomy if margin is involved
💡 查看答案
B

Mastectomy for DCIS >3cm when clear margins cannot be achieved by BCS.

BR·MCQ056 2017 MCQ8 MCQ What is the most common form of CA breast?

What is the most common form of CA breast?

  1. A Invasive ductal carcinoma
  2. B Invasive lobar carcinoma
💡 查看答案
A

Invasive ductal carcinoma is the most common form of breast cancer (~70-80%).

BR·MCQ057 2017 MCQ9 MCQ A 25 years old lady presented with a left breast mass. Physical examination finds a 1 cm well defined mass. There is no axillary lymph node. Ultrasound shows a …

A 25 years old lady presented with a left breast mass. Physical examination finds a 1 cm well defined mass. There is no axillary lymph node. Ultrasound shows a benign looking mass. FNAC shows no malignant cells. What is the next appropriate management?

  1. A Excision biopsy
  2. B Observation and reassessment ultrasound
  3. C Repeat FNAC
  4. D Simple mastectomy
  5. E Tamoxifen
💡 查看答案
B

25/F, 1cm, benign USG, benign FNAC = concordant triple assessment → observe and reassess. No need for excision unless discordant.

BR·MCQ058 2017 MCQ11 MCQ A woman in her forties presents with bloody nipple discharge, no breast mass. The bloody discharge from single duct in left nipple upon expression. ?Ductogram f…

A woman in her forties presents with bloody nipple discharge, no breast mass. The bloody discharge from single duct in left nipple upon expression. ?Ductogram found filling defect in the duct. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Cone excision
  2. B Microdochectomy
  3. C Nipple discharge for cytology
  4. D Observation and reassessment ultrasound
💡 查看答案
B

Bloody single-duct discharge + filling defect on ductogram = intraductal papilloma → microdochectomy (excision of the affected duct).

BR·MCQ059 2018 MCQ1 MCQ Mammogram, which is correct?

Mammogram, which is correct?

  1. A Show significantly more lesions if 2 views are taken
  2. B Recommended for patients aged <35
  3. C Better for detecting cysts than ultrasound
  4. D Should be done after core biopsy
  5. E If a mass shows no abnormality on mammogram, it does not need to be excised or investigated
💡 查看答案
A

Two-view mammography shows significantly more lesions than single view. Not recommended <35 (B wrong — USG preferred). USG better for cysts (C wrong). Mammogram before biopsy (D wrong). Normal mammogram doesn't exclude malignancy (E wrong).

BR·MCQ060 2018 MCQ2 MCQ Which of the following about DCIS is correct?

Which of the following about DCIS is correct?

  1. A Sentinel lymph node biopsy is never performed
  2. B Adjuvant RT should be given after mastectomy
  3. C For non-palpable lesion, should treat by stereotactic guided wide local excision with clear margin
  4. D 5 years tamoxifen should always be given
  5. E Routine levels I and II axillary dissection should be performed
💡 查看答案
C

Non-palpable DCIS → stereotactic-guided wide local excision with clear margins. SLNB can be done for large DCIS/mastectomy (A wrong). Adjuvant RT after BCS not mastectomy (B wrong). Tamoxifen not always (D wrong). Routine axillary dissection not needed (E wrong).

BR·MCQ061 2018 MCQ4 MCQ What is the best next step management for a 56 years old lady who was screened to have suspicious microcalcifications on mammography but normal ultrasonography?

What is the best next step management for a 56 years old lady who was screened to have suspicious microcalcifications on mammography but normal ultrasonography?

  1. A Interval mammogram after 6 months
  2. B Mastectomy
  3. C No intervention
  4. D Stereotactic-guided core biopsy or vacuum-assisted biopsy
  5. E Stereotactic-guided wire-guided excision
💡 查看答案
D

Suspicious microcalcifications, normal USG → stereotactic-guided core biopsy or vacuum-assisted biopsy for tissue diagnosis.

BR·MCQ062 2019 MCQ1 MCQ Triple negative breast cancer:

Triple negative breast cancer:

  1. A Can be associated with BRCA mutation
  2. B Can be treated with trastuzumab
  3. C Is often bilateral
  4. D Is less invasive in nature
  5. E Must be treated with surgery
💡 查看答案
A

Triple-negative breast cancer CAN be associated with BRCA mutation (especially BRCA1). Cannot use trastuzumab (HER2-negative, B wrong). Not often bilateral (C wrong). More aggressive (D wrong). Surgery is part of treatment but not 'must' (E wrong).

BR·MCQ063 2019 MCQ25 MCQ 20 years old pregnant lady found to have node-negative 1cm breast cancer at 12 o’clock 4 cm away from nipple. She is in 1st trimester. Which of the followings i…

20 years old pregnant lady found to have node-negative 1cm breast cancer at 12 o’clock 4 cm away from nipple. She is in 1st trimester. Which of the followings is true?

  1. A Breast conservative surgery can be done
  2. B Chemotherapy is absolutely contraindicated
  3. C Sentinel LN biopsy can be done
  4. D Tamoxifen is safe for bridging therapy for surgery
  5. E TOP is recommended
💡 查看答案
A

BCS can be done in pregnancy (1st trimester — RT deferred to post-partum). Chemo can be given in 2nd/3rd trimester (B wrong). SLNB with blue dye contraindicated in pregnancy but Tc99m acceptable (C debatable). Tamoxifen contraindicated in pregnancy (D wrong). TOP not routinely recommended (E wrong).

BR·MCQ064 2019 MCQ82 MCQ Phyllodes tumor:

Phyllodes tumor:

  1. A Can metastasize to lung
  2. B Is a benign tumor
  3. C More common in age >60
  4. D Is slow-growing
  5. E Only treated by wide local excision
💡 查看答案
A

Malignant phyllodes can metastasize to lung (haematogenous spread). Not always benign (B wrong). Occurs in 30-50 age (C wrong). Can grow rapidly (D wrong). Large/malignant may need mastectomy (E wrong).

BR·MCQ065 2020 MCQ56 MCQ 30-year-old man presented with bilateral breast mass, physical examination showed bilateral 2cm retro-areolar mass, what is the cause?

30-year-old man presented with bilateral breast mass, physical examination showed bilateral 2cm retro-areolar mass, what is the cause?

  1. A Abscess
  2. B Fibroadenoma
  3. C Gynecomastia
  4. D Breast cancer
  5. E Breast cyst
💡 查看答案
C

30/M bilateral breast mass, retro-areolar = gynecomastia. Most common cause of bilateral breast mass in males.

BR·MCQ066 2020 MCQ60 MCQ 50/F with history of right breast carcinoma (triple negative) with radical mastectomy done, now present with 1cm mass on the right chest wall, not fixed to over…

50/F with history of right breast carcinoma (triple negative) with radical mastectomy done, now present with 1cm mass on the right chest wall, not fixed to overlying skin or underlying muscle. What should be done for histological diagnosis?

  1. A Core needle biopsy
  2. B Excisional biopsy
  3. C FNAC
  4. D Incisional biopsy
  5. E Vacuum-guided biopsy
💡 查看答案
A

Core needle biopsy for histological diagnosis of chest wall recurrence after mastectomy — provides tissue architecture for definitive diagnosis.

BR·MCQ067 2021 MCQ11 MCQ 50/F present to breast clinic for right breast discharge:

50/F present to breast clinic for right breast discharge:

  1. A Bilateral ductogram should be done
  2. B Excision of alveolar complex is needed for pathological diagnosis
  3. C Single duct discharge is likely to be physiological
  4. D The yield of discharge cytology is low
💡 查看答案
D

Yield of nipple discharge cytology is low (sensitivity ~50%). Single-duct discharge is more likely pathological (C wrong). Bilateral ductogram not routinely needed (A wrong).

BR·MCQ068 2021 MCQ27 MCQ Which is true about breast MRI?

Which is true about breast MRI?

  1. A MRI is useful for patient with detected lymph nodes yet cannot find the primary tumour site
  2. B Used in older patients with a breast lesion
  3. C Less accurate than mammogram
  4. D Most useful in Paget’s disease
💡 查看答案
A

Breast MRI is useful for occult primary with axillary LN metastasis — finding the primary tumour when other imaging fails.

BR·MCQ069 2021 MCQ33 MCQ Microcalcification on breast imaging, which of the following is correct?

Microcalcification on breast imaging, which of the following is correct?

  1. A Coarse calcification has higher risk of malignancy
  2. B Microcalcification is suspicious until proven otherwise
  3. C Microcalcification should be biopsied by USG guided core biopsy
  4. D Suspicious microcalcification should be excised even if core biopsy showed no malignancy
💡 查看答案
D

Suspicious microcalcification should be excised even if core biopsy shows no malignancy (sampling error). Coarse calcification is benign (A wrong). Not all microcalcification is suspicious (B overstated). USG-guided not ideal for calcifications — stereotactic preferred (C wrong).

BR·MCQ070 2021 MCQ64 MCQ Regarding Phyllodes tumour, which is true?

Regarding Phyllodes tumour, which is true?

  1. A Benign phyllodes tumour need wide local excision of more than 1cm
  2. B Malignant phyllodes tumor spread via lymphatics
  3. C Hard to differentiate from fibroadenoma on core biopsy
  4. D HIFU can be treatment of phyllodes
💡 查看答案
C

Phyllodes tumour is hard to differentiate from fibroadenoma on core biopsy (similar fibroepithelial pattern). Benign phyllodes needs ≥1cm margin (A — debatable). Malignant spreads haematogenously not lymphatic (B wrong). HIFU not standard (D wrong).

BR·MCQ071 2021 MCQ?? MCQ Man with bilateral breast swelling, which treatment?

Man with bilateral breast swelling, which treatment?

  1. A Bilateral mastectomy
  2. B Check BRCA
  3. C Examine testes
  4. D Core biopsy
💡 查看答案
C

Man with bilateral breast swelling → examine testes (to rule out testicular tumour causing gynaecomastia from hCG/estrogen secretion).

BR·MCQ072 2021 MCQ?? MCQ Frail old lady with T1N0M0 ER PR +ve HER-ve tumour. Previous history of stroke. What is the most appropriate treatment?

Frail old lady with T1N0M0 ER PR +ve HER-ve tumour. Previous history of stroke. What is the most appropriate treatment?

  1. A MRM
  2. B Herceptin
  3. C Primary hormonal therapy
  4. D Palliative RT
💡 查看答案
C

Frail old lady, ER/PR+, HER2-, history of stroke → primary hormonal therapy (aromatase inhibitor or tamoxifen). Too frail for surgery or herceptin (not HER2+).

BR·MCQ073 2022 MCQ4 MCQ 20/M painless bilateral breast enlargement.

20/M painless bilateral breast enlargement.

  1. A Gynecomastia is a likely differential diagnosis
  2. B Incisional biopsy to check histology
  3. C MRI
  4. D Check serum prolactin
💡 查看答案
A

20/M painless bilateral breast enlargement = gynaecomastia is the most likely differential diagnosis.

BR·MCQ074 2022 MCQ57 MCQ 22 years old female with a mobile breast lump. Which is TRUE about breast investigation?

22 years old female with a mobile breast lump. Which is TRUE about breast investigation?

  1. A USG-guided FNAC can be done in the clinic
  2. B Inconclusive FNAC should be followed by incisional biopsy
  3. C Breast cancer does not occur in young patients, imaging and investigations not needed
💡 查看答案
A

USG-guided FNAC can be done in the clinic as part of triple assessment for young woman with breast lump.

BR·MCQ075 2022 MCQ62 MCQ 30/F lumpectomy & SLNB done for triple negative CA breast (pT1N0M0). Which of the following is true:

30/F lumpectomy & SLNB done for triple negative CA breast (pT1N0M0). Which of the following is true:

  1. A Adjuvant RT is not necessary
  2. B Genetic testing (e.g. BRCA1/2, etc.) should be offered
  3. C She should undergo adjuvant Herceptin treatment
  4. D Tamoxifen should be given for 5 years
💡 查看答案
B

30/F with triple-negative breast CA (pT1N0M0) after BCS → genetic testing (BRCA1/2) should be offered (young age + triple negative = high probability of germline mutation). Adjuvant RT IS needed after BCS (A wrong). Herceptin not for TN (C wrong). Tamoxifen not for TN (D wrong).

BR·MCQ076 2022 MCQ88 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a known complication of axillary dissection?

Which of the following is NOT a known complication of axillary dissection?

  1. A Lymphedema
  2. B Radial nerve injury
  3. C Seroma
  4. D Wound infection
💡 查看答案
B

Radial nerve injury is NOT a complication of axillary dissection. Long thoracic nerve (winged scapula) and thoracodorsal nerve are at risk. Lymphoedema, seroma, and wound infection are known complications.

BR·EMQ001 2002 EMQ4 EMQ BREAST CANCER

Theme: BREAST CANCER

Options
  1. A Infiltrating ductal carcinoma
  2. B Infiltrating lobular carcinoma
  3. C Tubular carcinoma
  4. D Inflammatory carcinoma
  5. E Ductal carcinoma-in-situ
  6. F Lobular carcinoma-in-situ
  7. G Paget’s disease of nipple
  8. H Papillary carcinoma
  9. I Medullary carcinoma
  10. J Mucinous carcinoma

Each of the following describes a type of breast cancer. Select the MOST likely condition.

Questions
  1. Q1. This is the most frequent type of breast cancer in Hong Kong.
  2. Q2. The cancer is usually located close to the nipple and presents as haemorrhagic nipple discharge.
  3. Q3. The cancer is associated with eczematous, excoriated lesion of the nipple and adjacent skin.
💡 查看答案
Q1. IDC — Invasive ductal carcinoma is the most common type of breast cancer in HK (and globally, ~70-80%).
Q2. Intraductal papilloma — Close to nipple + haemorrhagic discharge = intraductal papilloma.
Q3. Paget's disease — Eczematous excoriated nipple lesion = Paget's disease of nipple (associated with underlying DCIS/IDC).
BR·EMQ002 2003 EMQ1, 2009 EMQ4, 2010 EMQ1, 2012 EMQ2, 2014 EMQ4 EMQ TREATMENT OF BREAST DISEASE

Theme: TREATMENT OF BREAST DISEASE

Options
  1. A Observe
  2. B Modified radical mastectomy
  3. C Brachytherapy
  4. D Hookwire guided excisional biopsy
  5. E Wide local excision & axillary dissection
  6. F Excisional biopsy
  7. G Tamoxifen
  8. H Wide local excision
  9. I Simple mastectomy
  10. J Breast irradiation

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST suitable treatment from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 22 years old lady presented with a 1 cm left breast lump. The lump was firm and freely mobile. Ultrasonography showed a well-defined solid mass with homogenous internal echoes. Fine needle aspiration for cytology showed numerous cohesive sheets and clusters of normal epithelial cells amongst a background of stripped nuclei.
  2. Q2. A 55 years old lady presented with a 3 cm right breast mass at 12 o’clock position. The mass was hard and irregular. It was not fixed to skin but the nipple was retracted. She had no other complaints on systemic review. Bilateral mammograms showed a spiculated lesion at the corresponding site and there was no other lesions detected. Fine needle aspiration showed numerous poorly adhesive cells with markedly enlarged nuclei.
  3. Q3. An 83 years old, bed bound, lady with a recent history of stroke and known COAD, presented with a 4 cm right breast mass. A core biopsy of the mass showed features suggestive of an invasive ductal adenocarcinoma. The immunohistochemistry for estrogen and progesterone receptor status showed that the cells stained strongly for both receptors and tumour cell positivity was over 75%.
  4. Q4. A 55 years old lady was found to have a cluster of pleomorphic calcifications on screening mammography of left breast. Stereotactic biopsy showed atypical ductal hyperplasia.
  5. Q5. A lady presented a breast mass of 1 cm size, axillary lymph node was negative and there are no systemic symptoms. Trucut biopsy showed phyllodes tumour.
  6. Q6. F/23. Presented with 2 cm breast mass. Physical examination shows firm smooth freely mobile mass. USG shows regular hypoechoic lesion (2.1 cm) with posterior acoustic shadow. FNA of the lesion showed content of simple cyst and the lesion collapsed completely after aspiration.
  7. Q7. A 51 years old lady found a 2 cm left breast mass at 2 o’clock and 4 cm away from nipple. The mass was hard and irregular. Systemic review was unremarkable. Mammogram was performed and showed a speculated mass at the corresponding site and another mass at 4 o’clock position of the left breast. FNAC of the two masses showed numerous poorly adhesive cells with markedly enlarged nuclei.
  8. Q8. A 45 years old lady presented with a 1 cm lesion at the right breast at 3 o’clock position. Mammography and ultrasound showed features of indeterminate lesion at the corresponding site and no other lesions. FNA showed atypical cells. She had undergone excisional biopsy of the mass of 8 mm showing intermediate grade DCIS with comedo necrosis. Resection margin was >5 mm but <10 mm. She was keen to preserve her breast.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Observation/reassessment — 22/F, 1cm mobile firm lump, benign USG + benign FNAC = concordant benign triple assessment → reassurance and follow-up.
Q2. Modified radical mastectomy — 55/F, 3cm hard irregular mass, nipple retraction, no fixation → MRM (mastectomy + axillary clearance).
