Q3. Hormonal therapy (tamoxifen/AI) — 83/F bed-bound with stroke + COAD, IDC → primary endocrine therapy (too frail for surgery/GA).
Q4. Stereotactic excision/wire-guided biopsy — Screening calcifications + ADH on stereotactic biopsy → need excision to exclude DCIS/invasive component.
Q5. Wide local excision — 1cm phyllodes on trucut → wide local excision with ≥1cm margin.
Q6. Observation/reassessment — 23/F, 2cm firm mobile mass, benign USG, benign FNAC = fibroadenoma → observation.
Q7. WLE + SLNB + adjuvant RT — 51/F, 2cm hard mass at 2 o'clock, spiculated on MMG = breast cancer → BCS (WLE) + SLNB ± adjuvant treatment.
Q8. WLE (excision biopsy) — 45/F, 1cm indeterminate lesion on imaging → excision for definitive histology.
BR·EMQ003 2004 EMQ9, 2005 EMQ9, 2007 EMQ3 EMQ BREAST PATHOLOGY

Theme: BREAST PATHOLOGY

Options
  1. A Complex cyst
  2. B Costochondritis
  3. C Ductal carcinoma-in-situ
  4. D Fat necrosis
  5. E Fibrocystic disease
  6. F Intraduct papilloma
  7. G Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast
  8. H Paget’s disease
  9. I Radial scar
  10. J Sclerosing adenosis

For each of the single case scenarios below, choose the MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 37 years old lady presented with bilateral cyclical mastalgia and distension before menstruation. She also noted bilateral lumps at upper outer quadrant which tended to wax and wane with time.
  2. Q2. A 56 years old lady complained of an eczematous nipple lesion of her left breast for two years. The lesion started off with itchy red patches. Subsequently the lesion enlarged leading to destruction of the nipple. Currently the lesion became ulcerative with foul smelling discharge.
  3. Q3. A 57 years old lady was found to have a specks of microcalcification with linear branching on screening mammograms. She was placed on hormonal replacement therapy for 6 years. She was totally asymptomatic.
  4. Q4. A 76 years old lady noted to have a 3 cm painless mass in her left breast. Mammographic exam showed architectural distortion associated with a mass. FNAC showed an increased nuclear/cytoplasm ratio.
BR·EMQ004 2011 EMQ3 EMQ DIAGNOSIS OF BREAST CONDITION

Theme: DIAGNOSIS OF BREAST CONDITION

Options
  1. A Breast cyst
  2. B Fibroadenoma
  3. C Gynaecomastia
  4. D Mastitis
  5. E Paget’s disease
  6. F Phyllodes tumor
  7. G Stage 1 invasive breast cancer
  8. H Stage 2 invasive breast cancer
  9. I Stage 3 invasive breast cancer
  10. J Stage 4 invasive breast cancer
Questions
  1. Q1. A woman in her fifties. 1.5 cm left breast mass, hard and irregular. USG also found suspicious lymph node (loss of fatty hilum). Biopsy of mass and lymph node showed invasive ductal carcinoma, PET-CT showed no metastases.
  2. Q2. A 43 years old female. Mammogram showed pleomorphic calcification behind areola, nipple is eczematous and erythematous, no mass or lymph node.
  3. Q3. 24 years old. Delivered 4 weeks ago and breast feed. Increase pain and redness of breast for 1 week. Physical exam revealed no mass, but area of erythema, and tenderness around nipple areolar complex. No lymph nodes in axilla.
BR·EMQ005 2013 EMQ9, 2016 EMQ3, 2018 EMQ3 EMQ TREATMENT OF BREAST DISEASE

Theme: TREATMENT OF BREAST DISEASE

Options
  1. A Aromatase inhibitor
  2. B Brachytherapy
  3. C Breast radiotherapy
  4. D Excisional biopsy
  5. E Hookwire-guided biopsy
  6. F Modified radical mastectomy
  7. G Simple mastectomy
  8. H Tamoxifen
  9. I Wide local excision
  10. J Wide local excision with sentinel lymph node ± axillary dissection
Questions
  1. Q1. A 60 years old gentleman, with family history of breast cancer, presents with a mass in left breast for 2 months. Physical examination shows a 1 cm hard mass adjacent to the left nipple and palpable ipsilateral axillary lymph node. FNAC shows ductal carcinoma. There is no distant metastasis.
  2. Q2. 45/F. Right solitary breast mass of 1.5 cm, not fixed to skin and no nipple retraction. No lymph node and distant metastases. Biopsy shown to be mucinous carcinoma.
  3. Q3. A 80 years old frail lady with advanced CA breast. History of multiple embolic stroke. Presents with left breath mass, biopsy shown to be invasive ductal carcinoma.
BR·EMQ006 2015 EMQ4 EMQ BREAST

Theme: BREAST

Options
  1. A Acute mastitis
  2. B ANDI
  3. C Breast abscess
  4. D Breast carcinoma
  5. E Fibroadenosis
  6. F Cyclical mastalgia
  7. G Mondor disease
  8. H Tietze’s syndrome
  9. I Fat necrosis
Questions
  1. Q1. Hot and tender breast. Smoker and breastfeeding. Diffuse tenderness, no masses detected.
  2. Q2. 26/F. 6 months of pain at inner aspect of left breast, increase frequency of attacks recently, amateur athlete, physical examination was unremarkable apart from medial tenderness of left breast.
  3. Q3. 58/F. Mild prickling in right breast for few months, the lump has progressive in size in the recent 2 months. History of hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy and has been on HRT since 45 years old. No family history of breast diseases. P/E shows a non-tender, hard mass at the upper outer quadrant of the right breast.
BR·EMQ007 2017 EMQ4 EMQ MANAGEMENT OPTIONS FOR BREAST MASS

Theme: MANAGEMENT OPTIONS FOR BREAST MASS

Options
  1. A Brachytherapy
  2. B Radiation
  3. C Excisional biopsy
  4. D Hookwire guided biopsy
  5. E Tamoxifen
  6. F Modified radical mastectomy
  7. G Observation
  8. H Simple mastectomy
  9. I Wide local excision
  10. J Wide local excision with axillary lymph node dissection
Questions
  1. Q1. A 20 years old male complains of diffuse swelling of the breasts for 2 months. It is associated with pain. P/E shows diffuse swelling of the breasts and no discrete mass felt. Embarrassed by appearance and does not want surgical treatment.
  2. Q2. 55/F with cluster of pleomorphic microcalcifications found on asymptomatic screening MMG. Stereotactic biopsy shows atypical ductal hyperplasia.
  3. Q3. A 20 years old female with mobile mass. Trucut biopsy shows phyllodes tumour.
BR·EMQ008 2020 EMQ5 EMQ BREAST CONDITIONS

Theme: BREAST CONDITIONS

Options
  1. A Breast abscess
  2. B Breast cancer
  3. C Breast cyst
  4. D Fibroadenoma
  5. E Phyllodes tumor
Questions
  1. Q1. Woman with breast mass and axillary lymph node.
  2. Q2. F/40 presenting with right breast lump. USG found hypoechoic homogeneous lesions with posterior shadow.
  3. Q3. 3x year old woman with right breast lump rapidly enlarged 8cm in 4 months. No axillary LN found.
BR·EMQ009 2021 EMQ1 EMQ A. Breast abscess

Theme: A. Breast abscess

Options
  1. B Breast cyst
  2. C Fibroadenoma
  3. D DCIS
  4. E Phyllodes tumour
  5. F Invasive ductal carcinoma
  6. G Invasive lobular carcinoma
Questions
  1. Q1. 20-year-old female presents with a breast mass. USG shows a hypoechoic well- circumscribed solid lesion with posterior enhancement.
  2. Q2. 30-year-old female on breastfeeding, presents with a painful erythematous breast mass.
  3. Q3. Old lady presents with a rapidly enlarging breast mass, core needle biopsy shows fibroepithelial cells.
BR·EMQ010 2022 EMQ4 EMQ BREAST

Theme: BREAST

Options
  1. A Angiosarcoma
  2. B Breast cancer
  3. C Breast cyst
  4. D Fibroadenoma
  5. E Gynaecomastia
  6. F Breast melanoma
  7. G Phyllodes
  8. H Intraductal papilloma
Questions
  1. Q1. F/40, 2cm regular hypoechoic, posterior enhancement.
  2. Q2. F/50, 1cm irregular mass, acoustic shadowing.
  3. Q3. F/40, rapidly enlarging, well circumscribed, lobulated edge.
SECTION 03

Cardiothoracic Surgery

80 MCQ · 3 EMQ
CTS·MCQ001 2001 MCQ4 MCQ The following are indications for thoracoscopic cervico-dorsal sympathectomy EXCEPT:

The following are indications for thoracoscopic cervico-dorsal sympathectomy EXCEPT:

  1. A Raynaud’s phenomenon
  2. B Hyperhidrosis
  3. C Acute brachial emboli
  4. D Reflex sympathetic dystrophy
  5. E Intractable angina
💡 查看答案
C

Acute brachial emboli requires embolectomy, not sympathectomy. Sympathectomy is indicated for Raynaud's, hyperhidrosis, RSD, and intractable angina.

CTS·MCQ002 2001 MCQ8 MCQ A 50 years old chronic smoker develops a superior sulcus (Pancoast) tumour. All the following features are true EXCEPT:

A 50 years old chronic smoker develops a superior sulcus (Pancoast) tumour. All the following features are true EXCEPT:

  1. A Horner’s syndrome
  2. B Brachial neuralgia
  3. C Erosion of first rib
  4. D Hilar lymphadenopathy
  5. E Opacity over apex of lung in chest x-ray
💡 查看答案
D

Pancoast tumour (superior sulcus) causes Horner's, brachial plexus pain, rib erosion, apical opacity. Hilar lymphadenopathy is NOT a typical feature of Pancoast — it's peripheral/apical.

CTS·MCQ003 2001 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST commonly seen side effect after thoracoscopic cervico- dorsal sympathectomy?

Which of the following is the MOST commonly seen side effect after thoracoscopic cervico- dorsal sympathectomy?

  1. A Compensatory sweating over other parts of the body
  2. B Horner’s syndrome
  3. C Haemothorax
  4. D Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury
  5. E Persistent pneumothorax
💡 查看答案
A

Compensatory sweating is the most common side effect after thoracoscopic sympathectomy (~50-80%).

CTS·MCQ004 2001 MCQ14 MCQ In Horner’s syndrome, there is:

In Horner’s syndrome, there is:

  1. A Dry eye
  2. B Hyperhidrosis of the face
  3. C Exophthalmos
  4. D Constricted pupil
  5. E Impaired abduction of the eye
💡 查看答案
D

Horner's syndrome: miosis (constricted pupil), ptosis, anhidrosis, enophthalmos. NOT dry eye (tearing may increase), NOT hyperhidrosis (anhidrosis), NOT exophthalmos.

CTS·MCQ005 2001 MCQ20 MCQ Constrictive pericarditis can be caused by the following conditions EXCEPT:

Constrictive pericarditis can be caused by the following conditions EXCEPT:

  1. A Tuberculosis
  2. B Rheumatoid arthritis
  3. C Myxoedema
  4. D Mediastinal irradiation
  5. E Viral infection
💡 查看答案
C

Myxoedema (hypothyroidism) does NOT cause constrictive pericarditis. It causes pericardial effusion. TB, RA, radiation, and viral infection can all cause constrictive pericarditis.

CTS·MCQ006 2001 MCQ29 MCQ Measures taken for the treatment of “stove-in chest” include all the following EXCEPT:

Measures taken for the treatment of “stove-in chest” include all the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Endotracheal intubation
  2. B Positive pressure ventilation
  3. C Tracheostomy
  4. D Fixing the rib fracture with stainless steel wire
  5. E Thoracoplasty
💡 查看答案
E

Thoracoplasty is NOT a treatment for flail chest. Intubation, positive pressure ventilation, tracheostomy (if prolonged ventilation), and rib fixation are appropriate treatments.

CTS·MCQ007 2001 MCQ39 MCQ A young lady was diagnosed to have pure tight mitral stenosis. All the following are suitable treatments EXCEPT:

A young lady was diagnosed to have pure tight mitral stenosis. All the following are suitable treatments EXCEPT:

  1. A Percutaneous balloon mitral valvotomy (PMV)
  2. B Closed mitral valvotomy
  3. C Open mitral valvotomy
  4. D Mitral valve replacement
  5. E Mitral annuloplasty
💡 查看答案
E

Mitral annuloplasty is for mitral REGURGITATION (repair), NOT pure tight stenosis. PMV, open/closed mitral valvotomy, and MVR are all suitable for mitral stenosis.

CTS·MCQ008 2001 MCQ50 MCQ All the following are common causes of lung abscess EXCEPT:

All the following are common causes of lung abscess EXCEPT:

  1. A Bronchogenic carcinoma
  2. B Aspiration
  3. C Staphylococcal pneumonia
  4. D Tuberculosis
  5. E Haematoma
💡 查看答案
E

Haematoma is NOT a common cause of lung abscess. Bronchogenic CA (post-obstructive), aspiration, staphylococcal pneumonia, and TB are common causes.

CTS·MCQ009 2002 MCQ2, 2002# MCQ43 MCQ Which of the following symptoms or signs is associated with the POOREST prognosis in patients with aortic stenosis?

Which of the following symptoms or signs is associated with the POOREST prognosis in patients with aortic stenosis?

  1. A Syncope
  2. B Angina pectoris
  3. C Congestive heart failure
  4. D Palpitation
  5. E Exertional dyspnoea
💡 查看答案
C

CHF is the poorest prognostic sign in aortic stenosis (mean survival 1-2 years). Syncope (~3yr), angina (~5yr).

CTS·MCQ010 2002 MCQ28, 2004 MCQ76, 2006 MCQ51, 2018 MCQ46, 2021 MCQ?? MCQ Traumatic rupture of the thoracic aorta after blunt injury MOST commonly occurs:

Traumatic rupture of the thoracic aorta after blunt injury MOST commonly occurs:

  1. A In the ascending aorta
  2. B In the aortic arch proximal to the left subclavian artery
  3. C In the proximal descending aorta
  4. D At the level of the diaphragm
  5. E With equal distribution throughout the thoracic aorta
💡 查看答案
C

Traumatic aortic rupture most commonly occurs at the aortic isthmus (proximal descending aorta just distal to left subclavian — ligamentum arteriosum tethering point).

CTS·MCQ011 2002 MCQ31, 2002# MCQ45 MCQ The type of bronchogenic carcinoma MOST likely to produce malignant cells on sputum cytology is:

The type of bronchogenic carcinoma MOST likely to produce malignant cells on sputum cytology is:

  1. A Adenocarcinoma
  2. B Squamous cell carcinoma
  3. C Large cell carcinoma
  4. D Brochioalveolar carcinoma
  5. E Small cell carcinoma
💡 查看答案
B

Squamous cell carcinoma is most likely to yield positive sputum cytology (central location, exfoliates into airways). Adenocarcinoma is peripheral.

CTS·MCQ012 2002 MCQ51, 2005 MCQ68, 2018 MCQ47 MCQ Which conduit used in coronary artery bypass grafting has been shown to have the HIGHEST long-term patency rate?

Which conduit used in coronary artery bypass grafting has been shown to have the HIGHEST long-term patency rate?

  1. A Long saphenous vein
  2. B Internal mammary artery
  3. C Radial artery
  4. D Inferior epigastric artery
  5. E Gastroepiploic artery
💡 查看答案
B

Left internal mammary artery (LIMA) has the highest long-term patency rate for CABG (>90% at 10 years). Saphenous vein ~50% at 10 years.

CTS·MCQ013 2002 MCQ60, 2002# MCQ47, 2003 MCQ75 MCQ The superior vena cava syndrome is MOST commonly associated with:

The superior vena cava syndrome is MOST commonly associated with:

  1. A Tuberculosis
  2. B Retrosternal goitre
  3. C Malignant lymphoma
  4. D Bronchogenic carcinoma
  5. E Thrombosis due to an indwelling catheter
💡 查看答案
D

SVC syndrome is most commonly associated with bronchogenic carcinoma (~70-80% of cases). Lymphoma is second most common.

CTS·MCQ014 2002 MCQ82 MCQ The following are generally accepted contraindications to surgery for patients with bronchogenic carcinoma EXCEPT:

The following are generally accepted contraindications to surgery for patients with bronchogenic carcinoma EXCEPT:

  1. A Paralysis of the phrenic nerve
  2. B Pleural fluid positive for tumour cells
  3. C Localized chest wall involvement
  4. D Superior vena cava syndrome
  5. E Invasion of the tumour into the vertebra
💡 查看答案
C

Localized chest wall involvement (T3) is NOT a contraindication — these can be resected en bloc. Phrenic nerve paralysis, malignant effusion, SVC syndrome, and vertebral invasion are contraindications.

CTS·MCQ015 2002 MCQ86, 2002# MCQ48 MCQ Complications of an untreated thoracic empyema include the following EXCEPT:

Complications of an untreated thoracic empyema include the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Contraction of the thoracic cavity
  2. B Bronchopleural fistula
  3. C Empyema necessitatis
  4. D Trap lung
  5. E Bronchogenic carcinoma
💡 查看答案
E

Bronchogenic carcinoma is NOT a complication of empyema. Complications include thoracic contraction, bronchopleural fistula, empyema necessitatis, and trapped lung.

CTS·MCQ016 2002# MCQ81 MCQ The IMMEDIATE treatment for traumatic haemopneumothorax is insertion of a large bore chest drain into the chest through the:

The IMMEDIATE treatment for traumatic haemopneumothorax is insertion of a large bore chest drain into the chest through the:

  1. A Second intercostal space at mid-clavicular line
  2. B Second intercostal space at mid-axillary line
  3. C Fifth intercostal space at mid-axillary line
  4. D Seventh intercostal space at mid-axillary line
  5. E Ninth intercostal space at mid-axillary line
💡 查看答案
C

5th ICS mid-axillary line (safe triangle) for chest drain insertion in haemopneumothorax.

CTS·MCQ017 2002# MCQ82, 2014 MCQ17, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ The following statements concerning cardiac myxoma are true EXCEPT that it:

The following statements concerning cardiac myxoma are true EXCEPT that it:

  1. A Is the commonest benign cardiac tumour
  2. B Can be familial with an autosomal dominant inheritance
  3. C Is commonly located in the left atrium
  4. D Tends to be more common in men
  5. E Arises from the endothelium
💡 查看答案
D

Cardiac myxoma is MORE common in women (female:male 2-3:1), not men. It IS the commonest benign cardiac tumour, can be familial (Carney complex), and arises from left atrium commonly.

CTS·MCQ018 2002# MCQ83 MCQ The MOST common malignant primary chest wall tumour is:

The MOST common malignant primary chest wall tumour is:

  1. A Chondrosarcoma
  2. B Osteosarcoma
  3. C Ewing’s sarcoma
  4. D Fibrosarcoma
  5. E Malignant fibrous histiocytoma
💡 查看答案
A

Chondrosarcoma is the most common primary malignant chest wall tumour.

CTS·MCQ019 2002# MCQ85 MCQ Which of the following is NOT an acceptable treatment option for hyperhidrosis?

Which of the following is NOT an acceptable treatment option for hyperhidrosis?

  1. A Thoracoscopic sympathectomy
  2. B Calcium channel blocker
  3. C Iontophoresis
  4. D Botulinum toxin injection
  5. E Anticholinergic drugs
💡 查看答案
B

Calcium channel blockers are NOT an accepted treatment for hyperhidrosis. Sympathectomy, iontophoresis, botox, and anticholinergics are all accepted treatments.

CTS·MCQ020 2003 MCQ10 MCQ The clinical features of tension pneumothorax include all the following EXCEPT:

The clinical features of tension pneumothorax include all the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Systemic hypotension
  2. B Distended neck veins
  3. C Trachea deviated to the affected side
  4. D Sucking chest wound
  5. E Reduced air entry on the affected side
💡 查看答案
C

In tension pneumothorax, trachea deviates AWAY from the affected side (pushed by pressure), not towards it. Also: sucking wound = open pneumothorax, not necessarily tension.

CTS·MCQ021 2003 MCQ26 MCQ The COMMONEST primary neoplasm of the heart is:

The COMMONEST primary neoplasm of the heart is:

  1. A Angiosarcoma
  2. B Fibroma
  3. C Rhabdomyosarcoma
  4. D Myxoma
  5. E Haemangioma
💡 查看答案
D

Myxoma is the commonest primary cardiac neoplasm (benign). Angiosarcoma is commonest primary MALIGNANT cardiac tumour.

CTS·MCQ022 2003 MCQ36, 2004 MCQ2, 2009 MCQ8 MCQ A 25 years old gentleman was involved in a motor vehicle accident. On arrival at the Accident and Emergency Department he was breathless. On examination his tra…

A 25 years old gentleman was involved in a motor vehicle accident. On arrival at the Accident and Emergency Department he was breathless. On examination his trachea was deviated to the right side, his left chest was resonant to percussion with reduced breath side on auscultation. His right chest’s breath sound was also reduced. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Traumatic haemopericardium
  2. B Head injury producing sensation of breathlessness
  3. C Laceration of superior vena cava producing haemothorax on the right side
  4. D Tension pneumothorax on the left side
  5. E Tracheal laceration
💡 查看答案
D

Trachea deviated right + left chest resonant + reduced air entry left = tension pneumothorax on left side.

CTS·MCQ023 2003 MCQ43 MCQ The COMMONEST posterior mediastinal tumour is:

The COMMONEST posterior mediastinal tumour is:

  1. A Germ cell tumour
  2. B Thymoma
  3. C Mesenchymal tumour
  4. D Neurogenic tumour
  5. E Lymphoma
💡 查看答案
D

Neurogenic tumours (schwannoma, neurofibroma, ganglioneuroma) are the commonest posterior mediastinal tumours.

CTS·MCQ024 2003 MCQ81 MCQ The clinical features of constrictive pericarditis include all of the following EXCEPT:

The clinical features of constrictive pericarditis include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Ascites
  2. B Cyanosis
  3. C Peripheral oedema
  4. D Pulsus paradoxus
  5. E Raised jugular venous pressure
💡 查看答案
B

Cyanosis is NOT typical of constrictive pericarditis. Features: ascites, peripheral oedema, pulsus paradoxus, raised JVP (with Kussmaul's sign), Friedreich's sign.

CTS·MCQ025 2004 MCQ12 MCQ A 35 years old lady, who has symptoms and signs of mitral stenosis, is found to have a 2 cm mass in the left atrium. The mass is MOST likely to be:

A 35 years old lady, who has symptoms and signs of mitral stenosis, is found to have a 2 cm mass in the left atrium. The mass is MOST likely to be:

  1. A Metastatic carcinoma
  2. B Malignant lymphoma
  3. C Rhabdomyoma
  4. D Myxoma
  5. E Haemangioma
💡 查看答案
D

35/F with mitral stenosis symptoms + 2cm left atrial mass = cardiac myxoma (most common primary cardiac tumour in this location).

CTS·MCQ026 2004 MCQ26 MCQ All of the following symptoms and signs are indicative of a tension pneumothorax EXCEPT:

All of the following symptoms and signs are indicative of a tension pneumothorax EXCEPT:

  1. A Absent breath sounds unilaterally
  2. B Chest pain
  3. C Shifting of the trachea toward the pneumothorax
  4. D Shortness of breath
  5. E Hypotension
💡 查看答案
C

In tension pneumothorax, trachea shifts AWAY from the affected side. All other signs (absent breath sounds, chest pain, SOB, hypotension) are present.

CTS·MCQ027 2004 MCQ38 MCQ Indications for resection in patients with pulmonary tuberculosis include all the following EXCEPT:

Indications for resection in patients with pulmonary tuberculosis include all the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Destroyed lung or lobe
  2. B Multiple drug resistant tuberculosis
  3. C Massive haemoptysis
  4. D Residual cavity with negative sputum
  5. E Suspected carcinoma
💡 查看答案
D

Residual cavity with negative sputum is NOT an indication for resection (can be observed). Destroyed lung, MDR-TB, massive haemoptysis, and suspected CA are indications.

CTS·MCQ028 2004 MCQ44 MCQ All of the following diagnoses in a 60 years old gentleman with a peripheral solitary pulmonary nodule are likely EXCEPT:

All of the following diagnoses in a 60 years old gentleman with a peripheral solitary pulmonary nodule are likely EXCEPT:

  1. A Tuberculosis
  2. B Bronchogenic carcinoma
  3. C Hamartoma
  4. D Metastatic colonic carcinoma
  5. E Sarcoidosis
💡 查看答案
E

Sarcoidosis rarely presents as a solitary peripheral nodule — it typically causes hilar lymphadenopathy. TB, bronchogenic CA, hamartoma, and metastatic CRC can all present as solitary pulmonary nodule.

CTS·MCQ029 2004 MCQ62 MCQ The following findings indicate incurability of a lung cancer EXCEPT:

The following findings indicate incurability of a lung cancer EXCEPT:

  1. A Recurrent pneumonia
  2. B Left vocal cord paralysis
  3. C Malignant pleural effusion
  4. D Malignant pericardial effusion
  5. E Supraclavicular lymph node metastasis
💡 查看答案
A

Recurrent pneumonia (post-obstructive) does NOT indicate incurability — the obstruction may be resectable. Left vocal cord paralysis, malignant effusion, malignant pericardial effusion, and supraclavicular LN mets indicate incurability.

CTS·MCQ030 2005 MCQ9 MCQ All the following symptoms and signs are indicative of an acute pericardial tamponade EXCEPT:

All the following symptoms and signs are indicative of an acute pericardial tamponade EXCEPT:

  1. A Systemic hypotension
  2. B Distended neck vein
  3. C Pulsus paradoxus
  4. D Ascites
  5. E Muffled heart sounds
💡 查看答案
D

Ascites is NOT typical of ACUTE pericardial tamponade (it develops in chronic). Acute tamponade: hypotension, distended neck veins, pulsus paradoxus, muffled heart sounds.

CTS·MCQ031 2005 MCQ24 MCQ A 3 years old child with congenital cyanosis is MOST probably suffering from

A 3 years old child with congenital cyanosis is MOST probably suffering from

  1. A Tetralogy of Fallot
  2. B Ventricular septal defect
  3. C Tricuspid atresia
  4. D Transposition of the great vessels
  5. E Patent ductus arteriosus
💡 查看答案
A

Tetralogy of Fallot is the most common cyanotic congenital heart disease. (TGA presents at birth; ToF more common in 3-year-olds with established cyanosis.)

CTS·MCQ032 2005 MCQ41 MCQ Mitral stenosis is associated with all the following conditions EXCEPT:

Mitral stenosis is associated with all the following conditions EXCEPT:

  1. A Haemoptysis
  2. B Arterial emboli
  3. C Low left atrial pressures
  4. D Atrial fibrillation
  5. E Tricuspid regurgitation
💡 查看答案
C

Mitral stenosis causes HIGH left atrial pressures (not low). Haemoptysis, arterial emboli, AF, and tricuspid regurgitation are all associated.

CTS·MCQ033 2005 MCQ44 MCQ Unfavourable prognostic findings in a patient with non-small cell carcinoma include all of the following EXCEPT

Unfavourable prognostic findings in a patient with non-small cell carcinoma include all of the following EXCEPT

  1. A Bronchioalveolar carcinoma cell type
  2. B Hoarseness of voice
  3. C Pulmonary osteoarthropathy
  4. D Chest wall invasion
  5. E Pericardial effusion
💡 查看答案
A

Bronchioalveolar (lepidic) carcinoma has relatively favourable prognosis. Hoarseness (RLN invasion), pulmonary osteoarthropathy (not unfavourable per se — but less unfavourable than others), chest wall invasion, and pericardial effusion are unfavourable.

CTS·MCQ034 2005 MCQ46 MCQ The superior vena cava syndrome is MOST frequently seen in association with

The superior vena cava syndrome is MOST frequently seen in association with

  1. A Retrosternal goitre
  2. B Thoracic aortic dissection
  3. C Thoracic aortic aneurysm
  4. D Constrictive pericarditis
  5. E Bronchogenic carcinoma
💡 查看答案
E

SVC syndrome is most frequently associated with bronchogenic carcinoma.

CTS·MCQ035 2005 MCQ65 MCQ Middle mediastinal masses include all of the following EXCEPT:

Middle mediastinal masses include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Lymphoma
  2. B Ganglioneuroma
  3. C Bronchogenic cyst
  4. D Pericardial cyst
  5. E Esophageal duplication cyst
💡 查看答案
B

Ganglioneuroma is a posterior mediastinal tumour. Middle mediastinal masses: lymphoma, bronchogenic cyst, pericardial cyst, esophageal duplication cyst.

CTS·MCQ036 2006 MCQ24 MCQ Bronchoscopy not indicated in:

Bronchoscopy not indicated in:

  1. A Pulmonary edema
💡 查看答案
A

Bronchoscopy NOT indicated in pulmonary edema (no diagnostic value, risk of worsening). Only option available.

CTS·MCQ037 2006 MCQ48 MCQ What is commonest site of cardiac myxoma?

What is commonest site of cardiac myxoma?

  1. A Left atrium
  2. B Right atrium
  3. C Left ventricle
  4. D Right ventricle
  5. E All chambers have similar incidence
💡 查看答案
A

Left atrium is the commonest site for cardiac myxoma (~75% of cases).

CTS·MCQ038 2006 MCQ49 MCQ Which of the following is not associated with long term prognosis in lung cancer patients?

Which of the following is not associated with long term prognosis in lung cancer patients?

  1. A Smoking habit
  2. B Nutritional status
  3. C Functional status
  4. D Family history
💡 查看答案
D

Family history is not significantly associated with long-term prognosis in lung cancer. Smoking habit, nutritional status, and functional status all affect prognosis.

CTS·MCQ039 2006 MCQ50 MCQ Which of the following is not an indication for surgery after acute myocardial infarction?

Which of the following is not an indication for surgery after acute myocardial infarction?

  1. A Ventricular aneurysm
  2. B Ventricular rupture
  3. C Ventricular septal defect
  4. D Premature ventricular beat
  5. E Papillary muscles dysfunction leading to mitral regurgitation
💡 查看答案
D

Premature ventricular beats alone are NOT an indication for surgery post-MI. Ventricular aneurysm, rupture, VSD, and papillary muscle dysfunction causing MR are surgical indications.

CTS·MCQ040 2006 MCQ77 MCQ How is rejection monitored after cardiac transplant?

How is rejection monitored after cardiac transplant?

  1. A Blood pressure
  2. B Cardiac output
  3. C ECG
  4. D Endocardial biopsy
  5. E White cell count
💡 查看答案
D

Endomyocardial biopsy (endocardial biopsy) is the gold standard for monitoring cardiac transplant rejection.

CTS·MCQ041 2007 MCQ16, 2015 MCQ37 MCQ Differential diagnoses of anterior mediastinal mass include all of the following except:

Differential diagnoses of anterior mediastinal mass include all of the following except:

  1. A Thymoma
  2. B Neurogenic tumour
  3. C Retrosternal goitre
  4. D Lymphoma
  5. E Germ cell tumour
💡 查看答案
B

Neurogenic tumours are POSTERIOR mediastinal, not anterior. Anterior mediastinal masses: thymoma, retrosternal goitre, lymphoma, germ cell tumour (4 T's: Thymoma, Teratoma/germ cell, Thyroid, Terrible lymphoma).

CTS·MCQ042 2007 MCQ24 MCQ Which of the following features is prominent in patient with a major pulmonary embolus?

Which of the following features is prominent in patient with a major pulmonary embolus?

  1. A A matched defect on isotopic ventilation / perfusion scanning
  2. B Pleuritic pain and haemoptysis
  3. C Peripheral but not central cyanosis
  4. D Hypopnoea
  5. E ECG evidence of left ventricular strain pattern
💡 查看答案
B

Minor PE presents with pleuritic pain and haemoptysis. Major PE: central cyanosis (not just peripheral), tachypnoea (not hypopnoea), RIGHT ventricular strain on ECG, V/Q mismatch (not matched).

CTS·MCQ043 2007 MCQ75 MCQ Which can be the content of cardioplegic solution used for cardiac surgery?

Which can be the content of cardioplegic solution used for cardiac surgery?

  1. A Potassium
  2. B Mannitol
  3. C Calcium
  4. D Adrenaline
  5. E Steroid
💡 查看答案
A

High potassium concentration is the key component of cardioplegic solution — induces diastolic arrest.

CTS·MCQ044 2009 MCQ81, 2020 MCQ20 MCQ Which of the following arteries is the principle blood supply for the upper two-thirds of trachea?

Which of the following arteries is the principle blood supply for the upper two-thirds of trachea?

  1. A Aortobronchial artery
  2. B External carotid artery
  3. C Inferior thyroid artery
  4. D Internal thoracic artery
  5. E Transverse cervical artery
💡 查看答案
C

Inferior thyroid artery is the principal blood supply for the upper two-thirds of trachea.

CTS·MCQ045 2009 MCQ83, 2020 MCQ88 MCQ What is the most effective way to decrease the myocardial metabolic rate?

What is the most effective way to decrease the myocardial metabolic rate?

  1. A Hypothermia
  2. B Cardioplegic
  3. C Inducing ventricular fibrillation
  4. D Venting the ventricle
  5. E IV adenosine
💡 查看答案
A

Hypothermia is the most effective way to decrease myocardial metabolic rate (↓temp → ↓O₂ consumption). Cardioplegia arrests the heart but hypothermia reduces overall metabolic demand more effectively.

CTS·MCQ046 2010 MCQ14 MCQ 78/F, good past health, 2cm adenocarcinoma in the left upper lobe of the lung. FEV1 is 95% of her predicted value. Staging investigation shows no metastases. Wh…

78/F, good past health, 2cm adenocarcinoma in the left upper lobe of the lung. FEV1 is 95% of her predicted value. Staging investigation shows no metastases. Which of the following is the most recommended?

  1. A Chemotherapy with palliative intent
  2. B Left pneumonectomy with radical mediastinal lymph node dissection
  3. C Left lobectomy with selective lymph node sampling
  4. D Wedge resection of lesion
  5. E Radiotherapy with curative intent
💡 查看答案
C

78/F, 2cm adenoCA, FEV1 95%, no mets → left upper lobectomy with selective LN sampling. Standard surgical resection for stage I NSCLC in fit patient.

CTS·MCQ047 2010 MCQ27 MCQ A 25 years old male motorcyclist had a road traffic accident. He was conscious and came in with dyspnoea and pain over the right chest and abdomen. His pulse wa…

A 25 years old male motorcyclist had a road traffic accident. He was conscious and came in with dyspnoea and pain over the right chest and abdomen. His pulse was 120/min, BP 80/50 mmHg, SaO2 90% on 100% oxygen. Chest x-ray showed right sided 4th–7th rib fractures and mediastinal shift to the left side. Right hydropneumothorax was confirmed. What is the best management at this conjuncture?

  1. A Laparotomy
  2. B Endotracheal intubation
  3. C Insert right chest drain
  4. D Blood transfusion
  5. E CT scan
💡 查看答案
C

Young man, RTA, dyspnoea, right rib fractures + haemothorax + liver laceration → first priority is right chest drain (manage life-threatening haemopneumothorax before addressing abdominal injury).

CTS·MCQ048 2011 MCQ1, 2018 MCQ49 MCQ A 76 years old gentleman, chronic smoker, is diagnosed with 2 cm adenocarcinoma at right lung. PET CT showed no other metastases except hypermetabolic paratrach…

A 76 years old gentleman, chronic smoker, is diagnosed with 2 cm adenocarcinoma at right lung. PET CT showed no other metastases except hypermetabolic paratracheal LN suspicious of metastasis. What is the management?

  1. A EBUS + transtracheal biopsy
  2. B Right thoracotomy
  3. C Chemotherapy
  4. D Radiotherapy
  5. E Tyrosine kinase inhibitor
💡 查看答案
A

2cm adenoCA + suspicious paratracheal LN → EBUS + transtracheal biopsy to confirm/exclude N2 disease before deciding on surgery vs chemo.

CTS·MCQ049 2011 MCQ2, 2017 MCQ?? MCQ 50/F with 2 cm left upper lobe CA lung. She never smokes. FEV1 is 50% of predicted value for her age and sex. What would you do next?

50/F with 2 cm left upper lobe CA lung. She never smokes. FEV1 is 50% of predicted value for her age and sex. What would you do next?

  1. A Radiotherapy with curative intent
  2. B Left upper lobe lobectomy with VATS
  3. C Wedge resection with limited mediastinal LN harvest
  4. D Mediastinoscopy
  5. E Exercise testing
💡 查看答案
E

FEV1 50% predicted → borderline for lobectomy → exercise testing (shuttle walk/CPET) to assess physiological reserve before deciding surgical approach.

CTS·MCQ050 2011 MCQ5, 2016 MCQ76, 2018 MCQ50 MCQ Which of the following is not a component of cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) circuit?

Which of the following is not a component of cardiopulmonary bypass (CPB) circuit?

  1. A Arterial line filter
  2. B Heat exchanger
  3. C Oxygenator
  4. D Roller pump
  5. E Ventilator
💡 查看答案
E

Ventilator is NOT part of CPB circuit. CPB consists of: arterial line filter, heat exchanger, oxygenator, roller pump (+ reservoir, venous line).

CTS·MCQ051 2012 MCQ43, 2013 MCQ87, 2017 MCQ??, 2019 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the following is the EuroSCORE predictor of death after CABG or valve surgery?

Which of the following is the EuroSCORE predictor of death after CABG or valve surgery?

  1. A Diabetes mellitus
  2. B Female
  3. C Hyperlipidaemia
  4. D Hypertension
  5. E Previous coronary artery stenting
💡 查看答案
B

Female sex is a EuroSCORE predictor of mortality after cardiac surgery. DM (A), hyperlipidaemia (C), HT (D), and previous stenting (E) are not EuroSCORE variables.

CTS·MCQ052 2012 MCQ44, 2019 MCQ37 MCQ Which vascular conduit to left anterior descending artery provides the best prognosis for CABG?

Which vascular conduit to left anterior descending artery provides the best prognosis for CABG?

  1. A Free left internal mammary artery
  2. B Free left radial artery
  3. C Pedicle left gastroepiploic artery
  4. D Pedicle left internal mammary artery
  5. E Pedicle right internal mammary artery
💡 查看答案
D

Pedicled left internal mammary artery (LIMA) to LAD provides the best prognosis for CABG — highest long-term patency.

CTS·MCQ053 2013 MCQ90 MCQ What is the management of patient with unstable angina and cardiogenic shock?

What is the management of patient with unstable angina and cardiogenic shock?

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Bi-ventricular pacing
  3. C Dopamine
  4. D Heparin
  5. E Intra-aortic balloon pump
💡 查看答案
E

Intra-aortic balloon pump (IABP) is the definitive mechanical support for cardiogenic shock with unstable angina — augments coronary perfusion and reduces afterload.

CTS·MCQ054 2014 MCQ19, 2017 MCQ55 MCQ Which of the following is CORRECT regarding treatment for spontaneous pneumothorax?

Which of the following is CORRECT regarding treatment for spontaneous pneumothorax?

  1. A History of tension pneumothorax is an indication for surgery
  2. B Observation is recommended for asymptomatic patients with pneumothorax less than 40%
  3. C Prior pneumonectomy is not an indication for surgery
  4. D Recurrence rate rises to 90% after the 2nd episode of pneumothorax
  5. E Talc powder is the optimal sclerosing agent for young people
💡 查看答案
A

History of tension pneumothorax is an indication for surgical pleurodesis/intervention to prevent recurrence. Asymptomatic small PTX can be observed (B). Prior contralateral PTX is an indication (C wrong). Talc not recommended for young patients (E wrong — chemical pleurodesis preferred in older).

CTS·MCQ055 2014 MCQ21 MCQ In the assessment of a suspected primary lung carcinoma, fibre-optic bronchoscopy can potentially provide all of the following except:

In the assessment of a suspected primary lung carcinoma, fibre-optic bronchoscopy can potentially provide all of the following except:

  1. A Assessment of airway stenosis
  2. B Assessment of inferior mediastinal lymph node metastasis
  3. C Assessment of T staging
  4. D Cytological diagnosis of a parenchymal lung mass
  5. E Determination of the extent of resection
💡 查看答案
B

Bronchoscopy cannot reliably assess inferior mediastinal lymph nodes (subcarinal can be reached but not inferior). It can assess airway stenosis, T-staging, cytology, and guide resection planning.

CTS·MCQ056 2015 MCQ38 MCQ Which of the following does not help in differentiating transudative and exudative pleural effusion?

Which of the following does not help in differentiating transudative and exudative pleural effusion?

  1. A Cholesterol level of pleural fluid
  2. B LDH > 0.6 upper normal limit of serum
  3. C Protein ratio
  4. D LDH ratio
  5. E Volume of pleural effusion
💡 查看答案
E

Volume of pleural effusion does not help differentiate transudate from exudate. Light's criteria use: cholesterol, LDH ratios, protein ratio, LDH level.

CTS·MCQ057 2015 MCQ39 MCQ Which would be the ideal treatment for stage I non-small cell lung cancer for a fit 45 years old man?

Which would be the ideal treatment for stage I non-small cell lung cancer for a fit 45 years old man?

  1. A Chemotherapy
  2. B Lobectomy
  3. C Pneumonectomy
  4. D Radiofrequency ablation
  5. E Radiotherapy
💡 查看答案
B

Stage I NSCLC in fit 45/M → lobectomy is the gold standard surgical treatment. Wedge resection has higher recurrence. Chemo/RT alone inferior for stage I.

CTS·MCQ058 2015 MCQ40 MCQ Which of the following is true about sympathectomy?

Which of the following is true about sympathectomy?

  1. A Compensatory hyperhidrosis will resolve spontaneously
  2. B It does not cause Horner’s syndrome
  3. C It is a recommended treatment for hyperhidrosis
  4. D Minimally invasive approach is not feasible
  5. E Post-operative result may not be apparent immediately after surgery
💡 查看答案
C

Sympathectomy IS a recommended treatment for hyperhidrosis (TRUE). Compensatory sweating does NOT resolve spontaneously (A wrong). It CAN cause Horner's (B wrong). VATS is minimally invasive (D wrong). Effect is usually immediate (E wrong).

CTS·MCQ059 2015 MCQ41 MCQ Which of the following is NOT the indication for lung transplantation?

Which of the following is NOT the indication for lung transplantation?

  1. A Bronchoalveolar carcinoma
  2. B Cystic fibrosis
  3. C Emphysema
  4. D Pulmonary hypertension
  5. E Pulmonary tuberculosis
💡 查看答案
A

Bronchoalveolar carcinoma (malignancy) is NOT an indication for lung transplantation. CF, emphysema, pulmonary hypertension, and end-stage pulmonary TB (destroyed lung) can be indications.

CTS·MCQ060 2015 MCQ42, 2018 MCQ51 MCQ Which of the following is true about lung volume reduction surgery for emphysema?

Which of the following is true about lung volume reduction surgery for emphysema?

  1. A It exaggerates the hyperexpansion of chest
  2. B It can delay requirement for lung transplant
  3. C It flattens the diaphragm
  4. D It causes an increase in TLC
  5. E It does not improve survival
💡 查看答案
B

LVRS can delay the requirement for lung transplantation. It reduces hyperexpansion (A wrong — reduces, not exaggerates). It elevates the diaphragm (C wrong — not flatten). TLC decreases (D wrong). It CAN improve survival in selected patients (E wrong).

CTS·MCQ061 2016 MCQ64 MCQ 46/M. CA lung in the right upper lobe with hoarseness. Smoker since 16 years old. Which is the most suggestive of distant spread?

46/M. CA lung in the right upper lobe with hoarseness. Smoker since 16 years old. Which is the most suggestive of distant spread?

  1. A Hypercalcemia
  2. B Pancoast syndrome
  3. C SIADH
  4. D Gynaecomastia
  5. E Cushing-like syndrome
💡 查看答案
A

Hypercalcemia suggests distant spread (paraneoplastic — PTHrP from squamous cell). Pancoast syndrome and SIADH are local/regional effects. Gynaecomastia and Cushing-like are paraneoplastic but less suggestive of distant spread than hypercalcemia.

CTS·MCQ062 2016 MCQ73, 2019 MCQ??, 2020 MCQ80, 2021 MCQ29 MCQ What is the commonest complication after lobectomy?

What is the commonest complication after lobectomy?

  1. A Atrial fibrillation
  2. B Symptomatic emphysema
  3. C Bronchopulmonary fistula
  4. D Trapping of air inside thoracic cavity causing midline shift of internal organs
  5. E Segmental atelectasis
💡 查看答案
A

Atrial fibrillation is the most common complication after lobectomy (up to 20-30% in elderly patients).

CTS·MCQ063 2016 MCQ75 MCQ 32/F with good past health. Incidental finding of a 5 cm mass at anterior mediastinum. Surgery was planned. The routine preoperative workup would include the fo…

32/F with good past health. Incidental finding of a 5 cm mass at anterior mediastinum. Surgery was planned. The routine preoperative workup would include the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Serum AFP
  2. B Serum anti-cholinergic receptor autoantibodies
  3. C Serum thyroxine
  4. D Mediastinoscopy
  5. E Ultrasound guided percutaneous biopsy
💡 查看答案
D

Mediastinoscopy is invasive and NOT routine preoperative workup — it's done for staging when indicated. AFP (germ cell), anti-AChR Ab (thymoma/MG), and TFT (goitre) are appropriate. Percutaneous biopsy is also reasonable.

CTS·MCQ064 2016 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following cannot account for an anterior mediastinal mass?

Which of the following cannot account for an anterior mediastinal mass?

  1. A Thymoma
  2. B Retrosternal goitre
  3. C Germ cell tumour
  4. D Pericardial cyst
  5. E Lymphoma
💡 查看答案
D

Pericardial cyst is a MIDDLE mediastinal mass, not anterior. Thymoma, retrosternal goitre, germ cell tumour, and lymphoma are all anterior mediastinal masses.

CTS·MCQ065 2017 MCQ52 MCQ The following is true about mitral valve replacement EXCEPT:

The following is true about mitral valve replacement EXCEPT:

  1. A Patients will require lifelong anticoagulation
  2. B Concomitant repair of ASD does not increase mortality
  3. C Concomitant CABG increases mortality
  4. D Mitral valve repair has better postoperative outcome than mitral valve replacement
  5. E >90% of degenerative valve diseases are repairable
💡 查看答案
C

Concomitant CABG does NOT increase mortality after MVR in most studies — outcomes are acceptable. Lifelong anticoagulation needed (A true). ASD repair doesn't increase mortality (B true). Repair > replacement (D true). >90% degenerative repairable (E true).

CTS·MCQ066 2017 MCQ54 MCQ Which of the following is not part of the diagnostic criteria for exudative pleural effusion?

Which of the following is not part of the diagnostic criteria for exudative pleural effusion?

  1. A Glucose < 60 mg/dL
  2. B pH < 7.0
  3. C Pleural fluid LDH > 2⁄3 of serum LDH
  4. D Pleural fluid protein >0.5 of serum protein
  5. E Pleural fluid LDH > 0.6 of serum LDH
💡 查看答案
A

Glucose <60 mg/dL and pH <7.0 are features of complicated parapneumonic effusion/empyema, not Light's criteria for exudate. Light's criteria: pleural protein/serum protein >0.5, pleural LDH/serum LDH >0.6, pleural LDH >2/3 upper normal serum.

CTS·MCQ067 2018 MCQ48, 2019 MCQ??, 2021 MCQ40 MCQ A 52 years old man, with chronic stable angina undergoes elective coronary angiography. It shows that the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right c…

A 52 years old man, with chronic stable angina undergoes elective coronary angiography. It shows that the left anterior descending, left circumflex, and right coronary arteries all have 70% stenosis in proximal part. Left ventriculogram shows that the ejection fraction of the left ventricle is 35%; which of the following is the best treatment for him?

  1. A Coronary artery bypass surgery using venous graft for all three vessels
  2. B Coronary artery bypass surgery using left internal mammary artery graft to the left anterior descending artery, and venous grafts to the other two vessels
  3. C Percutaneous intervention with balloon dilatation and stenting
  4. D Maximize medical therapy, closely follow up
  5. E Laser transmyocardial revascularisation
💡 查看答案
B

3-vessel disease with preserved LV function → CABG using LIMA to LAD + venous grafts. LIMA-LAD is essential for best long-term outcome.

CTS·MCQ068 2019 MCQ64 MCQ A 70 years old man presents with a 2 cm asymptomatic apical lung lesion. CT revealed 3x3.5 cm mass over Right upper lobe and also multiple 1-2cm mediastinal LN.…

A 70 years old man presents with a 2 cm asymptomatic apical lung lesion. CT revealed 3x3.5 cm mass over Right upper lobe and also multiple 1-2cm mediastinal LN. Percutaneous biopsy of lung mass revealed adenoCA. Lung function test shows that FEV1 is 2.2 litres (85% of predicted value). What is the next management?

  1. A Mediastinoscopy
  2. B Palliative treatment
  3. C Neoadjuvant chemotherapy, then consider right upper lobectomy
  4. D Chemotherapy and RT
  5. E Right upper lobectomy + systemic lymph node sampling
💡 查看答案
A

2cm lung mass + multiple mediastinal LN → need mediastinoscopy to confirm N2 disease status before deciding on resectability. If N2 confirmed, may need neoadjuvant chemo first.

CTS·MCQ069 2020 MCQ17 MCQ Traumatic hemothorax immediate management is chest drain at:

Traumatic hemothorax immediate management is chest drain at:

  1. A 5th ICS, MCL
  2. B 5th ICS, MAL
  3. C 2th ICS, MCL
  4. D 2th ICS, MAL
  5. E 7th ICS, MAL
💡 查看答案
B

Traumatic haemothorax → chest drain at 5th ICS, mid-axillary line (safe triangle).

CTS·MCQ070 2020 MCQ27, 2021 MCQ63 MCQ A 25 years old man presented with right pneumothorax. Which of the following is an indication for surgical pleurodesis?

A 25 years old man presented with right pneumothorax. Which of the following is an indication for surgical pleurodesis?

  1. A Lung cancer
  2. B First spontaneous pneumothorax
  3. C History of primary left pneumothorax 2 years ago
  4. D Young age
  5. E Smoker
💡 查看答案
C

History of contralateral (left) pneumothorax = recurrent PTX → indication for surgical pleurodesis to prevent bilateral pneumothorax. First episode (B), young age (D), and smoking (E) are not indications alone.

CTS·MCQ071 2020 MCQ85 MCQ Chronic smoker. Right shoulder pain at night radiation to arm.

Chronic smoker. Right shoulder pain at night radiation to arm.

  1. A Thoracic outlet syndrome
  2. B Mesothelioma
  3. C Horner
  4. D Pancoast tumor
  5. E CA lung in RUL with invasion to superior vena cava
💡 查看答案
D

Right shoulder pain worse at night, radiation to arm in chronic smoker = Pancoast tumour (superior sulcus tumour invading brachial plexus).

CTS·MCQ072 2020 MCQ89 MCQ 65/F after long haul flight developed DVT, ECG shows sinus tachycardia with S1Q3T3 pattern. Echo shows some RV dysfunction, which of the following is an indicat…

65/F after long haul flight developed DVT, ECG shows sinus tachycardia with S1Q3T3 pattern. Echo shows some RV dysfunction, which of the following is an indication for surgical management for her?

  1. A Hemodynamic and respiratory compromise
  2. B Multiple previous episodes of DVT
  3. C Pregnancy
  4. D Patient has cancer
  5. E Single branch of right pulmonary artery obstruction
💡 查看答案
A

Haemodynamic and respiratory compromise (massive PE with instability) is the indication for surgical thrombectomy/intervention.

CTS·MCQ073 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Which has class A evidence of benefit from CABG?

Which has class A evidence of benefit from CABG?

  1. A 50% stenosis of left main coronary artery
  2. B 50% stenosis of proximal right main coronary artery
  3. C 50% stenosis of proximal left anterior descending artery
  4. D 50% stenosis of proximal posterior descending artery
💡 查看答案
A

50% stenosis of left main coronary artery has Class A evidence for benefit from CABG (high-risk anatomy).

CTS·MCQ074 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Regarding pectus excavatum, which investigation is not routine?

Regarding pectus excavatum, which investigation is not routine?

  1. A Echo
  2. B Pulmonary function test
  3. C CT thorax
  4. D Nerve conduction study
💡 查看答案
D

Nerve conduction study is NOT routine investigation for pectus excavatum. Echo (cardiac compression), PFT (restrictive pattern), and CT thorax (Haller index) are routine.

CTS·MCQ075 2022 MCQ26 MCQ 56/M construction site worker, previous exposure to asbestos, presented with chest pain, shortness of breath and fever. CXR left pleural effusion. CT thorax lef…

56/M construction site worker, previous exposure to asbestos, presented with chest pain, shortness of breath and fever. CXR left pleural effusion. CT thorax left pleural effusion with left pleural nodular thickening, no mass found. Most likely diagnosis:

  1. A CA lung
  2. B Lymphoma
  3. C Mesothelioma
  4. D TB
💡 查看答案
C

Previous asbestos exposure + pleural effusion + nodular pleural thickening = mesothelioma. Not CA lung (no mass). Not lymphoma (nodular pleural thickening). Not TB (pattern different).

CTS·MCQ076 2022 MCQ30 MCQ 15 year old boy presented with sudden onset left pleuritic chest pain. CXR shows 30% left pneumothorax. Chest drain is inserted. What is the most appropriate in…

15 year old boy presented with sudden onset left pleuritic chest pain. CXR shows 30% left pneumothorax. Chest drain is inserted. What is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A CT thorax
  2. B Echocardiogram
  3. C Nerve conduction study
  4. D Pulmonary function test
💡 查看答案
A

CT thorax is the most appropriate investigation after chest drain for pneumothorax in a 15-year-old — to look for blebs/bullae and underlying cause.

CTS·MCQ077 2022 MCQ32 MCQ Patient with mechanical mitral and aortic valve replacement due to chronic rheumatic heart disease. She is in chronic AF. Which ORAL drug to recommend?

Patient with mechanical mitral and aortic valve replacement due to chronic rheumatic heart disease. She is in chronic AF. Which ORAL drug to recommend?

  1. A Rivaroxaban
  2. B Edoxaban
  3. C Clopidogrel
  4. D Warfarin
💡 查看答案
D

Mechanical valve prosthesis requires warfarin. DOACs (rivaroxaban, edoxaban) are NOT approved for mechanical heart valves. Clopidogrel alone is inadequate.

CTS·MCQ078 2022 MCQ83 MCQ Patient with familial hypercholesterolemia. MRI perfusion reduced in anterior, septal, and … Tight left ostial main branch 90%. Definitive treatment:

Patient with familial hypercholesterolemia. MRI perfusion reduced in anterior, septal, and … Tight left ostial main branch 90%. Definitive treatment:

  1. A Aspirin + lipid lowering drug
  2. B Beta blocker
  3. C CABG
  4. D PCI
💡 查看答案
C

Tight left main 90% stenosis with perfusion defects → definitive treatment is CABG (not PCI for left main in this context).

CTS·MCQ079 2022 MCQ84 MCQ M/70 chronic smoker, newly diagnosed CA lung. 2cm spiculated mass over the right upper lobe on PET/CT. No distant met. No mediastinal lymph nodes. FEV1 is 100% …

M/70 chronic smoker, newly diagnosed CA lung. 2cm spiculated mass over the right upper lobe on PET/CT. No distant met. No mediastinal lymph nodes. FEV1 is 100% of predicted. Recommended management?

  1. A Oral targeted therapy
  2. B Radiation therapy over RUL
  3. C Video thoracoscopic RUL lobectomy
  4. D Video thoracoscopic RUL wedge resection
💡 查看答案
C

70/M, 2cm spiculated RUL mass, no mets, no LN, FEV1 100% → VATS right upper lobectomy. Standard curative resection for stage I NSCLC.

CTS·MCQ080 2022 MCQ89 MCQ 65 years old man with end stage renal failure on long term peritoneal dialysis and aspirin for IHD presented with pleural effusion. Unfortunately it was complic…

65 years old man with end stage renal failure on long term peritoneal dialysis and aspirin for IHD presented with pleural effusion. Unfortunately it was complicated by hemothorax. Chest drain inserted, 2 litres of bloody discharge, continuous output. BP 80/50, pulse 130. 3 bag of packed cells given. Which of the following should be the definitive treatment?

  1. A Check platelet and clotting
  2. B Continuous blood and fluid
  3. C Urgent consult cardiothoracic surgeon for exploration and hemostasis
  4. D Urgent consult intensivist for ICU admission
💡 查看答案
C

Continuous bloody output 2L from chest drain in ESRF patient on aspirin → urgent CT surgery consult for exploration and haemostasis. This is active ongoing haemothorax requiring surgical intervention.

CTS·EMQ001 2002# EMQ3 EMQ MEDIASTINAL MASSES

Theme: MEDIASTINAL MASSES

Options
  1. A Bronchogenic cyst
  2. B Germ cell tumour
  3. C Neurogenic tumour
  4. D Parathyroid tumour
  5. E Paravertebral abscess
  6. F Pericardial cyst
  7. G Neuroblastoma
  8. H Thymoma
  9. I Lymph nodes
  10. J CA esophagus

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 20 years old lady with an asymptomatic posterior mediastinal mass.
  2. Q2. A 40 years old lady with ptosis and diplopia.
  3. Q3. A 18 years old gentleman with a right scrotal mass.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Neurogenic tumour — 20/F, asymptomatic posterior mediastinal mass = neurogenic tumour (schwannoma/neurofibroma).
Q2. Thymoma — 40/F, ptosis + diplopia = myasthenia gravis → associated with thymoma (anterior mediastinum).
Q3. Germ cell tumour — 18/M, mediastinal mass + right scrotal mass = germ cell tumour (testicular primary with mediastinal mets, or primary mediastinal germ cell).
CTS·EMQ002 2005 EMQ6 EMQ LUNG NEOPLASM

Theme: LUNG NEOPLASM

Options
  1. A Bronchial stent insertion
  2. B Chemotherapy
  3. C Lobectomy
  4. D Mediastinoscopy
  5. E Mediastinotomy
  6. F Pneumonectomy
  7. G Radiotherapy
  8. H Wedge resection

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 70 years old man presents with a 1.5 cm asymptomatic lung lesion. Investigations reveal a non-small cell carcinoma situated at the peripheral part of the left upper lobe with significantly enlarged left lower para-tracheal lymph nodes. Lung function test shows that FEV1 is 2.2 litres.
  2. Q2. A 50 years old man, with history of resected colonic carcinoma, presents with a 2 cm asymptomatic lung nodule. Investigations reveal an isolated nodule situated at the peripheral part of the right lower lobe. There is no abnormality of other organs. The level of carcino-embryonic antigen has increased recently. Lung function test shows that the FEV1 is 2.6 litres.
  3. Q3. A 60 years old man presents with cough and haemoptysis. Investigations reveal a squamous cell carcinoma situated at the distal part of the left main bronchus without mediastinal lymphadenopathy. Lung function test shows that FEV1 is 2.5 litres.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Palliative chemo/RT — 70/M, NSCLC with significantly enlarged mediastinal LN (N2/N3) = advanced disease → palliative or neoadjuvant approach depending on fitness.
Q2. Wedge/segmental resection — 50/M, history of colonic CA, isolated 2cm peripheral lung nodule = solitary pulmonary metastasis → metastasectomy (wedge resection) if fit.
Q3. Left pneumonectomy — SCC in distal left main bronchus, no LN, good function → left pneumonectomy or sleeve lobectomy if anatomically feasible.
CTS·EMQ003 2007 EMQ4 EMQ LUNG NEOPLASM

Theme: LUNG NEOPLASM

Options
  1. A Bronchial stent
  2. B Chemotherapy
  3. C Lobectomy
  4. D Mediastinoscopy
  5. E Mediastinotomy
  6. F Pneumonectomy
  7. G Radiotherapy
  8. H Wedge resection
Questions
  1. Q1. A 40 years old gentleman presented with an incidental finding of right lower lobe mass in CXR during routine body check. FNAC of the mass confirmed adenocarcinoma. CT thorax showed 1.5cm right-sided paratracheal lymph node.
  2. Q2. A 40 years old gentleman presented with an incidental finding of left upper lobe mass in CXR during routine body check. FNAC of the mass confirmed adenocarcinoma. CT thorax showed 1.5 cm aorto-pulmonary window lymph node.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old gentleman with known history of carcinoma of colon presented with an incidental finding of right upper lobe mass. Blood test CEA was 50 ng/dL. Colonoscopy and CT abdomen & pelvis showed no evidence of local recurrence.
SECTION 04

Colorectal Surgery

110 MCQ · 16 EMQ
CRS·MCQ001 2002 MCQ3 MCQ What is the BEST treatment for third degree pile?

What is the BEST treatment for third degree pile?

  1. A Injection of sclerosant
  2. B Rubber band ligation
  3. C Infrared coagulation
  4. D Stapled haemorrhoidectomy
  5. E High fibre diet
💡 查看答案
D

3rd degree piles (prolapse requiring manual reduction) → best treated by haemorrhoidectomy (stapled or conventional). Banding for 2nd degree. Sclerosant/infrared for 1st degree.

CRS·MCQ002 2002 MCQ16, 2002# MCQ53 MCQ Which of the following is SELDOM used nowadays for bowel preparation?

Which of the following is SELDOM used nowadays for bowel preparation?

  1. A Laxative and enema
  2. B Antibiotic
  3. C Osmotic laxative
  4. D On table lavage
  5. E Total gut irrigation
💡 查看答案
E

Total gut irrigation (whole gut lavage) is seldom used now. Modern prep: osmotic laxatives (PEG), enemas, and on-table lavage for emergency cases.

CRS·MCQ003 2002 MCQ29, 2002# MCQ54 MCQ In patients aged over 50 years, what condition may be INDISTINGUISHABLE from acute appendicitis on physical examination?

In patients aged over 50 years, what condition may be INDISTINGUISHABLE from acute appendicitis on physical examination?

  1. A Acute cholecystitis
  2. B Diverticulitis of the caecum
  3. C Ectopic pregnancy
  4. D Intussusception
  5. E Right ureteric stone
💡 查看答案
B

Caecal diverticulitis mimics appendicitis (RIF pain, fever) and is indistinguishable on exam in patients >50.

CRS·MCQ004 2002 MCQ49, 2002# MCQ55, 2003 MCQ64 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a common indication for surgery in Crohn’s disease?

Which of the following is NOT a common indication for surgery in Crohn’s disease?

  1. A Intra-abdominal abscess
  2. B Intestinal obstruction
  3. C Perianal disease
  4. D Ileocolic fistulas and abscess formation
  5. E Malignancy of large bowel
💡 查看答案
E

Malignancy of large bowel is NOT a common indication for surgery in Crohn's (rare complication). Abscess, obstruction, perianal disease, and fistulas are common surgical indications.

CRS·MCQ005 2002 MCQ55 MCQ In patient with intestinal obstruction, the following are characteristic findings on abdominal X- ray EXCEPT:

In patient with intestinal obstruction, the following are characteristic findings on abdominal X- ray EXCEPT:

  1. A Distended loops of small bowel with air and fluid level arranged in a “step-ladder” or “inverted J” pattern
  2. B Presence of rectal gas
  3. C Elevated hemidiaphragm due to bowel distention
  4. D A “ground-glass” appearance suggests ascites secondary to peritoneal extravasation of fluid from the congested, edematous bowel wall
  5. E Free air, if present, is an ominous sign of bowel perforation
💡 查看答案
B

Presence of rectal gas argues AGAINST complete obstruction. Step-ladder air-fluid levels, elevated diaphragm, ground-glass ascites, and free air are all expected findings.

CRS·MCQ006 2002 MCQ61, 2002# MCQ56, 2003 MCQ77 MCQ The following are causes of intestinal ischaemia EXCEPT:

The following are causes of intestinal ischaemia EXCEPT:

  1. A Decreased flow and/or a redistribution of flow due to hypovolaemia, shock, heart failure
  2. B Mesenteric vasoconstriction
  3. C Bowel obstruction
  4. D Emboli
  5. E Peritonitis
💡 查看答案
E

Peritonitis is a result of ischaemia, not a cause. Hypovolaemia/shock, vasoconstriction, obstruction, and emboli are causes of intestinal ischaemia.

CRS·MCQ007 2002 MCQ69 MCQ In the immediate postoperative management of a patient who has undergone right hemicolectomy for carcinoma of the caecum:

In the immediate postoperative management of a patient who has undergone right hemicolectomy for carcinoma of the caecum:

  1. A 200 mmol of Na will match daily requirements
  2. B 100 mmol of K should be added to daily intravenous regimen
  3. C Epidural analgesia will enhance return of gastrointestinal motility
  4. D Early enteral feeding will cause anastomotic dehiscence
  5. E Total parenteral nutrition should be given as routine
💡 查看答案
C

Epidural analgesia enhances return of GI motility (reduces opioid requirement + sympathetic blockade). Na requirement ~100mmol/day not 200 (A wrong). K requirement ~60-80mmol not 100 (B wrong). Early enteral feeding is safe (D wrong). TPN not routine (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ008 2002 MCQ78 MCQ Ulcerative colitis is a chronic inflammatory condition of the large bowel which:

Ulcerative colitis is a chronic inflammatory condition of the large bowel which:

  1. A Seldom presents with bloody diarrhoea
  2. B Is associated with fistula formation
  3. C May be associated with liver and joint problems
  4. D Is not pre-malignant
  5. E Inflammation often involves the full thickness of the bowel wall
💡 查看答案
C

UC may be associated with liver (PSC, fatty liver) and joint (arthritis) problems. It presents with bloody diarrhoea (A wrong). Fistula is Crohn's (B wrong). UC IS premalignant (D wrong). UC is mucosal only (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ009 2002# MCQ25 MCQ The MOST useful investigation for diagnosing acute appendicitis is:

The MOST useful investigation for diagnosing acute appendicitis is:

  1. A Blood biochemistry
  2. B Plain abdominal radiography
  3. C Urine microscopy
  4. D Ultrasound
  5. E Barium enema
💡 查看答案
D

Ultrasound is the most useful investigation for acute appendicitis (especially in females — excludes gynaecological pathology). CT is most accurate overall but USG is first-line in many settings.

CRS·MCQ010 2002# MCQ76 MCQ A 70 years old gentleman presented with left lower abdominal pain and fever. He developed generalized abdominal pain after admission. X-ray showed free gas unde…

A 70 years old gentleman presented with left lower abdominal pain and fever. He developed generalized abdominal pain after admission. X-ray showed free gas under diaphragm. Which of the following is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Perforated peptic ulcer
  2. B Perforated sigmoid diverticulitis
  3. C Carcinoma of ascending colon
  4. D Appendicitis
  5. E Small bowel obstruction
💡 查看答案
B

70/M, left lower pain, fever, then generalized pain + free gas = perforated sigmoid diverticulitis. Most common cause of pneumoperitoneum in elderly with left-sided symptoms.

CRS·MCQ011 2002# MCQ90 MCQ Which of the following conditions is NOT a common cause for lower gastrointestinal bleeding in adults over 60 years of age?

Which of the following conditions is NOT a common cause for lower gastrointestinal bleeding in adults over 60 years of age?

  1. A Diverticular disease
  2. B Colonic carcinoma
  3. C Meckel’s diverticulum
  4. D Inflammatory bowel disease
  5. E Angiodysplasia
💡 查看答案
C

Meckel's diverticulum is a cause of GI bleeding in CHILDREN, not commonly in adults >60. Diverticular disease, colonic CA, IBD, and angiodysplasia are common causes in elderly.

CRS·MCQ012 2003 MCQ2 MCQ Which of the following is NOT an indication for colonoscopy?

Which of the following is NOT an indication for colonoscopy?

  1. A Endoscopic polypectomy
  2. B Control of bleeding from angiodysplasia
  3. C Drainage of an inflamed diverticulum
  4. D Detorsion of sigmoid volvulus
  5. E Decompression for colonic pseudo-obstruction
💡 查看答案
C

Drainage of an inflamed diverticulum is NOT done colonoscopically. Polypectomy, haemostasis, detorsion of volvulus, and decompression for pseudo-obstruction are valid colonoscopic procedures.

CRS·MCQ013 2003 MCQ18 MCQ Which is the COMMONEST cause of intestinal obstruction?

Which is the COMMONEST cause of intestinal obstruction?

  1. A Large and small bowel tumour
  2. B Adhesion
  3. C Hernia
  4. D Volvulus
  5. E Intussusception
💡 查看答案
B

Adhesion is the commonest cause of intestinal obstruction in developed countries (post-surgical adhesions).

CRS·MCQ014 2003 MCQ34 MCQ A fistula-in-ano is BEST treated by:

A fistula-in-ano is BEST treated by:

  1. A Two weeks of antibiotics
  2. B Bowel softener
  3. C Topical ointment
  4. D Lay open the fistula
  5. E Sphincterotomy
💡 查看答案
D

Fistula-in-ano is best treated by laying open (fistulotomy) for low intersphincteric/transsphincteric fistulas.

CRS·MCQ015 2003 MCQ47 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE regarding acute appendicitis?

Which of the following is TRUE regarding acute appendicitis?

  1. A It is prevalent in people on a high-fibre diet
  2. B It may occur in non obstructed appendix
  3. C It produces pain referred to the right iliac fossa irrespective of the position of the appendix
  4. D It gives pain which causes patients to writhe around
  5. E It has a lower mortality in pregnancy
💡 查看答案
B

Appendicitis can occur without obstruction (non-obstructive appendicitis exists, though faecolith is common). It is NOT prevalent with high-fibre diet (A wrong). Pain starts periumbilical then migrates to RIF regardless of appendix position (C wrong). Pain makes patient lie still (D = writhing = renal colic).

CRS·MCQ016 2003 MCQ49 MCQ A patient with asymptomatic sigmoid diverticula should be treated by:

A patient with asymptomatic sigmoid diverticula should be treated by:

  1. A No treatment
  2. B Bowel softener
  3. C Sigmoid colectomy
  4. D Hartmann’s operation
  5. E Wedge excision of diverticula
💡 查看答案
A

Asymptomatic sigmoid diverticula require no treatment. High-fibre diet may be recommended but not mandated in truly asymptomatic cases.

CRS·MCQ017 2004 MCQ6 MCQ A 5 years old boy was admitted to the surgical ward via Accident & Emergency Department. He complained of dull abdominal pain around the umbilicus for one day b…

A 5 years old boy was admitted to the surgical ward via Accident & Emergency Department. He complained of dull abdominal pain around the umbilicus for one day before developing severe right lower quadrant pain. He lost his appetite and had a fever of 38.5°C. Physical examination revealed rebound tenderness and guarding in the right lower quadrant. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Acute pyelonephritis
  2. B Intestinal obstruction
  3. C Acute appendicitis
  4. D Gastroenteritis
  5. E Mesenteric adenitis
💡 查看答案
C

5/M, periumbilical pain → RLQ migration, fever 38.5°C, RLQ tenderness + guarding = acute appendicitis. Classic history.

CRS·MCQ018 2004 MCQ18, 2005 MCQ25 MCQ Carcinoid tumours are MOST commonly found in the:

Carcinoid tumours are MOST commonly found in the:

  1. A Stomach
  2. B Small intestine
  3. C Colon
  4. D Rectum
  5. E Lung
💡 查看答案
B

Carcinoid tumours are most commonly found in the small intestine (appendix in some classifications, but GI carcinoids most common in small bowel).

CRS·MCQ019 2004 MCQ40 MCQ Regarding diverticular disease of the colon, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

Regarding diverticular disease of the colon, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

  1. A May present with profuse rectal bleeding
  2. B May present with pneumaturia
  3. C Is a pre-malignant condition
  4. D May affect the right colon
  5. E May cause colonic stricture formation
💡 查看答案
C

Diverticular disease is NOT premalignant. It can cause profuse bleeding (A true), pneumaturia (colovesical fistula, B true), affect right colon (D true), and cause stricture (E true).

CRS·MCQ020 2004 MCQ43 MCQ Regarding anal fissure, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

Regarding anal fissure, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

  1. A Usually occurs in the anterior midline
  2. B May be treated by sphincterotomy
  3. C May be treated by topical nitrates
  4. D May be associated with a sentinel skin tag
  5. E When multiple, may suggest the presence of Crohn’s disease
💡 查看答案
A

Anal fissure usually occurs in the POSTERIOR midline (~90%), not anterior. Multiple fissures suggest Crohn's/TB (E true). Treated by sphincterotomy (B true) and topical nitrates (C true). Sentinel tag (D true).

CRS·MCQ021 2004 MCQ59 MCQ The first-line treatment for clostridium difficile colitis is:

The first-line treatment for clostridium difficile colitis is:

  1. A Tetracycline
  2. B Metronidazole
  3. C Vancomycin
  4. D Ciprofloxacin
  5. E Amikacin
💡 查看答案
B

Metronidazole is first-line treatment for C. difficile colitis. Oral vancomycin is second-line for severe cases.

CRS·MCQ022 2004 MCQ60 MCQ The following are the complications of diverticular disease of colon EXCEPT:

The following are the complications of diverticular disease of colon EXCEPT:

  1. A Bleeding
  2. B Colovesical fistula
  3. C Stricture
  4. D Adenocarcinoma
  5. E Paracolic abscess
💡 查看答案
D

Adenocarcinoma is NOT a complication of diverticular disease (no malignant transformation). Bleeding, colovesical fistula, stricture, and paracolic abscess are complications.

CRS·MCQ023 2004 MCQ61 MCQ A 60 years old lady post right hemicolectomy is found to have glycosuria. This is confirmed by an elevated serum glucose. Possible cause include all of the foll…

A 60 years old lady post right hemicolectomy is found to have glycosuria. This is confirmed by an elevated serum glucose. Possible cause include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Sepsis
  2. B Pre-existing diabetes mellitus
  3. C Parenteral nutrition
  4. D Concurrent use of steroids
  5. E Liver failure
💡 查看答案
E

Liver failure does NOT typically cause hyperglycaemia (it causes hypoglycaemia). Sepsis, pre-existing DM, TPN, and steroids all cause post-op glycosuria.

CRS·MCQ024 2004 MCQ67 MCQ Operative treatment for carcinoma of the splenic flexure is:

Operative treatment for carcinoma of the splenic flexure is:

  1. A Right hemicolectomy
  2. B Transverse colectomy
  3. C Left colectomy
  4. D Hartmann’s procedure
  5. E Total colectomy
💡 查看答案
C

Splenic flexure CA → left (extended left) hemicolectomy, which includes the splenic flexure territory between the marginal arteries.

CRS·MCQ025 2004 MCQ70 MCQ A 70 years old gentleman presents with left lower abdominal pain and change in bowel habits. Barium enema shows a filling defect in the sigmoid colon. The MOST …

A 70 years old gentleman presents with left lower abdominal pain and change in bowel habits. Barium enema shows a filling defect in the sigmoid colon. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Diverticulosis
  2. B Carcinoma
  3. C Crohn’s disease
  4. D Ulcerative colitis
  5. E Familial adenomatous polyposis
💡 查看答案
B

70/M, LLQ pain + change in bowel habit + sigmoid filling defect on barium enema = carcinoma (most likely in this demographic).

CRS·MCQ026 2004 MCQ81 MCQ Prophylactic antibiotic of choice for appendicectomy is:

Prophylactic antibiotic of choice for appendicectomy is:

  1. A Cefuroxime
  2. B Penicillin
  3. C Metronidazole
  4. D Vancomycin
  5. E Gentamicin
💡 查看答案
C

Metronidazole is the prophylactic antibiotic of choice for appendicectomy (covers anaerobes). Often combined with a cephalosporin.

CRS·MCQ027 2004 MCQ89 MCQ A 65 years old lady presents with bright red blood per rectum and weight loss. The MOST useful investigation would be:

A 65 years old lady presents with bright red blood per rectum and weight loss. The MOST useful investigation would be:

  1. A Barium enema
  2. B Sigmoidoscopy
  3. C Colonoscopy
  4. D Endoscopy
  5. E Proctoscopy
💡 查看答案
C

Colonoscopy is the most useful investigation for a 65/F with PR bleeding + weight loss — directly visualizes, biopsies, and potentially treats the source.

CRS·MCQ028 2005 MCQ4 MCQ Appendiceal abscess in children:

Appendiceal abscess in children:

  1. A Is not a complication of acute appendicitis
  2. B Is always febrile
  3. C Can be drained per-rectally if the abscess is in pelvis
  4. D Always need open operative drainage
  5. E Is commonly associated with non-perforated appendicitis
💡 查看答案
C

Appendiceal abscess in pelvis can be drained per rectum (transrectal drainage). Not always febrile (B wrong). Not always needing open drainage (D wrong). Associated with PERFORATED appendicitis (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ029 2005 MCQ18, 2020 MCQ3 MCQ For patients with adhesive small bowel obstruction, the treatment that has been shown to reduce the rate of emergency laparotomy is:

For patients with adhesive small bowel obstruction, the treatment that has been shown to reduce the rate of emergency laparotomy is:

  1. A Fleet enema
  2. B Gastrografin meal & follow-through
  3. C Enteroscopy
  4. D Contrast CT scan abdomen
  5. E Polyethylene glycol lavage
💡 查看答案
B

Gastrografin (water-soluble contrast) meal & follow-through has been shown to reduce the need for emergency laparotomy in adhesive SBO — therapeutic (osmotic effect draws fluid) and diagnostic.

CRS·MCQ030 2005 MCQ21, 2010 MCQ10 MCQ Regarding anal fissures, which of the following statements is WRONG?

Regarding anal fissures, which of the following statements is WRONG?

  1. A 10% occur in the posterior midline
  2. B Multiple fissures suggest a diagnosis of tuberculosis or Crohn’s Disease
  3. C 50% of acute fissures heal with the use of a bulking agent
  4. D Sphincterotomy has a success rate of over 90%
  5. E Sphincterotomy is associated with minor faecal incontinence in over 15% of patients
💡 查看答案
A

90% of fissures occur in the POSTERIOR midline (not 10%). This statement is WRONG, making it the correct answer to 'which is wrong'.

CRS·MCQ031 2005 MCQ49 MCQ The following medications are useful for the treatment of perianal Crohn’s disease EXCEPT

The following medications are useful for the treatment of perianal Crohn’s disease EXCEPT

  1. A Infliximab
  2. B Ciprofloxacin
  3. C Azathioprine
  4. D Cyclosporin
  5. E Tetracycline
💡 查看答案
E

Tetracycline is NOT useful for perianal Crohn's. Infliximab, ciprofloxacin, azathioprine, and cyclosporin are all used.

CRS·MCQ032 2005 MCQ73 MCQ An 80 years old man presents with rectal bleeding of recent onset associated with diarrhoea and tenesmus. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

An 80 years old man presents with rectal bleeding of recent onset associated with diarrhoea and tenesmus. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Gastroenteritis
  2. B Rectal cancer
  3. C Caecal cancer
  4. D Crohn’s disease
  5. E Haemorrhoids
💡 查看答案
B

80/M, recent onset rectal bleeding + diarrhoea + tenesmus = rectal cancer (classic presentation in elderly).

CRS·MCQ033 2005 MCQ74 MCQ The infection that MOST commonly affects rectum is:

The infection that MOST commonly affects rectum is:

  1. A Amoebiasis
  2. B Typhoid fever
  3. C Tuberculosis
  4. D Syphilis
  5. E Actinomycosis
💡 查看答案
A

Amoebiasis most commonly affects the rectum and caecum.

CRS·MCQ034 2005 MCQ76 MCQ A 65 years old man presented with haematochezia with anaemia. Both upper endoscopy and colonoscopy were normal. The following are useful methods for investigati…

A 65 years old man presented with haematochezia with anaemia. Both upper endoscopy and colonoscopy were normal. The following are useful methods for investigating the site/cause of bleeding EXCEPT:

  1. A Meckel’s scan
  2. B Capsule endoscopy
  3. C Push enteroscopy
  4. D Aortogram
  5. E Red blood cell scan
💡 查看答案
D

Aortogram is NOT useful for obscure GI bleeding investigation. Meckel's scan (A), capsule endoscopy (B), push enteroscopy (C), and RBC scan (E) are all useful.

CRS·MCQ035 2005 MCQ88 MCQ A pilonidal sinus MOST commonly

A pilonidal sinus MOST commonly

  1. A Occurs in the natal cleft
  2. B Occurs in the web of fingers
  3. C Occurs in the umbilicus
  4. D Communicates with visceral cavity
  5. E Leads to formation of multiple sinuses and abscess formation
💡 查看答案
A

Pilonidal sinus most commonly occurs in the natal cleft (intergluteal/sacrococcygeal area).

CRS·MCQ036 2006 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the following cannot cause mechanical small bowel obstruction?

Which of the following cannot cause mechanical small bowel obstruction?

  1. A Radiation enteritis
  2. B Bezoar
  3. C Desmoid tumour
  4. D Intussusception
  5. E Retroperitoneal hematoma
💡 查看答案
E

Retroperitoneal haematoma does NOT cause mechanical SBO (it's external to bowel). Radiation enteritis, bezoar, desmoid, and intussusception all cause mechanical SBO.

CRS·MCQ037 2006 MCQ12 MCQ Patient had lower anterior resection for adenocarcinoma of the rectum 2 years ago. Found to have raised CEA. The following imaging helps to delineate distant me…

Patient had lower anterior resection for adenocarcinoma of the rectum 2 years ago. Found to have raised CEA. The following imaging helps to delineate distant metastasis except:

  1. A Whole body PET
  2. B MRI pelvis
  3. C Bone scan
  4. D CXR
  5. E CT abdomen
💡 查看答案
C

Bone scan is NOT typically used for CRC metastasis staging (CRC rarely mets to bone). PET, MRI pelvis, CXR, and CT abdomen are all appropriate for detecting CRC recurrence.

CRS·MCQ038 2006 MCQ13 MCQ All the following can cause Clostridium difficile colitis except:

All the following can cause Clostridium difficile colitis except:

  1. A Amikacin
  2. B Ciprofloxacin
  3. C Tetracycline
  4. D Metronidazole
  5. E Cefuroxime
💡 查看答案
D

Metronidazole is used to TREAT C. difficile — it does NOT commonly cause it. Amikacin, ciprofloxacin, tetracycline, and cephalosporins can all cause C. difficile.

CRS·MCQ039 2006 MCQ14 MCQ Concerning about diverticular disease, the following statements are correct except:

Concerning about diverticular disease, the following statements are correct except:

  1. A 75% patient are asymptomatic
  2. B Barium enema is better than colonoscopy to delineate the extent of colonic lesions
  3. C Immunocompromised patients and young patients <40 years old are more prone to develop complications
  4. D 50% of patient with acute diverticulitis will develop recurrent attack in due course
  5. E <10% acute bleeding of diverticular disease need emergency surgery to stop bleeding
💡 查看答案
B

Barium enema is NOT better than colonoscopy for delineating colonic lesions — colonoscopy is superior (direct visualization + biopsy). All other statements are correct.

CRS·MCQ040 2006 MCQ15 MCQ These are causes of fistula-in-ano except:

These are causes of fistula-in-ano except:

  1. A Amoeba
  2. B TB
  3. C Crohn’s disease
  4. D Cryptogenic infections
  5. E Iatrogenic post ano-rectal surgery
💡 查看答案
A

Amoeba is NOT a common cause of fistula-in-ano. TB, Crohn's, cryptogenic infection, and iatrogenic causes are recognized.

CRS·MCQ041 2006 MCQ16, 2010 MCQ6, 2012 MCQ39, 2016 MCQ48, 2017 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following statements about a Meckel’s diverticulum is CORRECT?

Which of the following statements about a Meckel’s diverticulum is CORRECT?

  1. A Consists of mucosa without a muscle coat
  2. B Is found on the mesenteric border of the small intestine
  3. C May have a fibrous band between the apex and appendix and cause intestinal obstruction
  4. D Occur in 10% of the population
