  5. E Which contains heterotopic gastric mucosa can ulcerate and cause brisk gastrointestinal bleeding
💡 查看答案
E

Meckel's with heterotopic gastric mucosa → peptic ulceration → brisk GI bleeding. It's a true diverticulum (has all layers, A wrong), on anti-mesenteric border (B wrong), fibrous band to umbilicus (C wrong — not appendix), occurs in 2% population (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ042 2006 MCQ17, 2009 MCQ23 MCQ Which of the following statements about colonic polyps is correct?

Which of the following statements about colonic polyps is correct?

  1. A Juvenile rectal polyp are adenomatous polyp
  2. B Metaplastic polyp are malignant
  3. C Risk of cancer is higher in tubular adenoma than villous adenoma
  4. D Villous adenoma may sometimes causes hyperkalaemia
  5. E All untreated familial adenomatous polyposis will eventually cause cancer MCQ43 (2006 MCQ60, 2009 MCQ38, 2010 MCQ21, 2011 MCQ9, 2013 MCQ4, 2015 MCQ10, 2019 MCQ32) What is the most commonest site of carcinoid tumour?
  6. A Foregut
  7. B Midgut
  8. C Hindgut
  9. D Anus
  10. E Lung
💡 查看答案
E

Untreated FAP will inevitably develop CRC (100% lifetime risk). Juvenile polyps are hamartomatous (A wrong). Metaplastic polyps are benign (B wrong). Villous > tubular malignancy risk (C wrong). Villous adenoma causes hypOkalaemia (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ044 2007 MCQ2, 2010 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the followings is correct for CA rectum?

Which of the followings is correct for CA rectum?

  1. A CT is the most accurate assessment of the depth of the invasion
  2. B Level of tumour is an important factor for the possibility of sphincter saving operation
  3. C Upper 1⁄3 CA is treated with APR
  4. D Local recurrence can be reduced by chemotherapy
  5. E Chemotherapy is used to treat Stage 1 (Duke’s A) CA
💡 查看答案
B

Level of tumour from anal verge determines sphincter preservation feasibility. CT is not most accurate for depth (MRI/EUS better, A wrong). Lower 1/3 rectal CA → APR (C wrong — upper 1/3 gets LAR). Local recurrence reduced by RT not just chemo (D wrong). Stage 1 doesn't need chemo (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ045 2007 MCQ29 MCQ A 12 years old girl was admitted with right-sided abdominal pain. She also complained of symptoms of upper respiratory tract infection. Physical examination rev…

A 12 years old girl was admitted with right-sided abdominal pain. She also complained of symptoms of upper respiratory tract infection. Physical examination revealed mild tenderness but no guarding in the right lower quadrant. Ultrasound showed no thickened tubular structure in the right abdomen. The most likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Pyelonephritis
  2. B Intestinal obstruction
  3. C Appendicitis
  4. D Gastroenteritis
  5. E Mesenteric adenitis
💡 查看答案
E

12/F, RIF pain + URTI + mild tenderness no guarding + normal USG = mesenteric adenitis (common in children post-viral).

CRS·MCQ046 2007 MCQ44, 2010 MCQ8 MCQ Regarding small bowel obstruction, which one is correct?

Regarding small bowel obstruction, which one is correct?

  1. A Most common cause in developed countries is small bowel tumour
  2. B Adhesive obstruction needs immediate operative treatment
  3. C Intussusception in adult is usually idiopathic
  4. D Bezoar obstruction usually occurs in patients with previous gastric surgery
  5. E Gallstone ileus is usually associated with strangulation
💡 查看答案
D

Bezoar obstruction usually occurs in patients with previous GASTRIC surgery (altered motility/anatomy). Most common cause is adhesions not tumour (A wrong). Adhesive SBO often managed conservatively initially (B wrong). Adult intussusception has a lead point (C wrong). Gallstone ileus rarely strangulates (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ047 2007 MCQ54 MCQ Regarding surgery for ulcerative colitis, which of the following statement is correct?

Regarding surgery for ulcerative colitis, which of the following statement is correct?

  1. A All patients with left sided colitis will require surgery within 5 years
  2. B Panproctocolectomy and punch formation is appropriate as an emergency operation
  3. C The ileal pouch is usually in a ‘J’ configuration
  4. D Perfect continence is expected in most patients with a pouch
  5. E The mean stool frequency is about 2 times per day in patients with a ileal pouch
💡 查看答案
C

Ileal pouch is usually in 'J' configuration. Not all left-sided colitis needs surgery (A wrong). Panproctocolectomy with pouch NOT ideal as emergency (B wrong). Stool frequency ~4-6/day not 2 (E wrong). Not perfect continence (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ048 2009 MCQ25 MCQ Concerning SMALL bowel obstruction, which of the following is CORRECT?

Concerning SMALL bowel obstruction, which of the following is CORRECT?

  1. A Abdominal distension is seen in all cases
  2. B All cases can be managed conservatively in the first 24 hours
  3. C Aggressive fluid resuscitation is rarely required
  4. D Colicky abdominal pain and vomiting commonly occurs
  5. E The most common cause in developed countries is hernia
💡 查看答案
D

Colicky abdominal pain and vomiting commonly occur in SBO. Not all cases have distension (proximal SBO, A wrong). Not all can be managed conservatively (strangulation needs surgery, B wrong). Aggressive fluid resus IS often required (C wrong). Most common cause is adhesions not hernia (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ049 2009 MCQ26, 2012 MCQ41, 2018 MCQ22, 2021 MCQ9 MCQ Regarding lower gastrointestinal tract bleeding, which of the following is CORRECT?

Regarding lower gastrointestinal tract bleeding, which of the following is CORRECT?

  1. A After insertion of a nasogastric tube, if the aspirated fluid is of clear colour, upper GI bleed can be excluded
  2. B Angiography can detect bleeding of <0.1 mL/min
  3. C Most diverticular bleeding require surgical intervention since only 10% of diverticular bleeding stop spontaneously
  4. D Endoscopic treatment is the mainstay of treatment for angiodysplasia
  5. E If bleeding source cannot be identified, blind right hemicolectomy should be performed to control bleeding
💡 查看答案
D

Endoscopic treatment is the mainstay for angiodysplasia bleeding (argon plasma coagulation). Clear NG aspirate doesn't rule out upper GI bleed (A wrong — duodenal). Angiography detects ≥0.5 mL/min (B wrong). Most diverticular bleeding stops spontaneously (C wrong). Never do blind right hemicolectomy (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ050 2009 MCQ65 MCQ An 8 years old boy presented with 2 day history of right-sided abdominal pain. On examination he was febrile at 38°C, and revealed abdominal tenderness with gua…

An 8 years old boy presented with 2 day history of right-sided abdominal pain. On examination he was febrile at 38°C, and revealed abdominal tenderness with guarding over right lower quadrant. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Acute pyelonephritis
  3. C Intussusception
  4. D Gastroenteritis
  5. E Mesenteric adenitis
💡 查看答案
A

8/M, 2 days RIF pain, fever 38°C, RLQ tenderness + guarding = acute appendicitis.

CRS·MCQ051 2009 MCQ69, 2010 MCQ59 MCQ A 12 years old boy, with history of appendectomy, was admitted with vomiting and abdominal pain. Physical exam showed distended abdomen. Which is NOT your immed…

A 12 years old boy, with history of appendectomy, was admitted with vomiting and abdominal pain. Physical exam showed distended abdomen. Which is NOT your immediate action?

  1. A Emergency laparotomy
  2. B Nil by mouth
  3. C IV drip
  4. D Nasogastric tube for decompression
  5. E Pain control
💡 查看答案
A

Emergency laparotomy is NOT immediate action for SBO with previous surgery — trial of conservative management first (NPO, IVF, NGT, monitor). Surgery if signs of strangulation.

CRS·MCQ052 2010 MCQ54 MCQ A 10-year-old girl is admitted with a 2-day history of right-sided abdominal pain. Physical examination reveals pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is tenderness and guard…

A 10-year-old girl is admitted with a 2-day history of right-sided abdominal pain. Physical examination reveals pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is tenderness and guarding in the right lower quadrant. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Crohn’s ileitis
  3. C Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  4. D Mid-gut volvulus
  5. E Perforated peptic ulcer
💡 查看答案
A

10/F, 2 days RIF pain, fever 38.5°C, RLQ tenderness + guarding = acute appendicitis.

CRS·MCQ053 2010 MCQ68 MCQ 55/M presented with 24 hours central abdominal colicky pain, vomiting with feculent content and dehydrated. BP 100/60, pulse 110. What is the first line managem…

55/M presented with 24 hours central abdominal colicky pain, vomiting with feculent content and dehydrated. BP 100/60, pulse 110. What is the first line management?

  1. A Emergency laparotomy
  2. B IV fluid
  3. C IV analgesics
  4. D US abdomen
💡 查看答案
B

55/M, acute SBO with dehydration + shock (BP 100/60, P 110) → first priority is IV fluid resuscitation, then investigate/operate.

CRS·MCQ054 2010 MCQ69, 2015 MCQ34 MCQ A 21 years old female presented with 12-hour history of pain in right iliac fossa. She had LMP 8 weeks ago. Examination shows tender right lower quadrant tender…

A 21 years old female presented with 12-hour history of pain in right iliac fossa. She had LMP 8 weeks ago. Examination shows tender right lower quadrant tenderness with guarding and rebound. BP 110/70 mmHg, pulse 100/min, body temperature 36.8°C. What is the most important investigation?

  1. A Abdominal ultrasound
  2. B Emergency laparoscopy
  3. C Pregnancy test
  4. D Serum amylase
  5. E Urine microscopy and culture
💡 查看答案
C

21/F, RIF pain, LMP 8 weeks ago → must do pregnancy test FIRST (rule out ectopic pregnancy before any other investigation or surgery).

CRS·MCQ055 2011 MCQ11 MCQ Regarding the prognosis of CA colon, which of the following is incorrect?

Regarding the prognosis of CA colon, which of the following is incorrect?

  1. A Adjuvant chemotherapy increases survival
  2. B RT decreases local recurrence
  3. C Obstructed CA colon leads to worse prognosis
  4. D Right sided CA colon has worse prognosis than left sided CA
  5. E Pre-op CEA level signifies the prognosis
💡 查看答案
D

Right-sided CA colon does NOT have worse prognosis than left-sided in general (stage-for-stage similar, though biology differs). Adjuvant chemo improves survival (A true). RT decreases local recurrence for rectal (B true). Obstructed CA has worse prognosis (C true). Pre-op CEA correlates with prognosis (E true).

CRS·MCQ056 2011 MCQ13 MCQ Which of the following statements about colonic polyps is correct?

Which of the following statements about colonic polyps is correct?

  1. A Colonoscopic polypectomy is associated with 5% risk of perforation
  2. B Juvenile polyps are adenomatous polyps
  3. C Metaplastic polyps are pre-malignant
  4. D The risk of malignancy is higher in tubular than villous adenoma
  5. E Villous adenoma occasionally cause hypokalaemia
💡 查看答案
E

Villous adenoma occasionally causes hypOkalaemia (secretory diarrhoea losing K⁺). Colonoscopic polypectomy perforation rate <0.5% not 5% (A wrong). Juvenile polyps are hamartomatous (B wrong). Metaplastic polyps are benign (C wrong). Villous > tubular malignancy risk (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ057 2011 MCQ14 MCQ Which of the following statements regarding small bowel obstruction is INCORRECT?

Which of the following statements regarding small bowel obstruction is INCORRECT?

  1. A Bezoar obstruction usually occurs in patients with previous colonic surgery
  2. B Colicky abdominal pain and vomiting commonly occur
  3. C Gallstone ileus is associated with gas in the biliary tree
  4. D Intussusception in adult is seldom idiopathic
  5. E The most common cause in developed countries is peritoneal adhesions
💡 查看答案
A

Bezoar obstruction occurs after previous GASTRIC surgery (not colonic). All other statements are correct: colicky pain/vomiting (B), biliary gas in gallstone ileus (C), adult intussusception has lead point (D), adhesions commonest cause (E).

CRS·MCQ058 2011 MCQ15, 2014 MCQ7, 2017 MCQ62, 2020 MCQ63 MCQ Which of the following statement about malignancy associated with ulcerative colitis is CORRECT?

Which of the following statement about malignancy associated with ulcerative colitis is CORRECT?

  1. A Better prognosis than that not associated with UC
  2. B Related to activity of disease
  3. C Related to duration of disease
  4. D Follow adenoma-carcinoma sequence
  5. E Malignancy more common in anorectal UC
💡 查看答案
C

UC-associated malignancy is related to duration of disease (risk increases after 8-10 years). Not better prognosis (A wrong). Not related to activity (B wrong). Does NOT follow adenoma-carcinoma sequence — follows dysplasia-carcinoma sequence (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ059 2011 MCQ17 MCQ Laparoscopic colectomy for colorectal cancer is:

Laparoscopic colectomy for colorectal cancer is:

  1. A Associated with shorter hospital stay
  2. B Associated with fewer number of lymph nodes harvested
  3. C Associated with more blood loss
  4. D Contraindicated in patients with previous laparoscopic surgery
  5. E Only appropriate for early cancer
💡 查看答案
A

Laparoscopic colectomy is associated with shorter hospital stay. Similar LN harvest (B wrong). Less blood loss (C wrong). Previous lap surgery not contraindication (D wrong). Not only for early cancer (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ060 2012 MCQ11 MCQ A 60 years old man presents with intestinal obstruction. Investigations show renal failure. What is the cause of renal failure?

A 60 years old man presents with intestinal obstruction. Investigations show renal failure. What is the cause of renal failure?

  1. A Hypertension
  2. B Hypovolemia
  3. C Renal artery stenosis
  4. D Diabetes mellitus
  5. E Renal stone
💡 查看答案
B

Intestinal obstruction → dehydration → hypovolaemia → prerenal failure. The direct cause is hypovolaemia from third-space losses and vomiting.

CRS·MCQ061 2012 MCQ20 MCQ A 10 years old girl, presented with acute right lower quadrant pain and fever. Physical exam showed tenderness and guarding at the right lower quadrant. Which o…

A 10 years old girl, presented with acute right lower quadrant pain and fever. Physical exam showed tenderness and guarding at the right lower quadrant. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Crohn’s ileitis
  2. B Acute appendicitis
  3. C Intussusception
💡 查看答案
B

10/F, acute RIF pain + fever + RLQ tenderness/guarding = acute appendicitis.

CRS·MCQ062 2012 MCQ37 MCQ What is the MOST APPROPRIATE treatment for a 58 years old gentleman with sigmoid colon cancer and a single 4 cm metastasis in left lobe of liver?

What is the MOST APPROPRIATE treatment for a 58 years old gentleman with sigmoid colon cancer and a single 4 cm metastasis in left lobe of liver?

  1. A Chemoirradiation
  2. B Chemotherapy
  3. C Colonic and hepatic resection
  4. D Colonic resection and chemotherapy
  5. E Colonic resection and radiotherapy
💡 查看答案
C

Sigmoid CA + single resectable liver met → combined colonic and hepatic resection (curative intent) is the most appropriate treatment.

CRS·MCQ063 2012 MCQ38 MCQ A 75 years old lady with per-rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found 1 cm tumour, 1 cm above dentate line, no distant metastasis. EUS found only submucosal invasion.…

A 75 years old lady with per-rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found 1 cm tumour, 1 cm above dentate line, no distant metastasis. EUS found only submucosal invasion. Biopsy found well- differentiated adenocarcinoma. What is the most appropriate treatment?

  1. A Abdominoperineal resection
  2. B Observation and rebiopsy
  3. C Systemic chemotherapy
  4. D Topical chemotherapy
  5. E Transanal whole thickness resection
💡 查看答案
E

1cm rectal tumour, 1cm above dentate line, submucosal invasion only, well-differentiated = early rectal CA → transanal full-thickness excision.

CRS·MCQ064 2012 MCQ40, 2018 MCQ25, 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Concerning small bowel obstruction, which of the following is INCORRECT?

Concerning small bowel obstruction, which of the following is INCORRECT?

  1. A Bezoar commonly occurs after gastric surgery
  2. B Biliary gas is usually present in gallstone ileus
  3. C Obturator hernia usually occurs in young adults
  4. D Peritoneal adhesions are the most common cause in developed areas
  5. E Intussusception in adult is usually due to intestinal lesion
💡 查看答案
C

Obturator hernia usually occurs in elderly thin WOMEN, not young adults. All other statements correct.

CRS·MCQ065 2012 MCQ42 MCQ Which of the following about post-op complications is correct?

Which of the following about post-op complications is correct?

  1. A Anastomotic leakage after low anterior resection less than right colectomy
  2. B Laparoscopic colectomy wound has higher risk of infection than open colectomy
  3. C Abdomino-perineal resection’s perineal wound has a higher risk of infection than abdominal wound
💡 查看答案
C

APR perineal wound has higher infection risk than abdominal wound (contaminated field, dead space). Leak rates vary (A debatable). Lap wound infection less than open (B wrong).

CRS·MCQ066 2012 MCQ46 MCQ In patients suffering from familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), the polyps in the gastrointestinal tract would commonly first appear in:

In patients suffering from familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), the polyps in the gastrointestinal tract would commonly first appear in:

  1. A Adolescence
  2. B Early adulthood
  3. C Infancy
  4. D Middle age
  5. E Old age
💡 查看答案
A

FAP polyps commonly first appear in adolescence (teens), with CRC by age 40 if untreated.

CRS·MCQ067 2012 MCQ47 MCQ 59/F, central colicky abdominal pain with vomiting and abdominal distension. Last opened bowel 2 days ago. Midline laparotomy scar for previous perforated appen…

59/F, central colicky abdominal pain with vomiting and abdominal distension. Last opened bowel 2 days ago. Midline laparotomy scar for previous perforated appendicitis. Clinical exam revealed abdomen mildly tender and distended. Rectum empty on DRE. AXR dilated small bowel loops. Which of the following is most likely?

  1. A Adhesions
  2. B Constipation
  3. C Hypothyroidism
  4. D Ischaemic bowel
  5. E Sigmoid volvulus
💡 查看答案
A

59/F, previous laparotomy for perforated appendicitis, now colicky pain + vomiting + distension + empty rectum = adhesive SBO.

CRS·MCQ068 2012 MCQ48 MCQ What is the most common presentation of caecal carcinoma in a 80 years old lady?

What is the most common presentation of caecal carcinoma in a 80 years old lady?

  1. A Palpable mass in the right iliac fossa
  2. B Iron deficiency anaemia
  3. C Change in bowel habit
  4. D Per-rectal bleeding
💡 查看答案
B

Caecal/right-sided colon CA in 80/F → most common presentation is iron deficiency anaemia (occult bleeding). Change in bowel habit and PR bleeding are more common in left-sided CA.

CRS·MCQ069 2012 MCQ49 MCQ Which of the following is not true regarding extra-intestinal manifestation of inflammatory bowel disease?

Which of the following is not true regarding extra-intestinal manifestation of inflammatory bowel disease?

  1. A Erythema nodosum usually occurs on flexural surface
  2. B Primary sclerosing cholangitis is associated with Crohn’s disease
  3. C Pyoderma gangrenosum usually occurs near stoma
  4. D Ankylosing spondylitis is associated with HLA B27
  5. E Anterior uveitis and episcleritis are common manifestation
💡 查看答案
B

Primary sclerosing cholangitis is associated with ULCERATIVE COLITIS, not Crohn's disease. Erythema nodosum on extensor (not flexural, A wrong). Pyoderma near stoma (C true). AS with HLA-B27 (D true). Uveitis/episcleritis common (E true).

CRS·MCQ070 2012 MCQ56 MCQ 25/M with history of chronic constipation presents with 1 week of acute perianal pain exacerbated in defecation associated with small amount of bright red blood…

25/M with history of chronic constipation presents with 1 week of acute perianal pain exacerbated in defecation associated with small amount of bright red blood on paper after wiping. Abdominal examination is unremarkable but rectal examination is not possible due to pain. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Anal fissure
  2. B Cancer of anal canal
  3. C Fistula-in-ano
  4. D Haemorrhoids
  5. E Perianal haematoma
💡 查看答案
A

25/M, chronic constipation, 1 week acute perianal pain worse on defecation + small bright red blood = anal fissure. Classic presentation.

CRS·MCQ071 2013 MCQ47 MCQ 50/M presented with bloody diarrhoea and mucus with distended abdomen and low grade fever. AXR showed dilated transverse colon with diameter of 8 cm. What is th…

50/M presented with bloody diarrhoea and mucus with distended abdomen and low grade fever. AXR showed dilated transverse colon with diameter of 8 cm. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Colonoscopic decompression with flatus tube
  2. B Emergency ileostomy
  3. C Emergency extended right hemicolectomy
  4. D Emergency total colectomy with end ileostomy
  5. E Emergency protocolectomy with ileal anal anastomosis
💡 查看答案
D

50/M, bloody diarrhoea + mucus + dilated transverse colon 8cm + fever = toxic megacolon (likely UC) → emergency total colectomy with end ileostomy.

CRS·MCQ072 2013 MCQ48 MCQ 80/M presented with large bowel obstruction due to locally advanced mid rectal cancer. Vitals stable with no peritoneal sign. What is the best management?

80/M presented with large bowel obstruction due to locally advanced mid rectal cancer. Vitals stable with no peritoneal sign. What is the best management?

  1. A Emergency lower anterior resection
  2. B Insertion of colonic stent followed by chemoirradiation
  3. C Loop ileostomy followed by chemoirradiation
  4. D Loop transverse colostomy followed by lower anterior resection
  5. E Palliative care with IV morphine
💡 查看答案
B

80/M, LBO from locally advanced mid-rectal CA, stable, no peritonism → colonic stent followed by neoadjuvant chemoRT (bridge to definitive management).

CRS·MCQ073 2013 MCQ49 MCQ 30/F with rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found ulcerative colitis confined to the rectum. Which is the most appropriate management?

30/F with rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found ulcerative colitis confined to the rectum. Which is the most appropriate management?

  1. A IV infliximab
  2. B IV prednisolone
  3. C Mesalazine rectal suppository
  4. D Oral mesalazine
  5. E Oral prednisolone
💡 查看答案
C

Rectal UC confined to rectum (proctitis) → topical therapy first: mesalazine rectal suppository.

CRS·MCQ074 2013 MCQ50 MCQ 60/F with rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found multiple diverticula over the ascending colon and sigmoid, but source of bleeding not identified. Bile-stained flui…

60/F with rectal bleeding. Colonoscopy found multiple diverticula over the ascending colon and sigmoid, but source of bleeding not identified. Bile-stained fluid found in the terminal ileum. Bleeding is ongoing, but vital signs are stable. Which is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A CT abdomen and pelvis
  2. B RBC scan
  3. C Visceral arteries angiogram
  4. D Small bowel enteroscopy
  5. E Laparotomy with on-table colonoscopy
💡 查看答案
C

Active ongoing diverticular bleeding, source not identified colonoscopically, stable → visceral arteries angiogram (CTA or conventional) for localization and potential embolization.

CRS·MCQ075 2014 MCQ10, 2015 MCQ43 MCQ Which of the following statements regarding sigmoid diverticulitis is INCORRECT?

Which of the following statements regarding sigmoid diverticulitis is INCORRECT?

  1. A All complicated diverticulitis should be treated by emergency resection
  2. B Colovesical fistula is the most common type of fistula associated with sigmoid diverticulitis
  3. C Elective anterior resection should be offered to young obese male after first attack of uncomplicated sigmoid diverticulitis
  4. D Immunocompromised patients are prone to develop complicated diverticulitis
  5. E One-third of patients who recur after first attack of uncomplicated diverticulitis will have recurrent attack subsequently
💡 查看答案
A

NOT all complicated diverticulitis needs emergency resection — Hinchey I/II can be managed with antibiotics ± percutaneous drainage. All other statements are correct.

CRS·MCQ076 2014 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the followings is CORRECT about colonic polyps?

Which of the followings is CORRECT about colonic polyps?

  1. A Colorectal cancer arising from IBD is mostly due to adenomatous polyps
  2. B Inflammatory polyps are premalignant
  3. C Juvenile rectal polyps are adenomatous polyps
  4. D The risk of malignancy is higher in villous than tubular adenoma
  5. E Villous adenoma occasionally cause hyperkalaemia
💡 查看答案
D

Villous adenoma has higher malignancy risk than tubular (correct). CRC from IBD is NOT from adenomatous polyps (A wrong — dysplasia sequence). Inflammatory polyps are not premalignant (B wrong). Juvenile are hamartomatous (C wrong). Villous causes hypoK not hyperK (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ077 2014 MCQ13, 2018 MCQ19, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following colonic polyp is pre-malignant?

Which of the following colonic polyp is pre-malignant?

  1. A Hamartomatous polyp
  2. B Inflammatory polyp
  3. C Juvenile polyp
  4. D Metaplastic polyp
  5. E Villous adenoma
💡 查看答案
E

Villous adenoma is a premalignant colonic polyp (adenoma-carcinoma sequence). Hamartomatous, inflammatory, juvenile, and metaplastic polyps have low/no malignant potential.

CRS·MCQ078 2014 MCQ15 MCQ A 27 years old male with low CA rectum extended to anal canal. Colonoscopy shows hundreds of colonic polyps. What is your management?

A 27 years old male with low CA rectum extended to anal canal. Colonoscopy shows hundreds of colonic polyps. What is your management?

  1. A APR
  2. B Anterior resection
  3. C Panproctocolectomy with ileostomy
  4. D Restorative proctocolectomy with ileal pouch anal anastomosis
  5. E Subtotal colectomy with ileorectal anastomosis
💡 查看答案
C

27/M, low rectal CA extending to anal canal + hundreds of polyps (FAP) → panproctocolectomy with permanent ileostomy (can't preserve rectum due to rectal/anal canal involvement).

CRS·MCQ079 2014 MCQ16 MCQ A 36 years old lady, with Crohn’s disease, presented as 2 weeks’ history of severe anal pain. There is anal skin tag but externally no signs of inflammation. Th…

A 36 years old lady, with Crohn’s disease, presented as 2 weeks’ history of severe anal pain. There is anal skin tag but externally no signs of inflammation. The patient is unable to tolerate a digital rectal exam. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Bed rest and analgesia
  2. B Exam under anaesthesia biopsy
  3. C Excision of skin tags
  4. D Glyceryl trinitrate cream
  5. E Lateral sphincterotomy
💡 查看答案
B

Crohn's + severe anal pain + anal skin tag but no external inflammation → likely abscess or fissure deep within → exam under anaesthesia with biopsy for definitive assessment.

CRS·MCQ080 2014 MCQ23, 2017 MCQ3 MCQ Carcinoid is rarely found in:

Carcinoid is rarely found in:

  1. A Appendix
  2. B Duodenum
  3. C Lung
  4. D Pancreas
  5. E Rectum
💡 查看答案
D

Carcinoid is rarely found in the pancreas. Common sites: appendix, small bowel, rectum, lung.

CRS·MCQ081 2015 MCQ44 MCQ Following statements regarding stoma are correct EXCEPT:

Following statements regarding stoma are correct EXCEPT:

  1. A A loop transverse colostomy is more prone to prolapse than an end ileostomy
  2. B A stoma should be sited above the fold of an abdominal wall
  3. C Emergency operation is indicated for a stoma retracted into the peritoneal cavity
  4. D Loop colostomy provides more complete diversion than loop-end colostomy
  5. E PEG is contraindicated in patients with previous gastric resection
💡 查看答案
D

Loop colostomy does NOT provide more complete diversion than loop-end colostomy (loop allows some spillover). All other statements about stomas are correct.

CRS·MCQ082 2015 MCQ45, 2018 MCQ20, 2020 MCQ76 MCQ A 70 years old man presented with rectal bleeding and pneumaturia. Investigations confirmed adeno- carcinoma of lower rectum with a fistula connecting to the tr…

A 70 years old man presented with rectal bleeding and pneumaturia. Investigations confirmed adeno- carcinoma of lower rectum with a fistula connecting to the trigonal area of the urinary bladder. CT scan showed no signs of metastasis. What is the appropriate treatment for him?

  1. A Chemoradiation
  2. B Endoscopic stenting
  3. C Low anterior resection
  4. D Pelvic exenteration
  5. E Transanal resection
💡 查看答案
D

Rectal CA with fistula to bladder trigone, no mets → pelvic exenteration (en bloc resection of rectum + bladder).

CRS·MCQ083 2015 MCQ46 MCQ Which of the following is INCORRECT about colonic pseudo-obstruction?

Which of the following is INCORRECT about colonic pseudo-obstruction?

  1. A Can occur after spinal surgery
  2. B Endoscopic decompression and flatus tube insertion is useful non-operative treatment
  3. C Loop ileostomy is the treatment of choice if failed conservative treatment
  4. D Neostigmine is useful medication
  5. E Mentally retarded patients are prone to this condition
💡 查看答案
C

Loop ileostomy is NOT the treatment of choice for failed conservative management of pseudo-obstruction — caecostomy or subtotal colectomy may be needed. Endoscopic decompression (B), neostigmine (D) are correct.

CRS·MCQ084 2015 MCQ47 MCQ Which of the following is not a risk factor for recurrence of stage II resected adenocarcinoma of rectum?

Which of the following is not a risk factor for recurrence of stage II resected adenocarcinoma of rectum?

  1. A Bowel obstruction on presentation
  2. B Bowel perforation on presentation
  3. C Lymphovascular invasion in resected tumour specimen
  4. D Positive tumour resection margin
  5. E Tumour showing microsatellite instability
💡 查看答案
E

Microsatellite instability (MSI-high) is actually a FAVOURABLE prognostic factor in stage II CRC, NOT a risk factor for recurrence. Bowel obstruction, perforation, LVI, and positive margin are all risk factors.

CRS·MCQ085 2015 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following regarding infective colitis is NOT TRUE?

Which of the following regarding infective colitis is NOT TRUE?

  1. A Discharge from actinomycosis typically contains sulphur granules
  2. B Amoebiasis is diagnosed by trophozoites in faeces
  3. C TB is typically found in ileocecal region
  4. D Vancomycin is the treatment of choice for Clostridium difficile toxin
💡 查看答案
D

Oral vancomycin is now recommended first-line for C. difficile (not metronidazole) in current guidelines. However, in the context of this exam, metronidazole WAS first-line. The question asks what's NOT true — metronidazole treatment IS true historically. Let me reconsider... Actually, the other options need checking. Actinomycosis with sulphur granules (A true). Trophozoites in faeces for amoebiasis (B true). TB ileocaecal (C true). D states vancomycin is 'treatment of choice' — this depends on era. In older guidelines, metronidazole was first-line.

CRS·MCQ086 2016 MCQ50, 2019 MCQ39 MCQ Which of the following is correct about rectal carcinoma?

Which of the following is correct about rectal carcinoma?

  1. A CT scan is the most accurate method of assessing the depth of invasion
  2. B Local excision is sufficient for T3 rectal CA without lymph node metastasis
  3. C Circumferential margin is the determinant factor for local recurrence
  4. D Local recurrence rate after surgery in CA rectum is equal to that of CA colon
  5. E For CA rectum with submucosal spread, take distal resection margin of 5 cm in all cases
💡 查看答案
C

Circumferential resection margin (CRM) is the key determinant of local recurrence in rectal cancer. CT not best for depth (A wrong — MRI/EUS better). Local excision insufficient for T3 (B wrong). Rectal CA has higher local recurrence than colon (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ087 2016 MCQ51 MCQ Which of the following is INCORRECT in Peutz-Jeghers syndrome?

Which of the following is INCORRECT in Peutz-Jeghers syndrome?

  1. A It is autosomal dominant inheritance
  2. B It is characterized by adenomatous polyps in small intestine
  3. C It is associated with circumoral pigmented lesion
  4. D Presentation with anaemia in childhood is common
  5. E Malignant change occurs in 2–3% polyps
💡 查看答案
B

Peutz-Jeghers polyps are HAMARTOMATOUS, not adenomatous. It IS autosomal dominant (A true), has circumoral pigmentation (C true), presents with anaemia (D true), and has 2-3% malignant transformation (E true).

CRS·MCQ088 2016 MCQ65 MCQ A 10 years old boy presented with central abdominal pain for a few hours. Physical exam found minimal tenderness over the RLQ, and digital rectal exam was norma…

A 10 years old boy presented with central abdominal pain for a few hours. Physical exam found minimal tenderness over the RLQ, and digital rectal exam was normal. What is your management?

  1. A Perform barium meal / follow-through
  2. B Go back to the patient later in the day for review
  3. C Immediate appendicectomy
  4. D Send the patient home with the instruction to come back if pain worsen
  5. E Follow up in 1 week
💡 查看答案
B

10/M, central abdominal pain few hours, minimal RLQ tenderness, normal DRE → re-examine later (active observation). Too early for definitive diagnosis. Don't operate immediately or send home.

CRS·MCQ089 2017 MCQ64 MCQ Which one regarding haemorrhoid is TRUE?

Which one regarding haemorrhoid is TRUE?

  1. A Haemorrhoidectomy can be done as ambulatory surgery
  2. B Most commonly occurs at the anterior and posterior position
  3. C Blood usually mix with stool
  4. D Rubber banding of haemorrhoids should be done under spinal anaesthesia
  5. E Grade III haemorrhoids are painful
💡 查看答案
A

Haemorrhoidectomy CAN be done as ambulatory (day case) surgery. Haemorrhoids are at 3, 7, 11 o'clock (B wrong). Blood usually separate from stool (C wrong). Banding without anaesthesia (D wrong). Internal haemorrhoids above dentate line are painless (E wrong — unless thrombosed).

CRS·MCQ090 2017 MCQ66 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE?

Which of the following is TRUE?

  1. A RBC scan exactly localizes the site of bleed
  2. B Bleeding from diverticular disease mostly stops spontaneously
  3. C Clear NG aspirate rules out upper GI bleed
  4. D Angiodysplasia occurs in young people
  5. E The sensitivity of angiogram is higher than that of RBC scan
💡 查看答案
B

Diverticular bleeding mostly stops spontaneously (~75-80%). RBC scan localizes region not exact site (A wrong). Clear NG doesn't rule out UGI bleed (C wrong). Angiodysplasia in elderly (D wrong). Angiogram sensitivity lower than RBC scan (E wrong).

CRS·MCQ091 2017 MCQ67 MCQ An elderly man with AF admitted for diffuse abdominal pain. Passes large amounts of blood per rectum. What is the most likely diagnosis?

An elderly man with AF admitted for diffuse abdominal pain. Passes large amounts of blood per rectum. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Ischaemic bowel
  2. B Volvulus
  3. C Diverticulitis
  4. D Intestinal obstruction
💡 查看答案
A

Elderly + AF + diffuse abdominal pain + large PR bleeding = acute mesenteric ischaemia (arterial embolism from AF).

CRS·MCQ092 2017 MCQ75 MCQ A 10 years old girl was admitted for 2 days’ history of right-sided abdominal pain. On physical examination, she has pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is guarding and te…

A 10 years old girl was admitted for 2 days’ history of right-sided abdominal pain. On physical examination, she has pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is guarding and tenderness over the right lower abdomen. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Crohn’s ileitis
  3. C Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  4. D Perforated peptic ulcer
  5. E Sigmoid volvulus
💡 查看答案
A

10/F, 2 days RIF pain, fever 38.5°C, RLQ guarding/tenderness = acute appendicitis.

CRS·MCQ093 2018 MCQ21, 2021 MCQ?? MCQ Certain young patient present with appendicitis. During laparoscopy, you find perforated appendix, which of the following is correct?

Certain young patient present with appendicitis. During laparoscopy, you find perforated appendix, which of the following is correct?

  1. A A central line should be inserted at the end of the operation to facilitate the use of total parenteral nutrition
  2. B Pre-operative prophylactic surgery antibiotics should be continued in post-operative period
  3. C Should convert to laparotomy immediately
  4. D Insertion of a drain is all that is needed
  5. E The post-operative risk of wound and pelvic infection is less than 10%
💡 查看答案
B

Perforated appendicitis found at laparoscopy → prophylactic antibiotics should be continued as therapeutic course post-operatively. No need to convert to open (C wrong). Drain alone insufficient (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ094 2018 MCQ23 MCQ Which is true about diverticular diseases?

Which is true about diverticular diseases?

  1. A Bleeding usually comes from several diverticulum at once
  2. B Fistula of rectum/ vagina is common in women without hysterectomy
  3. C Surgery is indicated after one episode of attack
  4. D Sigmoid colon diverticulum is true diverticulum containing all layers of bowel wall
  5. E Colovesical fistula presents as gas in urine
💡 查看答案
E

Colovesical fistula presents with pneumaturia (gas in urine). Bleeding from several diverticula simultaneously is rare (A wrong). Rectovaginal fistula more common post-hysterectomy (B wrong). Surgery after 2 complicated attacks (C wrong). Diverticula are false (no muscle layer, D wrong).

CRS·MCQ095 2018 MCQ24 MCQ Which of the following statements about appendicitis is INCORRECT?

Which of the following statements about appendicitis is INCORRECT?

  1. A It can still be a possible diagnosis even without pain
  2. B Fecalith is present in 75-80% of the resected specimens
  3. C 20% appendices are located extraperitoneally and retrocaecal
  4. D The mortality of appendicitis increases with age
  5. E Appendicitis has the highest incidence in elderly
💡 查看答案
E

Appendicitis has highest incidence in young adults (10-30 years), NOT elderly. Diagnosis possible without pain (A true). Faecolith found in ~30% not 75-80% (B wrong actually — sources vary). Retrocaecal 20% (C true). Mortality increases with age (D true).

CRS·MCQ096 2019 MCQ2 MCQ An edentulous drug addict with previous partial gastrectomy because of peptic ulcer disease. This time comes in with signs of intestinal obstruction. CT showed …

An edentulous drug addict with previous partial gastrectomy because of peptic ulcer disease. This time comes in with signs of intestinal obstruction. CT showed dilated small bowels with obstruction at the terminal ileum, and intraluminal lesion. What is the cause?

  1. A Bezoar obstruction
  2. B Carcinoma of caecum
  3. C Adhesive IO
💡 查看答案
A

Edentulous + previous partial gastrectomy + intraluminal obstruction at TI = bezoar obstruction (common after gastric surgery + poor dentition).

CRS·MCQ097 2019 MCQ67 MCQ Man had URTI and took broad spectrum antibiotics for 1 week. Now lower abdominal pain, watery diarrhoea, and dehydration. Colonoscopy found whitish yellowish pa…

Man had URTI and took broad spectrum antibiotics for 1 week. Now lower abdominal pain, watery diarrhoea, and dehydration. Colonoscopy found whitish yellowish patches. What is the causative organism?

  1. A Clostridium difficile
💡 查看答案
A

URTI + broad-spectrum antibiotics + watery diarrhoea + whitish-yellowish patches (pseudomembranes) = Clostridium difficile.

CRS·MCQ098 2019 MCQ?? MCQ 30 years old smoker, 120/70 mmHg, 38 degrees fever, peritoneal signs, no free gas under diaphragm. Absent bowel sound. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE emergency in…

30 years old smoker, 120/70 mmHg, 38 degrees fever, peritoneal signs, no free gas under diaphragm. Absent bowel sound. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE emergency investigation?

  1. A CT abdomen
  2. B AXR
  3. C Gastrograffin
  4. D Laparoscopy
  5. E Laparotomy
💡 查看答案
A

30/M smoker, fever, peritoneal signs, no free gas, absent bowel sounds → CT abdomen is the most appropriate emergency investigation (can identify cause — appendicitis, diverticulitis, mesenteric ischaemia, etc.).

CRS·MCQ099 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Old man came in for GI bleed. PE only found old melena. Vitals stable. No more signs of clinical bleeding after admission. How to investigate?

Old man came in for GI bleed. PE only found old melena. Vitals stable. No more signs of clinical bleeding after admission. How to investigate?

  1. A Elective CT scan
  2. B Urgent double balloon enteroscopy
  3. C Urgent RBC scan
  4. D Capsule enteroscopy
  5. E Emergency laparotomy with on-table enteroscopy
💡 查看答案
D

Old man, GI bleed resolved (old melena), stable, normal OGD → capsule endoscopy for occure mid-GI bleeding evaluation (non-invasive).

CRS·MCQ100 2020 MCQ55 MCQ 22-year-old man presented with fresh rectal bleeding for 2 weeks. Upon physical examination, he was found to have second degree internal haemorrhoid. Haemorrhoi…

22-year-old man presented with fresh rectal bleeding for 2 weeks. Upon physical examination, he was found to have second degree internal haemorrhoid. Haemorrhoid was subsequently banded. What is your follow up management?

  1. A Colonoscopy
  2. B Flexible sigmoidoscopy
  3. C Conservative management
  4. D FOBT
  5. E CT colonography
💡 查看答案
C

22/M with 2nd degree haemorrhoids, successfully banded → conservative management with high-fibre diet. No need for colonoscopy or sigmoidoscopy in young patient with clear diagnosis.

CRS·MCQ101 2020 MCQ70 MCQ What is NOT a complication of colonoscopy?

What is NOT a complication of colonoscopy?

  1. A Cardiopulmonary complications
  2. B Colonic stricture
  3. C Electrolyte imbalance
  4. D Haemorrhage
  5. E Perforation
💡 查看答案
B

Colonic stricture is NOT a complication of colonoscopy. Cardiopulmonary complications, electrolyte imbalance (from bowel prep), haemorrhage, and perforation are complications.

CRS·MCQ102 2020 MCQ78 MCQ Which of the following is NOT associated with CA colon?

Which of the following is NOT associated with CA colon?

  1. A Familial adenoma
  2. B Lynch syndrome
  3. C Marfan syndrome
  4. D Peutz-Jeghers syndrome
  5. E Sessile polyposis syndrome
💡 查看答案
C

Marfan syndrome is NOT associated with colon cancer. FAP, Lynch syndrome, Peutz-Jeghers, and sessile serrated polyposis are all associated.

CRS·MCQ103 2021 MCQ1 MCQ Which one is the most important prognostic factor for colorectal cancer?

Which one is the most important prognostic factor for colorectal cancer?

  1. A CEA level
  2. B Number of lymph nodes involved on histology
  3. C Degree of differentiation
  4. D TNM staging at clinical presentation
💡 查看答案
D

TNM staging at clinical presentation is the most important prognostic factor for CRC (determines treatment and survival). LN number is part of staging (B is a component).

CRS·MCQ104 2021 MCQ?? MCQ What is true about intestinal obstruction?

What is true about intestinal obstruction?

  1. A Absence of bowel sound can rule out mechanical obstruction
  2. B Should listen to bowel sounds to differentiate between functional and mechanical obstruction
💡 查看答案
B

Should listen to bowel sounds to differentiate — high-pitched tinkling = mechanical; absent = ileus. Absence of bowel sounds does NOT rule out mechanical obstruction (A wrong — late/strangulated mechanical SBO can have absent sounds).

CRS·MCQ105 2022 MCQ13 MCQ 70 year old male presents with left lower quadrant pain for 3 days. A left lower quadrant mass is palpated. There is leukocytosis. CT shows sigmoid colon mural …

70 year old male presents with left lower quadrant pain for 3 days. A left lower quadrant mass is palpated. There is leukocytosis. CT shows sigmoid colon mural thickening with pericolic abscess and fat stranding. Colonoscopy 6 months ago shows no abnormality. What is the most likely differential diagnosis?

  1. A Left pyelonephritis
  2. B Sigmoid cancer
  3. C Sigmoid diverticulitis
  4. D Ulcerative colitis
💡 查看答案
C

70/M, LLQ pain ×3 days + mass + leucocytosis + mural thickening + abscess + normal colonoscopy 6 months ago = sigmoid diverticulitis with abscess (Hinchey II).

CRS·MCQ106 2022 MCQ24 MCQ Crohn’s patient . Which of the following is incorrect?

Crohn’s patient . Which of the following is incorrect?

  1. A CT/MR enterography provides good surgical planning
  2. B For stricture it is always better to do endoscopic stricturoplasty than to do surgery
  3. C Bowel preservation should be attempted by limiting bowel resection
  4. D Essential to correct malnutrition before surgery
💡 查看答案
B

Endoscopic stricturoplasty is NOT always better than surgery for Crohn's stricture — depends on length, location, and patient factors. Surgery with limited resection is often needed. CT/MR enterography (A true), bowel preservation (C true), and nutrition correction (D true) are correct.

CRS·MCQ107 2022 MCQ55 MCQ Higher incidence of complication of ileostomy compared to colostomy:

Higher incidence of complication of ileostomy compared to colostomy:

  1. A Dehydration
  2. B Necrosis/ischaemia
  3. C Prolapse
  4. D Hernia
💡 查看答案
A

Ileostomy has higher incidence of dehydration than colostomy (liquid output → more water/electrolyte loss). Colostomy has more prolapse and parastomal hernia.

CRS·MCQ108 2022 MCQ70 MCQ Colonic polyp. Which of the following need regular surveillance?

Colonic polyp. Which of the following need regular surveillance?

  1. A Adenomatous
  2. B Hyperplastic
  3. C Inflammatory
  4. D Hamartomatous
💡 查看答案
A

Adenomatous polyps need regular surveillance colonoscopy (premalignant potential). Hyperplastic, inflammatory, and hamartomatous polyps generally don't (some hamartomatous syndromes do need surveillance).

CRS·MCQ109 2022 MCQ71 MCQ Volvulus of the colon:

Volvulus of the colon:

  1. A Surgical resection is the only definitive treatment
  2. B Endoscopic decompression is still indicated even with ischemic bowels
  3. C Associated with long redundant bowel with wide mesenteric attachment
  4. D Sigmoid colon is the only location that can have volvulus
💡 查看答案
A

Surgical resection is the only definitive treatment for colonic volvulus (prevents recurrence). Endoscopic decompression is temporary (B wrong if bowel ischemic). Narrow mesentery, not wide (C wrong). Caecal and sigmoid can both have volvulus (D wrong).

CRS·MCQ110 2022 MCQ72 MCQ Man presents with sigmoid volvulus. Bowel is not ischemic or perforated.

Man presents with sigmoid volvulus. Bowel is not ischemic or perforated.

  1. A Colonoscopy and decompression
  2. B Hartmann’s procedure
  3. C NGT only
  4. D Surgical resection of the sigmoid
💡 查看答案
A

Sigmoid volvulus, viable bowel → colonoscopic decompression (first-line). Elective sigmoid resection after decompression to prevent recurrence.

CRS·MCQ111 2022 MCQ84 MCQ Regarding massive lower Gi bleeding, which is correct:

Regarding massive lower Gi bleeding, which is correct:

  1. A Bowel preparation is a must before urgent colonoscopy
  2. B CT angiogram can only detect bleeding greater than 5ml per min
  3. C Localisation of bleeding by RBC scan is rather vague
  4. D Angiogram is non-invasive
💡 查看答案
C

RBC scan localization is rather vague (identifies region, not exact point). Bowel prep not mandatory for urgent colonoscopy (A wrong). CT angiogram detects >0.3-0.5 mL/min (B wrong). Angiogram is INVASIVE (D wrong).

CRS·EMQ001 2002 EMQ6 EMQ TREATMENT OPTIONS FOR LARGE BOWEL CANCER

Theme: TREATMENT OPTIONS FOR LARGE BOWEL CANCER

Options
  1. A Open sigmoid colectomy
  2. B Laparoscopic assisted sigmoid colectomy
  3. C Hartmann’s operation
  4. D Open sigmoid colectomy, on table irrigation and primary anastomosis
  5. E Loop transverse colostomy
  6. F Bypass surgery
  7. G Open and close
  8. H Stenting
  9. I Observation
  10. J Colonoscopy

What is the MOST appropriate procedure for each of the following patients?

Questions
  1. Q1. A 54 years old good risk patient presented with acutely obstructed carcinoma of sigmoid colon. At laparotomy, the bowel was healthy looking.
  2. Q2. A 54 years old good risk patient presented with a non-obstructing carcinoma of sigmoid colon. There was slow continuous blood loss from the tumour causing anaemia. CT scan of the liver showed multiple small liver secondaries in both lobes of the liver.
  3. Q3. A 54 years old good risk patient presented with perforated sigmoid carcinoma and peritonitis.
💡 查看答案
Q1. D — Good risk + acutely obstructed sigmoid CA + healthy bowel → open sigmoid colectomy + on-table irrigation + primary anastomosis (one-stage).
Q2. F — Sigmoid CA with multiple liver mets → bypass surgery or stenting (palliative, not curative resection of primary with such extensive mets).
Q3. C — Perforated sigmoid CA + peritonitis → Hartmann's operation (resection + end colostomy, no primary anastomosis in contaminated field).
CRS·EMQ002 2003 EMQ2 EMQ BOWEL DISEASES

Theme: BOWEL DISEASES

Options
  1. A Intermittent infective diarrhoea
  2. B Ulcerative colitis
  3. C Crohn’s disease
  4. D Irradiation proctitis
  5. E Diverticulitis
  6. F Tuberculosis ileitis
  7. G Short gut syndrome
  8. H Irritable bowel syndrome
  9. I Amoebic colitis
  10. J Typhoid ileitis

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 45 years old patient presenting with history of intermittent bloody diarrhoea for 10 years with periodic exacerbation and remission.
  2. Q2. A 25 years old patient presenting with history of intermittent abdominal colic and fistula-in-ano.
  3. Q3. A 75 years old patient presenting with episodic massive fresh PR bleeding.
💡 查看答案
Q1. UC — 10 years intermittent bloody diarrhoea with exacerbation/remission = ulcerative colitis.
Q2. Crohn's — Intermittent abdominal colic + fistula-in-ano in young patient = Crohn's disease.
Q3. Diverticular disease — 75/M, episodic massive fresh PR bleeding = diverticular bleeding (commonest cause of massive lower GI bleed in elderly).
CRS·EMQ003 2004 EMQ3 EMQ ACUTE ABDOMINAL PAIN

Theme: ACUTE ABDOMINAL PAIN

Options
  1. A Appendicitis
  2. B Diverticulitis
  3. C Torsion of ovarian cyst
  4. D Sigmoid volvulus
  5. E Threatened abortion
  6. F Gastroenteritis
  7. G Colorectal cancer
  8. H Ectopic pregnancy
  9. I Ureteric colic
  10. J Urinary tract infection

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 65 years old lady presented with left lower quadrant pain and fever. She had two previous attacks over the past four years. Physical examination showed tenderness and minimal guarding at the left lower quadrant.
  2. Q2. A 20 years old lady presented with sudden onset of right lower abdominal pain accompanied by an attack of fainting. Her last menstrual period was six weeks ago. There was no vaginal bleeding or discharge. On physical examination she was afebrile but pale with tachycardia. There was tenderness and rebound tenderness at the right lower quadrant without guarding.
  3. Q3. A 35 years old lady presented with vomiting and left lower quadrant pain with radiation to the left groin for one day. There was no bowel symptoms nor dysuria. However she noticed slight darkening of urine. Her last menstrual period was three weeks ago with normal flow. Urine microscopy revealed no white cells but abundant red blood cells.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Acute diverticulitis — 65/F, recurrent LLQ pain + fever + LLQ tenderness/guarding = acute diverticulitis.
Q2. Ruptured ectopic — 20/F, sudden RLQ pain + fainting + LMP 6 weeks ago = ruptured ectopic pregnancy.
Q3. Left ureteric colic — 35/F, colicky LLQ pain → left groin radiation, dark urine (haematuria) = left ureteric stone.
CRS·EMQ004 2005 EMQ1 EMQ TREATMENT OF COLORECTAL DISEASES

Theme: TREATMENT OF COLORECTAL DISEASES

Options
  1. A Endoscopic stenting
  2. B Chemoirradiation
  3. C Pelvic exenteration
  4. D Abdominoperineal resection
  5. E Transanal excision
  6. F Chemotherapy
  7. G Radiotherapy
  8. H Rectal advancement flap
  9. I Endoscopic polypectomy
  10. J Anterior resection

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST suitable treatment from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 50 years old lady presented with on and off per-rectal bleeding for 6 months. Colonoscopy showed a 1 cm pedunculated lesion at 30 cm above the anal verge.
  2. Q2. A 75 years old man presented with a 3.5 cm ulcerative growth at the anal orifice occupying 1⁄2 of the anal circumference. Biopsy confirmed squamous cell carcinoma.
  3. Q3. A 55 years old man presented with per-rectal bleeding and pneumaturia. Investigation confirmed an adenocarcinoma of the lower rectum with fistulation into the trigone of the urinary bladder. CT scan revealed no distant metastasis.
CRS·EMQ005 2007 EMQ8 EMQ EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OF INTESTINAL DISEASE

Theme: EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT OF INTESTINAL DISEASE

Options
  1. A CT abdomen and pelvis
  2. B Gastrografin meal and follow through
  3. C Gastrografin enema
  4. D Small bowel enema
  5. E Hartmann’s operation
  6. F Extended right hemicolectomy
  7. G Sigmoidoscopy decompression
  8. H Colostomy
  9. I Anterior resection
Questions
  1. Q1. 70/M. abdominal pain, bowel not open for 3 days. Physical examination shows a distended abdomen with active bowel sound. Digital rectal exam shows an empty rectum with no palpable mass. Abdominal x-ray shows dilated large bowel with coffee bean sign.
  2. Q2. 65/F history of cholecystectomy 20 years ago. 2 days of abdominal pain, vomiting and constipation. Physical examination shows that abdomen is soft but distended, with active bowel sounds. Abdominal x-ray shows dilated small bowel with fluid level. Given intravenous fluid and nasogastric tube. No improvement in 48 hours. Remain haemodynamically stable.
  3. Q3. 75/F, fever, abdominal pain for 1 day. Physical examination: febrile, tenderness and guarding over lower abdomen. Digital rectal examination is normal. Laparotomy shows perforated sigmoid diverticulitis with faecal contamination.
CRS·EMQ006 2009 EMQ2 EMQ LOWER GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Theme: LOWER GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Amoebic colitis
  2. B CMV colitis
  3. C Cecal diverticulitis
  4. D Crohn’s colitis
  5. E Diversion colitis
  6. F Ischemic colitis
  7. G Pseudomembranous colitis
  8. H Radiation proctocolitis
  9. I Sigmoid diverticulitis
  10. J Ulcerative colitis

Select the most likely diagnosis in the following patients.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 50 years old gentleman recently suffered from pneumonia and was treated by antibiotics. He developed abdominal pain and bloody diarrhea 2 days after commencement of antibiotics. Examination of stool revealed Clostridium difficile toxin.
  2. Q2. A 30 years old lady presented with intermittent bloody diarrhoea. Colonoscopy revealed cobblestone appearance in sigmoid and ascending colons. Biopsy revealed granuloma inflammation.
CRS·EMQ007 2010 EMQ3 EMQ ABDOMINAL PAIN

Theme: ABDOMINAL PAIN

Options
  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Acute cholecystitis
  3. C Acute diverticulitis
  4. D Acute pancreatitis
  5. E Acute salpingitis
  6. F Biliary colic
  7. G Constipation
  8. H Peptic ulcer exacerbation
  9. I Large bowel obstruction
  10. J Mesenteric ischaemia
  11. K Small bowel obstruction
  12. L Ureteric colic
Questions
CRS·EMQ008 2010 EMQ5, 2013 EMQ3, 2015 EMQ2, 2018 EMQ1 EMQ TREATMENT FOR RECTAL NEOPLASM

Theme: TREATMENT FOR RECTAL NEOPLASM

Options
  1. A Abdominal perineal resection
  2. B Chemoirradiation
  3. C Hartmann’s operation
  4. D Lower anterior resection
  5. E Metallic stent
  6. F Proctocolectomy and end-ileostomy
  7. G Proctocolectomy with IPAA
  8. H Radiotherapy
  9. I Transanal excision
Questions
  1. Q1. 60/M rectal bleeding for 3 months. 2 cm ulcer at dentate line, SCC. No lymph or distant metastasis.
  2. Q2. 24/F rectal bleeding. Mass 2 cm from anal verge. Colonoscopy showed multiple polyps throughout colon and rectum. CT shows no metastasis. TRUS showed tumour invaded part of muscle layer.
  3. Q3. 55/M, <1 cm mass at rectum 4 cm from anal verge. no obvious mucosal involvement. TRUS: 0.8 cm at submucosa, no metastasis. Histology: carcinoid tumour.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Chemoradiotherapy — 2cm SCC at dentate line = anal canal SCC → chemoRT (Nigro protocol) is standard treatment (not surgery first).
Q2. Proctocolectomy + IPAA — 24/F, rectal mass + multiple polyps throughout colon (FAP) with muscle invasion → restorative proctocolectomy with ileal pouch-anal anastomosis.
Q3. Transanal excision — Small (<1cm) submucosal rectal carcinoid, no mets → transanal local excision.
CRS·EMQ009 2012 EMQ6 EMQ A. Amoebic colitis

Theme: A. Amoebic colitis

Options
  1. B Carcinoma of sigmoid
  2. C Irritable bowel syndrome
  3. D Ischaemic colitis
  4. E Pseudomembranous colitis
  5. F Radiation colitis
  6. G Sigmoid diverticulitis
  7. H Sigmoid volvulus
  8. I Ulcerative colitis
Questions
  1. Q1. A 48 years old female, history of hysterectomy 3 years ago, has had 2 episodes of left abdominal pain with fever and was treated with antibiotics; she recently found gas and faecal material from the vagina.
  2. Q2. F/85 admitted for pneumonia. was given piperacillin and tazobactam. Developed fever and bloody diarrhoea. Pus and mucus were found in stool.
  3. Q3. Patient recently diagnosed of primary sclerosing cholangitis developed blood and mucus in stool.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Diverticular disease with fistula — 48/F post-hysterectomy, recurrent LLQ pain + gas/faeces from vagina = colovaginal fistula from diverticular disease.
Q2. C. difficile colitis — Elderly, on antibiotics, fever + bloody diarrhoea + pus/mucus = C. difficile colitis.
Q3. UC — PSC + bloody mucus stool = ulcerative colitis (PSC is strongly associated with UC).
CRS·EMQ010 2013 EMQ6 EMQ RECTAL BLEEDING

Theme: RECTAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Anal fissure
  2. B Angiodysplasia
  3. C Colonic polyp
  4. D Crohn’s disease
  5. E Diverticular disease
  6. F Haemorrhoid
  7. G Meckel’s diverticulum
  8. H Rectal CA
  9. I Ulcerative colitis
Questions
  1. Q1. 4/F. Massive PR bleed with maroon coloured blood, hypovolaemia.
  2. Q2. 45/M. Diarrhoea with blood and mucus, low grade fever, painful joint.
  3. Q3. 65/F. Bright red PR bleed, recently developed tenesmus, long history of haemorrhoid.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Meckel's diverticulum — 4/F, massive PR bleed, maroon blood, hypovolaemia = Meckel's diverticulum (commonest cause of massive GI bleeding in children).
Q2. IBD (Crohn's/UC) — 45/M, diarrhoea with blood/mucus, low-grade fever, joint pain = inflammatory bowel disease.
Q3. Rectal carcinoma — 65/F, long-standing haemorrhoids, now new tenesmus + bright PR bleed = concern for rectal carcinoma (new symptom of tenesmus is red flag).
CRS·EMQ011 2014 EMQ5 EMQ INVESTIGATION FOR PER-RECTAL BLEEDING

Theme: INVESTIGATION FOR PER-RECTAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Emergency surgery
  2. B Gastroscopy
  3. C Colonoscopy
  4. D Sigmoidoscopy and proctoscopy
  5. E RBC scan
  6. F Mesenteric angiography
Questions
  1. Q1. M/65 with history of chronic hepatitis and ischaemic heart disease presented with massive per-rectal fresh bleeding. Shock at first but responded well to fluid.
  2. Q2. M/35 with constipation and per-rectal bleeding. Grandfather had CA colon diagnosed at 80 years old.
  3. Q3. F/65 with hypothyroidism presented with per-rectal bleeding. No change in bowel habits, weight loss or family history of CA colon.
CRS·EMQ012 2015 EMQ7, 2017 EMQ1 EMQ TREATMENT OF INTESTINAL NEUROENDOCRINE DISEASE

Theme: TREATMENT OF INTESTINAL NEUROENDOCRINE DISEASE

Options
  1. A Chemoembolization of liver metastasis and treatment with somatostatin analogue
  2. B Observation
  3. C Right hemicolectomy, lymphadenectomy, liver resection
  4. D Segmental small bowel resection
  5. E Somatostatin receptor radionuclide therapy
  6. F Systemic therapy with 5-FU and carboplatin
  7. G Treatment with somatostatin analogue
Questions
  1. Q1. An 82 years old gentleman presents with a 6-week history of diarrhoea, vomiting and flushing. CT abdomen showed mass at the mesentery and disseminated bulky bilobar liver deposits. Ultrasound-guided liver biopsy showed a (G1 Ki-67 1%) neuroendocrine cancer. Somatostatin receptor based PET/CT showed pathological uptake is evident in the liver, mesentery and distal ileum. Cardiological assessment showed signs of carcinoid heart disease.
  2. Q2. A 62 years old lady with previously resected G1 ileal neuroendocrine tumour presented with occasional upper abdominal discomfort. Her Karnofsky index is 0. Ultrasound of liver shows deposits in both liver lobes. Somatostatin receptor-based PET/CT confirms disease in the liver. In addition, multiple sites of pathologic uptake in skeletal system are seen.
  3. Q3. A 32 years old gentleman presented with 2 weeks of abdominal pain, diarrhea and occasional flushes. CT scan of the abdomen demonstrated deposits in the right liver lobe. Liver biopsy shows features of well-differentiated G1 neuroendocrine carcinoma. On video capsule endoscopy, a bulky submucosal tumor is seen in the terminal ileum. Somatostatin receptor-based PET/CT demonstrated pathologic uptake in the right liver lobe and in the caecal area. Serum chromogranin A and B was elevated as is 5-HIAA in the 24-hour urine.
CRS·EMQ013 2016 EMQ4 EMQ ANORECTAL CONDITIONS

Theme: ANORECTAL CONDITIONS

Options
  1. A Anal fissure
  2. B Anal melanoma
  3. C Colonic pseudo-obstruction
  4. D Fibrous anal polyp
  5. E Haemorrhoids
  6. F Rectal adenocarcinoma
  7. G Rectal prolapse
  8. H Rectal villous adenoma
  9. I Sigmoid diverticulitis with stricture
  10. J Sigmoid volvulus
Questions
  1. Q1. A 30 years old woman with painful defecation, fresh blood on tissue paper. Examination shows superficial linear lesion at posterior midline of anal region.
  2. Q2. A 50 years old gentleman complains of intermittent fresh rectal bleeding associated with defecation. Digital rectal examination shows a polypoid lesion at 5cm above the anal verge. Endorectal ultrasound confirms the lesion to be limited to the rectal mucosa.
  3. Q3. Elderly lady with symptoms of IO. AXR shows coffee bean sign. Colonoscopy found collapsed rectum and twist in sigmoid.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Anal fissure — 30/F, painful defecation, blood on tissue, superficial linear posterior midline lesion = anal fissure.
Q2. Rectal polyp/carcinoid — 50/M, intermittent PR bleeding, polypoid lesion at 5cm, submucosal on ERUS = submucosal rectal tumour (carcinoid or GIST).
Q3. Sigmoid volvulus — Elderly + IO + coffee bean sign + collapsed rectum + sigmoid twist on colonoscopy = sigmoid volvulus.
CRS·EMQ014 2019 EMQ1, 2020 EMQ3 EMQ RECTAL BLEEDING

Theme: RECTAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Anal fistula
  2. B Angiodysplasia
  3. C Colonic polyps
  4. D Crohn’s disease
  5. E Diverticular disease
  6. F Merkel’s diverticulum
  7. G Rectal carcinoma
  8. H Thrombosed external haemorrhoid
  9. I Ulcerative colitis
  10. J Anal fissure

For each of the following scenarios, choose the single MOST LIKELY diagnosis from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. F/30 has painful defecation when passing hard stool. Associated with fresh per rectal bleeding. Pain stopped after defecation.
  2. Q2. A 75-year-old gentleman has been admitted with passage of altered blood. The patient has been told to have multiple “air pockets” over the bowel and shown on contrast study before. He is also taking anticoagulants for underlying atrial fibrillation.
  3. Q3. An 80-year-old lady presents with tenesmus and weight loss for months. She complains of rectal bleeding and mucus passage at the same time.
  4. Q4. A 35-year-old gentleman complains of a painful anal nodule after excessive straining during defaecation. The anal nodule becomes softened after taking warm Sitz bath and analgesics for a few days.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Anal fissure — 30/F, painful defecation with hard stool + fresh PR bleed, stops after defecation = acute anal fissure.
Q2. Diverticular bleeding — 75/M, altered blood PR, known 'air pockets' (diverticula) on previous imaging, on aspirin = diverticular bleeding.
Q3. Rectal carcinoma — 80/F, tenesmus + weight loss + rectal bleeding + mucus = rectal carcinoma.
Q4. Thrombosed external haemorrhoid — 35/M, painful anal nodule after straining, softens with warm bath = thrombosed external haemorrhoid.
CRS·EMQ015 2021 EMQ5 EMQ GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Theme: GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Rectal carcinoma
  2. B Haemorrhoids
  3. C Meckel’s diverticulum
  4. D Crohn’s disease
  5. E Ulcerative colitis
  6. F Angiodysplasia
Questions
  1. Q1. Massive GI bleeding in 4-year-old girl, maroon color, presented with signs of hypovolemia.
  2. Q2. Young man joint pain and diarrhea with blood and mucus.
  3. Q3. Old lady long standing history of haemorrhoids recently has sense of incomplete bowel emptying now fresh PRB.
CRS·EMQ016 2022 EMQ7 EMQ ANORECTAL PATHOLOGIES

Theme: ANORECTAL PATHOLOGIES

Options
  1. A AdenoCA of anal canal
  2. B Anal fissure
  3. C Anal intraepithelial neoplasia
  4. D Anal stenosis
  5. E Melanoma
  6. F Fistula in ano
  7. G Haemorrhoid
  8. H Anal SCC
  9. I Anal wart
Questions
  1. Q1. 34 years old woman complaining of 2 years of anal swelling. Fresh blood on tissue paper. Swelling worse on straining. Occasional constipation.
  2. Q2. 29 years old man, anorectal abscess 5 months ago. On and off turbid blood staining fluid. Small opening with discharge and blood.
  3. Q3. 33 years old man, painful defaecating cannot tolerate PR exam and proctoscopy. Large skin tag at 6 o’ clock on inspection.
SECTION 05

ENT / Head & Neck Surgery

101 MCQ · 20 EMQ
ENT·MCQ001 2001 MCQ5 MCQ A 45 years old fisherman presented with an enlarging right neck swelling for two months. There were no chest or ear, nose and throat symptoms. Physical examinat…

A 45 years old fisherman presented with an enlarging right neck swelling for two months. There were no chest or ear, nose and throat symptoms. Physical examination revealed a 2 × 2 cm hard mass over right level II region fixed to underlying muscle. No other neck swelling was identified. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Carcinoma of oesophagus
  2. B Parotitis
  3. C Nasopharyngeal carcinoma
  4. D Branchial cyst
  5. E Viral lymphadenopathy
💡 查看答案
C

45/M fisherman, hard fixed level II neck mass = most likely NPC metastasis (HK endemic). Branchial cyst is smooth/mobile. Parotitis is preauricular.

ENT·MCQ002 2001 MCQ6 MCQ Which of the following statements is TRUE for vocal cord nodules?

Which of the following statements is TRUE for vocal cord nodules?

  1. A They may become malignant
  2. B They are bilateral
  3. C Speech therapy is not an effective treatment
  4. D They are related to smoking
  5. E Microlaryngoscopic removal is the first treatment of choice
💡 查看答案
B

Vocal cord nodules are bilateral ('singer's/screamer's nodules'). They don't become malignant (A wrong). Speech therapy IS effective first-line (C wrong). Related to voice abuse not smoking (D wrong). Surgery is second-line (E wrong).

ENT·MCQ003 2001 MCQ12 MCQ For acoustic neuroma:

For acoustic neuroma:

  1. A There is conductive deafness
  2. B It is not responsive to irradiation therapy
  3. C It may result in swallowing difficulties
  4. D It rarely causes tinnitus
  5. E It is frequently bilateral in tuberous sclerosis
💡 查看答案
C

Acoustic neuroma can cause swallowing difficulties (CN IX/X compression) when large. It causes sensorineural (not conductive) deafness (A wrong). It IS responsive to radiosurgery (B wrong). It causes tinnitus (D wrong). Bilateral in NF2 not tuberous sclerosis (E wrong).

ENT·MCQ004 2001 MCQ19, 2002 MCQ79, 2003 MCQ54, 2007 MCQ13 MCQ A 58 years old gentleman presented with sudden and spontaneous onset of right-sided weakness of the upper and lower face. There was no hearing loss or tinnitus.…

A 58 years old gentleman presented with sudden and spontaneous onset of right-sided weakness of the upper and lower face. There was no hearing loss or tinnitus. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Otitis media
  2. B Cerebral infarction
  3. C Temporal arteritis
  4. D Parotid tumour
  5. E Bell’s palsy
💡 查看答案
E

58/M, sudden unilateral upper + lower face weakness, no hearing loss = Bell's palsy (idiopathic LMN facial nerve palsy). UMN lesion spares forehead.

ENT·MCQ005 2001 MCQ28 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman has carcinoma of oral tongue. What is the MOST common level of neck lymph node metastasis?

A 60 years old gentleman has carcinoma of oral tongue. What is the MOST common level of neck lymph node metastasis?

  1. A I
  2. B II
  3. C III
  4. D IV
  5. E V
💡 查看答案
B

Oral tongue CA most commonly drains to level I and II cervical lymph nodes. Level II receives most tongue lymphatic drainage.

ENT·MCQ006 2001 MCQ35 MCQ Which of the following statements is TRUE for tonsillitis?

Which of the following statements is TRUE for tonsillitis?

  1. A Acute tonsillitis is commonest in the second decade of life
  2. B Chronic tonsillitis can cause obstructive sleep apnoea
  3. C Tonsillectomy is indicated for acute peritonsillar abscess
  4. D Staphylococcus aureus is the commonest bacteria isolated
  5. E Ciprofloxacin is the antibiotic of choice
💡 查看答案
B

Chronic tonsillitis with enlarged tonsils CAN cause obstructive sleep apnoea. Acute tonsillitis commonest in children 5-15 (A wrong). Tonsillectomy for quinsy is interval (C wrong). Group A Strep most common (D wrong). Penicillin/amoxicillin first-line (E wrong).

ENT·MCQ007 2001 MCQ44 MCQ Which of the following statements is FALSE for acoustic neuroma?

Which of the following statements is FALSE for acoustic neuroma?

  1. A It arises from vestibular nerve
  2. B Surgery is the first treatment of choice
  3. C Radiosurgery can avoid the risk of facial nerve paralysis
  4. D MRI scan is better than CT scan for screening
  5. E It usually presents as progressive unilateral sensorineural hearing loss
💡 查看答案
C

Radiosurgery (Gamma knife) CANNOT completely avoid facial nerve paralysis risk — there is still a small risk. All other statements about acoustic neuroma are true.

ENT·MCQ008 2001 MCQ54 MCQ Which of the following statements is FALSE for chronic rhinitis?

Which of the following statements is FALSE for chronic rhinitis?

  1. A Allergic rhinitis is more common than vasomotor rhinitis
  2. B The commonest allergen is house dust mite
  3. C Antihistamine is the first line drug of choice
  4. D Oral steroid is indicated for patients with poor response to antihistamine
  5. E Topical vasoconstrictive nose drop should not be used for a long duration
💡 查看答案
D

Oral steroid is NOT first-line for chronic rhinitis — it's reserved for severe/refractory cases. Intranasal steroid spray is first-line. Antihistamines for allergic (C true). Topical vasoconstrictors limited use (E true).

ENT·MCQ009 2002 MCQ13 MCQ Which of the following antibiotics is RECOMMENDED for first line treatment of chronic suppurative otitis media?

Which of the following antibiotics is RECOMMENDED for first line treatment of chronic suppurative otitis media?

  1. A Oral penicillin
  2. B Intravenous gentamicin
  3. C Topical ofloxacin
  4. D Topical gentamicin
  5. E Oral tetracycline
💡 查看答案
C

Topical ofloxacin (fluoroquinolone) ear drops are recommended first-line for chronic suppurative otitis media — safe, effective, no ototoxicity (unlike gentamicin).

ENT·MCQ010 2002 MCQ24, 2002# MCQ67 MCQ Which of the following drugs is RECOMMENDED for first line treatment of allergic rhinitis?

Which of the following drugs is RECOMMENDED for first line treatment of allergic rhinitis?

  1. A Oral steroid
  2. B Topical cromolyn
  3. C Topical sympathomimetic
  4. D Topical cholinergic
  5. E Oral antihistamine
💡 查看答案
E

Oral antihistamine is recommended first-line for allergic rhinitis (non-sedating H1 blockers like cetirizine/loratadine).

ENT·MCQ011 2002 MCQ47 MCQ All of the followings are associated with conductive deafness EXCEPT:

All of the followings are associated with conductive deafness EXCEPT:

  1. A Otosclerosis
  2. B Extensive tympanosclerosis
  3. C Acute otitis media
  4. D Meniere’s disease
  5. E Otitis media with effusion
💡 查看答案
D

Meniere's disease causes sensorineural hearing loss, NOT conductive. Otosclerosis, tympanosclerosis, acute OM, and OME all cause conductive hearing loss.

ENT·MCQ012 2002 MCQ54, 2002# MCQ65 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE regarding carcinoma of the oral tongue?

Which of the following is TRUE regarding carcinoma of the oral tongue?

  1. A Does not occur in patients younger than 20
  2. B Is best treated with intravenous cisplatinum
  3. C Is not painful
  4. D Does invade the mandible
  5. E Does not metastasize to the liver
💡 查看答案
D

Oral tongue CA does invade the mandible (direct extension). It CAN occur in young patients (A wrong). Surgery ± RT is mainstay not IV chemo alone (B wrong). It IS painful (C wrong). It CAN metastasize to liver (E wrong — distant mets possible).

ENT·MCQ013 2002 MCQ74, 2002# MCQ70 MCQ Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be primary cancer spreading to the neck lymph nodes?

Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be primary cancer spreading to the neck lymph nodes?

  1. A Bronchogenic carcinoma
  2. B Squamous cell carcinoma of the scalp
  3. C Adenocarcinoma of the stomach
  4. D Squamous carcinoma of the hypopharynx
  5. E Squamous carcinoma of the maxillary sinus
💡 查看答案
A

Bronchogenic CA metastasizes to MEDIASTINAL/hilar nodes, not typically cervical neck nodes as primary presentation. Scalp SCC, gastric CA (Virchow's), hypopharynx CA, and maxillary sinus CA all spread to cervical nodes.

ENT·MCQ014 2002 MCQ44 MCQ All of the following commonly give rise to otalgia EXCEPT:

All of the following commonly give rise to otalgia EXCEPT:

  1. A Acute otitis media
  2. B Otitis externa
  3. C Acoustic neuroma
  4. D Herpes zoster infection of external ear
  5. E Carcinoma of hypopharynx
💡 查看答案
C

Acoustic neuroma does NOT commonly cause otalgia. AOM, otitis externa, herpes zoster (Ramsay Hunt), and hypopharynx CA (referred otalgia) all cause ear pain.

ENT·MCQ015 2002# MCQ46 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a feature of facial nerve palsy?

Which of the following is NOT a feature of facial nerve palsy?

  1. A Facial asymmetry
  2. B Epiphora
  3. C Dysphonia
  4. D Conjunctival injection
  5. E Drooling
💡 查看答案
C

Dysphonia (voice change) is NOT a feature of facial nerve palsy — facial nerve doesn't innervate laryngeal muscles (that's recurrent laryngeal/vagus). Facial asymmetry, epiphora (eye tearing from poor lid closure), conjunctival injection, and drooling are features.

ENT·MCQ016 2002# MCQ66 MCQ Which of the following bacteria is LEAST commonly found in sinusitis?

Which of the following bacteria is LEAST commonly found in sinusitis?

  1. A Pneumococci
  2. B Staphylococcus aureus
  3. C Haemophilus influenzae
  4. D Pseudomonas aeruginosa
  5. E Escherichia coli
💡 查看答案
E

E. coli is the least commonly found in sinusitis. Common organisms: Pneumococcus, S. aureus, H. influenzae, and Pseudomonas (in chronic/nosocomial).

ENT·MCQ017 2002# MCQ68, 2011 MCQ80, 2015 MCQ20, 2019 MCQ62, 2020 MCQ67 MCQ Which of the following pathology is the MOST common in parotid tumour?

Which of the following pathology is the MOST common in parotid tumour?

  1. A Adenoid cystic carcinoma
  2. B Acinic cell carcinoma
  3. C Pleomorphic adenoma
  4. D Warthin’s tumour
  5. E Mucoepidermoid carcinoma
💡 查看答案
C

Pleomorphic adenoma is the most common parotid tumour (~65-80% of all parotid tumours). It is benign.

ENT·MCQ018 2002# MCQ69 MCQ All of the followings are causes of facial pain EXCEPT:

All of the followings are causes of facial pain EXCEPT:

  1. A Torus palatini
  2. B Dental infection
  3. C Trigeminal neuralgia
  4. D Nasopharyngeal carcinoma
  5. E Herpes zoster
💡 查看答案
A

Torus palatinus (bony exostosis) does NOT cause facial pain. Dental infection, trigeminal neuralgia, NPC, and herpes zoster all cause facial pain.

ENT·MCQ019 2002# MCQ88 MCQ Which of the following is CORRECT regarding tuberculous cervical lymphadenitis?

Which of the following is CORRECT regarding tuberculous cervical lymphadenitis?

  1. A It may have some clinical features similar to lymphoma
  2. B It is always associated with pulmonary tuberculosis
  3. C It is best treated with excision and then anti-tuberculous drugs treatment
  4. D It is an indication for radical neck dissection
  5. E It is treated with radiotherapy
💡 查看答案
A

TB cervical lymphadenitis may mimic lymphoma clinically (painless LN, constitutional symptoms). NOT always with pulmonary TB (B wrong). Excision alone insufficient — need anti-TB drugs (C wrong). Not an indication for radical neck dissection (D wrong). Not treated with RT (E wrong).

ENT·MCQ020 2002# MCQ89 MCQ Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be a cause of epistaxis?

Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be a cause of epistaxis?

  1. A Foreign body in the nose
  2. B Acute sinusitis
  3. C Nasal lymphoma
  4. D Chronic sinusitis
  5. E Nasal vestibulitis
💡 查看答案
D

Chronic sinusitis is unlikely to cause epistaxis (unlike acute sinusitis with mucosal inflammation/congestion). Foreign body, acute sinusitis, nasal lymphoma, and nasal vestibulitis can all cause epistaxis.

ENT·MCQ021 2003 MCQ7 MCQ The COMMONEST benign tumour of the parotid gland is:

The COMMONEST benign tumour of the parotid gland is:

  1. A Warthin’s tumour
  2. B Pleomorphic adenoma
  3. C Monomorphic adenoma
  4. D Haemangioma
  5. E Lymphangioma
💡 查看答案
B

Pleomorphic adenoma is the most common benign parotid tumour.

ENT·MCQ022 2003 MCQ14 MCQ The first branchial arch will form one of the following structures:

The first branchial arch will form one of the following structures:

  1. A Long process of incus
  2. B Stapes
  3. C Body and short process of incus
  4. D Great cornu of hyoid bone
  5. E Styloid
💡 查看答案
A

First branchial arch forms the long process of incus (and malleus). Stapes is from 2nd arch. Body/short process of incus from 1st arch too, but question asks 'one of' — A is correct.

ENT·MCQ023 2003 MCQ23, 2007 MCQ68, 2010 MCQ37 MCQ The COMMONEST malignant tumour of the parotid gland is:

The COMMONEST malignant tumour of the parotid gland is:

  1. A Squamous cell carcinoma
  2. B Carcinoma ex-pleomorphic adenoma
  3. C Mucoepidermoid carcinoma
  4. D Adenoid cystic carcinoma
  5. E Malignant lymphoma
💡 查看答案
C

Mucoepidermoid carcinoma is the most common malignant parotid tumour.

ENT·MCQ024 2003 MCQ30 MCQ The COMMONEST organism responsible for bullous myringitis is:

The COMMONEST organism responsible for bullous myringitis is:

  1. A Staphylococcus aureus
  2. B Pseudomonas aeruginosa
  3. C Candida albicans
  4. D Influenza virus
  5. E Mycoplasma pneumoniae
💡 查看答案
E

Mycoplasma pneumoniae is the commonest organism responsible for bullous myringitis (haemorrhagic blebs on TM).

ENT·MCQ025 2003 MCQ35 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a differential diagnosis of a mid-line neck swelling?

Which of the following is NOT a differential diagnosis of a mid-line neck swelling?

  1. A Subhyoid bursa
  2. B Thyroglossal cyst
  3. C Epidermoid cyst
  4. D Pharyngeal pouch
  5. E Pretracheal lymph node
💡 查看答案
D

Pharyngeal pouch (Zenker's diverticulum) is a POSTERIOR midline swelling at the neck, not true midline neck swelling. Subhyoid bursa, thyroglossal cyst, epidermoid cyst, and pretracheal LN are midline.

ENT·MCQ026 2003 MCQ48 MCQ Negative Rinne tuning fork test (i.e. bone conduction better than air conduction) is suggestive of:

Negative Rinne tuning fork test (i.e. bone conduction better than air conduction) is suggestive of:

  1. A Ototoxicity
  2. B Otosclerosis
  3. C Noise-induced deafness
  4. D Meniere’s disease
  5. E Sensorineural deafness
💡 查看答案
B

Negative Rinne (BC > AC) = conductive hearing loss. Otosclerosis is a classic cause of conductive hearing loss. Other options cause sensorineural loss.
