Hypokalemic hypochloremic metabolic alkalosis is the classic electrolyte disturbance of gastric outlet obstruction (loss of HCl from vomiting).

EGI·MCQ056 2007 MCQ9, 2009 MCQ90 MCQ A 60 years old man, with good past health, complained of weight loss 10 kg in 3 months. He was otherwise asymptomatic. Physical examination found hepatomegaly. …

A 60 years old man, with good past health, complained of weight loss 10 kg in 3 months. He was otherwise asymptomatic. Physical examination found hepatomegaly. Upper endoscopy and biopsy showed adenocarcinoma in the fundus of stomach. Chest x-ray showed numerous cannonball lesions. Ultrasound of the abdomen showed multiple nodules in both lobes of liver. What is the most appropriate management for him?

  1. A FNA liver
  2. B Regional intra-arterial chemotherapy
  3. C Systemic chemotherapy
  4. D Total gastrectomy
  5. E Gastrojejunostomy
💡 查看答案
A

Gastric adenoCA + hepatomegaly → FNA liver to confirm metastatic disease and guide treatment (histological confirmation of mets).

EGI·MCQ057 2007 MCQ60, 2008 MCQ39, 2010 MCQ26, 2017 MCQ25, 2021 MCQ?? MCQ A 65 years old gentleman with CA esophagus complains of dysphagia, and cannot tolerate even fluid diet. He also has dyspnoea at rest. SaO2 91% on 50% oxygen. Br…

A 65 years old gentleman with CA esophagus complains of dysphagia, and cannot tolerate even fluid diet. He also has dyspnoea at rest. SaO2 91% on 50% oxygen. Bronchoscopy showed that both vocal cords are mobile, but the tumour has invaded the trachea with 80% obstruction of the lumen. What is the most appropriate treatment to relieve his airway obstruction?

  1. A Chemotherapy
  2. B Tracheostomy
  3. C Tracheal endoscopic stenting
  4. D Esophageal stenting
  5. E Esophagectomy
💡 查看答案
C

CA oesophagus invading trachea + vocal cords mobile → tracheal endoscopic stenting (maintains airway patency). Oesophageal stenting alone won't address tracheal invasion/compression.

EGI·MCQ058 2010 MCQ28, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ A 30 years old gentleman complained of dysphagia and also regurgitation of fluid at night especially when he was supine. Investigations confirmed the diagnosis …

A 30 years old gentleman complained of dysphagia and also regurgitation of fluid at night especially when he was supine. Investigations confirmed the diagnosis of idiopathic achalasia. He was treated with pneumatic balloon dilatation once a year ago. Initially, his symptoms were somewhat improved but for the recent three months his symptoms had emerged again. He was otherwise well with no other illness. Which of the following would you offer to this patient as the most beneficial treatment?

  1. A Botulinum toxin injection
  2. B Calcium antagonist
  3. C Laparoscopic myotomy and Dor hemifundoplication
  4. D Esophageal resection
  5. E Second time balloon dilatation
💡 查看答案
C

30/M, achalasia, recurrence after balloon dilation → laparoscopic Heller myotomy + Dor fundoplication (definitive surgical treatment after failed dilation).

EGI·MCQ059 2010 MCQ46 MCQ What is the most appropriate treatment for bleeding oesophageal varices?

What is the most appropriate treatment for bleeding oesophageal varices?

  1. A Arteriogram and embolization
  2. B Intravenous PPI
  3. C Surgical exploration
  4. D Upper endoscopy with band ligation
  5. E Upper endoscopy with clip application
💡 查看答案
D

Bleeding oesophageal varices → upper endoscopy with band ligation (first-line endoscopic treatment for variceal bleeding).

EGI·MCQ060 2010 MCQ65 MCQ Which of the following is the least likely complication following a distal gastrectomy with Billroth II reconstruction?

Which of the following is the least likely complication following a distal gastrectomy with Billroth II reconstruction?

  1. A Delayed gastric emptying
  2. B Diarrhoea
  3. C Dumping syndrome
  4. D Duodenal stump blowout
  5. E Steatorrhea
💡 查看答案
A

Delayed gastric emptying is the LEAST likely complication after distal gastrectomy with Billroth II (the stomach is partially removed, reducing capacity). Diarrhoea, dumping, duodenal stump blowout, and steatorrhoea are recognized complications.

EGI·MCQ061 2011 MCQ23 MCQ 76/M, diffuse abdominal pain for 2 days. BP 90/50, pulse 110, abdomen tender and rigid. Laparotomy shows that peritoneal cavity grossly contaminated with bile a…

76/M, diffuse abdominal pain for 2 days. BP 90/50, pulse 110, abdomen tender and rigid. Laparotomy shows that peritoneal cavity grossly contaminated with bile and food. 2 cm peptic ulcer in fundus, 1 cm perforation. What is the best management?

  1. A Excision of ulcer and primary closure
  2. B Insertion of 2 drains in subhepatic space
  3. C Proximal gastrectomy
  4. D Total gastrectomy
  5. E Truncal vagotomy and pyloroplasty
💡 查看答案
A

76/M, peritonitis + 2cm fundal ulcer perforated → excision of ulcer and primary closure (need to excise to exclude malignancy + repair perforation).

EGI·MCQ062 2011 MCQ24, 2015 MCQ54 MCQ Which of the following arteries supplies the remnant stomach after a distal radical gastrectomy for cancer?

Which of the following arteries supplies the remnant stomach after a distal radical gastrectomy for cancer?

  1. A Left gastric artery
  2. B Left gastroepiploic artery
  3. C Right gastroepiploic artery
  4. D Short gastric artery
  5. E Splenic artery
💡 查看答案
D

After distal radical gastrectomy, the remnant stomach is supplied by short gastric arteries (from splenic artery). Left gastric, right gastroepiploic, and left gastroepiploic are all divided.

EGI·MCQ063 2011 MCQ42 MCQ 75/F dysphagia for 3 months, with weight loss and recent onset of hoarseness. Can only tolerate soft diet. Frequent regurgitation. No palpable axillary or cervi…

75/F dysphagia for 3 months, with weight loss and recent onset of hoarseness. Can only tolerate soft diet. Frequent regurgitation. No palpable axillary or cervical lymph nodes. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Achalasia
  2. B CA lung
  3. C CA esophagus
  4. D NPC
  5. E Esophageal diverticulum
💡 查看答案
C

75/F, progressive dysphagia + weight loss + hoarseness + regurgitation = carcinoma of oesophagus.

EGI·MCQ064 2011 MCQ44, 2016 MCQ53, 2018 MCQ27 MCQ In a patient with Boerhaave syndrome, the MOST LIKELY site of esophageal perforation is:

In a patient with Boerhaave syndrome, the MOST LIKELY site of esophageal perforation is:

  1. A Cervical esophagus on the left side
  2. B Cervical esophagus on the right side
  3. C Lower esophagus on the left side
  4. D Lower esophagus on the right side
  5. E Middle esophagus on the right side
💡 查看答案
C

Boerhaave syndrome: perforation occurs at the left posterolateral lower oesophagus (weakest point, just above the diaphragm).

EGI·MCQ065 2011 MCQ45 MCQ Which of the following requires the most clinical attention in patients with a columnar-lined esophagus?

Which of the following requires the most clinical attention in patients with a columnar-lined esophagus?

  1. A Erosive esophagitis
  2. B High-grade dysplastic change
  3. C Low-grade dysplastic change
  4. D Non-dysplastic change
  5. E Symptoms of heartburn
💡 查看答案
B

High-grade dysplasia in Barrett's requires most clinical attention (highest risk of progression to adenocarcinoma → consider endoscopic therapy or surgery).

EGI·MCQ066 2011 MCQ46 MCQ Which of the following requires the MOST URGENT attention in patient who have attempted suicide by drinking caustic solution?

Which of the following requires the MOST URGENT attention in patient who have attempted suicide by drinking caustic solution?

  1. A Gastric perforation
  2. B Gastric outlet obstruction
  3. C Metabolic acidosis/alkalosis
  4. D Esophageal perforation
  5. E Upper airway obstruction
💡 查看答案
E

Upper airway obstruction is the MOST URGENT concern after caustic ingestion (oedema of pharynx/larynx can cause asphyxiation). GI perforation is serious but airway takes priority.

EGI·MCQ067 2011 MCQ89 MCQ A 45 years old lady with long-standing history of rheumatoid arthritis presented with sudden onset of severe epigastric pain. She lied still and breathed shallo…

A 45 years old lady with long-standing history of rheumatoid arthritis presented with sudden onset of severe epigastric pain. She lied still and breathed shallowly. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute cholangitis
  2. B Acute pancreatitis
  3. C Indigestion
  4. D Myocardial infarction
  5. E Perforated peptic ulcer
💡 查看答案
E

45/F, long-standing RA (on NSAIDs) + sudden severe epigastric pain + lying still + shallow breathing = perforated peptic ulcer (NSAID-induced).

EGI·MCQ068 2012 MCQ6, 2014 MCQ29, 2017 MCQ23, 2018 MCQ26, 2020 MCQ14 MCQ A 35 years old lady, who drank toilet cleanser 2 hours ago, now presented with sore throat, drooling of saliva, increased breathlessness and hoarseness of voice…

A 35 years old lady, who drank toilet cleanser 2 hours ago, now presented with sore throat, drooling of saliva, increased breathlessness and hoarseness of voice. She was afebrile, HR 120 bpm. RR 40, with distress. What should be the immediate management?

  1. A 100% O2
  2. B Direct laryngoscopy
  3. C Indirect laryngoscopy
  4. D Upper endoscopy
  5. E IV NS 1 L full rate
💡 查看答案
A

Caustic ingestion 2h ago + SOB + stridor + hoarseness + tachycardia = airway compromise → 100% O₂ first, then secure airway. Direct laryngoscopy risky (may worsen oedema).

EGI·MCQ069 2012 MCQ7, 2015 MCQ49, 2018 MCQ28 MCQ A 50 years old man, with known history of chronic GERD, presented with mild dysphagia. Upper endoscopy showed salmon-pink patch extending 5 cm above the gastroe…

A 50 years old man, with known history of chronic GERD, presented with mild dysphagia. Upper endoscopy showed salmon-pink patch extending 5 cm above the gastroesophageal junction, with no visible nodule. What is the most likely histology?

  1. A Squamous cell carcinoma
  2. B Adenocarcinoma
  3. C Specialized intestinal dysplasia
  4. D Adenosquamous carcinoma
  5. E Fundic cell dysplasia
💡 查看答案
C

Chronic GERD + 5cm salmon-pink patch above GEJ = Barrett's oesophagus → specialized intestinal metaplasia (with goblet cells) is the expected histology.

EGI·MCQ070 2012 MCQ21 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST SIGNIFICANT risk factor predicting mortality of perforated duodenal ulcer?

Which of the following is the MOST SIGNIFICANT risk factor predicting mortality of perforated duodenal ulcer?

  1. A Chronic smoker
  2. B Aspirin use
  3. C Known liver cirrhosis
  4. D Male sex
  5. E Perforated ulcer diameter > 0.5cm
💡 查看答案
C

Known liver cirrhosis is the most significant mortality risk factor for perforated DU (poor physiological reserve, coagulopathy, impaired healing).

EGI·MCQ071 2012 MCQ22 MCQ Which of the following concerning the treatment of gastric adenocarcinoma is correct?

Which of the following concerning the treatment of gastric adenocarcinoma is correct?

  1. A Curative resection could not be achieved if perigastric lymph node is found
  2. B Direct invasion to the transverse colon is a contraindication for gastrectomy
  3. C Systemic chemotherapy can improve the survival for multiple liver metastasis
  4. D D3 lymphadenopathy has better survival outcome than D2
  5. E Vitamin B12 supplements have to be given to those after distal radical gastrectomy
💡 查看答案
C

Systemic chemotherapy can improve survival for gastric adenoCA with multiple liver mets. Perigastric LN doesn't preclude curative resection (A wrong). Direct colon invasion → en bloc resection possible (B wrong). D3>D2 not proven (D wrong). B12 supplement needed after TOTAL gastrectomy not distal (E wrong).

EGI·MCQ072 2012 MCQ45 MCQ 65/F, with history of OA and currently on NSAIDs, presents with sudden onset of epigastric pain. What is the most likely diagnosis?

65/F, with history of OA and currently on NSAIDs, presents with sudden onset of epigastric pain. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute cholecystitis
  2. B Acute pancreatitis
  3. C Myocardial infarction
  4. D Perforation of peptic ulcer
💡 查看答案
D

60/F on NSAIDs + sudden epigastric pain = perforation of peptic ulcer (NSAID-related).

EGI·MCQ073 2013 MCQ72 MCQ 52/F history of psychiatric illness swallowed a cuter blade, asymptomatic and P/E finds no abnormality. She has no fever. AXR shows an opaque object in the cent…

52/F history of psychiatric illness swallowed a cuter blade, asymptomatic and P/E finds no abnormality. She has no fever. AXR shows an opaque object in the central abdomen and no free gases. Upper endoscopy could not identify the object. What is the appropriate management?

  1. A Emergency capsule endoscopy
  2. B Emergency CT abdomen
  3. C Emergency gastrograffin meal and follow through
  4. D Emergency operation
  5. E Nasogastric tube
💡 查看答案
B

Swallowed cutter blade, asymptomatic, no free gas, past UGD junction → emergency CT abdomen to locate and plan retrieval.

EGI·MCQ074 2013 MCQ79 MCQ 25/M presented with tarry stool with haematemesis on admission. BP 60/40, pulse rate 150. On IV NS 80 mL/hr, Haemocue shows Hb 5.9 g/dL. What is the immediate m…

25/M presented with tarry stool with haematemesis on admission. BP 60/40, pulse rate 150. On IV NS 80 mL/hr, Haemocue shows Hb 5.9 g/dL. What is the immediate management?

  1. A Check serum K
  2. B Use 5% Dextrose instead of NS
  3. C Give unmatched but group specific blood
  4. D Insert central catheter for monitor
  5. E Take blood for CEA level
💡 查看答案
C

25/M, massive haematemesis + shock (BP 60/40, P 150) + Hb 5.9 → give uncrossmatched but group-specific blood immediately (life-threatening haemorrhagic shock).

EGI·MCQ075 2014 MCQ31 MCQ M/73 presented with acute melaena. OGD showed 2 cm ulcer at antrum, acute bleeding from edge. What is the least useful haemostatic treatment?

M/73 presented with acute melaena. OGD showed 2 cm ulcer at antrum, acute bleeding from edge. What is the least useful haemostatic treatment?

  1. A Argon beam coagulation
  2. B Banding
  3. C Clip
  4. D Heat probe coagulation
  5. E Injection of adrenaline
💡 查看答案
B

Banding is LEAST useful for bleeding gastric ulcer at antrum edge (banding is for varices, not ulcers). APC, clip, heat probe, and adrenaline injection are all appropriate for ulcer bleeding.

EGI·MCQ076 2014 MCQ33 MCQ A lady with family history of FAP. History of pancolectomy for FAP of colon. Recent endoscopy shows two 1–2 cm polyps at the fundus and body of stomach. What is…

A lady with family history of FAP. History of pancolectomy for FAP of colon. Recent endoscopy shows two 1–2 cm polyps at the fundus and body of stomach. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A FAP of the stomach
  2. B Fundic gland polyps
  3. C Hyperplastic polyps
  4. D Juvenile polyps
  5. E Peutz-Jegher syndrome
💡 查看答案
B

FAP patient with pancolectomy + 1-2cm gastric polyps at fundus/body = fundic gland polyps (very common in FAP, usually benign).

EGI·MCQ077 2014 MCQ35, 2015 MCQ55, 2016 MCQ24, 2017 MCQ12, 2018 MCQ14 MCQ Which of the following is not a cause of free gas under diaphragm for plain CXR and AXR?

Which of the following is not a cause of free gas under diaphragm for plain CXR and AXR?

  1. A Laparotomy
  2. B Laparoscopy
  3. C Perforated gastric ulcer
  4. D Pleuroperitoneal fistula
  5. E Ruptured ectopic pregnancy
💡 查看答案
E

Ruptured ectopic pregnancy does NOT cause free gas under diaphragm (it causes haemoperitoneum, not pneumoperitoneum). Laparotomy, laparoscopy, perforated gastric ulcer, and pleuroperitoneal fistula can all cause free gas.

EGI·MCQ078 2015 MCQ50, 2016 MCQ54, 2021 MCQ28 MCQ The formation of a epiphrenic oesophageal diverticulum is most commonly associated with which of the following?

The formation of a epiphrenic oesophageal diverticulum is most commonly associated with which of the following?

  1. A Botox injection
  2. B Diabetic neuropathy
  3. C Oesophageal motility disorder
  4. D Oesophageal tuberculosis
  5. E Scleroderma
💡 查看答案
C

Epiphrenic diverticulum is most commonly associated with oesophageal motility disorder (distal oesophageal spasm or achalasia causing pulsion diverticulum).

EGI·MCQ079 2015 MCQ51 MCQ Duodenal ulcer is least associated with which of the following?

Duodenal ulcer is least associated with which of the following?

  1. A Perforation
  2. B Malignancy
  3. C Pancreatitis
  4. D Intestinal obstruction
  5. E Bleeding
💡 查看答案
B

DU is LEAST associated with malignancy (DU almost never becomes malignant, unlike gastric ulcers). Perforation, pancreatitis, obstruction, and bleeding are all DU complications.

EGI·MCQ080 2016 MCQ36 MCQ Which of the following is the GOLD STANDARD for diagnosis of GERD?

Which of the following is the GOLD STANDARD for diagnosis of GERD?

  1. A 24-hour pH study
  2. B Contrast meal
  3. C Milk scan
  4. D Esophageal manometry
  5. E Esophagogastroscopy
💡 查看答案
A

24-hour pH study is the gold standard for diagnosis of GERD (quantifies acid exposure time).

EGI·MCQ081 2016 MCQ56 MCQ Woman presented with epigastric pain. Upper endoscopy showed 3.5 cm deep gastric ulcer at antrum. Biopsy showed no malignancy, and H. pylori negative. Patient w…

Woman presented with epigastric pain. Upper endoscopy showed 3.5 cm deep gastric ulcer at antrum. Biopsy showed no malignancy, and H. pylori negative. Patient was then treated with PPI, but symptoms continue. Repeated endoscopy showed the same 3.5 cm gastric ulcer. Repeated biopsy showed no malignancy. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE treatment?

  1. A Distal gastrectomy
  2. B Endoscopic submucosal dissection
  3. C Lifelong PPI
  4. D Local excision of ulcer (and primary closure?)
  5. E Vagotomy and pyloroplasty
💡 查看答案
A

3.5cm deep gastric ulcer at antrum, PPI-refractory, persistent → distal gastrectomy (non-healing large gastric ulcer despite adequate PPI trial → excise to exclude malignancy + definitive treatment).

EGI·MCQ082 2016 MCQ62 MCQ A 60 years old drinker, presented to the A&E department because of haematemesis and fresh melaena. Which is the most likely cause?

A 60 years old drinker, presented to the A&E department because of haematemesis and fresh melaena. Which is the most likely cause?

  1. A Acute myocardial infarction
  2. B Bleeding esophageal varices
  3. C Perforated duodenal ulcer
  4. D Ruptured AAA
  5. E Ruptured HCC
💡 查看答案
B

60/M drinker + haematemesis + melena = most likely bleeding oesophageal varices (alcoholic cirrhosis → portal hypertension).

EGI·MCQ083 2017 MCQ17 MCQ Which of the following is true for gastric adenocarcinoma?

Which of the following is true for gastric adenocarcinoma?

  1. A Adjuvant chemotherapy should be given to all stage 1 patients after resection
  2. B Curative resection is impossible in the presence of perigastric LN
  3. C Endoscopic stent is better than bypass in unresectable obstructing gastric cancer without metastasis in a fit patient
  4. D Splenectomy should be avoided if possible
  5. E Total gastrectomy has better survival than subtotal gastrectomy
💡 查看答案
D

Splenectomy should be avoided if possible during gastrectomy (to reduce complications). Adjuvant chemo not for all stage 1 (A wrong). Perigastric LN doesn't preclude cure (B wrong). Gastrojejunostomy better than stent for unresectable (C debatable). Total ≠ better survival than subtotal for distal tumours (E wrong).

EGI·MCQ084 2017 MCQ18 MCQ An obese lady, with type 2 DM not on insulin, received sleeve gastrectomy. Which of the following is the LEAST likely long term outcome after surgery?

An obese lady, with type 2 DM not on insulin, received sleeve gastrectomy. Which of the following is the LEAST likely long term outcome after surgery?

  1. A Remission of DM
  2. B Improvement of BP
  3. C Sustained weight loss
  4. D Increased long term survival
  5. E Iron deficiency anaemia
💡 查看答案
E

Iron deficiency anaemia IS a likely long-term outcome after sleeve gastrectomy (reduced acid production → impaired iron absorption). DM remission (A), BP improvement (B), sustained weight loss (C), and increased survival (D) are all expected benefits.

EGI·MCQ085 2017 MCQ20 MCQ 21/M, commit suicide by swallowing battery acid. 4 hours later he presented to the A&E with burns on lips (but no intraoral damage). Which tests below can most …

21/M, commit suicide by swallowing battery acid. 4 hours later he presented to the A&E with burns on lips (but no intraoral damage). Which tests below can most likely give an indication of amount of battery acid ingested and the damage done?

  1. A ABG
  2. B CT + IV contrast
  3. C Upper endoscopy
  4. D Double contrast barium swallow
  5. E Water soluble contrast swallow
💡 查看答案
C

Caustic ingestion 4h ago, lip burns → upper endoscopy gives the best indication of extent of damage (Zargar classification of caustic injury severity guides management).

EGI·MCQ086 2017 MCQ21 MCQ 64/M, just had esophagectomy for CA oesophagus, now 48 hours post-op. Presents with atrial fibrillation. His RR is 20, temperature is 37.8°C. What is the most l…

64/M, just had esophagectomy for CA oesophagus, now 48 hours post-op. Presents with atrial fibrillation. His RR is 20, temperature is 37.8°C. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Myocardial infarction
  2. B Pulmonary embolism
  3. C Tension pneumothorax
  4. D Anastomotic leak
  5. E Aspiration pneumonia
💡 查看答案
D

Post-oesophagectomy 48h, AF + mild fever + tachypnoea → anastomotic leak must be excluded first (AF is a harbinger of leak). It's the most dangerous diagnosis to miss.

EGI·MCQ087 2017 MCQ22 MCQ 75/M, episodic diarrhoea for 3 years since partial gastrectomy for CA. What is the most likely cause?

75/M, episodic diarrhoea for 3 years since partial gastrectomy for CA. What is the most likely cause?

  1. A B12 deficiency
  2. B Bile acid malabsorption
  3. C Has had vagotomy
  4. D Impaired calcium absorption
  5. E Paraneoplastic phenomenon due to residual disease
💡 查看答案
C

Episodic diarrhoea ×3yr after partial gastrectomy = post-vagotomy diarrhoea (vagotomy performed during gastrectomy → altered gut motility + bile acid malabsorption). This is the most likely cause given the chronicity.

EGI·MCQ088 2017 MCQ24 MCQ After a 3-phase esophagectomy, a man was admitted into ICU for post-op care. He is on mechanical ventilator. 3 hours later, there was surgical emphysema bilater…

After a 3-phase esophagectomy, a man was admitted into ICU for post-op care. He is on mechanical ventilator. 3 hours later, there was surgical emphysema bilaterally over chest wall up to the neck. What is the most likely cause?

  1. A Air leak from small lung bullae
  2. B Air leak from major airway
  3. C Air leak from chest drain
  4. D Air leak from OG anastomosis
  5. E Dehiscence of OG anastomosis
💡 查看答案
B

Post-3-phase oesophagectomy on ventilator → bilateral surgical emphysema 3h later = air leak from major airway (tracheal/bronchial injury during surgery). Anastomotic leak (D/E) is too early at 3h and wouldn't cause bilateral emphysema this rapidly.

EGI·MCQ089 2018 MCQ29 MCQ A 30 years old man with obesity visit you at your clinic complaining of heartburn which is more severe at night and only slightly relieved by drinking water. Wh…

A 30 years old man with obesity visit you at your clinic complaining of heartburn which is more severe at night and only slightly relieved by drinking water. What is the MOST USEFUL next step?

  1. A Oesophageal manometry and 24 hour pH monitoring
  2. B Order magnetic resonance imaging
  3. C Trial of proton pump inhibitor to see if there is symptomatic relief
💡 查看答案
C

30/M, heartburn worse at night → trial of PPI is the most useful initial step (diagnostic and therapeutic — if symptoms improve, confirms acid reflux).

EGI·MCQ090 2018 MCQ30 MCQ Which of the followings is the most common histology of polyp in stomach?

Which of the followings is the most common histology of polyp in stomach?

  1. A Adenomatous polyp
  2. B Fundic gland polyp
  3. C Hamartomatous polyp
  4. D Hyperplastic polyp
  5. E Inflammatory polyp
💡 查看答案
B

Fundic gland polyp is the most common gastric polyp histologically (especially with PPI use).

EGI·MCQ091 2018 MCQ31 MCQ Most likely to be found in submucosal layer of stomach:

Most likely to be found in submucosal layer of stomach:

  1. A Adenoma
  2. B Ectopic pancreas
  3. C Gastrointestinal stromal tumour
  4. D MALT lymphoma
  5. E Signet ring cell carcinoma
💡 查看答案
C

GIST most commonly arises from the muscularis propria (submucosa-muscularis junction) of the stomach. Ectopic pancreas is also submucosal but GIST is more common.

EGI·MCQ092 2018 MCQ32 MCQ Which of the following histochemical marker is the most specific for detection of gastrointestinal stromal tumour?

Which of the following histochemical marker is the most specific for detection of gastrointestinal stromal tumour?

  1. A CD117
  2. B CK7
  3. C Desmin
  4. D MUC2
  5. E S-100
💡 查看答案
A

CD117 (c-KIT) is the most specific marker for GIST detection (~95% positive).

EGI·MCQ093 2019 MCQ3 MCQ Found gastric GIST 7cm mitotic figure 8/50 hpf and resected. What should be offered?

Found gastric GIST 7cm mitotic figure 8/50 hpf and resected. What should be offered?

  1. A Imatinib
  2. B Sunitinib
  3. C Check EGFR mutation
  4. D Check KRAS mutation
  5. E Surveillance every 6 months
💡 查看答案
A

7cm GIST with mitotic figure 8/50 HPF = high-risk GIST after resection → adjuvant imatinib (tyrosine kinase inhibitor) to reduce recurrence.

EGI·MCQ094 2019 MCQ6 MCQ 64/M presented with tarry stool for 2 days. He is asymptomatic and past medical history was insignificant. Physical examination was normal except for old melena…

64/M presented with tarry stool for 2 days. He is asymptomatic and past medical history was insignificant. Physical examination was normal except for old melena on PR exam. OGD, colonoscopy were normal except for old melena throughout the colon. The patient is now stable without signs of further bleeding. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Elective balloon enteroscopy
  2. B Elective capsule enteroscopy
  3. C Elective CT abdomen
  4. D Emergency laparotomy with on-table enteroscopy
  5. E Emergency RBC scan
💡 查看答案
B

64/M, old melena, asymptomatic, normal OGD + colonoscopy, old melena throughout colon → elective capsule endoscopy (small bowel source, stable patient, non-urgent).

EGI·MCQ095 2019 MCQ27, 2022 MCQ31 MCQ M/35 after drinking with friends shortly vomited and presented with severe chest pain. He came to the hospital 3 hours later and he said he also vomited some bl…

M/35 after drinking with friends shortly vomited and presented with severe chest pain. He came to the hospital 3 hours later and he said he also vomited some blood. There was breathlessness as well. He is febrile and has a pulse rate of 120 bpm. PE showed reduced air entry on left lung. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Mallory Weiss Syndrome
  2. B Boerhaave syndrome
  3. C Aspiration pneumonitis
  4. D Pneumonia
💡 查看答案
B

35/M, vomiting after drinking → chest pain + haematemesis + SOB + fever + tachycardia + left pleural effusion = Boerhaave syndrome (spontaneous oesophageal perforation).

EGI·MCQ096 2019 MCQ56 MCQ 30/M history of heartburn and acid regurgitation. Endoscopy found grade C esophagitis. Treated with 8 weeks of PPI and endoscopy revealed healing esophagitis. H…

30/M history of heartburn and acid regurgitation. Endoscopy found grade C esophagitis. Treated with 8 weeks of PPI and endoscopy revealed healing esophagitis. He has no more heartburn but complains of continuous acid regurgitation. Surgery considered. What is the appropriate management?

  1. A Add H2 antagonist
  2. B Balloon dilatation of lower esophageal sphincter
  3. C Botox to lower esophageal sphincter
  4. D Change the class of PPI
  5. E High resolution manometry and 24 hour pH monitoring
💡 查看答案
E

Post-PPI healing but persistent regurgitation before considering surgery → high-resolution manometry + 24h pH monitoring (to confirm diagnosis, assess LES function, and rule out motility disorder before fundoplication).

EGI·MCQ097 2019 MCQ?? MCQ 30/F, epigastric discomfort, multiple polyps at fundus and body of stomach of 3mm on OGD, biopsy taken, what other investigation will you do?

30/F, epigastric discomfort, multiple polyps at fundus and body of stomach of 3mm on OGD, biopsy taken, what other investigation will you do?

  1. A Colonoscopy
  2. B Capsule endoscopy
  3. C Balloon enteroscopy
💡 查看答案
A

30/F, multiple small gastric polyps at fundus/body → need colonoscopy (to exclude FAP — fundic gland polyps can be associated with FAP).

EGI·MCQ098 2020 MCQ1 MCQ Middle-age woman with mild epigastric discomfort went for a Barium swallow and meal for investigation. There is an incidental finding of a 1.5cm outpouch in mid…

Middle-age woman with mild epigastric discomfort went for a Barium swallow and meal for investigation. There is an incidental finding of a 1.5cm outpouch in mid-oesophagus. Tertiary contractions are seen along the esophagus. She is otherwise asymptomatic. What is the best treatment option?

  1. A Calcium antagonist
  2. B Diverticulectomy
  3. C Diverticulectomy + long esophageal myotomy
  4. D Observation
  5. E Balloon dilatation
💡 查看答案
D

1.5cm incidental mid-oesophageal diverticulum + tertiary contractions, asymptomatic → observation (small asymptomatic diverticulum with mild dysmotility doesn't need intervention).

EGI·MCQ099 2020 MCQ9 MCQ Which of the following is the most appropriate indication for endoscopic submucosal dissection of gastric lesion?

Which of the following is the most appropriate indication for endoscopic submucosal dissection of gastric lesion?

  1. A 0.5cm mucosal cancer at incisura
  2. B 1cm pedunculated polyp at antrum
  3. C 2cm submucosal lesion at body
  4. D 3cm gastric cancer at antrum
  5. E 5cm polypoid lesion at body
💡 查看答案
A

0.5cm mucosal cancer at incisura = early gastric CA confined to mucosa → most appropriate for ESD (endoscopic submucosal dissection). Criteria: mucosal, well-differentiated, ≤2cm (or larger without ulceration).

EGI·MCQ100 2020 MCQ32 MCQ Middle aged man, sudden SOB after vomiting some blood. Crepitation over neck and anterior chest. Left side chest auscultation dull. He claimed he has lost 10kg …

Middle aged man, sudden SOB after vomiting some blood. Crepitation over neck and anterior chest. Left side chest auscultation dull. He claimed he has lost 10kg in the last few months. Diagnosis?

  1. A CA esophagus
  2. B Boerhaave syndrome
  3. C Mallory Weiss syndrome
  4. D Reflux esophagitis
💡 查看答案
B

Sudden SOB after vomiting blood + neck/chest crepitation + left pleural dullness + weight loss = Boerhaave syndrome. Weight loss may be coincidental or from underlying oesophageal pathology.

EGI·MCQ101 2020 MCQ48, 2021 MCQ?? MCQ An 82-year-old lady was presented with vomiting. She was hemodynamically stable and generally well. On chest X-ray, there was fluid level behind the heart, and …

An 82-year-old lady was presented with vomiting. She was hemodynamically stable and generally well. On chest X-ray, there was fluid level behind the heart, and gas shadow up to tracheal bifurcation. On OGD, distorted anatomy was noted and the endoscopist could not locate the pylorus.

  1. A Hiatal hernia type 1
  2. B Hiatal hernia type 2
  3. C Achalasia
  4. D Bochdalek hernia
  5. E Oesophageal cancer
💡 查看答案
B

82/F, vomiting + fluid level behind heart on CXR + gas to carina + distorted anatomy on OGD = type 2 (paraesophageal) hiatal hernia (organoaxial volvulus of stomach herniating through hiatus).

EGI·MCQ102 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following is a marker for gastric leiomyoma?

Which of the following is a marker for gastric leiomyoma?

  1. A CD117
  2. B CD7
  3. C Desmin
  4. D DOG1
  5. E S-100
💡 查看答案
C

Desmin is a marker for smooth muscle tumours (leiomyoma/leiomyosarcoma). CD117 and DOG1 are GIST markers. Gastric leiomyoma stains positive for desmin/SMA.

EGI·MCQ103 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Most likely have least biliary reflux after distal gastrectomy?

Most likely have least biliary reflux after distal gastrectomy?

  1. A Smaller gastric remnant
  2. B Larger gastric remnant
  3. C Billroth I
  4. D Billroth II
  5. E Roux-en-Y
💡 查看答案
E

Roux-en-Y reconstruction has least biliary reflux after distal gastrectomy (bile diverted away from gastric remnant through long Roux limb).

EGI·MCQ104 2020 MCQ?? MCQ M/50, BMI 36, tried diet restriction and exercising but failed. He was referred to you for surgical management. What would you do?

M/50, BMI 36, tried diet restriction and exercising but failed. He was referred to you for surgical management. What would you do?

  1. A Pharmacological treatment
  2. B Duodenal switch
  3. C Jejunal-ileal bypass
  4. D Laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy
  5. E Gastric balloon
💡 查看答案
D

50/M, BMI 36, failed diet/exercise → laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy (first-line bariatric surgery for BMI 35-40 with comorbidities or >40).

EGI·MCQ105 2021 MCQ?? MCQ 70/50mmHg, pulse 130, endoscopy finds stomach with fresh blood and clotted blood, bleeding source undetermined location but esophagus is normal, what is the imm…

70/50mmHg, pulse 130, endoscopy finds stomach with fresh blood and clotted blood, bleeding source undetermined location but esophagus is normal, what is the immediate action?

  1. A IV trasamine
  2. B IV dopamine
  3. C IV PPI
  4. D Arrange urgent operation
💡 查看答案
D

Actively bleeding patient (BP 70/50, P 130), fresh blood in stomach, source unidentified, oesophagus normal → urgent operation (endoscopy failed to identify source + hemodynamically unstable).

EGI·MCQ106 2021 MCQ?? MCQ 45 year old lady with end stage Her2 positive CA stomach, cannon ball lesions on CXR, multiple Bone metastasis. What is the best treatment for her?

45 year old lady with end stage Her2 positive CA stomach, cannon ball lesions on CXR, multiple Bone metastasis. What is the best treatment for her?

  1. A Chemotherapy + trastuzumab
  2. B Chemotherapy + nivolumab
  3. C Gastrojejunostomy
  4. D Palliative distal gastrectomy
💡 查看答案
A

End-stage HER2+ gastric CA + lung + bone mets = palliative chemotherapy + trastuzumab (anti-HER2 targeted therapy).

EGI·MCQ107 2022 MCQ7 MCQ Day 6 post op 3 stage esophagectomy, neck wound red and indurated. CXR clear. SpO2 93% on 50% O2.

Day 6 post op 3 stage esophagectomy, neck wound red and indurated. CXR clear. SpO2 93% on 50% O2.

  1. A UTI
  2. B Wound infection
  3. C Aspiration pneumonia
  4. D Anastomotic leakage
💡 查看答案
D

Day 6 post-3-stage oesophagectomy, neck wound red/indurated, SpO₂ 93% on 50% O₂ → anastomotic leakage (neck anastomosis leak presents with wound erythema/induration + sepsis).

EGI·MCQ108 2022 MCQ15 MCQ 35 year old man with tarry stool stopped now. OGD and colonoscopy normal. Investiagtion?

35 year old man with tarry stool stopped now. OGD and colonoscopy normal. Investiagtion?

  1. A CT
  2. B Meckel’s scan
  3. C Capsule
  4. D Mesenteric angiography
💡 查看答案
C

35/M, tarry stool stopped, normal OGD + colonoscopy → capsule endoscopy (small bowel evaluation for obscure GI bleeding in stable patient).

EGI·MCQ109 2022 MCQ36 MCQ OGD: young woman, there is one with three 8mm? gastric polyps at body of stomach, taking acid-lowering agent for GERD relief; Rapid urease -ve.

OGD: young woman, there is one with three 8mm? gastric polyps at body of stomach, taking acid-lowering agent for GERD relief; Rapid urease -ve.

  1. A Fundic gland polyp
  2. B Hyperplastic polyp
  3. C Adenomatous polyp
  4. D Hamartomatous polyp
💡 查看答案
A

Young woman, 8mm gastric polyps on PPI for GERD, H. pylori negative = fundic gland polyps (associated with chronic PPI use).

EGI·MCQ110 2022 MCQ57 MCQ You are a GP, suspected GERD. MOST USEFUL next step?

You are a GP, suspected GERD. MOST USEFUL next step?

  1. A Manometry and 24h pH monitoring
  2. B Barium swallow
  3. C Trial of proton pump inhibitor
💡 查看答案
C

Suspected GERD in GP setting → trial of PPI is the most useful initial step (empiric therapy serves as both diagnostic and therapeutic approach).

EGI·EMQ001 2002# EMQ1 EMQ INVESTIGATION OF GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Theme: INVESTIGATION OF GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Upper endoscopy
  2. B Colonoscopy
  3. C CAT scan abdomen
  4. D MRI scan abdomen
  5. E Mesenteric angiogram
  6. F Barium enema
  7. G Barium meal and follow through
  8. H Small bowel enema
  9. I Meckel’s scan
  10. J RBC scan

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST helpful option from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 19 years old gentleman presented with melaena three days ago. Upper endoscopy was normal. He is passing normal stool now.
  2. Q2. A 78 years old gentleman presented with fresh per rectal bleeding. Both upper endoscopy and proctoscopy were normal. He remained stable haemodynamically. He continued to pass trace amount of fresh blood per rectally.
  3. Q3. A 60 years old lady presented with fresh melaena. Both upper endoscopy and colonoscopy were normal. She continued to pass fresh melaena. She developed sinus tachycardia of 100/min.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Meckel's scan — 19/M, melena resolved, normal OGD → Meckel's diverticulum scan (young male with obscure GI bleeding).
Q2. Colonoscopy — 78/M, trace PR bleeding, normal OGD + proctoscopy, stable → colonoscopy (full colonic evaluation for source).
Q3. Mesenteric angiogram — 60/F, ongoing fresh melena, normal OGD + colonoscopy, tachycardic → mesenteric angiography (active bleeding requiring localization for embolization).
EGI·EMQ002 2002# EMQ7 EMQ EPIGASTRIC PAIN

Theme: EPIGASTRIC PAIN

Options
  1. A Acute cholangitis
  2. B Acute cholecystitis
  3. C Acute myocardial infarction
  4. D Acute pancreatitis
  5. E Duodenal ulcer with pyloric stenosis
  6. F Gastroenteritis
  7. G Leaking aortic aneurysm
  8. H Gastric ulcer
  9. I Perforated peptic ulcer
  10. J Small bowel obstruction

For each of the following patients with epigastric pain, select the MOST likely diagnosis.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 64 years old lady presented with epigastric pain for 1 day associated with fever, chills and tea-colour urine. She had a temperature of 38.5°C and right upper quadrant tenderness. Initial laboratory studies showed leucocytosis and a raised serum bilirubin level.
  2. Q2. A 39 years old housewife with history of appendicectomy presented with colicky epigastric pain for 2 days associated with repeated vomiting. Physical examination showed that she was moderately dehydrated with a distended and tympanic abdomen. The bowel sound was diminished. An abdominal X-ray showed dilated bowel loops with multiple fluid levels.
EGI·EMQ003 2002# EMQ9 EMQ ENDOSCOPIC DIAGNOSIS

Theme: ENDOSCOPIC DIAGNOSIS

Options
  1. A Achalasia
  2. B Cancer of the sigmoid colon
  3. C Esophageal perforation
  4. D Esophagitis
  5. E Gastric stromal tumour
  6. F Hiatus hernia
  7. G Peptic ulcer disease
  8. H Sigmoid diverticular disease
  9. I Ulcerative colitis
  10. J Watermelon stomach

For each of the following patients with an indication for endoscopic examination, select the MOST likely diagnosis.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 20 years old gentleman presented with haematemesis and melaena. His pulse rate was 120/min, his blood pressure was 90/50 mmHg. He appeared breathless. SaO2 was 99% on 4L/minute of oxygen delivered via a nasal cannula.
  2. Q2. A 35 years old lady complained of 2-year history of on and off dysphagia but was getting worse for the recent 3 months. She had lost a body weight of 10 lbs gradually over the past year. Her dysphagia was mixed and sometimes she had difficulty with solids but she also experienced difficulty with liquid. She also complained of nocturnal regurgitation of frothy fluid when she retired at night.
EGI·EMQ004 2005 EMQ3 EMQ DYSPHAGIA

Theme: DYSPHAGIA

Options
  1. A Botulinum toxin injection
  2. B Pneumatic balloon dilatation
  3. C Calcium channel antagonist
  4. D Diagnostic laparoscopy
  5. E Laparoscopic esophageal myotomy with Dor hemi-fundoplication
  6. F Esophagectomy
  7. G Endoscopic ultrasound
  8. H Stenting with metallic self-expanding metallic stent across the gastroesophageal junction

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely / appropriate option from the list above. Each option maybe used once, more than once or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 35 years old man with a diagnosis of achalasia. Balloon dilatation was performed two years ago, now he has recurrence and worsening of symptoms of dysphagia. His past health is good except an open appendicectomy 10 years ago.
  2. Q2. An 85 years old lady with the diagnosis of achalasia. She has ischaemic heart disease and chronic obstructive airway disease. She has significant regurgitation symptoms and also needs water to “flush her food down”. Barium swallow shows a dilated esophagus with a diameter of 3 cm. She is judged to have extremely high risk of any surgical operation.
  3. Q3. A 60 years old man with a 2 months’ history of dysphagia. He had a barium swallow contrast study performed, which shows a slightly dilated esophagus with tapering at the gastroesophageal junction with hold-up of contrast. The radiologist suggests a possible diagnosis of achalasia. Endoscopy was performed which revealed some retained food stuff in the esophagus. The endoscope could not be passed into the stomach because of resistance. Observing from the top of the area of resistance, no mucosal lesion is obvious. What would be the next appropriate step in your management?
EGI·EMQ005 2005 EMQ5 EMQ TREATMENT FOR GASTRIC TUMOUR

Theme: TREATMENT FOR GASTRIC TUMOUR

Options
  1. A Endoscopic mucosal resection
  2. B Endoscopic polypectomy
  3. C Endoscopic stenting
  4. D Laparoscopic wedge resection
  5. E Laparoscopic distal radical gastrectomy
  6. F Distal radical gastrectomy
  7. G Total radical gastrectomy
  8. H Gastrojejunostomy
  9. I Systemic chemotherapy
  10. J Regional intra-arterial chemotherapy

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST relevant treatment option from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 60 years old man presented with epigastric discomfort for 4 months. His past health was good. Physical examination was unremarkable. Upper endoscopy showed a 5 cm polypoid nodule on the greater curve of the gastric body. The overlying mucosa was normal. The lesion was located in the muscularis layer on endoscopic ultrasound.
  2. Q2. An 85 years old man presented with repeated vomiting for 2 weeks. He developed acute myocardial infarction and was admitted to the Coronary Care Unit. Inotropic support was given for cardiogenic shock. After stabilisation, upper endoscopy and biopsy confirmed the presence of gastric adenocarcinoma of antrum. Abdominal ultrasonography revealed multiple liver metastases. A nasogastric tube was inserted with an output of about 1.5 litres per day since admission.
  3. Q3. A 65 years old man presented with epigastric pain for 3 months. His past health was good. Physical examination was unremarkable. Upper endoscopy and biopsy confirmed the presence of gastric adenocarcinoma of the antrum. Subsequent investigations did not reveal any evidence of systemic metastasis. Endoscopic ultrasound showed a T3N1 tumour.
EGI·EMQ006 2005 EMQ7 EMQ ALIMENTARY INVESTIGATIONS

Theme: ALIMENTARY INVESTIGATIONS

Options
  1. A CT scan
  2. B Barium enema
  3. C Colonoscopy
  4. D Mesenteric angiography
  5. E Serum CEA level
  6. F Barium meal
  7. G Trucut biopsy
  8. H 24-hour pH monitoring
  9. I Upper GI endoscopy
  10. J Trans-oesophageal ultrasound

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST appropriate investigation.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 60 years old man with a previous history of colonic adenomas notices blood on his stool on several occasions.
  2. Q2. A 70 years old man presents with weight loss. On examination the liver is enlarged and irregular.
  3. Q3. A 30 years old man presents with haematemesis and melaena. He is shocked with a systolic blood pressure of 80 mmHg.
EGI·EMQ007 2006 EMQ2 EMQ A. Distal gastrectomy

Theme: A. Distal gastrectomy

Options
  1. B Endoscopic stenting
  2. C Laparoscopic wedge resection
  3. D Laparoscopic radical gastrectomy
  4. E Total radical gastrectomy
  5. F Endoscopic mucosal resection
  6. G Systemic chemotherapy
  7. H Gastrojejunostomy
  8. I Intraarterial chemotherapy
Questions
  1. Q1. A 82 years old man with vomiting for 2 weeks. Good past health. Recent weight loss. Upper endoscopy and biopsy confirmed gastric adenocarcinoma of antrum. Multiple liver metastasis. NG tube inserted with daily output of 1.5 L.
  2. Q2. An old man with good past health has T3N0 tumour.
  3. Q3. A young man presents with melaena. Endoscopy revealed a polyp from the muscularis layer, and there is an ulcer on top.
EGI·EMQ008 2007 EMQ7 EMQ A. Barrett’s esophagus

Theme: A. Barrett’s esophagus

Options
  1. B SCC of esophagus
  2. C Caustic stricture
  3. D Food bolus obstruction
  4. E Diffuse esophageal spasm
  5. F GERD
  6. G Adenocarcinoma of cardia
  7. H Achalasia
Questions
  1. Q1. A 60 years old gentleman with progressive dysphagia for 4 weeks. He can only tolerate soft diet. He has lost 5 kg over last 4 weeks. He is a chronic smoker. P/E reveals 1 cm right supraclavicular fossa lymph node.
  2. Q2. A 40 years old lady presents with dysphagia, which is worse with solid than fluid. Barium swallow shows rat tail sign.
EGI·EMQ009 2009 EMQ3 EMQ OESOPHAGEAL CANCER

Theme: OESOPHAGEAL CANCER

Options
  1. A Aorto-oesophageal fistula
  2. B Chest pain
  3. C Direct infiltration of trachea from oesophageal carcinoma
  4. D Haemoptysis
  5. E Left recurrent laryngeal nerve palsy
  6. F Lung metastasis
  7. G Liver metastasis
  8. H Tracheo-oesophageal fistula

Select the most appropriate underlying cause for the patient’s presenting symptoms.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 75 years old gentleman has 2-month history of progressive dysphagia. He could barely tolerate solid food without choking and found fluids more intolerable due to choking. He was admitted to hospital due to fever. Bilateral infiltrates were seen on chest X-ray.
  2. Q2. A 65 years old lady was diagnosed with carcinoma of oesophagus 24 cm from incisor. She complained of haemoptysis for the past 1 week. There was no hoarseness.
  3. Q3. A 70 years old gentleman with recently diagnosed carcinoma of the oesophagus presented with retrosternal chest pain and hoarseness. The tumor was located 5 cm from the oesophagogastric junction.
EGI·EMQ010 2010 EMQ6 EMQ UPPER GASTROINTESTINAL BLEED

Theme: UPPER GASTROINTESTINAL BLEED

Options
  1. A Angiodysplasia
  2. B Aorto-enteric fistula
  3. C Boerhaave syndrome
  4. D Dieulafoy syndrome
  5. E Carcinoma of stomach
  6. F Gastro-intestinal stromal tumor
  7. G Gastritis
  8. H Mallory Weiss tear
  9. I Carcinoma of esophagus
  10. J Esophageal varices
Questions
EGI·EMQ011 2013 EMQ8, 2018 EMQ8, 2019 EMQ6, 2020 EMQ2 EMQ POST-OESOPHAGEAL SURGERY COMPLICATIONS

Theme: POST-OESOPHAGEAL SURGERY COMPLICATIONS

Options
  1. A Anastomotic leakage
  2. B Aspiration pneumonia
  3. C Chylothorax
  4. D Congestive heart failure
  5. E Gastric outlet obstruction
  6. F Ischaemic gastric conduit
  7. G Pulmonary embolism
  8. H Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury
  9. I Tracheal injury
  10. J Wound infection
Questions
  1. Q1. 60/M day 7 post-op, 3-stage esophagectomy. Started fluid diet 2 days ago, tolerate well. Now 38.5°C. Mild swelling and tenderness below the wound on the left neck.
  2. Q2. A woman, with history of IHD, underwent transhiatal esophagectomy. Continuous output 1 L/day in the drain on the right side, first blood now straw colour. Now, on post-op day 7, she developed SOB. CXR right chest is clear but there is blunting of costophrenic angle on the left side.
  3. Q3. Day 3 post-op, develop fever, fast AF 140 bpm. Chest PE normal. CXR clear. What is the most important complication to rule out?
  4. Q4. 65/M post-esophagectomy, developed shortness of breath in the recovery room, PE found surgical emphysema over chest wall and neck.
  5. Q5. Woman, post-op day 5, mild fever otherwise well, chest drain daily drained 1.5L clear fluid since surgery.
  6. Q6. Day 5 or 6 post op, mild fever, erythema of the wound.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Anastomotic leak — Day 7 post-3-stage oesophagectomy, fever + neck wound swelling/tenderness after starting diet = cervical anastomotic leak.
Q2. Chylothorax — Post-transhiatal oesophagectomy, continuous straw-coloured drainage 1L/day from right drain = chylothorax (thoracic duct injury → chylous effusion).
Q3. Anastomotic leak — Day 3 post-op, fever + fast AF = must rule out anastomotic leak first (AF is an early sign of leak/sepsis post-oesophagectomy).
Q4. Tracheobronchial injury — Post-oesophagectomy + bilateral surgical emphysema immediately post-op = tracheobronchial injury (air leak from airway).
Q5. Chylothorax — Day 5, mild fever, 1.5L clear daily drainage since surgery = chylothorax.
Q6. Wound infection — Day 5-6, mild fever + wound erythema = wound infection.
EGI·EMQ012 2015 EMQ3 EMQ MANAGEMENT OF GASTRIC LESIONS

Theme: MANAGEMENT OF GASTRIC LESIONS

Options
  1. A Distal radical gastrectomy
  2. B Endoscopic gastric balloon
  3. C Laparoscopic distal radical gastrectomy
  4. D Laparoscopic partial gastrectomy
  5. E Laparoscopic total radical gastrectomy
  6. F Laparoscopic wedge resection
  7. G Partial gastrectomy
  8. H Proximal gastrectomy
  9. I Laparoscopic sleeve gastrectomy
  10. J Total radical gastrectomy
Questions
  1. Q1. 37/F. 3 cm sessile polypoid antrum. EUS showed lesion in submucosa. No metastasis.
  2. Q2. 64/M. Tarry stool for 2 days, asymptomatic, good past health, old melaena, ulcerated 4 cm polypoid submucosal lesion at greater curvature of body. EUS shows lesion in muscular layer.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old woman with BMI 40 kg/m2 with HT, DM, obstructive sleep apnoea. She has tried multiple methods for weight loss but failed.

Theme: HAEMETAMESIS

Options
  1. A Acute gastric ulcer
  2. B Acute gastritis
  3. C Angiodysplasia of stomach
  4. D Gastric adenocarcinoma
  5. E Diverticulum
  6. F Gastric volvulus
  7. G Mallory-Weiss tear
  8. H Bleeding esophageal varices
Questions
  1. Q1. A 71 years old lady, with good past health, sprained her ankle with uneven road. Bought ibuprofen from pharmacy 4 days ago. Attended emergency department with vomiting of a small amount of blood.
  2. Q2. A 31 years old gentleman vomited 500 mL of blood. Physical exam shows multiple spider naevi. Look icteric.
  3. Q3. A 25 years old gentleman drank 8 pints of beers in his friend’s party. Much of his dismay, he vomited up his meal and continue to retch. During his 4th emesis, fresh blood found in the vomitus.
EGI·EMQ014 2016 EMQ9 EMQ MANAGEMENT OF GASTRIC LESIONS

Theme: MANAGEMENT OF GASTRIC LESIONS

Options
  1. A Laparoscopic wedge resection
  2. B Laparoscopic distal radical gastrectomy
  3. C Open distal radical gastrectomy
  4. D Total radical gastrectomy
  5. E Endoscopic stenting
  6. F Gastrojejunostomy
Questions
  1. Q1. An elderly woman, with good past health, presents with a 8 cm mass in proximal stomach. Biopsy showed adenocarcinoma. No other sites of metastasis found. T3N0.
  2. Q2. A 57 years old gentleman presented with tarry stool for 2 days. He enjoyed good health in the past. Abdominal examination was unremarkable. Digital rectal examination showed old melaena. Upper endoscopy shows a 4 cm polypoid submucosal nodule. There was a 1cm mucosal defect over the nodule. Endoscopic USG reveals that the mass is at muscularis level.
  3. Q3. A 78 years old gentleman presents with repeated vomiting for 2 days. He has lost 10 kg of body weight over the same period. He was diagnosed to have acute myocardial infarction just 3 weeks ago. Upper endoscopy and biopsy confirmed presence of an obstructive gastric adenocarcinoma of antrum. Chest x-ray reveals multiple lung metastases. A nasogastric tube is inserted with an output of about 1.2 L per day.

  6. F Endoscopic stenting

Choose the most appropriate management for the following scenarios.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 72 years old female with severe bronchiectasis has a 6-mm well-differentiated nodular carcinoma T1N0M0 without ulceration at the incisura with no evidence of malignant disease elsewhere on PET-CT scanning.
  2. Q2. 56/M, marathon runner. Hb 12. Locally advanced CA antrum, with no weight loss. PET found metabolically active nodes along lesser curvature.
  3. Q3. Fragile 80/M, subtotal gastrectomy done for antral CA 2 years ago. Then received palliative chemotherapy for recurrence 3 months ago in gastric remnant. Liver metastasis was found. The bleeding gastric tumour required weekly blood transfusion.

  7. G PET scan

  2. Q2. 70/F with tarry stool for 2 days, presented to A&E. Enjoyed good past health. Upper endoscopy and colonoscopy were both normal. Currently stable, passed brownish stool the next day.

Questions

Options
  1. A Aorto-oesophageal fistula

Questions
  1. Q1. Man with progressive dysphagia. Can barely tolerate solids but no choking. Always choking with fluids. Admitted for SOB and fever. CXR shows bilateral patchy infiltrates. No hoarseness.
  2. Q2. Man with history of CA oesophagus. Incessant cough last week. Present with haemoptysis. No dysphagia or choking. No problem with drinking water at all. Private OGD tumour at 24 cm from incisor.
  3. Q3. Woman after oesophagectomy with hoarseness and retrosternal discomfort histology shows adenocarcinoma, site just above EGJ.
SECTION 08

General and Ambulatory Surgery

27 MCQ · 2 EMQ
GS·MCQ001 2002# MCQ77 MCQ An 80 years old lady presented with acute onset of left groin mass for one day. Physical examination showed a 3cm mass below and lateral to the pubic tubercle. …

An 80 years old lady presented with acute onset of left groin mass for one day. Physical examination showed a 3cm mass below and lateral to the pubic tubercle. The mass was neither reducible nor pulsatile. Cough impulse was present. Which of the following is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Inguinal hernia
  2. B Femoral hernia
  3. C Inguinal lymphadenopathy
  4. D Femoral artery pseudoaneurysm
  5. E Saphenous varix
💡 查看答案
B

80/F, acute groin mass below + lateral to pubic tubercle, irreducible, cough impulse present = femoral hernia. Femoral hernia emerges below and lateral to pubic tubercle. Inguinal is above and medial.

GS·MCQ002 2003 MCQ32 MCQ Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding inguinal canal?

Which of the following statements is NOT true regarding inguinal canal?

  1. A It transmits the ilioinguinal nerve
  2. B It is covered anteriorly by external oblique
  3. C It is covered anteriorly by the conjoint tendon
  4. D It ends at the external ring
  5. E It lies anterior to the external iliac vessels
💡 查看答案
C

Conjoint tendon forms the POSTERIOR wall (medial part), NOT the anterior wall. Anterior wall = external oblique aponeurosis.

GS·MCQ003 2004 MCQ41 MCQ A 70 years old lady presents with vomiting. On examination there is a tense and tender groin lump present below and lateral to the pubic tubercle. It is not red…

A 70 years old lady presents with vomiting. On examination there is a tense and tender groin lump present below and lateral to the pubic tubercle. It is not reducible and there is no cough impulse. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Strangulated indirect inguinal hernia
  2. B Strangulated direct inguinal hernia
  3. C Saphena varix
  4. D Psoas abscess
  5. E Strangulated femoral hernia
💡 查看答案
E

70/F, tense tender groin lump below + lateral to pubic tubercle, irreducible, no cough impulse = strangulated femoral hernia.

GS·MCQ004 2004 MCQ48 MCQ Which of the following statements about hernia is FALSE?

Which of the following statements about hernia is FALSE?

  1. A Inguinal hernia is more common than femoral hernia in lady
  2. B A sliding hernia is a type of inguinal hernia in which the wall of viscus forms a portion of the wall of the hernia sac
  3. C Laparoscopic inguinal hernia repair is most suitable for patients with recurrent or bilateral hernias
  4. D The treatment of choice of a large incisional hernia is primary fascial closure
  5. E The incidence of strangulation is higher in femoral hernias than that of inguinal hernias
💡 查看答案
D

Primary fascial closure is NOT the treatment of choice for large incisional hernia — mesh repair is standard (lower recurrence). All other statements are true.

GS·MCQ005 2004 MCQ84 MCQ The MOST likely hernia to strangulate is:

The MOST likely hernia to strangulate is:

  1. A Umbilical
  2. B Indirect
  3. C Direct
  4. D Femoral
  5. E Obturator
💡 查看答案
D

Femoral hernia has the highest strangulation risk (narrow rigid ring — femoral canal boundaries are all ligamentous/bony).

GS·MCQ006 2006 MCQ61 MCQ A patient has a mass bulging just lateral to rectus muscles in the semilunaris. What is the diagnosis?

A patient has a mass bulging just lateral to rectus muscles in the semilunaris. What is the diagnosis?

  1. A Femoral hernia
  2. B Indirect inguinal hernia
  3. C Spigelian hernia
  4. D Lipoma
💡 查看答案
C

Mass bulging lateral to rectus at linea semilunaris = Spigelian hernia.

GS·MCQ007 2006 MCQ62 MCQ A 50 years old gentleman presented with an irreducible mass in the groin for 3 months. What is the most likely diagnosis?

A 50 years old gentleman presented with an irreducible mass in the groin for 3 months. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Inguinal hernia
  2. B Incarcerated hernia
  3. C Hydrocele
  4. D Subcutaneous lipoma
  5. E Saphena varix
💡 查看答案
A

50/M, irreducible groin mass ×3 months = inguinal hernia (most common groin hernia). Irreducible but not obstructed/strangulated if no pain/obstruction symptoms.

GS·MCQ008 2006 MCQ73 MCQ Which of the following is true about hernia?

Which of the following is true about hernia?

  1. A Inguinal direct hernia more common than indirect hernia
  2. B Hiatus hernias usually are of paraesophageal type
  3. C Diaphragmatic hernia can occur after trauma
💡 查看答案
C

Diaphragmatic hernia CAN occur after trauma (traumatic diaphragmatic rupture). Direct hernia is LESS common than indirect (A wrong). Sliding hiatus hernia is more common than paraesophageal (B wrong).

GS·MCQ009 2007 MCQ36 MCQ The femoral canal:

The femoral canal:

  1. A Lies lateral to the femoral vein
  2. B Has the inguinal ligament as its medial border
  3. C Has the lacunar ligament as it lateral border
  4. D Has the pectineal ligament as its posterior border
  5. E Contains the spermatic cord
💡 查看答案
D

Femoral canal has pectineal ligament (Cooper's ligament) as its posterior border. Lies MEDIAL to femoral vein (A wrong). Lacunar ligament is MEDIAL border (B/C wrong). Contains fat and lymph node of Cloquet (E wrong).

GS·MCQ010 2010 MCQ9, 2021 MCQ21 MCQ Regarding anatomy of inguinal canal, which one is correct?

Regarding anatomy of inguinal canal, which one is correct?

  1. A Conjoint tendon forms the lateral part of posterior wall
  2. B External oblique aponeurosis forms the posterior boundary
  3. C The external ring lies midway between symphysis pubis and anterior superior iliac spine
  4. D Inguinal ligament forms the inferior boundary
  5. E Internal ring lies medial to inferior epigastric vessels
💡 查看答案
D

Inguinal ligament forms the inferior boundary of the inguinal canal. Conjoint tendon is MEDIAL posterior wall (A wrong). External oblique is anterior (B wrong). External ring is medial (C wrong). Internal ring is LATERAL to inferior epigastric (E wrong).

GS·MCQ011 2010 MCQ31, 2014 MCQ37 MCQ 60/M presented with bilateral indirect inguinal hernia which is not any associated with discomfort or pain. Which of the following is the gold standard for ingu…

60/M presented with bilateral indirect inguinal hernia which is not any associated with discomfort or pain. Which of the following is the gold standard for inguinal hernia repair?

  1. A Laparoscopic mesh repair
  2. B Open mesh repair with local anaesthesia
  3. C Open mesh repair with spinal anaesthesia
  4. D Observation
  5. E Wear a truss
💡 查看答案
B

Open mesh repair (Lichtenstein) with local anaesthesia is the gold standard for inguinal hernia repair — evidence-based, low recurrence, can be done as day case.

GS·MCQ012 2011 MCQ10 MCQ Which statement concerning the anatomy of the inguinal canal is incorrect?

Which statement concerning the anatomy of the inguinal canal is incorrect?

  1. A Conjoint tendon is in the lateral posterior wall
  2. B External oblique aponeurosis is the anterior boundary
  3. C Inguinal ligament in the inferior boundary
  4. D Internal ring lies halfway between pubic tubercle and ASIS
  5. E Internal ring is lateral to inferior epigastric vessels
💡 查看答案
A

Conjoint tendon is in the MEDIAL posterior wall (not lateral). All other statements about inguinal canal anatomy are correct.

GS·MCQ013 2011 MCQ84, 2012 MCQ8, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ 60/M, groin swelling, bigger on standing and smaller when lying down. Varicose veins on both legs. Positive cough impulse. What is the cause for the groin swell…

60/M, groin swelling, bigger on standing and smaller when lying down. Varicose veins on both legs. Positive cough impulse. What is the cause for the groin swelling?

  1. A Femoral artery aneurysm
  2. B Femoral hernia
  3. C Hydrocele of spermatic cord
  4. D Incarcerated femoral hernia
  5. E Saphena varix
💡 查看答案
E

Groin swelling bigger standing/smaller lying + varicose veins + cough impulse = saphena varix (dilated saphenous vein at saphenofemoral junction — transmits cough impulse, associated with varicose veins).

GS·MCQ014 2012 MCQ9 MCQ Which of the following hernias, if left untreated, would have the highest probability in causing intestinal obstruction?

Which of the following hernias, if left untreated, would have the highest probability in causing intestinal obstruction?

  1. A Direct inguinal hernia
  2. B Incisional hernia
  3. C Indirect inguinal hernia
  4. D Sliding hernia
  5. E Spigelian hernia
💡 查看答案
E

Spigelian hernia has high probability of causing intestinal obstruction if untreated (narrow neck, interparietal location, difficult to detect clinically).

GS·MCQ015 2012 MCQ50 MCQ A 40 years old obese lady presents at the clinic with a one-month history of non-tender mass in the periumbilical region. She denies any vomiting, abdominal pai…

A 40 years old obese lady presents at the clinic with a one-month history of non-tender mass in the periumbilical region. She denies any vomiting, abdominal pain, abdominal distension or absolute constipation and is otherwise well. On examination a plum-sized lump is found at the umbilicus with cough impulse and is reducible. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A AXR to rule out obstruction
  2. B CT abdomen
  3. C To organize elective surgical repair
  4. D To organize emergency surgical repair
  5. E Ultrasound
💡 查看答案
C

40/F obese, painless periumbilical mass ×1 month, reducible, no obstruction symptoms = paraumbilical/umbilical hernia → organize elective surgical repair.

GS·MCQ016 2017 MCQ??, 2018 MCQ16 MCQ Man presented with first episode of unilateral indirect inguinal hernia. Complains of mild discomfort upon prolonged walking. Which of the following is GOLD STA…

Man presented with first episode of unilateral indirect inguinal hernia. Complains of mild discomfort upon prolonged walking. Which of the following is GOLD STANDARD for management?

  1. A Laparoscopic mesh repair with GA
  2. B Observe
  3. C Open mesh repair with LA
  4. D Open mesh repair with SA
  5. E Wear a truss
💡 查看答案
C

First unilateral indirect inguinal hernia with mild discomfort → open mesh repair (Lichtenstein) with local anaesthesia is the gold standard.

GS·MCQ017 2019 MCQ53 MCQ 60/M, first occurrence of bilateral inguinal hernia, complaint of discomfort after prolonged walking. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

60/M, first occurrence of bilateral inguinal hernia, complaint of discomfort after prolonged walking. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

  1. A Watchful waiting
  2. B Laparoscopic repair
  3. C Shouldice repair
  4. D Bassini repair
  5. E Lichtenstein repair
💡 查看答案
B

First bilateral inguinal hernia with discomfort → laparoscopic repair (TEP/TAPP) is most appropriate for bilateral hernias (single anaesthetic, both sides repaired).

GS·MCQ018 2019 MCQ76 MCQ Which of the following is NOT an advantage of laparoscopic totally extraperitoneal repair (TEP) surgery for inguinal hernia?

Which of the following is NOT an advantage of laparoscopic totally extraperitoneal repair (TEP) surgery for inguinal hernia?

  1. A Can detect ipsilateral femoral hernia
  2. B Can inspect for the contralateral side for hernia
  3. C Less pain
  4. D Shorter hospital stay
  5. E Less post-operative wound complications
💡 查看答案
A

TEP can detect ipsilateral femoral hernia — this IS an advantage (A is TRUE, so NOT the answer). Wait — the question asks what is NOT an advantage. Let me reconsider. TEP CAN inspect contralateral side (B true), has less pain (C true), shorter stay (D true), fewer wound complications (E true). A is also true. Hmm — actually the question format asks 'NOT an advantage'. All listed ARE advantages. But if forced: A — detecting ipsilateral femoral hernia is more about completeness than advantage over open repair. Actually A IS an advantage. Let me go with D — shorter hospital stay is debatable for TEP vs open with LA.

GS·MCQ019 2019 MCQ?? MCQ 90 years old fragile lady with good past health, presented with IO signs, no abdominal and groin mass, which of the following is most likely the diagnosis?

90 years old fragile lady with good past health, presented with IO signs, no abdominal and groin mass, which of the following is most likely the diagnosis?

  1. A Indirect inguinal hernia
  2. B Direct inguinal hernia
  3. C Obturator hernia
  4. D Hiatal hernia
  5. E Femoral hernia
💡 查看答案
C

90/F fragile, IO signs, no abdominal/groin mass = obturator hernia (classic: elderly thin woman with IO from hernia not palpable externally, Howship-Romberg sign).

GS·MCQ020 2020 MCQ39 MCQ Which is not true for laparoscopic intraperitoneal onlay mesh repair for incisional hernia?

Which is not true for laparoscopic intraperitoneal onlay mesh repair for incisional hernia?

  1. A Better cosmetic outcome
  2. B Faster return to normal activities
  3. C Less bowel injury
  4. D Less seroma
  5. E Less wound infection
💡 查看答案
C

Laparoscopic IPOM for incisional hernia does NOT have less bowel injury risk — bowel injury (adhesiolysis) is actually a concern with laparoscopic approach. Less wound infection (E true), better cosmesis (A true), faster recovery (B true).

GS·MCQ021 2021 MCQ3 MCQ Absolute contraindication for TEP:

Absolute contraindication for TEP:

  1. A Concurrent inguinal and femoral hernia
  2. B Bilateral inguinal hernia
  3. C Patient unfit for general anaesthesia
  4. D >80-year-old
💡 查看答案
C

Patient unfit for general anaesthesia is an absolute contraindication for TEP (requires GA). Age >80 is not absolute. Concurrent inguinal/femoral and bilateral hernias can be done with TEP.

GS·MCQ022 2021 MCQ12 MCQ Small (<1cm) paraumbilical hernia. Best method to repair?

Small (<1cm) paraumbilical hernia. Best method to repair?

  1. A Lap intraperitoneal onlay repair
  2. B Open Mayo repair
  3. C Open mesh repair
  4. D Open simple repair
💡 查看答案
C

Small (<1cm) paraumbilical hernia → open simple suture repair (Mayo repair or primary closure). Too small for mesh. Lap approach excessive for this size.

GS·MCQ023 2021 MCQ54 MCQ Which of the following meshes is recommended for laparoscopic extraperitoneal onlay repair of incisional hernia?

Which of the following meshes is recommended for laparoscopic extraperitoneal onlay repair of incisional hernia?

  1. A Biological mesh
  2. B Composite mesh
  3. C Polyester mesh
  4. D PolyPropylene mesh
💡 查看答案
B

Composite mesh (dual-sided: one side for peritoneal contact, other for tissue ingrowth) is recommended for laparoscopic IPOM repair of incisional hernia — prevents bowel adhesion to mesh.

GS·MCQ024 2021 MCQ67 MCQ Regarding incisional hernia, which one is the most recommended?

Regarding incisional hernia, which one is the most recommended?

  1. A Open inlay mesh
  2. B Open onlay mesh
  3. C Open sublay mesh
  4. D Open simple repair
💡 查看答案
C

Open sublay (retromuscular/Rives-Stoppa) mesh repair is the most recommended technique for incisional hernia repair — lowest recurrence rate.

GS·MCQ025 2022 MCQ68 MCQ Which of the following is an absolute contraindication of TEP?

Which of the following is an absolute contraindication of TEP?

  1. A Complicated hernia
  2. B Unfit for GA
  3. C Unilateral in direct hernia
  4. D Age>80
💡 查看答案
B

Patient unfit for GA is an absolute contraindication for TEP.

GS·MCQ026 2022 MCQ79 MCQ Management of small (<1cm) periumbilical hernia.

Management of small (<1cm) periumbilical hernia.

  1. A Intraperitoneal onlay mesh repair
  2. B Laparoscopic/open mesh repair
  3. C Open simple repair
  4. D Mayo’s repair
💡 查看答案
C

Small (<1cm) periumbilical hernia → open simple repair (suture repair adequate for small defect).

GS·MCQ027 2022 MCQ80 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a benefit of intraperitoneal laparoscopic onlay mesh repair for incisional hernia repair?

Which of the following is NOT a benefit of intraperitoneal laparoscopic onlay mesh repair for incisional hernia repair?

  1. A Less pain
  2. B Less wound infection
  3. C Better cosmesis
  4. D Better visualization of Swiss-cheese fascia defect
💡 查看答案
D

Better visualization of Swiss-cheese (multiple) fascial defects is actually an advantage of laparoscopic IPOM, making it NOT the answer. Actually — D says it IS a benefit, so if the question asks 'NOT a benefit', D might be the answer if it's debatable. But laparoscopy DOES provide good visualization. Let me go with: all listed are benefits. If forced, seroma is actually MORE common with lap IPOM (D should be 'less seroma' — not listed). Going with the best answer: the question likely tests that IPOM doesn't reduce seroma — but that's not an option. Going with D as correct answer since 'better visualization of Swiss-cheese' is indeed a benefit.

GS·EMQ001 2003 EMQ7 EMQ HERNIAS

Theme: HERNIAS

Options
  1. A Indirect inguinal hernia
  2. B Direct inguinal hernia
  3. C Obturator hernia
  4. D Spigelian hernia
  5. E Femoral hernia
  6. F Epigastric hernia
  7. G Para-umbilical hernia
  8. H Umbilical hernia
  9. I Richter’s hernia
  10. J Diaphragmatic hernia

For each of the following anatomical locations below, choose the single MOST likely hernia from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A hernia emerging above and medial to the pubic tubercle and descends into the scrotum.
  2. Q2. A hernia emerging below and lateral to the pubic tubercle; and is difficult to reduce.
  3. Q3. A hernia emerging from the right side of the abdominal wall just lateral to the rectus muscle.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Indirect inguinal hernia — Above and medial to pubic tubercle + descends into scrotum = indirect inguinal hernia.
Q2. Femoral hernia — Below and lateral to pubic tubercle + difficult to reduce = femoral hernia.
Q3. Spigelian hernia — Right abdominal wall lateral to rectus = Spigelian hernia.
GS·EMQ002 2011 EMQ7 EMQ A. Diaphragmatic hernia

Theme: A. Diaphragmatic hernia

Options
  1. B Direct inguinal hernia
  2. C Epigastric hernia
  3. D Femoral hernia
  4. E Hiatus hernia
  5. F Incisional hernia
  6. G Indirect inguinal hernia
  7. H Obturator hernia
  8. I Spigelian hernia
  9. J Umbilical hernia
Questions
  1. Q1. A 90 years old lady with no history of abdominal surgery presented with small bowel obstruction. There was no mass on physical examination. She complained of right hip pain and pain on right hip movement.
  2. Q2. A 85 years old lady presented with a painful groin mass. Emergency exploration revealed a 3 cm hernia below the inguinal ligament with strangulated small bowel.
  3. Q3. A 30 years old gentleman complained of bulge and pain in the midline of the abdomen, located between the umbilicus and xiphoid.
💡 查看答案
Q1. Obturator hernia — 90/F, no abdominal surgery, IO + no mass + right hip pain = obturator hernia (Howship-Romberg sign).
Q2. Strangulated femoral hernia — 85/F, painful groin mass below inguinal ligament + strangulated bowel = strangulated femoral hernia.
Q3. Epigastric hernia — 30/M, midline bulge between umbilicus and xiphoid = epigastric hernia.
SECTION 09

Hepatobiliary & Pancreatic Surgery

75 MCQ · 12 EMQ
HBP·MCQ001 2001 MCQ1 MCQ Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be an indication for endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography?

Which of the following is UNLIKELY to be an indication for endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography?

  1. A Acute pancreatitis
  2. B Acute cholangitis
  3. C Acute cholecystitis
  4. D Biliary fistula after cholecystectomy
  5. E Obstructive jaundice
💡 查看答案
C

Acute cholecystitis is NOT an indication for ERCP (no CBD pathology). ERCP indicated for: acute pancreatitis (with cholangitis/stone), acute cholangitis, biliary fistula, and obstructive jaundice.

HBP·MCQ002 2001 MCQ48, 2004 MCQ5 MCQ A 42 years old gentleman has a history of liver cirrhosis and gross ascites. He presents with generalized abdominal pain and fever of 38.5°C for one day. Physic…

A 42 years old gentleman has a history of liver cirrhosis and gross ascites. He presents with generalized abdominal pain and fever of 38.5°C for one day. Physical examination shows generalized abdominal tenderness with rebound tenderness. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Perforated peptic ulcer
  2. B Acute appendicitis
  3. C Ruptured hepatocellular carcinoma
  4. D Primary bacterial peritonitis
  5. E Gastroenteritis
💡 查看答案
D

Cirrhotic + ascites + generalized abdominal pain + fever + rebound tenderness = spontaneous bacterial peritonitis (SBP/primary bacterial peritonitis).

HBP·MCQ003 2002 MCQ7 MCQ Which of the following statements regarding endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) is TRUE?

Which of the following statements regarding endoscopic retrograde cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) is TRUE?

  1. A Allows direct visualization of the biliary tree by endoscope
  2. B Is useful in the diagnosis of carcinoma of pancreas
  3. C Is extremely helpful in the management of Klatskin tumour
  4. D Should not be performed early in acute pancreatitis
  5. E Should not be done in jaundiced patient with serum bilirubin > 300 umol/L MCQ4 (2002 MCQ12, 2002# MCQ24, 2003 MCQ31, 2004 MCQ66, 2005 MCQ80, 2007 MCQ10, 2009 MCQ72) Which of the following conditions is LEAST likely to give rise to back pain?
  6. A Renal stones
  7. B Pending rupture of abdominal aortic aneurysm
  8. C Pancreatitis
  9. D Cholecystitis
  10. E Posterior wall stomach ulcer
💡 查看答案
B

ERCP is useful in diagnosing pancreatic CA (via biopsy/brushings of pancreatic duct stricture). It doesn't directly visualize biliary tree (A wrong — uses fluoroscopy). It IS useful for Klatskin tumour (C wrong — for stenting/drainage). ERCP IS indicated early in gallstone pancreatitis with cholangitis (D wrong).

HBP·MCQ005 2002 MCQ18, 2022# MCQ40 MCQ High serum amylase level is NOT a feature of the following conditions:

High serum amylase level is NOT a feature of the following conditions:

  1. A Parotid gland infection
  2. B Carcinoma of pancreas
  3. C Teratoma of testis
  4. D Acute pancreatitis
  5. E Perforated peptic ulcer
💡 查看答案
C

Teratoma of testis does NOT cause high serum amylase. Parotitis, pancreatic CA, acute pancreatitis, and perforated PU all elevate amylase.

HBP·MCQ006 2002 MCQ33 MCQ Fever and leucocytosis in a post-hepatectomy patient is MOST likely due to:

Fever and leucocytosis in a post-hepatectomy patient is MOST likely due to:

  1. A Myocardial infarction
  2. B Liver failure
  3. C Bile duct ligation
  4. D Infected subphrenic fluid collection
  5. E Hepatic artery thrombosis
💡 查看答案
D

Post-hepatectomy fever + leucocytosis → most likely infected subphrenic/subhepatic fluid collection (common after liver resection).

HBP·MCQ007 2002 MCQ44 MCQ The following statements are applicable to acute cholecystitis EXCEPT:

The following statements are applicable to acute cholecystitis EXCEPT:

  1. A Perforation of gallbladder may occur
  2. B Emergency cholecystectomy if empyema occurs
  3. C Metronidazole is always necessary
  4. D Conservative treatment may be successful
  5. E Gallstones may be absent in the gallbladder
💡 查看答案
C

Metronidazole is NOT always necessary for acute cholecystitis — first-line is a cephalosporin ± metronidazole. Perforation can occur (A true). Emergency cholecystectomy for empyema (B true). Conservative treatment may work (D true). Acalculous cholecystitis exists (E true).

HBP·MCQ008 2002 MCQ63 MCQ Preoperative investigation and preparation of a patient with obstructive jaundice SHOULD NOT include:

Preoperative investigation and preparation of a patient with obstructive jaundice SHOULD NOT include:

  1. A Measurement of coagulation status
  2. B Measurement of 24-hour urinary output
  3. C Measurement of serum urea and electrolytes
  4. D Fluid restriction during the 24 hours preoperatively
  5. E Administration of antibiotics during invasive diagnostic procedures (e.g. PTC, ERCP)
💡 查看答案
D

Fluid RESTRICTION is NOT appropriate preoperatively for obstructive jaundice — these patients are at risk of hepatorenal syndrome and need ADEQUATE hydration. Coagulation, UO, electrolytes, and prophylactic antibiotics are all appropriate.

HBP·MCQ009 2002 MCQ80, 2002# MCQ63 MCQ Which of the following is ASSOCIATED with gallstones in paediatric patients?

Which of the following is ASSOCIATED with gallstones in paediatric patients?

  1. A Obesity
  2. B Fertility
  3. C Spherocytosis
  4. D Thrombocytopenia
  5. E Thrombocytosis
💡 查看答案
C

Spherocytosis (haemolytic anaemia → bilirubin gallstones) is associated with paediatric gallstones. Obesity and fertility are adult risk factors.

HBP·MCQ010 2002 MCQ89, 2002# MCQ41 MCQ The following are complications of recurrent pyogenic cholangitis EXCEPT:

The following are complications of recurrent pyogenic cholangitis EXCEPT:

  1. A Cholangitic liver abscesses
  2. B Liver cirrhosis
  3. C Cholangiocarcinoma
  4. D Budd-Chiari syndrome
  5. E Broncho-biliary fistula
💡 查看答案
D

Budd-Chiari syndrome (hepatic vein thrombosis) is NOT a complication of RPC. Cholangitic abscess, cirrhosis, cholangiocarcinoma, and broncho-biliary fistula are complications.

HBP·MCQ011 2002# MCQ16 MCQ A 45 years old gentleman had known hepatitis B cirrhosis for 3 years. Otherwise his past health was unremarkable. Ultrasonography of liver revealed a small tumo…

A 45 years old gentleman had known hepatitis B cirrhosis for 3 years. Otherwise his past health was unremarkable. Ultrasonography of liver revealed a small tumour in the right lobe and contrast CT scan showed a 3cm solitary hepatocellular carcinoma without evidence of venous invasion. There was no evidence of extrahepatic metastasis. Which of the following is the BEST treatment option for the hepatocellular carcinoma in this patient?

  1. A Hepatic resection
  2. B Liver transplantation
  3. C Percutaneous ethanol injection
  4. D Transarterial oily chemoembolization
  5. E Systemic chemotherapy
💡 查看答案
A

3cm solitary HCC + known HBV cirrhosis + no portal vein involvement → hepatic resection (if adequate liver function reserve). Best curative option for this scenario.

HBP·MCQ012 2002# MCQ17 MCQ A 35 years old lady develops right upper quadrant abdominal pain and fever for 2 days. There is right upper abdominal tenderness and Murphy’s sign is positive. …

A 35 years old lady develops right upper quadrant abdominal pain and fever for 2 days. There is right upper abdominal tenderness and Murphy’s sign is positive.
