💡 查看答案
A

Right elbow OA + ulnar nerve palsy pattern → nerve conduction study (NCS) to confirm ulnar neuropathy at elbow and assess severity/conduction velocity.

OT·MCQ097 2022 MCQ27 MCQ F/48 with DM, presented with bilateral LL numbness and ankle clonus. UL and cranial nerves normal. Most likely diagnosis:

F/48 with DM, presented with bilateral LL numbness and ankle clonus. UL and cranial nerves normal. Most likely diagnosis:

  1. A Brainstem glioma
  2. B Lumbar disc herniation
  3. C Mononeuritis multiplex
  4. D Thoracic spine meningioma
💡 查看答案
D

48/F DM, bilateral LL numbness + ankle clonus + normal UL/cranial nerves → thoracic spine meningioma (UMN signs in LL only = thoracic cord compression). Brainstem glioma would affect cranial nerves. Lumbar disc = LMN. Mononeuritis = peripheral.

OT·MCQ098 2022 MCQ28 MCQ 30 year old arm wrestler sustained a spiral fracture of distal humeral shaft. What is the most common associated nerve injury?

30 year old arm wrestler sustained a spiral fracture of distal humeral shaft. What is the most common associated nerve injury?

  1. A Axonotmesis of median nerve
  2. B Contusion of musculocutaneous nerve
  3. C Laceration of ulna nerve
  4. D Neuropraxia of radial nerve
💡 查看答案
D

Arm wrestling → spiral humeral shaft fracture → most common associated nerve injury is neuropraxia of radial nerve (spiral groove).

OT·MCQ099 2022 MCQ37 MCQ A 10-year-old boy who is overweight, complained of sudden onset right hip pain and inability to bear weight. Examination showed decreased right hip range of mot…

A 10-year-old boy who is overweight, complained of sudden onset right hip pain and inability to bear weight. Examination showed decreased right hip range of motion and obligatory external rotation on passive flexion of the hip. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE investigation?

  1. A Complete blood count and inflammatory markers
  2. B Magnetic resonance imaging of both hips
  3. C Right hip aspiration
  4. D Xray anteroposterior and frog position of both hips
💡 查看答案
B

Incomplete question — likely about SCFE (slipped capital femoral epiphysis) in overweight adolescent boy.

OT·MCQ100 2022 MCQ38 MCQ 35 year old man with a thigh mass gradually increasing in size over 7cm. Physical examination found a mass in the posterior lower thigh. Best investigation?

35 year old man with a thigh mass gradually increasing in size over 7cm. Physical examination found a mass in the posterior lower thigh. Best investigation?

  1. A Contrast MRI of mass
  2. B USG-guided biopsy of mass
  3. C Open biopsy of mass
  4. D Excisional biopsy
OT·MCQ101 2022 MCQ49 MCQ A girl with pes planus, which of the following is considered non-physiological when she asks to tiptoe?

A girl with pes planus, which of the following is considered non-physiological when she asks to tiptoe?

  1. A Hindfoot at valgus
  2. B Hindfoot at varus
  3. C Tightness at Achilles
  4. D Tenderness at medial arch
OT·MCQ102 2022 MCQ58 MCQ 10/M presents with left knee pain. PE on left knee normal. X-Ray of the left knee shows no abnormality. Next step:

10/M presents with left knee pain. PE on left knee normal. X-Ray of the left knee shows no abnormality. Next step:

  1. A Discharge home and FU
  2. B Prescribe analgesics
  3. C Prescribe physiotherapy for ?? of left knee
  4. D X-ray of left hip
OT·MCQ103 2022 MCQ69 MCQ Spinal cord injury, presents with HT, tachycardia, LL rigidity/hypertonic.

Spinal cord injury, presents with HT, tachycardia, LL rigidity/hypertonic.

  1. A Autonomic dysreflexia
  2. B Malignant hyperthermia
  3. C Spinal shock
OT·MCQ104 2022 MCQ74 MCQ Young man good past health, with twisting injury after basketball, presented with hemarthrosis, which is most likely?

Young man good past health, with twisting injury after basketball, presented with hemarthrosis, which is most likely?

  1. A ACL
  2. B Hemophilia
  3. C Patella dislocation
OT·MCQ105 2022 MCQ76 MCQ Man with right intertrochanteric fracture done with internal fixation. No complications. What is the appropriate advice for immediately after discharge?

Man with right intertrochanteric fracture done with internal fixation. No complications. What is the appropriate advice for immediately after discharge?

  1. A Cannot flex hip more than 90 degrees or else will have hip dislocation
  2. B Walk up stairs with left foot first
  3. C Prescribe non-weight-bearing exercise for 6 weeks
  4. D When sitting up put the left foot forward compared to right
OT·MCQ106 2022 MCQ78 MCQ 18 year old boy, first episode of anterior shoulder dislocation, what is the most likely chance of recurrence?

18 year old boy, first episode of anterior shoulder dislocation, what is the most likely chance of recurrence?

  1. A 10%
  2. B 30%
  3. C 60%
  4. D 90%
OT·EMQ001 2002 EMQ10, 2002# EMQ10, 2003 EMQ10, 2005 EMQ8 EMQ HIP PROBLEMS

Theme: HIP PROBLEMS

Options
  1. A Osteoid osteoma
  2. B Slipped upper femoral epiphysis
  3. C Perthes disease
  4. D Developmental dysplasia of the hip
  5. E Septic arthritis
  6. F Osteosarcoma
  7. G Acute haematogenous osteomyelitis
  8. H Transient synovitis
  9. I Juvenile chronic arthritis
  10. J Displaced femoral neck fracture

Select the MOST likely diagnosis for each of the following patients.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 16 years old gentleman sustained a trivial injury to his right hip during a basketball game 3 weeks ago. Right hip pain began about a week after the injury and became worse. The pain was present at rest, but there is no fever. Radiographs showed periosteal new bone formation around the proximal femur.
  2. Q2. A mother brought her 2 weeks old baby to you because the baby was irritable and failed to thrive. On examination, the baby was not moving the right lower limb. You could see some purulent discharge over the umbilical stump. The body temperature was slightly elevated and the white cell count was normal.
  3. Q3. A 5 years old boy suffered from left hip pain and walked with a limp for 6 months. In the radiograph, the acetabulum was normal, but there was change in body density at the femoral head, and the lateral part of the femoral head was flattened.
  4. Q4. An 18 years old man complained of localized pain over the proximal thigh for 6 months. The pain was more severe in the night time, and was relieved by salicylates. Radiographs showed a small radiolucent area that was surrounded by a sclerotic rim.
  5. Q5. The mother brought her 2 years 3 months old son to you because the boy could not walk as well as other children of the same age. She also told you that there was difficulty in putting on the napkins all along.
OT·EMQ002 2002# EMQ11, 2004 EMQ2, 2007 EMQ1, 2009 EMQ7, 2019 EMQ9, 2020 EMQ10 EMQ PHYSICAL SIGNS

Theme: PHYSICAL SIGNS

Options
  1. A Thomas test
  2. B McMurray test
  3. C Pivot shift test
  4. D Finkelstein test
  5. E Phalen test
  6. F Froment sign
  7. G Tinel sign
  8. H Trendelenburg sign
  9. I Hoffman sign
  10. J Apprehension sign

Select the MOST appropriate sign / test for each of the following conditions.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 17 years old young gentleman suffered traumatic dislocation of his right shoulder 2 years ago. Since then, the shoulder dislocated for 4 other times.
  2. Q2. The mother complained of right wrist pain after giving birth to her baby 3 months ago.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old man complains of right hip pain. He was a heavy drinker for 15 years. He now walks with an obvious limp, with the upper trunk lurches towards the right side every time the affected limb is bearing weight.
  4. Q4. A 55 years old gentleman had an injury to his left elbow 20 years ago. He complained of subjective weakness of his left hand in the past two years. On physical examination, there is marked cubitus valgus, and wasting of the small muscles in the left hand.
  5. Q5. A 50 years old lady complained of pins and needle sensation in the fingers of the right hand. On further enquiry, the little finger is not affected. This appeared to be relieved by shaking her hand.
  6. Q6. A 20 years old male college student twisted his knee during a soccer game. The knee was swollen and he cannot achieve a full active extension of the knee.
  7. Q7. A 75 years old lady complains of numbness and clumsiness of her fingers. She also experiences difficulty in walking.
OT·EMQ003 2011 EMQ6 EMQ A. Colles

Theme: A. Colles

Options
  1. B Galeazzi
  2. C Monteggia
  3. D Chance
  4. E Pott
  5. F Salter-Harris
  6. G Hill-Sachs
  7. H Smith
  8. I Barton
Questions
  1. Q1. A girl fell from a bicycle. Elbow x-ray shows ulnar fracture, and that head of radius is not pointing towards the capitulum.
  2. Q2. An old lady fell with outstretched hand. X-ray shows dorsal displacement of distal fragment.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old man bumped into a rail when driving. Seatbelt mark over the front chest. He complains of severe back pain.
OT·EMQ004 2012 EMQ1, 2016 EMQ7 EMQ PAINFUL HIP

Theme: PAINFUL HIP

Options
  1. A Ankylosing spondylitis
  2. B Avascular necrosis
  3. C Developmental dysplasia of the hip
  4. D Fracture of the neck of femur
  5. E Osteoarthritis
  6. F Perthes’ disease
  7. G Reactive synovitis
  8. H Rheumatoid arthritis
  9. I Septic arthritis
  10. J Slipped capital femoral epiphysis
Questions
  1. Q1. 35/M complaining of progressive left hip pain and stiffness. Also has 5 years of lower back pain and stiffness. Hip x-ray found joint space narrowing and osteophyte formation. His father also had similar complaints and had surgery for the hip and spine in the past. Squaring of the vertebrae and ?fibrosis of the ligaments.
  2. Q2. 78/F had a slip-and-fell injury 3 years ago, found to have fracture right neck of femur and treated by closed reduction and screw fixation. She complained of progressive right hip pain in this past 1 year. X-ray showed flattening of femoral head.
  3. Q3. A 3 years old girl has diffuse hip pain. Walk with a limping posture. Body temperature: 37.8 degree. Mother said that she had upper respiratory symptoms a few days ago. Cannot proceed to PE since she resisted it. X-ray hip is normal.
OT·EMQ005 2013 EMQ4, 2018 EMQ9, 2019 EMQ10 EMQ KNEE PAIN AFTER SPORTS

Theme: KNEE PAIN AFTER SPORTS

Options
  1. A Anterior cruciate ligament rupture
  2. B Discoid meniscus
  3. C Fracture of distal femur
  4. D Haemophilia
  5. E Iliotibial band syndrome
  6. F Lateral meniscal tear
  7. G Osteoarthritis of the knee
  8. H Osteosarcoma
  9. I Patellar dislocation
  10. J Rheumatoid arthritis
Questions
  1. Q1. A 28 years old marathon runner presents with right knee painful snapping after increasing his mileage recently.
  2. Q2. A 14 years old girl presents with painful clunking of her right knee after attending a basketball game in school. On physical examination, similar clunking is also noted in her left knee, but it is not painful.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old gentleman has injured his left knee during a soccer match. He cannot continue the game and notices painful swelling in the knee. Subsequent arthrocentesis at the Emergency Department yielded 50 mL of frank blood.
OT·EMQ006 2013 EMQ5 EMQ SPINE INVESTIGATIONS

Theme: SPINE INVESTIGATIONS

Options
  1. A Bone scan
  2. B CT spine
  3. C DEXA scan
  4. D MRI spine
  5. E Myelogram
  6. F PET-CT
  7. G Ultrasound
  8. H White cell scan
  9. I X-ray spine
Questions
  1. Q1. 80/F. Fell and severe back pain around thoracolumbar region. X-ray: mild wedge collapse of T12.
  2. Q2. 50/M smoker. Sudden onset of bilateral LL weakness, lax anal tone. Spine x-ray: loss of pedicle shadow of L1. CXR: coin lesion over LLZ.
OT·EMQ007 2014 EMQ8 EMQ LOW BACK PAIN

Theme: LOW BACK PAIN

Options
  1. A Trauma
  2. B Prolapsed intervertebral disc
  3. C Osteoporotic collapse of vertebrae
  4. D Non-specific low back strain
  5. E Spinal stenosis
  6. F Spinal spondylolisthesis
  7. G Metastasis to the spine
Questions
  1. Q1. An 75 years old woman with sedentary lifestyle. Pain in the thoracolumbar region after a minor fall.
  2. Q2. An elderly woman with low back pain. She would experience pain going down to the foot after walking for 200 m. Got better after bending down and sitting.
  3. Q3. A 30 years old man. Sprained back after very heavy lifting. Developed severe low back pain radiating to the first 2 toes. Mild numbness over the same area but without motor deficit.
OT·EMQ008 2015 EMQ5, 2017 EMQ9 EMQ HEAD TILTING

Theme: HEAD TILTING

Options
  1. A Atlantoaxial rotatory subluxation
  2. B Brachial plexus injury
  3. C Cerebral palsy
  4. D Cervical spine deformity
  5. E Muscular (fibrotic) torticollis
  6. F Postural torticollis
  7. G Sternomastoid tumour
  8. H Strabismus
  9. I Unilateral hearing loss
  10. J Upper thoracic scoliosis
Questions
  1. Q1. A 4 months old child with persistent head tilting to the left since birth. On physical examination there was limitation of neck rotation to the left. A small palpable mass over the left sternocleidomastoid muscle.
  2. Q2. A 6 months old child with persistent head tilting to the left since birth. Physical exam shows limitation of neck rotation to the left. But the sternocleidomastoid muscle was not tight at the ends of range of motion.
  3. Q3. A 2 years old girl with head tilting to the left side for most of the time. Full range of neck motion. Ophthalmological exam reveals no abnormalities in eye.
OT·EMQ009 2015 EMQ6 EMQ HIP FRACTURES

Theme: HIP FRACTURES

Options
  1. A Bed rest
  2. B Close reduction and internal fixation
  3. C Excision arthroplasty
  4. D External fixator apparatus
  5. E Hemiarthroplasty
  6. F Open reduction and internal fixation
  7. G Protected weight bearing walking
  8. H Skin traction
  9. I Skeletal traction
  10. J Total hip arthroplasty
Questions
  1. Q1. A 40 years old man falls from height from 3m and suffers a displaced fracture of the left neck of femur. Closed reduction was attempted but was not successful. There were no associated injuries.
  2. Q2. A 68 years old lady with known steroid-induced avascular necrosis of the right femoral head (Ficat type IV ) sustained a simple fall. X-ray showed a displaced femoral neck fracture.
  3. Q3. A 72 years old man fell on level ground. He has history of COPD with satisfactory control. X-ray examination showed a minimally displaced femoral intertrochanteric fracture.
OT·EMQ010 2016 EMQ8, 2018 EMQ10 EMQ INITIAL MANAGEMENT

Theme: INITIAL MANAGEMENT

Options
  1. A Curettage
  2. B Debridement
  3. C Emergency surgical fasciotomy
  4. D High dose steroid
  5. E Incision and drainage
  6. F Magnetic resonance imaging
  7. G Reduction and stable fixation
  8. H Short leg back slab
  9. I X-ray
Questions
  1. Q1. 25/M road traffic accident. Crush injury of foot. Complained of progressive left foot pain and swelling. Physical examination could not find anything compatible with the excruciating pain.
  2. Q2. 35/M construction site worker. Fall from 10 metres height. Radiograph showed closed fracture left neck of talus with severe displacement.
  3. Q3. 55/M diabetic. Experienced fluctuating swelling over the right foot. Diagnostic aspiration showed pus.
OT·EMQ011 2017 EMQ10 EMQ TREATMENT OF MUSCULOSKELETAL NEOPLASM

Theme: TREATMENT OF MUSCULOSKELETAL NEOPLASM

Options
  1. A Bisphosphonate infusion
  2. B Chemotherapy
  3. C Marginal excision
  4. D Observation
  5. E Radiotherapy
  6. F Surgical stabilization with implant
  7. G Tru-cut biopsy
  8. H Wide resection

For each of the clinical situations below, choose the single MOST APPROPRIATE treatment from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. 14-year-old boy complains of a painless bony lump over the distal femur for 4 years. There is no symptom associated with the lump and it has not increased significantly in size. There is no other similar lump elsewhere. X-ray shows a well-defined bony out-growth over the metaphyseal area typical of exostosis. The size is about 2 cm.
  2. Q2. A 65-year-old lady with a known history of carcinoma of the breast treated in the recent 2 years presents with severe left thigh pain, so much so that she is now chair-bound. X-ray shows a lytic lesion over the shaft of the left femur with significant destruction of the cortex. Bone scan shows multiple hot spots over the spine, ribs and left shaft of femur. The general condition of the patient is still satisfactory.
  3. Q3. A 55-year-old gentleman complains of a medial thigh mass for 6 months. It has doubled its sized over the past 6 months. Clinical examination shows a 10-cm subcutaneous mass fixed to the skin. Magnetic resonance imaging confirms the lesion. No metastatic lesion is detected. Biopsy shows malignant liposarcoma.
OT·EMQ012 2020 EMQ9 EMQ HIP PAIN

Theme: HIP PAIN

Options
  1. A Developmental dysplasia of the hip
  2. B Osteomyelitis of femur
  3. C Perthes disease
  4. D Septic arthritis of the hip
  5. E Transient synovitis
Questions
  1. Q1. Young kid, limping and refusal to move left hip for one day, came in with high fever and shock. Ultrasound shows large effusion in hip.
  2. Q2. First-born female child born in breech presentation, routine exam by paediatrician found instability of left hip.
  3. Q3. An 8-year-old obese girl develops limping gait and pain in right hip for 3 months, initial X-ray was normal, however repeated X-ray later shows right femoral shortening and collapse of epiphyses.
OT·EMQ013 2021 EMQ10 EMQ A. Osteosarcoma

Theme: A. Osteosarcoma

Options
  1. B Ewing’s sarcoma
  2. C Chondrosarcoma
  3. D Osteoid osteoma
  4. E Aneurysmal bone cyst
  5. F Simple bone cyst
  6. G Chronic osteomyelitis
  7. H Non-ossifying fibroma
  8. I Giant cell tumor
  9. J Fibrous dysplasia
Questions
  1. Q1. 15 year old Caucasian boy with thigh pain and fever. Onion skin appearance on imaging. Histology showed permeative small blue malignant cells.
  2. Q2. 10 year old boy pick up school bag and feel pain. X ray shows fracture at proximal humerus with centric lytic lesion and cortical expansion with a cortical fragment in the caudal end of the lesion.
  3. Q3. 14 year old girl with 2 months thigh pain suddenly present with 1 day difficulty in walking. X ray with periosteal reaction with sunburst sign. Histology shows permeative neoplastic cells.
OT·EMQ014 2021 EMQ?? EMQ A. Iliotibial band syndrome

Theme: A. Iliotibial band syndrome

Options
  1. B ACL rupture
  2. C PCL rupture
  3. D MCL injury
  4. E Bucket handle tear of medial meniscus
  5. F Patella dislocation
  6. G Osteochondritis dessicans
  7. H Quadricep tendon rupture
  8. I Knee dislocation Mary is a 30 year old basketball player who had immediate knee pain after stopping the component from scoring.
Questions
  1. Q1. Delayed knee swelling and pain, can still manage to walk with a limping gait. Presents a week or two after. Cannot extend knee fully.
  2. Q2. Immediately swollen, one month later can walk but feel instability.
  3. Q3. Immediate knee swelling and pain, had to immediately stop playing basketball. Quickly relieved by brace provided by the coach. Resolved after several weeks. Now presents a week or two after with problems with squatting and walking downhill. Fear of squatting.
OT·EMQ015 2022 EMQ5 EMQ REHABILITATION (WALKING AIDS)

Theme: REHABILITATION (WALKING AIDS)

Options
  1. A No walking aid
  2. B Cane stick of left hand
  3. C Cane stick of right hand
  4. D Left forearm crutch
  5. E Right forearm crutch
  6. F Bilateral forearm crutches
  7. G Bilateral cane sticks
  8. H Rolling walker
  9. I Standard walker
Questions
  1. Q1. Old lady spine problem (a part of vertebral collapse). Pain improved with analgesics. bilateral lower limb weakness (4⁄5) + weak hand grip. Poor gait stability, requires supervision. domestic helper, lift access to house.
  2. Q2. Fat middle aged lady with right OA knee, (…), antalgic gait. Gait stable and needs to walk 2 flights of stairs to home.
  3. Q3. Young man, right ankle fracture post fixation. Recommended non weight bearing walking exercise for 6 weeks.
OT·EMQ016 2022 EMQ8 EMQ A. Ankylosing spondylitis

Theme: A. Ankylosing spondylitis

Options
  1. B ACL disruption
  2. C Calcium pyrophosphate crystal deposition
  3. D Charcot arthropathy
  4. E OA exacerbation
  5. F Gonorrhoeal arthritis
  6. G Gouty arthritis
  7. H Psoriatic arthritis
  8. I Pyogenic arthritis
  9. J RA
Questions
  1. Q1. 80/F, 1 day history of left knee painful swelling with gross effusion, X ray showed chondrocalcinosis, joint aspiration slightly turbid, increased cell count and polymorphs, weak positive rhomboid shaped crystal in birefringence under microscope.
  2. Q2. 50/M, history of bilateral knee pain, left knee pain for 3 days, moderate effusion, X ray showed decreased joint space, osteophytes, aspiration 50ml straw colour, increased cell and polymorphs, no crystal, negative culture.
  3. Q3. 60/F with DM, right foot deformity, no history of trauma, rocker bottom deformity, reduced sensation, plantar ulcer.
SECTION 13

Pediatric Surgery

63 MCQ · 14 EMQ
PS·MCQ001 2001 MCQ2 MCQ Regarding cleft lip deformity, it

Regarding cleft lip deformity, it

  1. A Occurs in approximately 1 in 3,000 live births
  2. B Affects the male more frequently
  3. C Happens more frequently on the right side
  4. D Is associated with severe airway problem
  5. E Is usually inherited as an autosomal recessive condition
💡 查看答案
B

Cleft lip affects males more frequently (2:1 male:female ratio). Incidence ~1:700. More common on LEFT side (not right). Not associated with severe airway problems. Multifactorial inheritance (not simple AR).

PS·MCQ002 2002 MCQ6 MCQ For cleft palate deformity, which of the following statements is TRUE?

For cleft palate deformity, which of the following statements is TRUE?

  1. A Is associated with middle ear effusion
  2. B Always results in malnutrition if unrepaired
  3. C Is often associated with upper airway obstruction
  4. D Should be repaired at one month of age
  5. E Occurs more commonly in male if it is isolated cleft palate
💡 查看答案
A

Cleft palate is associated with middle ear effusion (Eustachian tube dysfunction → OME/glue ear). Repair at ~9-12 months not 1 month. Isolated CP more common in females.

PS·MCQ003 2002 MCQ8 MCQ An 8 months old boy with a body weight of 9 kg was sent to the emergency department because of paroxysms of irritability, crying, and vomiting for one day, with…

An 8 months old boy with a body weight of 9 kg was sent to the emergency department because of paroxysms of irritability, crying, and vomiting for one day, with passage of bloody mucus per rectum. Appropriate management includes the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Supply of intravenous fluid to correct possible fluid and electrolyte imbalance
  2. B Continuous sedation to calm down the baby
  3. C Careful physical examination, which may reveal an elongated mass in the right upper quadrant or epigastrium
  4. D Air or barium enema to confirm the diagnosis and for treatment, provided that no peritoneal signs are present
  5. E Laparotomy, if peritoneal signs develop
💡 查看答案
B

8-month-old with paroxysmal irritability + bloody mucus PR = intussusception. Continuous sedation is CONTRAINDICATED (masks deterioration). IVF (A), physical exam for sausage mass (C), air enema (D), and laparotomy if peritonitis (E) are all appropriate.

PS·MCQ004 2002 MCQ20, 2002# MCQ60 MCQ Common factors found in most infants with necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC) include the following EXCEPT:

Common factors found in most infants with necrotizing enterocolitis (NEC) include the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Intestinal ischaemia
  2. B Bacterial colonization of the gut
  3. C Delivery at term
  4. D Enteral feedings of synthetic formula
  5. E Low birth body weight
💡 查看答案
C

NEC is associated with PREMATURITY, not term delivery. Intestinal ischaemia, bacterial colonization, formula feeding, and low birth weight are all risk factors.

PS·MCQ005 2002 MCQ42, 2002# MCQ61 MCQ The MOST commonly accepted management option for a newborn baby with high imperforate anus without other anomalies is:

The MOST commonly accepted management option for a newborn baby with high imperforate anus without other anomalies is:

  1. A Emergent posterior sagittal anorectoplasty
  2. B Immediate total parenteral nutrition
  3. C Ileostomy
  4. D Gastrostomy for feeding
  5. E Colostomy
💡 查看答案
E

High imperforate anus → colostomy (first stage), then definitive repair (posterior sagittal anorectoplasty) at 3-6 months.

PS·MCQ006 2002 MCQ53, 2002# MCQ62 MCQ What is the optimum age for operation on a child with inguinal hernia?

What is the optimum age for operation on a child with inguinal hernia?

  1. A Six months old
  2. B 1 year old
  3. C 2 years old
  4. D As early as possible once diagnosis is made
  5. E Before going to school
💡 查看答案
D

Paediatric inguinal hernia should be repaired as early as possible once diagnosed (high risk of incarceration/strangulation in infants).

PS·MCQ007 2002 MCQ66 MCQ During the resuscitation process for a baby with diaphragmatic hernia, which step is CONTRAINDICATED?

During the resuscitation process for a baby with diaphragmatic hernia, which step is CONTRAINDICATED?

  1. A Nasopharyngeal aspiration
  2. B Insertion of a nasogastric tube
  3. C Giving oxygen by “bagging” with an oxygen mask
  4. D Setting up an intravenous line
  5. E Oximeter for oxygen monitoring
💡 查看答案
C

Bag-mask ventilation is CONTRAINDICATED in diaphragmatic hernia (inflates stomach/bowel in chest → further compresses lung). Intubate + decompress with NG tube.

PS·MCQ008 2002# MCQ64 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE regarding cleft lip and cleft palate deformities?

Which of the following is TRUE regarding cleft lip and cleft palate deformities?

  1. A Never occur in combination
  2. B Are associated with large bowel anomalies
  3. C Need surgical repair immediately after birth
  4. D Are caused by viral infection
  5. E Affect facial growth
💡 查看答案
E

Cleft lip and palate affect facial growth (maxillary growth restriction from surgical scarring and tissue deficiency).

PS·MCQ009 2002# MCQ86 MCQ The MOST clearcut indication for surgery in patients with neonatal necrotizing enterocolitis is:

The MOST clearcut indication for surgery in patients with neonatal necrotizing enterocolitis is:

  1. A Pneumatosis intestinalis
  2. B Portal gas
  3. C Dilated bowel on X-ray
  4. D Free gas on abdominal X-ray
  5. E Absence of rectal gas
💡 查看答案
D

Free gas on AXR = bowel perforation → clear surgical indication in NEC. Pneumatosis (A) and portal gas (B) are concerning but can be managed conservatively initially.

PS·MCQ010 2003 MCQ16 MCQ Regarding Meckel’s diverticulum, which of the following statements is CORRECT?

Regarding Meckel’s diverticulum, which of the following statements is CORRECT?

  1. A It does not cause a volvulus
  2. B It presents as a fistula
  3. C It should be considered in patients presenting with appendicitis
  4. D It occurs 5 feet proximal to the ileocaecal valve
  5. E It does not lead to GI bleeding
💡 查看答案
C

Meckel's diverticulum should be considered in patients presenting with appendicitis (located near appendix in RLQ, can mimic appendicitis). Found 2 feet from ileocaecal valve (not 5, D wrong). CAN cause volvulus, GI bleeding, and does not present as fistula.

PS·MCQ011 2003 MCQ28 MCQ A 2 weeks old boy presents with repeated non-bilious projectile vomiting. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

A 2 weeks old boy presents with repeated non-bilious projectile vomiting. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Duodenal atresia
  2. B Hirschsprung’s disease
  3. C Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis
  4. D Appendicitis
  5. E Intussusception
💡 查看答案
C

2-week-old with non-bilious projectile vomiting = hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (classic presentation at 2-6 weeks of age).

PS·MCQ012 2003 MCQ45 MCQ In a one year-old boy, what is the no. 1 cause of painless, massive gastrointestinal bleeding?

In a one year-old boy, what is the no. 1 cause of painless, massive gastrointestinal bleeding?

  1. A Meckel’s diverticulum
  2. B Liver cirrhosis
  3. C Colonic carcinoma
  4. D Gastric carcinoma
  5. E Hirschsprung’s disease
💡 查看答案
A

Meckel's diverticulum is the #1 cause of painless massive GI bleeding in a 1-year-old (ectopic gastric mucosa → peptic ulceration).

PS·MCQ013 2003 MCQ60, 2010 MCQ56 MCQ A premature 2 days old baby presented with sudden onset of abdominal distension, fever and PR bleeding. AXR showed dilated bowel loops with foamy appearance in …

A premature 2 days old baby presented with sudden onset of abdominal distension, fever and PR bleeding. AXR showed dilated bowel loops with foamy appearance in a segment of small intestine. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Intussusception
  2. B Haemorrhoids
  3. C Anal fissure
  4. D Necrotizing enterocolitis
  5. E Appendicitis
💡 查看答案
D

Premature 2-day-old + abdominal distension + fever + PR bleeding + pneumatosis (foamy appearance) = necrotizing enterocolitis.

PS·MCQ014 2003 MCQ69 MCQ A 2 years old boy presented with a 3-month history of increasing abdominal distension. He was otherwise well. Physical examination revealed a 10 cm hard mass in…

A 2 years old boy presented with a 3-month history of increasing abdominal distension. He was otherwise well. Physical examination revealed a 10 cm hard mass in the right side of the abdomen. Urinary VMA and HVA were raised. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Neuroblastoma
  2. B Adenocarcinoma of the colon
  3. C Hodgkin’s disease
  4. D Wilms’ tumour
  5. E Ewing’s sarcoma
💡 查看答案
D

2-year-old + 10cm hard flank mass + VMA in urine = Wilms' tumour (nephroblastoma). VMA can be elevated in neuroblastoma too, but Wilms is the most common renal tumour of childhood.

PS·MCQ015 2003 MCQ79, 2015 MCQ?? MCQ Regarding myelomeningocele, which of the following is CORRECT?

Regarding myelomeningocele, which of the following is CORRECT?

  1. A Most has autosomal dominant inheritance
  2. B It is associated with vitamin B12 deficiency
  3. C It is associated with renal failure
  4. D Usually affects the thoracic spine
  5. E Mental retardation
💡 查看答案
C

Myelomeningocele is associated with renal failure (neurogenic bladder → VUR → hydronephrosis → CKD). Not autosomal dominant (A wrong). Associated with folic acid deficiency not B12 (B wrong). Usually lumbar/sacral (D wrong).

PS·MCQ016 2003 MCQ83 MCQ A 3 weeks old baby born full term was admitted because of recurrent forceful vomiting after each feed. Physical examination revealed a baby with sunken eyes and…

A 3 weeks old baby born full term was admitted because of recurrent forceful vomiting after each feed. Physical examination revealed a baby with sunken eyes and a small vague mass in the epigastrium. Blood taken showed hyponatraemia, hypokalaemia and metabolic alkalosis. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Gastroenteritis
  2. B Symptomatic pyloric stenosis
  3. C Intussusception
  4. D Jejunal atresia
  5. E Necrotizing enterocolitis
💡 查看答案
B

3-week-old, projectile vomiting after feeds, epigastric mass = pyloric stenosis (same as MCQ11).

PS·MCQ017 2003 MCQ88 MCQ Which of the following is TRUE concerning cleft lip and palate deformities?

Which of the following is TRUE concerning cleft lip and palate deformities?

  1. A The incidence is about 1 in 600 and is true for all races
  2. B Antenatal diagnosis is still not possible because they are small deformities
  3. C Cleft lip deformities are associated with nasal deformities
  4. D Baby with micrognathia is an indication for early cleft palate repair
  5. E Middle ear effusion is common in babies with cleft lip deformities
💡 查看答案
C

Cleft lip deformities ARE associated with nasal deformities (nasal tip/ala deviation, septum deflection). Incidence varies by race (A wrong). Antenatal diagnosis IS possible (B wrong).

PS·MCQ018 2004 MCQ4 MCQ A 5 months old boy with good past health has flu-like symptoms for three days. He becomes irritable and develops abdominal distension, colicky abdominal pain an…

A 5 months old boy with good past health has flu-like symptoms for three days. He becomes irritable and develops abdominal distension, colicky abdominal pain and bile-stained vomiting. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Hirschsprung’s disease
  2. B Pyloric stenosis
  3. C Duodenal atresia
  4. D Intussusception
  5. E Acute appendicitis
💡 查看答案
D

5-month-old with colicky pain + bile-stained vomiting + abdominal distension post-URTI = intussusception (peak age 6-18 months, often post-viral).

PS·MCQ019 2004 MCQ56 MCQ Which of the following conditions is NOT a predisposing factor for necrotizing enterocolitis?

Which of the following conditions is NOT a predisposing factor for necrotizing enterocolitis?

  1. A Prematurity
  2. B Low APGAR score
  3. C Cow’s milk allergy
  4. D Congenital heart disease
  5. E Umbilical artery catheterization
💡 查看答案
C

Cow's milk allergy is NOT a recognized predisposing factor for NEC. Prematurity, low APGAR, CHD, and UAC are predisposing factors.

PS·MCQ020 2004 MCQ69 MCQ What is the electrolyte imbalance associated with pyloric stenosis?

What is the electrolyte imbalance associated with pyloric stenosis?

  1. A Hyperchloraemic alkalosis
  2. B Hypochloraemic alkalosis
  3. C Hyponatraemia
  4. D Hyperkalaemia
  5. E Metabolic acidosis
💡 查看答案
B

Pyloric stenosis → loss of HCl → hypochloraemic hypokalaemic metabolic alkalosis.

PS·MCQ021 2004 MCQ73, 2007 MCQ30, 2011 MCQ73 MCQ Regarding congenital anal atresia, which of the following is NOT an associated anomaly?

Regarding congenital anal atresia, which of the following is NOT an associated anomaly?

  1. A Esophageal atresia
  2. B Renal agenesis
  3. C Malrotation
  4. D Hemi-vertebra
  5. E Coloboma
💡 查看答案
C

Malrotation is NOT part of VACTERL association. VACTERL: Vertebral (hemivertebra), Anal (atresia), Cardiac, TEF/Esophageal atresia, Renal, Limb. Coloboma is part of CHARGE syndrome.

PS·MCQ022 2005 MCQ13 MCQ Which of the following statements is NOT correct in intussusception in children?

Which of the following statements is NOT correct in intussusception in children?

  1. A Usually self limiting and requires no urgent intervention
  2. B Can present as passing blood-stained stool described as “red-currant jelly”
  3. C Can be diagnosed with ultrasonogram
  4. D Close reduction with air or saline enema will be successful in majority of patients
  5. E A sausage shaped mass may be palpable in right upper quadrant of abdomen
💡 查看答案
A

Intussusception is NOT self-limiting — it requires urgent intervention (air/hydrostatic reduction or surgery). All other statements are correct.

PS·MCQ023 2005 MCQ31 MCQ What is the MOST concerned in the management of a cleft palate patient at the age of one to three?

What is the MOST concerned in the management of a cleft palate patient at the age of one to three?

  1. A Speech development
  2. B Feeding
  3. C Dentition
  4. D Facial appearance
  5. E Mental development
💡 查看答案
A

Speech development is the primary concern for cleft palate patients aged 1-3 years (velopharyngeal competence for speech is critical during language acquisition).

PS·MCQ024 2005 MCQ32 MCQ Which of the following statements is NOT correct for neonatal hernia?

Which of the following statements is NOT correct for neonatal hernia?

  1. A Bilateral disease is common
  2. B It is associated with prematurity
  3. C Strangulation risk is higher compared with the old patients
  4. D Bowel content in hernia is ruled out if transillumination test is positive
  5. E Vas deferens injury can be a surgical complication is male
💡 查看答案
D

Transillumination test being positive does NOT rule out bowel — it just suggests fluid (hydrocele). Bowel content still possible. All other statements about neonatal hernia are correct.

PS·MCQ025 2005 MCQ35 MCQ A one year old boy has unilateral descended testis felt in groin, the contralateral testis is normal in position and size. The OPTIMAL treatment is:

A one year old boy has unilateral descended testis felt in groin, the contralateral testis is normal in position and size. The OPTIMAL treatment is:

  1. A Laparoscopy
  2. B CT scan abdomen
  3. C Blood test for alpha fetoprotein
  4. D Early elective open orchidopexy
  5. E Observation
💡 查看答案
D

1-year-old with unilateral undescended testis palpable in inguinal canal → early elective open orchidopexy (fix testis in scrotum — reduces infertility/malignancy risk).

PS·MCQ026 2005 MCQ52 MCQ Cleft lip deformity

Cleft lip deformity

  1. A Occurs in 1:2500 live births
  2. B Best repaired at the age of 9 months
  3. C Causes severe speech problems
  4. D Associated with psycho-social problems
  5. E Usually due to a major genetic defect
💡 查看答案
D

Cleft lip deformity is associated with psychosocial problems (stigma, self-esteem issues). Repaired at 3-6 months not 9 months. Speech problems mainly from cleft PALATE.

PS·MCQ027 2006 MCQ35 MCQ A 3 years old girl present with bilious vomiting and colicky abdominal pain. What is the most likely pathology?

A 3 years old girl present with bilious vomiting and colicky abdominal pain. What is the most likely pathology?

  1. A Intussusception
  2. B Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis
  3. C Gastroenteritis
💡 查看答案
A

3-year-old + bilious vomiting + colicky pain = intussusception (still within common age range).

PS·MCQ028 2006 MCQ40 MCQ Which of the following is the most accurate imaging for maldescended testis?

Which of the following is the most accurate imaging for maldescended testis?

  1. A Laparoscopy
  2. B CT
  3. C Ultrasound
  4. D MRI
💡 查看答案
A

Laparoscopy is the most accurate imaging for impalpable/intra-abdominal maldescended testis (gold standard — diagnostic + therapeutic).

PS·MCQ029 2007 MCQ56 MCQ A 10 months old baby girl complains of reddish mass on her right cheek. It was not present at birth, but it grew rapidly in the past 5 months. What is the most …

A 10 months old baby girl complains of reddish mass on her right cheek. It was not present at birth, but it grew rapidly in the past 5 months. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Port-wine stain
  2. B Low flow arteriovenous malformation
  3. C Lymphangioma
  4. D Strawberry naevus
  5. E Naevus of Ota
💡 查看答案
D

10-month-old + rapidly growing reddish cheek mass not present at birth = strawberry naevus (infantile haemangioma — appears at 2-4 weeks, proliferates rapidly, then involutes).

PS·MCQ030 2007 MCQ79 MCQ In a premature baby with NEC, which of the follow is an absolute indication for surgery?

In a premature baby with NEC, which of the follow is an absolute indication for surgery?

  1. A Porto-venous gas
  2. B Pancytopenia
  3. C Perforation of the intestines
  4. D Metabolic acidosis
  5. E Need for intubation
💡 查看答案
C

Perforation (free air) is the ABSOLUTE indication for surgery in NEC. Portal venous gas (A) and metabolic acidosis (D) are relative indications.

PS·MCQ031 2009 MCQ68, 2016 MCQ39 MCQ Regarding Hirschsprung’s disease, which of the following is NOT a feature?

Regarding Hirschsprung’s disease, which of the following is NOT a feature?

  1. A Vomiting
  2. B Constipation
  3. C Cough
  4. D Failure to thrive
  5. E Fever
💡 查看答案
C

Cough is NOT a feature of Hirschsprung's disease. Vomiting, constipation (failure to pass meconium), failure to thrive, and fever (enterocolitis) are features.

PS·MCQ032 2009 MCQ70, 2016 MCQ40 MCQ A 4 weeks old boy is referred because of persistent jaundice. Abdominal ultrasound showed a contracted gallbladder. EHIDA showed no excretion after 24 hours. Th…

A 4 weeks old boy is referred because of persistent jaundice. Abdominal ultrasound showed a contracted gallbladder. EHIDA showed no excretion after 24 hours. The MOST LIKELY diagnosis is:

  1. A Biliary atresia
  2. B Choledochal cyst
  3. C Gallbladder atresia
  4. D Hepatoma
  5. E Pancreatic carcinoma
💡 查看答案
A

4-week-old + persistent jaundice + contracted GB on USG + no excretion on HIDA = biliary atresia.

PS·MCQ033 2010 MCQ55 MCQ Which of the following is not associated with esophageal atresia?

Which of the following is not associated with esophageal atresia?

  1. A Malrotation
💡 查看答案
A

Malrotation is NOT classically associated with isolated oesophageal atresia (though it can co-occur). VACTERL anomalies are associated.

PS·MCQ034 2010 MCQ58, 2014 MCQ66, 2020 MCQ30 MCQ A newborn baby was suspected to have Hirschsprung disease. Rectal biopsy is performed. The histology that would confirm the diagnosis is:

A newborn baby was suspected to have Hirschsprung disease. Rectal biopsy is performed. The histology that would confirm the diagnosis is:

  1. A Absence of endothelial cells
  2. B Absence of fibroblast
  3. C Absence of ganglionic cells
  4. D Absence of myenteric plexus
  5. E Absence of smooth muscle cells
💡 查看答案
C

Hirschsprung's disease = absence of ganglionic cells (ganglion cells absent in myenteric/Auerbach's and submucosal/Meissner's plexuses) on rectal biopsy.

PS·MCQ035 2011 MCQ74 MCQ A 2 days old baby born full term was referred to you with bile stained vomiting. He had history of polyhydramnios antenatally. AXR showed absence of bowel gas d…

A 2 days old baby born full term was referred to you with bile stained vomiting. He had history of polyhydramnios antenatally. AXR showed absence of bowel gas distal to mid ileum. Blood investigations are normal. What is the diagnosis?

  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Diverticulitis
  3. C Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  4. D Small bowel atresia
💡 查看答案
D

2-day-old + bile-stained vomiting + polyhydramnios + absent gas distal to mid-ileum = small bowel atresia (jejunal/ileal atresia).

PS·MCQ036 2011 MCQ75 MCQ A mother brought her 3 weeks old baby to Department of A&E with a history of repeated vomiting of milk after each feed. Physical exam revealed a vague mass in t…

A mother brought her 3 weeks old baby to Department of A&E with a history of repeated vomiting of milk after each feed. Physical exam revealed a vague mass in the epigastrium. What should be the investigation of choice to confirm clinical diagnosis?

  1. A AXR
  2. B Barium enema
  3. C CT
  4. D MRI
  5. E Ultrasound
💡 查看答案
E

3-week-old with projectile vomiting + epigastric mass → ultrasound (pyloric stenosis — USG is investigation of choice, shows thickened pylorus muscle).

PS·MCQ037 2011 MCQ77 MCQ A 2 days old baby presented with abdominal distension after feeding. On physical examination, the abdomen is distended but non-tender. AXR show dilated large bo…

A 2 days old baby presented with abdominal distension after feeding. On physical examination, the abdomen is distended but non-tender. AXR show dilated large bowel with absence rectal gas. What is the gold standard investigation to confirm your diagnosis?

  1. A Anal manometry
  2. B Barium enema
  3. C CT
  4. D Endorectal ultrasound
  5. E Rectal biopsy
💡 查看答案
E

2-day-old + abdominal distension + dilated large bowel + absent rectal gas → rectal biopsy (Hirschsprung's disease — definitive diagnosis by absence of ganglion cells).

PS·MCQ038 2012 MCQ19 MCQ An 8 days old baby, presented with bilious vomiting. AXR showed no gas shadows distal to the duodenum. What is the most likely diagnosis?

An 8 days old baby, presented with bilious vomiting. AXR showed no gas shadows distal to the duodenum. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Intussusception
  2. B Mid-gut volvulus
  3. C Small bowel atresia
💡 查看答案
B

8-day-old + bilious vomiting + no gas distal to duodenum = midgut volvulus (malrotation). Emergency — bilious vomiting in neonate = volvulus until proven otherwise.

PS·MCQ039 2013 MCQ57, 2017 MCQ76 MCQ A 6 weeks old baby presents with 10-day history of pyloric stenosis. The most likely electrolyte disturbance will be:

A 6 weeks old baby presents with 10-day history of pyloric stenosis. The most likely electrolyte disturbance will be:

  1. A HyperK hypoCl metabolic acidosis
  2. B HypoNa hypoCl metabolic acidosis
  3. C HypoNa hypoCl metabolic alkalosis
  4. D HypoNa hypoCl respiratory acidosis
  5. E HypoNa hypoCl respiratory alkalosis
💡 查看答案
C

Pyloric stenosis electrolyte disturbance = hyponatraemic, hypochloraemic metabolic alkalosis (loss of HCl + Na + K in vomiting + renal compensation).

PS·MCQ040 2013 MCQ59 MCQ A 2 days old baby boy presents with sudden onset of bile-stained vomiting and collapse. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

A 2 days old baby boy presents with sudden onset of bile-stained vomiting and collapse. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Duodenal atresia
  2. B Hirschsprung’s disease
  3. C Malrotation volvulus
  4. D Pyloric stenosis
  5. E Urinary tract infection
💡 查看答案
C

2-day-old + sudden bile-stained vomiting + collapse = malrotation with midgut volvulus (neonatal surgical emergency).

PS·MCQ041 2013 MCQ61, 2018 MCQ78 MCQ Infant with NEC. Which is not needed?

Infant with NEC. Which is not needed?

  1. A Rectal washout
  2. B NPO
  3. C NG tube
  4. D TPN
  5. E IV antibiotics
💡 查看答案
A

Rectal washout is NOT part of NEC management. NPO (B), NG decompression (C), TPN (D), and IV antibiotics (E) are all essential.

PS·MCQ042 2013 MCQ65, 2015 MCQ88, 2019 MCQ77 MCQ You are a surgical resident at the outpatient clinic and are referred a 3 months old boy with hypospadias. Which feature below is not regularly seen with this d…

You are a surgical resident at the outpatient clinic and are referred a 3 months old boy with hypospadias. Which feature below is not regularly seen with this disease on physical examination?

  1. A Axial rotation of the penis
  2. B Chordee
  3. C Dorsal hood
  4. D Hypoplastic scrotum
  5. E Ventral urethral opening
💡 查看答案
D

Hypoplastic scrotum is NOT a regular feature of hypospadias. Axial rotation, chordee (ventral curvature), dorsal hood (incomplete prepuce), and ventral urethral opening are characteristic.

PS·MCQ043 2014 MCQ63 MCQ A 3 years old boy presented with per rectal bleeding for 2 weeks. He was pallor on examination. Blood tests showed mild iron deficiency anaemia. What is the mos…

A 3 years old boy presented with per rectal bleeding for 2 weeks. He was pallor on examination. Blood tests showed mild iron deficiency anaemia. What is the most likely cause of the problem?

  1. A Adenocarcinoma of rectum
  2. B Intussusception
  3. C Midgut volvulus
  4. D Inflammatory bowel disease
  5. E Meckel’s diverticulum
💡 查看答案
E

3-year-old + PR bleeding ×2 weeks + iron deficiency anaemia = Meckel's diverticulum (painless rectal bleeding + anaemia in young child).

PS·MCQ044 2014 MCQ64 MCQ A 10 years old girl is admitted with 2-day history of right sided abdominal pain. Physical examination reveals pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is tenderness and guardi…

A 10 years old girl is admitted with 2-day history of right sided abdominal pain. Physical examination reveals pyrexia of 38.5°C. There is tenderness and guarding in the right lower quadrant. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute appendicitis
  2. B Crohn’s ileitis
  3. C Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  4. D Midgut volvulus
  5. E Perforated peptic ulcer
💡 查看答案
A

10/F, 2 days RIF pain, fever 38.5°C, RLQ tenderness + guarding = acute appendicitis.

PS·MCQ045 2014 MCQ65, 2018 MCQ77 MCQ A 3 weeks old boy with recurrent non-bilious vomiting after each feed, with sunken eye, small vague mass at epigastrium, hyponatraemia. What is the most likely …

A 3 weeks old boy with recurrent non-bilious vomiting after each feed, with sunken eye, small vague mass at epigastrium, hyponatraemia. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Gastroenteritis
  2. B Intussusception
  3. C Jejunal atresia
  4. D Necrotizing enterocolitis
  5. E Pyloric stenosis
💡 查看答案
E

3-week-old, non-bilious projectile vomiting, epigastric mass, hyponatraemia = pyloric stenosis.

PS·MCQ046 2015 MCQ87, 2020 MCQ46, 2021 MCQ89 MCQ A mother bought her 3 months old child to the Accident and Emergency department with history of repeated vomiting after feeding with milk. Physical examination …

A mother bought her 3 months old child to the Accident and Emergency department with history of repeated vomiting after feeding with milk. Physical examination showed a vague epigastric mass. Which of the following investigation could confirm your clinical diagnosis?

  1. A Abdominal X-ray
  2. B Barium enema
  3. C CT abdomen
  4. D MRI
  5. E Ultrasound
💡 查看答案
E

3-month-old, vomiting after feeds, epigastric mass → ultrasound (pyloric stenosis evaluation — though 3 months is late for HPS, USG is still the investigation of choice).

PS·MCQ047 2015 MCQ90 MCQ A 2 years old boy diagnosed to have bilateral Grade III VUR, which of the following is not a possible management option?

A 2 years old boy diagnosed to have bilateral Grade III VUR, which of the following is not a possible management option?

  1. A Long-term prophylactic antibiotics
  2. B Observe only
  3. C Deflux injection
  4. D Ureter reimplantation
  5. E Vesicostomy
💡 查看答案
B

2-year-old with bilateral Grade III VUR → observation alone is NOT appropriate as a standalone option (Grade III needs active management). All other options (prophylactic antibiotics, Deflux injection, reimplantation, vesicostomy) are valid.

PS·MCQ048 2016 MCQ13 MCQ Which of the following is INCORRECT about cleft?

Which of the following is INCORRECT about cleft?

  1. A Black less than White
  2. B Isolated cleft palate is more common than isolated cleft lip
  3. C Increased incidence in smoking mothers
  4. D Left side two times more common than right
  5. E More cleft palate in girls
💡 查看答案
A

Black race has LOWER incidence of cleft than White race — this IS true, so A is NOT incorrect. The question asks what IS incorrect. B says isolated cleft palate is more common than isolated cleft lip — actually isolated CP is LESS common. So B is incorrect.

PS·MCQ049 2016 MCQ38 MCQ Which of the following is not an accepted treatment for VUR?

Which of the following is not an accepted treatment for VUR?

  1. A Bladder training
  2. B Observation
  3. C Long term antibiotics
  4. D Injection of bulking agent at the ureteric opening
  5. E Reimplantation of ureter
💡 查看答案
A

Bladder training is NOT an accepted treatment for VUR in children. Observation, long-term antibiotics, injection of bulking agent, and reimplantation are all accepted treatments.

PS·MCQ050 2017 MCQ77, 2018 MCQ74, 2021 MCQ73 MCQ A 15 years old girl presented with primary amenorrhoea. 46, XY. No uterus, but have female external genitalia. 17-OH normal.

A 15 years old girl presented with primary amenorrhoea. 46, XY. No uterus, but have female external genitalia. 17-OH normal.

  1. A Androgen insensitivity
  2. B CAH
  3. C Mixed gonadal dysgenesis
  4. D Turner syndrome
  5. E True hermaphrodite
💡 查看答案
A

15/F, 46 XY, female external genitalia, no uterus, normal 17-OH = androgen insensitivity syndrome (testicular feminization — XY with complete androgen resistance).

PS·MCQ051 2018 MCQ73 MCQ A girl with high type imperforate anus, where would the distal end connect to?

A girl with high type imperforate anus, where would the distal end connect to?

  1. A Small bowel
  2. B Vagina
  3. C Ureter
  4. D Vertebra
  5. E Perineum
💡 查看答案
B

Female with high imperforate anus — the distal bowel commonly connects to the vagina (rectovaginal fistula). In males, rectourethral fistula.

PS·MCQ052 2018 MCQ75 MCQ A congenital diaphragmatic hernia was diagnosed in a foetus by antenatal ultrasound. Which of the following is the LEAST HELPFUL in improving the respiratory ph…

A congenital diaphragmatic hernia was diagnosed in a foetus by antenatal ultrasound. Which of the following is the LEAST HELPFUL in improving the respiratory physiology after birth?

  1. A Emergency repair of the hernia after birth
  2. B Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation
  3. C Nitric oxide
  4. D Surfactant
  5. E High frequency oscillation ventilation
💡 查看答案
A

Emergency hernia repair after birth is LEAST helpful for respiratory physiology in CDH. The problem is pulmonary hypoplasia + pulmonary HTN, not the herniated contents per se. ECMO, NO, surfactant, and HFOV all address the respiratory pathology.

PS·MCQ053 2018 MCQ76 MCQ 5 days old full term baby, presents with bile stained vomiting. On physical examination, the abdomen is slightly distended with no tenderness. Abdominal X-ray s…

5 days old full term baby, presents with bile stained vomiting. On physical examination, the abdomen is slightly distended with no tenderness. Abdominal X-ray shows gas filling in stomach and duodenum, but paucity of gas in the rest of the small bowel. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Gastroenteritis
  2. B Hirschsprung’s disease
  3. C Intussusception
  4. D Malrotation
  5. E Necrotizing enterocolitis
💡 查看答案
D

5-day-old + bile-stained vomiting + stomach/duodenum gas only = malrotation with volvulus (bilious vomiting in neonate = volvulus until proven otherwise).

PS·MCQ054 2019 MCQ31 MCQ A 8 days old baby born full term presents with bile-stained vomiting. Abdominal X-ray shows absence of bowel gas distal to duodenum. Complete blood count shows …

A 8 days old baby born full term presents with bile-stained vomiting. Abdominal X-ray shows absence of bowel gas distal to duodenum. Complete blood count shows leucocytosis and metabolic acidosis. Which of the following is the baby suffering from?

  1. A Intussusception
  2. B Meckel’s diverticulum
  3. C Midgut volvulus
  4. D Necrotizing enterocolitis
  5. E Small bowel atresia
💡 查看答案
C

8-day-old + bilious vomiting + no gas distal to duodenum + leucocytosis + metabolic acidosis = midgut volvulus (ischaemic compromise — urgent surgical emergency).

PS·MCQ055 2019 MCQ73 MCQ A 18 months old boy diagnosed of UTI. Later found to have grade 3 VUR by DMSA. Which is NOT an appropriate treatment?

A 18 months old boy diagnosed of UTI. Later found to have grade 3 VUR by DMSA. Which is NOT an appropriate treatment?

  1. A Circumcision
  2. B Sub-ureteric injection of bulking agent
  3. C Ureter reimplantation
  4. D Ureterostomy
  5. E Long term prophylactic antibiotics
💡 查看答案
D

Ureterostomy is NOT appropriate treatment for Grade 3 VUR in 18-month-old. Circumcision (reduces UTI), sub-ureteric injection, reimplantation, and prophylactic antibiotics are all appropriate.

PS·MCQ056 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following is not a complications of circumcision?

Which of the following is not a complications of circumcision?

  1. A Erectile dysfunction
  2. B Meatal stenosis
  3. C Injury to glans
  4. D Excessive foreskin removal
💡 查看答案
A

Erectile dysfunction is NOT a recognized complication of circumcision. Meatal stenosis, injury to glans, and excessive foreskin removal are complications.

PS·MCQ057 2020 MCQ53 MCQ A 3-month-old boy has repeated urinary tract infection due to E. coli. USG was done which showed right hydroureter and hydronephrosis; the left side was normal.…

A 3-month-old boy has repeated urinary tract infection due to E. coli. USG was done which showed right hydroureter and hydronephrosis; the left side was normal. What is the cause?

  1. A Duplex kidney
  2. B Vesicoureteric reflux
  3. C Posterior urethral valve
  4. D Vesicoureteric junction obstruction
  5. E Pelvi-ureteric junction obstruction
💡 查看答案
C

3-month-old boy + recurrent UTI + right hydronephrosis/hydroureter + left normal = posterior urethral valve is most likely (causes bilateral changes typically, but question shows only right — could be PUV with asymmetric obstruction, or VUJ obstruction). Given the boy's age and recurrent UTI, PUV is high on differential.

PS·MCQ058 2021 MCQ4 MCQ A 8-year-old boy complained of dragging sensation at right groin. P/E: testicle bag of worm appearance, non-tender. Right testis is slightly smaller than left t…

A 8-year-old boy complained of dragging sensation at right groin. P/E: testicle bag of worm appearance, non-tender. Right testis is slightly smaller than left testis.

  1. A Epididymo-orchitis
  2. B Varicocele
  3. C Testicular tumour
  4. D Hydrocele of the cord
💡 查看答案
B

8-year-old boy + right groin dragging + bag of worms + non-tender + smaller right testis = varicocele (right-sided varicocele in a child — investigate for retroperitoneal mass!).

PS·MCQ059 2021 MCQ81 MCQ Full term baby, 3 days old, with dilated large bowel loops and no distal rectal gas. Abdomen distended but nontender.

Full term baby, 3 days old, with dilated large bowel loops and no distal rectal gas. Abdomen distended but nontender.

  1. A Caecal duplication cyst
  2. B NEC
  3. C Meckel’s
  4. D Hirschsprung disease
💡 查看答案
D

Full-term 3-day-old + dilated large bowel + no rectal gas + distended non-tender abdomen = Hirschsprung disease.

PS·MCQ060 2022 MCQ9 MCQ Kid with sausage-like mass. What is the imaging modality of choice?

Kid with sausage-like mass. What is the imaging modality of choice?

  1. A MRI
  2. B CT
  3. C PET
  4. D Ultrasound
💡 查看答案
D

Sausage-like abdominal mass in child (intussusception) → ultrasound is the imaging modality of choice (target/pseudokidney sign).

PS·MCQ061 2022 MCQ54 MCQ Bilious vomiting, inconsolable, 5 day old young infant, NG tube aspirated deep green bile. Abdominal X ray show paucity of small bowel gas. Investigation?

Bilious vomiting, inconsolable, 5 day old young infant, NG tube aspirated deep green bile. Abdominal X ray show paucity of small bowel gas. Investigation?

  1. A Meckel scan
  2. B Contrast follow-through
  3. C MRI
  4. D PET
💡 查看答案
B

5-day-old + bilious vomiting + paucity of small bowel gas = suspected malrotation/volvulus → contrast (upper GI) follow-through to confirm malrotation.

PS·MCQ062 2022 MCQ61 MCQ 10-month-old boy, 2nd episode of UTI, MCUG found left grade 2-3 VUR. Which is NOT recommended for treatment:

10-month-old boy, 2nd episode of UTI, MCUG found left grade 2-3 VUR. Which is NOT recommended for treatment:

  1. A Circumcision
  2. B Injection of bulking agent at left ureteric orifice
  3. C Left nephrectomy
  4. D Oral prophylactic antibiotics
💡 查看答案
C

Left nephrectomy is NOT appropriate for Grade 2-3 VUR in a 10-month-old (kidney is preservable). Circumcision, bulking agent, and prophylactic antibiotics are appropriate.

PS·MCQ063 2022 MCQ87 MCQ You are referred a 7 month old boy for right undescended testis. A good sized testis is found in the right inguinal canal. What is the most appropriate manageme…

You are referred a 7 month old boy for right undescended testis. A good sized testis is found in the right inguinal canal. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Laparoscopy and Fowler Stephen’s operation
  2. B Observation
  3. C Right orchidectomy
  4. D Right orchidopexy
💡 查看答案
D

7-month-old + right undescended testis palpable in inguinal canal → right orchidopexy (fix in scrotum before age 1 if possible).

PS·EMQ001 2002# EMQ5 EMQ INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION IN NEONATES

Theme: INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION IN NEONATES

Options
  1. A Pyloric stenosis
  2. B Jejunoileal atresia
  3. C Colonic atresia
  4. D Hirschsprung’s disease
  5. E Imperforate anus
  6. F Postoperative paralytic ileus
  7. G Duodenal atresia
  8. H Necrotizing enterocolitis
  9. I Meconium ileus
  10. J Intussusception

Each of the following patients suffers from vomiting, select the MOST likely diagnosis for them.

Questions
  1. Q1. A newborn baby was admitted with a history of bilious vomiting beginning several hours after birth. Physical examination revealed a somewhat distended epigastrium without generalized abdominal distension. Pulse rate is 120/min, and BP is 60/38 mmHg. Bloody stool has never been found. Abdominal X-ray shows double bubble sign.
  2. Q2. A 7 days old premature boy who has been fed uneventfully since day 2 with regular bowel openings, was found to have abdominal distention and bloody stool for 1 day. Abdominal X-ray demonstrates distended bowels with suspicious intramural gas in the small bowel.
  3. Q3. A 10 days old boy has a history of delayed passage of meconium. He was found to suffer from progressive abdominal distention. Milk feeding becomes intolerable gradually. Bowel has not opened in the past 5 days. Abdominal X-ray shows dilated sigmoid colon and descending colon. Digital examination triggered a sudden “gush-out” of large amount of stool.
PS·EMQ002 2003 EMQ6 EMQ INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION IN PAEDIATRIC PATIENTS

Theme: INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION IN PAEDIATRIC PATIENTS

Options
  1. A Intussusception
  2. B Malrotation
  3. C Midgut volvulus
  4. D Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis
  5. E Hirschsprung’s disease
  6. F Duodenal atresia
  7. G Annular pancreas
  8. H Small bowel atresia
  9. I Adhesion band
  10. J Inguinal hernia

For each of the paediatric patients who has intestinal obstruction below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 9 months old infant admitted into hospital with vomiting, abdominal distention and bleeding per rectum was found to have a right upper quadrant mass.
  2. Q2. A 5 days old baby was found to have vomiting and passing blood per rectum. Physical examination did not show abdominal distention, but there was tenderness over the abdomen. Abdominal x-ray showed gaseous distention of the stomach and first part of the duodenum.
  3. Q3. A new born baby had abdominal distention and bile-stained vomitus. He passed meconium 6 hours after birth. General condition was satisfactory. Abdomen was distended but non- tender. Abdominal x-ray showed dilated small bowel loops with fluid levels. Contrast enema showed a small size colon.
PS·EMQ003 2004 EMQ7 EMQ CAUSES OF PAEDIATRIC GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Theme: CAUSES OF PAEDIATRIC GASTROINTESTINAL BLEEDING

Options
  1. A Duodenal ulcer
  2. B Gastritis
  3. C Esophageal varices
  4. D Midgut volvulus
  5. E Meckel’s diverticulum
  6. F Intussusception
  7. G Anal fissure
  8. H Juvenile polyp
  9. I Gastric ulcer
  10. J Necrotizing enterocolitis

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST suitable diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A full-term newborn baby started to have vomiting soon after birth. The vomiting was bile stained. He suddenly developed shock and passed a large amount of fresh blood per rectum. Physical examination showed a tender abdomen with guarding.
  2. Q2. A 9 months old girl, who was normal all along, started to have intermittent crying episodes. Soon, she started to have vomitings with bile-stained vomitus and passed blood in the stool. On physical examination, the abdomen was found to be distended and a sausage-shape mass was palpable on the right side of the abdomen.
  3. Q3. A 3 years old boy complained of passing fresh blood per rectum. Usually he passed stool every three days. Physical examination was normal except an indentable mass was palpable at left lower abdomen. He cried severely when rectal examination was attempted.
PS·EMQ004 2005 EMQ4 EMQ TREATMENT OF ACUTE NEONATAL INTESTINAL CONDITION

Theme: TREATMENT OF ACUTE NEONATAL INTESTINAL CONDITION

Options
  1. A Ladd’s procedure
  2. B Laparotomy ± gut resection
  3. C Ramstedt’s operation
  4. D Duodeno-duodenostomy
  5. E Colostomy
  6. F Drip, Ryle’s tube aspiration and antibiotics
  7. G Daily laxative
  8. H Hydrostatic reduction
  9. I Thoracotomy
  10. J Bedside abdominal drain insertion

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST suitable treatment from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 12 days old premature baby boy with birth weight of 1,250 gm presented with abdominal distension. Plain X-ray abdomen showed pneumatosis intestinalis and dilated small bowel loops with fluid level. Gas was found at the portal venous system.
  2. Q2. A 2,500 gm, full-term baby presented with vomiting on day 1 after birth. The vomitus was bile-stained. Plain X-ray abdomen showed “double-bubble” sign.
  3. Q3. A 7 days old girl presented with vomiting. The vomitus was bile-stained. On examination, abdomen was soft and non-tender. The bowel sounds were not active. Contrast meal and follow-through showed the duodeno-jejunal junction situated to the right side of the spine.
PS·EMQ005 2007 EMQ6 EMQ A. Congenital diaphragmatic hernia

Theme: A. Congenital diaphragmatic hernia

Options
  1. B Hirschsprung disease
  2. C NEC
  3. D Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis
  4. E Esophageal atresia with TOF
  5. F Small bowel atresia
  6. G Malrotation of gut ± volvulus
  7. H Omphalocele
  8. I Biliary atresia

Choose the right diagnosis.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 3 weeks old full term baby boy, presented with bilious vomiting, blood in stool, good past health.
  2. Q2. A baby girl, 1 day after birth. Antenatal USG showed absent gastric bubble and polyhydramnios. Presented with distended abdomen and excessive salivation. Attempt feeding causes choking, coughing and regurgitation.
  3. Q3. A 4 days old baby boy who has just passed meconium was noticed to have a distended abdomen. On PR exam, the rectum was empty and there was a gush of air on withdrawal of finger.
PS·EMQ006 2009 EMQ10 EMQ PAEDIATRIC SURGERY

Theme: PAEDIATRIC SURGERY

Options
  1. A Oesophageal atresia
  2. B Hirschsprung’s disease
  3. C Necrotizing enterocolitis
  4. D Jejunal atresia
  5. E Intussusception
  6. F Imperforate anus
  7. G Meckel’s diverticulum
  8. H Acute gastroenteritis
  9. I GERD
  10. J Hypertrophic pyloric stenosis
Questions
  1. Q1. A 6 months old boy presented with irritability, bilious vomiting, per-rectal bleeding and a right-sided abdominal mass.
  2. Q2. A newborn baby was noted with increased salivation and choking during feeding. Orogastric tube cannot be passed through.
PS·EMQ007 2010 EMQ7 EMQ PAEDIATRIC ACUTE ABDOMEN

Theme: PAEDIATRIC ACUTE ABDOMEN

Options
  1. A Anal atresia
  2. B Acute appendicitis
  3. C Gastroenteritis
  4. D Hirschsprung’s disease
  5. E Incarcerated hernia
  6. F Intussusception
  7. G Malrotation
  8. H Necrotizing enterocolitis
  9. I Post-operation adhesions
  10. J Small bowel atresia
Questions
PS·EMQ008 2011 EMQ4 EMQ (no theme — see question cases)

Theme: (no theme header in source)

Options
Questions
  1. Q1. A 2 days old baby was born full-term with no significant antenatal history. You were consulted for his symptoms of vomiting of bile-stained fluid and undigested milk after feed. Abdominal X-ray showed absence of bowel gas distal to mid-jejunum. Contrast follow-through study confirmed the location of D-J flexion being on the left side of the vertebrae.
PS·EMQ009 2013 EMQ1 EMQ VOMITING IN CHILDREN

Theme: VOMITING IN CHILDREN

Options
  1. A Appendicitis
  2. B Constipation
  3. C Duodenal atresia
  4. D Gastroenteritis
  5. E Hirschsprung disease
  6. F Intussusception
  7. G Malrotation or midgut volvulus
  8. H Meckel’s diverticulum
  9. I Necrotizing enterocolitis
  10. J Pyloric stenosis
Questions
PS·EMQ010 2014 EMQ2 EMQ PAEDIATRIC INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION

Theme: PAEDIATRIC INTESTINAL OBSTRUCTION

Options
  1. A Anorectal atresia
  2. B Appendicitis
  3. C Meckel’s diverticulum
  4. D Hirschsprung disease
  5. E Incarcerated hernia
  6. F Intussusception
  7. G Malrotation / midgut volvulus
  8. H NEC
  9. I Pyloric stenosis
  10. J Small bowel atresia
Questions
  1. Q1. A 3 weeks old baby presents with bilious vomiting for 7 days. Physical examination showed signs of poor capillary refill and sunken eyes. AXR show dilated stomach and duodenum, with paucity of gas in the small bowel.
  2. Q2. A 10 years old boy presented with repeated vomiting and colicky abdominal pain. He had not passed stool for 2 days. Physical examination showed hyperactive bowel sounds. X ray showed paucity of large bowel gas. Tender mass in the right groin.
  3. Q3. Sausage-shaped mass at right side of abdomen.
PS·EMQ011 2015 EMQ1, 2018 EMQ6 EMQ ABDOMINAL DISTENSION IN CHILDREN

Theme: ABDOMINAL DISTENSION IN CHILDREN

Options
  1. A Anal atresia
  2. B Acute appendicitis
  3. C Gastroenteritis
  4. D Hirschsprung’s disease
  5. E Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  6. F Intussusception
  7. G Malrotation / mid-gut volvulus
  8. H Necrotizing enterocolitis
  9. I Post-operative adhesions
  10. J Small intestinal atresia
  11. K Congenital diaphragmatic hernia
  12. L Gastrochisis
  13. M Meconium ileus
Questions
  1. Q1. A 5 days old full term baby boy presented with sudden onset of bilious vomiting. He had no significant antenatal history. On physical examination, his general condition was poor, he had cold extremities and low blood pressure. Abdominal x-ray showed paucity of gas shadow in small bowel.
  2. Q2. A 3 days old baby presented with abdominal distension and vomiting. On further questioning, meconium was only passed one day after birth. Abdominal x-ray showed no rectal gas.
  3. Q3. An 8 months old girl with no significant antenatal history. One day presented with abdominal distension, repeated vomiting and inconsolable cry. Physical examination showed a sausage shaped mass in the right side of the abdomen.
  4. Q4. Neonate mild abdominal fullness, perineal with dimple at anus and no anal opening.
  5. Q5. Premature bb present with fever and shock. Erythematous abdominal wall; foamy gas shadow on AXR.
PS·EMQ012 2016 EMQ1, 2017 EMQ2 EMQ PAEDIATRIC SURERY

Theme: PAEDIATRIC SURERY

Options
  1. A Malrotation / midgut volvulus
  2. B Intussusception
  3. C Pyloric stenosis
  4. D Necrotizing enterocolitis
  5. E Small bowel atresia
  6. F Hirschsprung
  7. G Meconium ileus
  8. H Gastrochisis
  9. I Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  10. J Anal atresia
Questions
  1. Q1. A 3 weeks old baby, normal antenatally and perinatally, presented with bilious vomiting. X ray only showed stomach and duodenum. Paucity of gas in small bowel.
  2. Q2. A prematurely born baby. Normal until the 7th day of life. Presented with fever and hypotension. Poor general condition. Erythematous abdomen. X-ray abdomen reveals gas at intestinal wall.
  3. Q3. A 2 days old boy full term, normal antenatal history, presented with bilious vomiting and undigested milk, P/E show poor GC. XR show the D-J flexure is on the left side of vertebrae, gas in stomach and duodenum, absent of bowel gas in the small bowel distal to duodenum.
  4. Q4. When doctor performed PR on the baby, there was a gush of air.
PS·EMQ013 2019 EMQ3 EMQ PEDIATRIC INVESTIGATIONS

Theme: PEDIATRIC INVESTIGATIONS

Options
  1. A Bronchoscopy
  2. B Cystoscopy
  3. C CT
  4. D MRI scan
  5. E Meckel’s scan
  6. F OGD
  7. G Erect AXR
  8. H Erect CXR
  9. I USG
Questions
  1. Q1. 15/M, epigastric pain for 4 hours. Fever 39°C, BP 9x/50x mmHg, HR 130. PE generalized tenderness.
  2. Q2. 2/F frothy saliva and vomiting after dinner. Her mother suspects she has ingested a fish bone. Bedside direct laryngoscopy does not reveal any foreign body.
  3. Q3. F/7, previous normal bowel opening. Presented with 1 day history of fever and RLQ pain. Physical examination showed tender mass at RLQ. Blood count is normal.
PS·EMQ014 2021 EMQ7 EMQ A. Acute appendicitis

Theme: A. Acute appendicitis

Options
  1. B Hirschsprung disease
  2. C Intestinal atresia
  3. D Intestinal malrotation and acute mid gut volvulus
  4. E Intussusception
  5. F Meckel’s diverticulum
  6. G Necrotising enterocolitis
  7. H Pyloric stenosis
Questions
  1. Q1. Preterm boy, 28 week of gestation, 900g. Day 13 greenish fluid aspirated from orogastric tube, fever, abdominal distension, metabolic acidosis, AXR shows pneumatosis intestinalis.
  2. Q2. 13-month-old boy, sudden vomiting for 1 day, flu like symptoms, low grade fever for few days, blood and mucus per rectum on admission, crying intermittently, colicky abdominal pain.
  3. Q3. 3-day-old boy, sudden greenish vomiting, feeding no problem since birth, sick, slightly hypotensive(?), small amount of blood in nappy, AXR show paucity of intestinal gas, metabolic acidosis.
SECTION 14

Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery

44 MCQ · 6 EMQ
PRS·MCQ001 2001 MCQ17 MCQ In flame burn of the forearm,

In flame burn of the forearm,

  1. A The injured forearm should be hung down in order to improve the circulation
  2. B It is very painful if it is third degree burn
  3. C Blister formation is a poor prognostic sign
  4. D Escharotomy is indicated in full thickness circumferential burn
  5. E Fasciotomy is indicated if the finger circulation is impaired
💡 查看答案
D

Full-thickness circumferential burn → escharotomy indicated (to relieve compartment pressure from inelastic eschar). 3rd degree burn is PAINLESS (B wrong). Blisters = partial thickness (C wrong).

PRS·MCQ002 2001 MCQ25 MCQ The following are signs of malignancy in a pigmented skin lesion EXCEPT:

The following are signs of malignancy in a pigmented skin lesion EXCEPT:

  1. A Itchiness
  2. B Decrease in pigmentation
  3. C Increase in size
  4. D Bleeding
  5. E Ulceration
💡 查看答案
B

Decrease in pigmentation of a mole is NOT a sign of malignancy. ABCDEs of melanoma: Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation (increase, not decrease), Diameter >6mm, Evolving. Itchiness, increase in size, bleeding, ulceration are malignant signs.

PRS·MCQ003 2001 MCQ41 MCQ Inhalation injuries are associated with the following EXCEPT:

Inhalation injuries are associated with the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Hoarseness
  2. B Soots in oral and nasal cavity
  3. C Drop of carboxyhaemoglobin level
  4. D Facial burn
  5. E Laryngeal oedema 16 hours later
💡 查看答案
C

Inhalation injury causes ELEVATED (not dropped) carboxyhaemoglobin level. Hoarseness, soots, facial burn, and delayed laryngeal oedema are all associated.

PRS·MCQ004 2001 MCQ47 MCQ Regarding squamous cell carcinoma,

Regarding squamous cell carcinoma,

  1. A It commonly spreads by blood stream
  2. B Local chemotherapy is an effective modality of treatment
  3. C It can arise from longstanding pressure ulcer
  4. D It is a common histology of bladder tumour
  5. E Radiotherapy is not effective if it is larger than 2 cm in diameter
💡 查看答案
C

SCC can arise from longstanding pressure ulcer (Marjolin's ulcer — malignant transformation of chronic wound/scar). SCC spreads via lymphatics primarily (not blood, A wrong).

PRS·MCQ005 2002 MCQ19, 2002# MCQ87 MCQ Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding hypertrophic scars developed after a traumatic injury?

Which of the following statements is FALSE regarding hypertrophic scars developed after a traumatic injury?

  1. A Should be managed by excision
  2. B Should be treated with non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs
  3. C Is very painful
  4. D Will usually regress spontaneously
  5. E Can be treated with radiotherapy
💡 查看答案
B

NSAIDs are NOT the treatment for hypertrophic scars. Steroid injection, pressure garments, silicone sheets, and observation (spontaneous regression) are treatments. Excision alone leads to recurrence.

PRS·MCQ006 2002 MCQ27 MCQ Which of the following lesions is NOT typically brownish or black in colour?

Which of the following lesions is NOT typically brownish or black in colour?

  1. A Seborrheic keratosis
  2. B Melanocytic naevus
  3. C Naevus sebaceous cyst
  4. D Hutchison’s freckles
  5. E Resolving subcutaneous haematoma
💡 查看答案
C

Naevus sebaceous (of Jadassohn) is typically yellowish/orange, NOT brownish or black. Seborrhoeic keratosis, melanocytic naevus, Hutchinson's freckles, and haematoma are all brown/black.

PRS·MCQ007 2002 MCQ68 MCQ Full thickness skin burn of the leg:

Full thickness skin burn of the leg:

  1. A Is very painful
  2. B Causes extensive muscle necrosis
  3. C Results in osteoporosis
  4. D Is an indication for fasciotomy
  5. E Needs excision of the necrotic skin
💡 查看答案
E

Full-thickness burn of leg needs excision of necrotic skin (eschar) and skin grafting. Full-thickness burn is PAINLESS (A wrong). Doesn't cause extensive muscle necrosis (B wrong).

PRS·MCQ008 2002 MCQ68 MCQ A 40 years old factory worker suffers from deep dermal burn of 35% body surface area:

A 40 years old factory worker suffers from deep dermal burn of 35% body surface area:

  1. A Requires daily outpatient dressing of the wound
  2. B Requires intravenous morphine for pain relief
  3. C Will certainly die of pneumonia
  4. D Must be intubated
  5. E Is likely to have myoglobinuria
💡 查看答案
E

35% deep dermal burn → likely to have myoglobinuria (deep burns → rhabdomyolysis → myoglobin in urine). Not necessarily die of pneumonia (C wrong). May need intubation if respiratory compromise but not always (D wrong).

PRS·MCQ009 2002# MCQ20 MCQ Which of the following conditions has a SIGNIFICANT chance of malignant change?

Which of the following conditions has a SIGNIFICANT chance of malignant change?

  1. A Pigmented mole
  2. B Seborrhoeic keratosis
  3. C Naevus sebaceous
  4. D Verrucus vulgaris
  5. E Neurofibroma
💡 查看答案
C

Naevus sebaceous (Jadassohn) has significant risk of malignant change (BCC, less commonly SCC — ~5-10% risk). Seborrhoeic keratosis is benign. Pigmented moles have low risk. Warts and neurofibromas have minimal risk.

PRS·MCQ010 2003 MCQ39 MCQ The characteristic features found in a basal cell carcinoma in Asians include the following EXCEPT:

The characteristic features found in a basal cell carcinoma in Asians include the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Rolled ulcer edge
  2. B Telangiectasia
  3. C Pigmentation
  4. D Keratinization
  5. E Slow progression
💡 查看答案
D

Keratinization is NOT characteristic of BCC in Asians (BCC doesn't produce keratin — that's SCC). Rolled edge, telangiectasia, pigmentation (60% in Asians), and slow progression are BCC features.

PRS·MCQ011 2003 MCQ56 MCQ Which of the following is NOT true for basal cell carcinoma?

Which of the following is NOT true for basal cell carcinoma?

  1. A Exposure to sunlight is a predisposing factor
  2. B It is the second commonest skin malignancy after squamous cell carcinoma
  3. C The ulcer has a typical rolled edge
  4. D Metastasis to lymph node occurs very rarely
  5. E Squamous differentiation is a rare occurrence
💡 查看答案
B

BCC is actually the MOST common skin malignancy (not second to SCC). BCC > SCC in incidence.

PRS·MCQ012 2003 MCQ62 MCQ Whether the nutritional needs of a patient 10 days after a major burn are being met is BEST assessed by:

Whether the nutritional needs of a patient 10 days after a major burn are being met is BEST assessed by:

  1. A Calorie counts
  2. B Measurement of daily weight
  3. C Measurement of red cell mass
  4. D Measurement of serum albumin levels
  5. E Nitrogen balance studies
💡 查看答案
E

Nitrogen balance studies best assess whether nutritional needs are being met in a burn patient (reflects protein metabolism — positive balance = anabolic).

PRS·MCQ013 2003 MCQ67 MCQ Which of the following statements concerning skin conditions is TRUE?

Which of the following statements concerning skin conditions is TRUE?

  1. A Cutaneous horns are always benign
  2. B Verrucous contagiosum is best treated with excision and primary closure
  3. C Some keloids become sarcomas
  4. D Strawberry naevus is often not noticeable at birth
  5. E Plantar wart is not painful
💡 查看答案
D

Strawberry naevus (infantile haemangioma) is often NOT noticeable at birth — typically appears at 2-4 weeks and grows rapidly. Cutaneous horns can be malignant (A wrong). Keloids don't become sarcomas (C wrong). Plantar warts ARE painful (E wrong).

PRS·MCQ014 2004 MCQ7 MCQ A 25 years old gentleman who weighted 60 kg was brought to the Accident and Emergency Department after sustaining flame burn injury while at work. The injury in…

A 25 years old gentleman who weighted 60 kg was brought to the Accident and Emergency Department after sustaining flame burn injury while at work. The injury involved 40% of total body surface area (TBSA). Which of the following is the MOST appropriate amount of crystalloid solution to be given in the first eight hours?

  1. A 1000ml
  2. B 2000ml
  3. C 6000ml
  4. D 11000ml
  5. E 15000ml
💡 查看答案
D
