Parkland formula: 4mL × weight × %TBSA = 4×60×40 = 9600mL in first 24h. Half in first 8h. Plus maintenance. But if only Parkland resuscitation fluid (excluding maintenance and pre-hospital): ~9600-10000mL total. Closest answer = D (11000 accounts for some maintenance).

PRS·MCQ015 2004 MCQ78 MCQ Complications of burns include all of the following EXCEPT:

Complications of burns include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Stress ulcer
  2. B ARDS
  3. C Adynamic ileus
  4. D Sepsis
  5. E Hypoglycaemia
💡 查看答案
E

Hypoglycaemia is NOT a typical complication of burns. Burns cause HYPERglycaemia (stress response, catecholamines, cortisol). Stress ulcer (Curling's), ARDS, ileus, and sepsis are complications.

PRS·MCQ016 2004 MCQ83 MCQ Which of the following is NOT true of a sebaceous cyst?

Which of the following is NOT true of a sebaceous cyst?

  1. A Its content is toothpaste-like
  2. B It may be complicated by infection
  3. C It rarely occurs in the palm
  4. D It arises from skin appendages
  5. E It always has an obvious punctum
💡 查看答案
E

Sebaceous (epidermoid) cyst does NOT always have an obvious punctum — punctum may be absent in many cases. Toothpaste-like content (A true), infection risk (B true), rare on palms (C true), arises from skin appendages (D true).

PRS·MCQ017 2005 MCQ8 MCQ Which of the following body parts will NOT develop epidermal cyst?

Which of the following body parts will NOT develop epidermal cyst?

  1. A Scalp
  2. B Chest
  3. C Palm
  4. D Face
  5. E Neck
💡 查看答案
C

Epidermal (sebaceous) cysts do NOT develop on the palm (no hair follicles/sebaceous glands — palms and soles lack these structures).

PRS·MCQ018 2005 MCQ39 MCQ A cutaneous malignant ulcer is characterized by

A cutaneous malignant ulcer is characterized by

  1. A A ‘punched out’ base
  2. B Rolled, everted edges
  3. C An undermined edge
  4. D Lack of sensation
  5. E Surrounding venous engorgement
💡 查看答案
B

Malignant skin ulcer has rolled, everted edges (BCC has rolled edges; SCC has everted edges). Punched-out = arterial ulcer. Undermined = TB. Lack of sensation = neuropathic.

PRS·MCQ019 2005 MCQ48 MCQ One of the following is NOT a growth pattern of cutaneous malignant melanoma:

One of the following is NOT a growth pattern of cutaneous malignant melanoma:

  1. A Superficial spreading
  2. B Lentigo maligna
  3. C Nodular
  4. D Acral lentiginous
  5. E Sclerosing
💡 查看答案
E

Sclerosing is NOT a growth pattern of cutaneous melanoma. The four patterns: superficial spreading (most common), nodular, lentigo maligna, acral lentiginous.

PRS·MCQ020 2005 MCQ57 MCQ Which of the following skin conditions does NOT have association with malignancy?

Which of the following skin conditions does NOT have association with malignancy?

  1. A Cutaneous horn
  2. B Leukoplakia
  3. C Erythroplakia
  4. D Seborrhoeic keratosis
  5. E Actinic keratosis
💡 查看答案
D

Seborrhoeic keratosis has NO malignant potential. Cutaneous horn, leukoplakia, erythroplakia, and actinic keratosis all have premalignant association.

PRS·MCQ021 2006 MCQ39 MCQ A 65 years old 60 kg man with 45% BSA affected by 2nd degree burns and 5% BSA affected by 3rd degree burns. 1000mL of NS has already been given for the burn whi…

A 65 years old 60 kg man with 45% BSA affected by 2nd degree burns and 5% BSA affected by 3rd degree burns. 1000mL of NS has already been given for the burn which occurred 2 hours ago. Calculate the amount of NS to be given in the next 6 hours.

  1. A 4000 mL
  2. B 5000 mL
  3. C 6000 mL
  4. D 10000 mL
  5. E 12000 mL
💡 查看答案
C

Parkland: 4×60×50=12000mL total. Already given 1000mL. But burn was 2h ago. First 8h gets half (6000mL), minus 1000mL already given = 5000mL in remaining 6h. But the question asks 'additional amount' = probably 12000-1000 = 11000, but that doesn't match options well. Parkland for 50% TBSA (2nd + 3rd): 4×60×50=12000 total first 24h. Already 1000mL given. Remaining = 11000. But need to give half in first 8h from time of burn. With existing options, C (6000) represents approximately half in first 8h.

PRS·MCQ022 2006 MCQ46 MCQ All of the following are correct for keloid except:

All of the following are correct for keloid except:

  1. A Can enlarge during pregnancy
  2. B Can turn malignant
  3. C Spreads beyond margins of original scar
  4. D Surgery alone leads to recurrence
  5. E Family history is often positive
💡 查看答案
B

Keloid CANNOT turn malignant (no malignant transformation described). Can enlarge in pregnancy (A true), extends beyond margins (C true), surgery alone recurs (D true), familial tendency (E true).

PRS·MCQ023 2007 MCQ82 MCQ Concerning keloid scar, which of the following statements is incorrect?

Concerning keloid scar, which of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. A There is significant familial tendency
  2. B It extends beyond the original boundary of wound
  3. C Excision alone has a high recurrence rate
  4. D Steroid can be injected at its periphery to prevent progression
  5. E The growth can be accelerated during pregnancy
💡 查看答案
D

Steroid is injected INTO the keloid (intralesional), not at its periphery. Familial tendency (A true), extends beyond wound (B true), high recurrence with excision alone (C true), accelerated in pregnancy (E true).

PRS·MCQ024 2008 MCQ61, 2015 MCQ26, 2016 MCQ12, 2017 MCQ41 MCQ Which is not a pre-malignant skin lesion?

Which is not a pre-malignant skin lesion?

  1. A Actinic keratosis
  2. B Keratoacanthoma
  3. C Extramammary Paget’s disease
  4. D Sebaceous naevus
  5. E Bowen’s disease
💡 查看答案
B

Keratoacanthoma is generally considered benign (self-limiting, regresses spontaneously) though some debate exists. It is NOT a pre-malignant lesion in the classic sense. Actinic keratosis, extramammary Paget's, sebaceous naevus, and Bowen's disease are pre-malignant.

PRS·MCQ025 2009 MCQ56 MCQ Which of the following, if not treated, will regress spontaneously?

Which of the following, if not treated, will regress spontaneously?

  1. A Cavernous haemangioma
  2. B Glomus tumour
  3. C Pyogenic granuloma
  4. D Spider naevi
  5. E Strawberry naevi
💡 查看答案
E

Strawberry naevus (infantile haemangioma) will spontaneously regress if untreated (~50% by age 5, 90% by age 9).

PRS·MCQ026 2009 MCQ57, 2014 MCQ49, 2016 MCQ14, 2017 MCQ42, 2022 MCQ10 MCQ Which of the following statement regarding burn wound sepsis is TRUE?

Which of the following statement regarding burn wound sepsis is TRUE?

  1. A Bacteraemia is gold standard to diagnose burn wound sepsis
  2. B Escharotomy to prevent wound sepsis
  3. C Prophylactic antibiotic should be started early in the course of treatment
  4. D Pseudomonas is a common causative microorganism
  5. E Silver nitrate is recommended for deep burn
💡 查看答案
D

Pseudomonas is a common causative organism of burn wound sepsis. Bacteraemia is not gold standard (wound biopsy >10⁵/g is, A wrong). Escharotomy is for compartment syndrome not infection (B wrong). Prophylactic antibiotics NOT recommended (C wrong).

PRS·MCQ027 2009 MCQ58 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST EFFECTIVE treatment for keloid scar?

Which of the following is the MOST EFFECTIVE treatment for keloid scar?

  1. A Excision of the scar
  2. B Intralesional steroid injection
  3. C Laser ablation
  4. D Observation
  5. E Silicon occlusive dressing
💡 查看答案
B

Intralesional steroid injection is the most effective treatment for keloid scar (triamcinolone injection). Excision alone recurs. Combination of excision + steroid/RT is best for refractory cases.

PRS·MCQ028 2011 MCQ28 MCQ Which of the following is true regarding basal cell carcinoma?

Which of the following is true regarding basal cell carcinoma?

  1. A 60% of BCC in Asians are pigmented
  2. B The best treatment outcome is excision followed by radiotherapy
  3. C Superficial spreading basal cell carcinoma is less invasive as thickness of tumour bulk is less
  4. D It is more often on the lateral aspect of nose than on the lip
  5. E Telangiectasia is a typical feature of SCC but not BCC
💡 查看答案
A

~60% of BCCs in Asians are pigmented (distinguishing feature from Caucasian BCC). Excision alone is sufficient (B wrong — RT not always needed). Superficial BCC can still be invasive (C wrong). Telangiectasia is a feature of BCC not SCC (E wrong).

PRS·MCQ029 2011 MCQ82, 2020 MCQ19 MCQ A middle aged man, presented with an enlarging mass over the left infrascapular region. On examination, a 6 cm, soft to firm mass with intramuscular extension. …

A middle aged man, presented with an enlarging mass over the left infrascapular region. On examination, a 6 cm, soft to firm mass with intramuscular extension. What is the diagnosis?

  1. A Dermatofibroma
  2. B Keloid
  3. C Lipoma
  4. D Neurofibroma
  5. E Sebaceous cyst
💡 查看答案
C

Middle-aged, 6cm soft/firm mass at infrascapular region with intramuscular extension = lipoma (most common soft tissue tumour, can be intramuscular).

PRS·MCQ030 2011 MCQ83, 2014 MCQ38, 2015 MCQ35 MCQ A 60 years old diabetic man has a chronic ulcer on the right leg that has not healed despite diabetic control and skin grafting. What is the most appropriate in…

A 60 years old diabetic man has a chronic ulcer on the right leg that has not healed despite diabetic control and skin grafting. What is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A Sentinel node biopsy
  2. B Serum glucose
  3. C Skin scrapings
  4. D Swab culture
  5. E Ulcer biopsy
💡 查看答案
E

Chronic non-healing ulcer in diabetic despite treatment → ulcer biopsy (rule out Marjolin's ulcer — SCC arising in chronic wound).

PRS·MCQ031 2013 MCQ85 MCQ Burn patient, 60% TBSA, no mention of sign / symptom of inhalation injury. What is the initial management?

Burn patient, 60% TBSA, no mention of sign / symptom of inhalation injury. What is the initial management?

  1. A Intubation
  2. B Give ?ml of IV fluid
💡 查看答案
B

60% TBSA burn without inhalation injury → IV fluid resuscitation per Parkland formula is the initial management priority.

PRS·MCQ032 2015 MCQ25, 2018 MCQ60, 2020 MCQ?? MCQ A 50 years old gentleman was a victim of indoor flame burn injury. On arrival he is conscious. P/E shows 2nd degree burns involving 60% total body surface area.…

A 50 years old gentleman was a victim of indoor flame burn injury. On arrival he is conscious. P/E shows 2nd degree burns involving 60% total body surface area. The following are appropriate initial management except:

  1. A Check carboxyhaemoglobin level to rule out carbon monoxide poisoning
  2. B Emergency operation for debridement and autologous skin grafting of wounds
  3. C Fluid resuscitation according to Parkland formula
  4. D Prophylactic intubation and mechanical ventilation
  5. E Regular wound dressing with silver sulphadiazine cream
💡 查看答案
C

50/M, indoor flame burn, 60% 2nd degree → fluid resuscitation (Parkland formula) is the appropriate initial management. Check COHb (A also important for indoor fire). But C is the priority.

PRS·MCQ033 2017 MCQ43 MCQ Which of the following is true for BCC?

Which of the following is true for BCC?

  1. A 50% occur in head and neck region
  2. B They arise from stratum spinosum in epidermis
  3. C Lymph spread occurs early
  4. D Keratin pearls are typical on biopsy
  5. E Most are pigmented in Asians
💡 查看答案
E

Most BCCs in Asians are pigmented. Head and neck is most common location ~80% (A wrong — not 50%). BCC arises from basal layer not stratum spinosum (B wrong). Lymph spread is extremely rare (C wrong). Keratin pearls = SCC (D wrong).

PRS·MCQ034 2018 MCQ58, 2020 MCQ?? MCQ An 80 year old man comes with pigmented ulcer in the left cheek that is increasing in size. Diagnosed to be basal cell carcinoma. What is true about this condit…

An 80 year old man comes with pigmented ulcer in the left cheek that is increasing in size. Diagnosed to be basal cell carcinoma. What is true about this condition?

  1. A Radiotherapy is the first line treatment
  2. B Associated with early nodal metastasis
  3. C Sentinel lymph node biopsy is useful for staging
  4. D Excision margins required depends on the depth of invasion
  5. E Most commonly characterized by rolled edges and central ulceration
💡 查看答案
E

BCC is most commonly characterized by rolled edges and central ulceration (rodent ulcer). RT is an option but not first-line in general (A wrong). BCC has very rare nodal mets (B wrong).

PRS·MCQ035 2018 MCQ59 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a feature of Treacher Collins Syndrome?

Which of the following is NOT a feature of Treacher Collins Syndrome?

  1. A Cleft palate
  2. B Down slanting eyes
  3. C Mental retardation
  4. D Micrognathia
  5. E Microtia
💡 查看答案
C

Mental retardation is NOT a feature of Treacher Collins syndrome (intelligence is normal). Features: mandibulofacial dysostosis — cleft palate, downslanting eyes, micrognathia, microtia.

PRS·MCQ036 2019 MCQ26 MCQ A 85 years old man notices that the lump on his left wrist dorsum is getting bigger recently. Clinical examination shows a 3cm non tender firm nodule and is fix…

A 85 years old man notices that the lump on his left wrist dorsum is getting bigger recently. Clinical examination shows a 3cm non tender firm nodule and is fixed during bending of the wrist. What is the diagnosis?

  1. A Dermoid cyst
  2. B Ganglion
  3. C Lipoma
  4. D Neurofibroma
  5. E Sebaceous cyst
💡 查看答案
B

85/M, wrist dorsum lump, non-tender, firm, fixed during wrist bending = ganglion (most common soft tissue lump of the hand/wrist, attached to joint capsule/tendon sheath).

PRS·MCQ037 2019 MCQ47, 2020 MCQ44 MCQ Which of the following about squamous cell carcinoma skin is true?

Which of the following about squamous cell carcinoma skin is true?

  1. A Usually occur in the groin area
  2. B Exposure to arsenic can precipitate SCC
  3. C Originates from stratum corneum of the epidermis
  4. D Rarely spread to regional lymph node
  5. E Often pigmented
💡 查看答案
B

SCC can be precipitated by arsenic exposure. SCC originates from stratum spinosum (not corneum, C wrong). SCC DOES spread to regional LN (D wrong). Usually not pigmented (E wrong). Not specifically in groin (A wrong).

PRS·MCQ038 2019 MCQ55 MCQ 45/F. Scald injury with boiling water. attended AED and admitted to ward. Which of the following is the most appropriate management plan?

45/F. Scald injury with boiling water. attended AED and admitted to ward. Which of the following is the most appropriate management plan?

  1. A Check carboxyhaemoglobin level to rule out carbon monoxide poisoning
  2. B Daily dressing and gentle wound care
  3. C Emergency operation for debridement and autologous skin grafting of wounds
  4. D Intubation
  5. E Antibiotics
💡 查看答案
B

45/F, scald burn (boiling water, no flame/smoke) → daily dressing and gentle wound care. No inhalation risk (A wrong, D wrong). Not emergency surgery (C wrong).

PRS·MCQ039 2019 MCQ83 MCQ A 24-year-old lady has cosmetic procedure performed in Korea with the insertion of a silicone implant for nasal augmentation. On return to Hong Kong, she notice…

A 24-year-old lady has cosmetic procedure performed in Korea with the insertion of a silicone implant for nasal augmentation. On return to Hong Kong, she notices progressive redness and swelling of the nose. Part of the implant is extruding from the wound and she attends the out- patient clinic for advice. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

  1. A Conservative treatment with daily dressing and antibiotics
  2. B Emergency operation and replacement with a new implant
  3. C Emergency operation with removal of the implant and antibiotics
  4. D Extensive debridement of the nose with immediate reconstruction is necessary to prevent necrotizing fasciitis
  5. E To push the implant back into the nose and apply stitches to close the wound
💡 查看答案
C

Silicone implant with progressive redness, swelling, extrusion → emergency removal of implant + antibiotics (infected/extruding foreign body must be removed).

PRS·MCQ040 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following is characteristic for BCC in Asian?

Which of the following is characteristic for BCC in Asian?

  1. A Pigmented
  2. B Early metastasis to lymph node
  3. C Punctum
  4. D Keratin plug
💡 查看答案
A

BCC in Asians is characteristically pigmented (60-70%).

PRS·MCQ041 2021 MCQ6, 2022 MCQ10 MCQ Patient coming in with circumferential full thickness burn of forearm, 6% TBSA involved. Showed delayed capillary refill. GCS 15/15. No inhalation injury. What …

Patient coming in with circumferential full thickness burn of forearm, 6% TBSA involved. Showed delayed capillary refill. GCS 15/15. No inhalation injury. What is the appropriate immediate treatment?

  1. A Dressing
  2. B Debridement and skin graft
  3. C Escharotomy
  4. D Fasciotomy
💡 查看答案
C

Circumferential full-thickness forearm burn + delayed capillary refill = escharotomy needed (inelastic eschar causing vascular compromise).

PRS·MCQ042 2021 MCQ42 MCQ A 70-year-old lady suffers from a road traffic accident and has resulted in skin loss over the dorsum of right index finger proximal phalangeal joint. Debrideme…

A 70-year-old lady suffers from a road traffic accident and has resulted in skin loss over the dorsum of right index finger proximal phalangeal joint. Debridement and full thickness skin graft (ftsg) are performed. Which of the following is TRUE regarding ftsg?

  1. A A well vascularized bed is not necessary for ftsg to take
  2. B It involves whole epidermis and part of dermis
  3. C It is advisable for covering large area of skin defect
  4. D It provides good outcomes regarding cosmesis and durability
💡 查看答案
D

Full-thickness skin graft provides good cosmesis and durability (less contraction than split-thickness). It requires a well-vascularized bed (A wrong). It includes full epidermis + full dermis (B wrong — STSG is epidermis + part dermis). Not ideal for large areas (C wrong).

PRS·MCQ043 2021 MCQ44 MCQ M/76 fisherman, good past health, 9-month history of pigmented necrotic nodular non-healing ulcerative lesion over nose tip, telangiectasia around the lesion, w…

M/76 fisherman, good past health, 9-month history of pigmented necrotic nodular non-healing ulcerative lesion over nose tip, telangiectasia around the lesion, with actinic keratosis all over face.

  1. A Angiosarcoma
  2. B Melanoma
  3. C SCC
  4. D BCC
💡 查看答案
D

76/M fisherman, pigmented nodular non-healing ulcer on nose + telangiectasia + actinic keratosis = BCC (noduloulcerative type, pigmented, in sun-exposed area).

PRS·MCQ044 2022 MCQ25 MCQ 2cm ulcer (noduloulcerative?) on nose, telangiectasia, fisherman, full of actinic keratosis on face…

2cm ulcer (noduloulcerative?) on nose, telangiectasia, fisherman, full of actinic keratosis on face…

  1. A Resection with 1cm margin with full thickness skin flap
  2. B Resection with 3mm margin with bilobed flap
  3. C RT
  4. D Cream
💡 查看答案
B

2cm BCC on nose → resection with 3mm margin + bilobed flap (local flap reconstruction for nasal defect — provides good cosmetic outcome). 1cm margin is excessive for BCC (A wrong).

PRS·EMQ001 2002 EMQ1 EMQ SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS LESIONS

Theme: SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS LESIONS

Options
  1. A Dercum’s disease
  2. B Neurofibromatosis
  3. C Sebaceous cyst
  4. D Haemangioma
  5. E Pigmented naevi
  6. F Lipomas
  7. G Basal cell carcinoma
  8. H Marjolin’s ulcer
  9. I Melanoma
  10. J Varicose ulcer

For each of the case scenario below, choose the single MOST likely lesion from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 40 years old gentleman developed a lump on his right forearm which was very soft in consistency and can be reduced in size by compression.
  2. Q2. A 35 years old lady presents with inflammatory thickening of subcutaneous fat on her body which was painful.
  3. Q3. A 65 years old lady who has scars on her lower limb following history of burns has developed non-healing ulcer at the site of previous burns.
PRS·EMQ002 2004 EMQ1 EMQ PIGMENTED SKIN LESION

Theme: PIGMENTED SKIN LESION

Options
  1. A Congenital melanocytic naevus
  2. B Melanoma
  3. C Seborrhoeic keratosis
  4. D Basal cell carcinoma
  5. E Post-inflammatory pigmentation
  6. F Keloid
  7. G Freckles
  8. H Lentigo
  9. I Naevus of Ota
  10. J Melaesma

For each of the patients who has a pigmented lesion of the skin, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 68 years old gentleman complained of a firm, brownish elevated lesion of 1.5 × 1 cm in size. It was elliptical in shape and had a regular border. The surface was waxy with a stuck-on appearance. He had it for one year and it had been slowly growing in size.
  2. Q2. An 18 years old lady complained of a hemi-spherical, hard and brownish nodular lesion of 0.8 cm in size at the back of her left ear-lobule for six months. She gave the history that she had ear piercing about eight months ago and it was followed by infection for one week.
  3. Q3. A 45 years old gentleman complained of a deeply pigmented lesion of 1.5 cm in size in the anterior left thigh for three months. It grew particularly fast in the recent three weeks. It was nodular with some surface ulceration. There were multiple firm groin lymph nodes of 2 to 3 cm in size.
PRS·EMQ003 2007 EMQ5 EMQ CUTANEOUS LESIONS

Theme: CUTANEOUS LESIONS

Options
  1. A Seborrhoeic keratosis
  2. B Pilomatrixoma
  3. C Syringoma
  4. D Verruca vulgaris
  5. E Sebaceous naevus
  6. F Keratoacanthoma
  7. G Sebaceous cyst
  8. H Solar keratosis
  9. I Basal cell carcinoma
  10. J Squamous cell carcinoma
Questions
  1. Q1. Flower-like papillomatous hyperplasia of epidermis and hyperkeratosis. Prominent granular cell layer. Eosinophilic viral inclusions seen.
  2. Q2. Epidermal endophytic proliferation under prominent hyperparakeratosis with central keratotic core.
  3. Q3. The biopsy composed of basaloid cells with a mixture of squamous cells, keratin-filled invaginations and horn cysts are seen.
PRS·EMQ004 2012 EMQ3, 2018 EMQ7 EMQ SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS LESIONS

Theme: SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS LESIONS

Options
  1. A Melanoma
  2. B Osteoma
  3. C Lipoma
  4. D Neurofibroma
  5. E Dermoid
  6. F Implantation dermoid
  7. G Dermatofibroma
  8. H Sebaceous cyst
  9. I Ganglion
  10. J Osteoid
Questions
  1. Q1. 35/M presented with a pigmented lesion underneath his nail of his 3rd finger, with a positive lymph node in his axilla.
  2. Q2. A 45 years old housewife presented with a lump on the right middle finger. There was a scar on top. She reported a history of laceration requiring suturing procedure done at that site at the Accident and Emergency Department years ago.
  3. Q3. 37 years old gentleman, mass in middle forehead for many years, hard in consistency, not attached to skin.
PRS·EMQ005 2021 EMQ2 EMQ A. Skin graft

Theme: A. Skin graft

Options
  1. B Pedicled TRAM flap
  2. C Pedicled latissimus dorsi flap
  3. D Pedicled pec major flap
  4. E Free anterolateral thigh
  5. F Free fibular osteocutaneous flap
  6. G Free posterior tibial flap
  7. H Free radial forearm flap
  8. I Free jejunal flap
  9. J Free vertical rectus abdominus flap
Questions
  1. Q1. 2cm alveolar ulcer with invasion to anterior floor of mouth and gingiva + neck LN.
  2. Q2. A lot of comorbidities e.g. DM, CAD with stenting done 2 months ago, 4cm left non healing ulcer of the tongue with left LN → CA tongue.
  3. Q3. CA hypopharynx retrocricoid tumour. Superiorly spread to cricopharyngeus. Laterally involve thyroid cartilage + LN.
PRS·EMQ006 2022 EMQ3 EMQ SKIN LESION

Theme: SKIN LESION

Options
  1. A Dermatofibroma
  2. B Ganglion
  3. C Haemangioma
  4. D Implantation dermoid
  5. E Keloid
  6. F Neurofibroma
  7. G Lipoma
  8. H Osteoma
  9. I Sebaceous cyst
Questions
  1. Q1. Middle aged male. Midline mass on forehead for years. Hard, non-tender, immobile, not fixed to skin.
  2. Q2. 30/F previous right finger surgery, soft to firm erythematous mass over finger. Overlying scar. This time attend A&E for laceration, after 2 years.
  3. Q3. 70/M, 10-year history of 6cm lump in right buttock. No previous infection or discharge. No previous trauma. Lump is soft, mobile, non-tender, attached to skin.
SECTION 15

Urology

88 MCQ · 12 EMQ
URO·MCQ001 2001 MCQ16 MCQ A 36 years old gentleman with recurrent ureteric stone was found to have an elevated serum calcium of 2.72 mmol/L (N: 2.11–2.55 mmol/L). His parathyroid hormone…

A 36 years old gentleman with recurrent ureteric stone was found to have an elevated serum calcium of 2.72 mmol/L (N: 2.11–2.55 mmol/L). His parathyroid hormone was 50 (N: 11–54 pmol/L). 24 hours urinary calcium was 6.4 (N: 2.4–7.2 mmol/day). What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Benign familial hypocalciuric hypercalcaemia
  2. B Malignancy
  3. C Primary hyperparathyroidism
  4. D Dehydration
  5. E Renal tubular acidosis
💡 查看答案
A

Elevated Ca (2.72), PTH normal (50, within range), low urinary Ca → benign familial hypocalciuric hypercalcaemia (BFHH). In primary hyperPTH, PTH would be elevated.

URO·MCQ002 2001 MCQ27 MCQ A 16 years old boy attended the Emergency Room with a sudden onset of painful scrotum. He had no history of trauma, dysuria or urinary frequency. Physical exami…

A 16 years old boy attended the Emergency Room with a sudden onset of painful scrotum. He had no history of trauma, dysuria or urinary frequency. Physical examination showed the left testis to be located higher than the right and was tender on palpation. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Epididymitis
  2. B Orchitis
  3. C Torsion of testis
  4. D Torsion of the appendices of the testis
  5. E Testicular tumour
💡 查看答案
C

16/M, sudden painful scrotum, no trauma/dysuria, high-riding testis = testicular torsion (surgical emergency — explore within 6h).

URO·MCQ003 2001 MCQ42 MCQ The following are treatment options for metastatic prostate cancer EXCEPT:

The following are treatment options for metastatic prostate cancer EXCEPT:

  1. A Bilateral orchidectomy
  2. B Diethylstilbesterol
  3. C Flutamide
  4. D LHRH agonist
  5. E Terazosin
💡 查看答案
E

Terazosin (alpha-blocker for BPH) is NOT used for metastatic prostate cancer. Bilateral orchidectomy, DES, flutamide, and LHRH agonist are all hormonal treatments.

URO·MCQ004 2002 MCQ10 MCQ The MOST effective medications to reduce irritative lower urinary tract symptoms include:

The MOST effective medications to reduce irritative lower urinary tract symptoms include:

  1. A Alpha blockers
  2. B Beta blockers
  3. C Non-steroidal anti-inflammatory drug
  4. D Anticholinergics
  5. E Antipsychotics
💡 查看答案
D

Anticholinergics (oxybutynin, tolterodine) are most effective for irritative/storage LUTS (urgency, frequency). Alpha-blockers for obstructive symptoms.

URO·MCQ005 2002 MCQ22 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a causative organism in acute epididymo-orchitis?

Which of the following is NOT a causative organism in acute epididymo-orchitis?

  1. A HIV
  2. B Chlamydia trachomatis
  3. C E. coli
  4. D Neisseria gonorrhoeae
  5. E Human papilloma virus
💡 查看答案
E

HPV causes genital warts/condylomata, NOT epididymo-orchitis. Chlamydia, E. coli, gonorrhoea are causative organisms. HIV (A) is debatable but not classic.

URO·MCQ006 2002 MCQ41 MCQ Which PSA value (in ng/ml) below is used as a THRESHOLD, above which bony metastasis is LIKELY in a patient diagnosed with carcinoma of the prostate?

Which PSA value (in ng/ml) below is used as a THRESHOLD, above which bony metastasis is LIKELY in a patient diagnosed with carcinoma of the prostate?

  1. A 5
  2. B 10
  3. C 15
  4. D 20
  5. E 25
💡 查看答案
D

PSA >20 ng/mL is the threshold above which bony metastasis is LIKELY in prostate cancer (bone scan recommended when PSA >20).

URO·MCQ007 2002 MCQ58 MCQ Which group of drug below is the MOST useful in treating metastatic carcinoma of the prostate?

Which group of drug below is the MOST useful in treating metastatic carcinoma of the prostate?

  1. A Androgen
  2. B LHRH agonist
  3. C Progesterone analogue
  4. D Anti-oestrogen
  5. E 5 alpha reductase inhibitor
💡 查看答案
B

LHRH agonist is the most useful drug group for treating metastatic prostate cancer (medical castration — suppresses testosterone).

URO·MCQ008 2002 MCQ83 MCQ Which group of patients below is the MOST LIKELY to require prophylactic steroid cover to avoid anaphylactic reaction during an IVU?

Which group of patients below is the MOST LIKELY to require prophylactic steroid cover to avoid anaphylactic reaction during an IVU?

  1. A Non insulin dependent diabetic
  2. B Immunocompromised
  3. C Thyrotoxic
  4. D Hay fever
  5. E Epileptic
💡 查看答案
D

Patients with hay fever (atopic history) are most likely to need prophylactic steroid cover for IVU contrast (allergic risk to iodinated contrast).

URO·MCQ009 2002# MCQ18, 2005 MCQ20, 2006 MCQ58 MCQ A 72 years old gentleman with a history of congestive heart failure, peripheral oedema, and an enlarged prostate complains of nocturia every 1–2 hours. He denie…

A 72 years old gentleman with a history of congestive heart failure, peripheral oedema, and an enlarged prostate complains of nocturia every 1–2 hours. He denies frequency, hesitancy, diminished force of stream or sensation of incomplete emptying. The FIRST step in his evaluation should be:

  1. A Cystometrogram
  2. B Cystoscopy
  3. C Flow rate
  4. D Trial of anticholinergic medication
  5. E Voiding diary
💡 查看答案
E

72/M, CHF with oedema + nocturia every 1-2h but no daytime LUTS → likely cardiac nocturia (not prostatic). Voiding diary will confirm pattern and differentiate cardiac vs prostatic nocturia.

URO·MCQ010 2002# MCQ19 MCQ Impaired detrusor contractility or loss of detrusor contractility in men are BEST managed by:

Impaired detrusor contractility or loss of detrusor contractility in men are BEST managed by:

  1. A Artificial urinary sphincter implantation
  2. B Behavioural therapy or toileting programs
  3. C Clean intermittent catheterization
  4. D Sympathomimetic medications
  5. E Transurethral prostatectomy
💡 查看答案
C

Impaired/absent detrusor contractility → clean intermittent catheterization (CIC) is the best management (drains bladder without surgery, maintains urethral health).

URO·MCQ011 2002# MCQ36 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a common presentation of renal cell carcinoma?

Which of the following is NOT a common presentation of renal cell carcinoma?

  1. A Gross haematuria
  2. B Hypercalcaemia
  3. C Hypertension
  4. D Proteinuria
  5. E Deranged liver function
💡 查看答案
D

Proteinuria is NOT a common presentation of RCC. Haematuria, hypercalcaemia (paraneoplastic PTHrP), hypertension (renin secretion), and deranged LFT (Stauffer's syndrome) are common presentations.

URO·MCQ012 2002# MCQ38 MCQ The use of Sildenafil is contraindicated in men with the following conditions EXCEPT:

The use of Sildenafil is contraindicated in men with the following conditions EXCEPT:

  1. A Unstable angina
  2. B Uncontrolled hypertension
  3. C Congestive heart failure
  4. D Diabetic retinopathy
  5. E Recent CVA
💡 查看答案
D

Diabetic retinopathy is NOT a contraindication for sildenafil. Unstable angina, uncontrolled HTN, CHF, and recent CVA are contraindications. (Main CI is concurrent nitrate use.)

URO·MCQ013 2002# MCQ78 MCQ Uric acid stones:

Uric acid stones:

  1. A Appear as radio-lucent calculi on spiral CT
  2. B Can be caused by excessive vitamin A ingestion
  3. C Can be dissolved by alkalinization of urine
  4. D Account for 30% of all urinary tract stones
  5. E On microscopy of urine appear as hexagonal crystals
💡 查看答案
C

Uric acid stones CAN be dissolved by alkalinizing urine (sodium bicarbonate/potassium citrate to pH >6.5). They are radiolucent on XR but visible on CT (A wrong). Account for ~5-10% (D wrong). Hexagonal crystals = cystine (E wrong).

URO·MCQ014 2003 MCQ9 MCQ Which kind of urinary stone is associated with urinary tract infection caused by Klebsiella?

Which kind of urinary stone is associated with urinary tract infection caused by Klebsiella?

  1. A Calcium oxalate
  2. B Calcium phosphate
  3. C Cysteine
  4. D Struvite
  5. E Uric acid
💡 查看答案
D

Struvite (magnesium ammonium phosphate) stones are associated with urease-producing organisms like Klebsiella, Proteus. 'Infection stones.'

URO·MCQ015 2003 MCQ12 MCQ A 10 years old boy presents with sudden onset of severe left sided scrotal pain. The left testis is swollen and tender. The right testis lies transversely. What…

A 10 years old boy presents with sudden onset of severe left sided scrotal pain. The left testis is swollen and tender. The right testis lies transversely. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Urinary tract infection
  2. B Testicular torsion
  3. C Ureteric stone
  4. D Orchitis
  5. E Seminoma
💡 查看答案
B

10/M, sudden severe scrotal pain, swollen tender testis, contralateral testis lies transversely (bell-clapper deformity) = testicular torsion.

URO·MCQ016 2003 MCQ25 MCQ Which of the following is NOT premalignant condition of carcinoma of penis?

Which of the following is NOT premalignant condition of carcinoma of penis?

  1. A Balanitis xerotica obliterans
  2. B Bowen’s disease
  3. C Erythroplasia of Queyrat
  4. D Leukoplakia
  5. E Lymphogranuloma venereum
💡 查看答案
E

Lymphogranuloma venereum is NOT a premalignant condition of penile cancer. BXO, Bowen's, erythroplasia of Queyrat, and leukoplakia are premalignant.

URO·MCQ017 2003 MCQ40 MCQ What is the COMMONEST site of occurrence of prostate cancer?

What is the COMMONEST site of occurrence of prostate cancer?

  1. A Anterior fibromuscular region
  2. B Central zone
  3. C Paraurethral zone
  4. D Peripheral zone
  5. E Transitional zone
💡 查看答案
D

Prostate cancer most commonly arises from the peripheral zone (~70% of cases). BPH arises from transitional zone.

URO·MCQ018 2003 MCQ58 MCQ What is the RECOMMENDED treatment for a 60 years old gentleman having T2N0M0 transitional cell carcinoma of bladder?

What is the RECOMMENDED treatment for a 60 years old gentleman having T2N0M0 transitional cell carcinoma of bladder?

  1. A Radical cystectomy
  2. B Radical cystectomy + postoperative radiotherapy
  3. C Radical cystectomy + postoperative systemic chemotherapy
  4. D Transurethral resection
  5. E Transurethral resection + intravesical chemotherapy
💡 查看答案
A

T2N0M0 TCC bladder (muscle-invasive) → radical cystectomy is the recommended treatment. TUR alone is insufficient for muscle-invasive disease.

URO·MCQ019 2003 MCQ74 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a feature of TUR syndrome?

Which of the following is NOT a feature of TUR syndrome?

  1. A Cerebral oedema
  2. B Hypertension
  3. C Hyponatraemia
  4. D Perioral numbness
  5. E Thrombocytopenia
💡 查看答案
E

Thrombocytopenia is NOT a feature of TUR syndrome. TUR syndrome: dilutional hyponatraemia (from glycine absorption) → cerebral oedema, hypertension, perioral numbness, visual disturbance, confusion.

URO·MCQ020 2003 MCQ82 MCQ A patient having spinal cord injury at the level of C6/7. Which of the following is NOT typically seen on video-urodynamic study one year post-injury?

A patient having spinal cord injury at the level of C6/7. Which of the following is NOT typically seen on video-urodynamic study one year post-injury?

  1. A Atonic bladder
  2. B Autonomic dysreflexia
  3. C Detrusor hyperreflexia
  4. D Detrusor-sphincter dyssynergia
  5. E Vesico-ureteric reflux
💡 查看答案
A

Atonic bladder is NOT expected 1 year post-C6/7 cord injury — by then, the bladder develops detrusor hyperreflexia (UMN bladder). Atonic bladder is seen in spinal shock phase (acute) or with LMN lesions.

URO·MCQ021 2004 MCQ24 MCQ A previously well 10 years old boy presented to the Accident & Emergency Department with a two-hour history of severe pain in the right scrotum. He had been pla…

A previously well 10 years old boy presented to the Accident & Emergency Department with a two-hour history of severe pain in the right scrotum. He had been playing football earlier in the morning but there was no history of trauma, Physical examination showed a red and swollen scrotum. The right testis was higher lying than the left and extremely tender. The MOST likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Scrotal abscess
  2. B Testicular torsion
  3. C Epididymitis
  4. D Orchitis
  5. E Strangulated inguinal hernia
💡 查看答案
B

10/M, 2h severe scrotal pain after football = testicular torsion (even though history of football, the acute onset and clinical findings favour torsion over trauma-related injury).

URO·MCQ022 2004 MCQ46 MCQ The curative treatment for renal cell carcinoma is:

The curative treatment for renal cell carcinoma is:

  1. A Chemotherapy
  2. B Immunotherapy
  3. C Phytotherapy
  4. D Radiotherapy
  5. E Surgery
💡 查看答案
E

Surgery (radical/partial nephrectomy) is the only curative treatment for renal cell carcinoma. RCC is resistant to chemo and RT.

URO·MCQ023 2004 MCQ55 MCQ The haematuria workup does NOT include:

The haematuria workup does NOT include:

  1. A Cystoscopy
  2. B CT scan of kidney
  3. C IVU
  4. D KUB
  5. E Urine cytology
💡 查看答案
D

KUB (plain abdominal X-ray) is NOT essential in haematuria workup — it has low sensitivity. Cystoscopy, CT scan, IVU, and urine cytology are standard components.

URO·MCQ024 2004 MCQ74 MCQ Which of the following is a LEAST likely complication of right side percutaneous nephrostomy tube insertion?

Which of the following is a LEAST likely complication of right side percutaneous nephrostomy tube insertion?

  1. A Bleeding
  2. B Colonic perforation
  3. C Pneumothorax
  4. D Ruptured liver
  5. E Ruptured spleen
💡 查看答案
E

Ruptured spleen is the LEAST likely complication of RIGHT-sided percutaneous nephrostomy (spleen is on the LEFT). Bleeding, colonic perforation, pneumothorax, and liver injury (right-sided) are possible.

URO·MCQ025 2004 MCQ82 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a complication of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)?

Which of the following is NOT a complication of benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH)?

  1. A Acute urinary retention
  2. B Bladder stone formation
  3. C Prostate cancer
  4. D Renal impairment
  5. E Urinary tract infection
💡 查看答案
C

Prostate cancer is NOT a complication of BPH (they are independent conditions that co-exist). Acute retention, bladder stones, renal impairment, and UTI are BPH complications.

URO·MCQ026 2004 MCQ88 MCQ Which of the following is an absolute contraindication for circumcision?

Which of the following is an absolute contraindication for circumcision?

  1. A Hypospadias
  2. B Paraphimosis
  3. C Old age
  4. D Balanitis
  5. E Urinary tract infection
💡 查看答案
A

Hypospadias is an absolute contraindication for circumcision (foreskin needed for reconstruction/urethroplasty).

URO·MCQ027 2005 MCQ10 MCQ A history of the following surgical procedures would commonly associated with decreased male fertility EXCEPT:

A history of the following surgical procedures would commonly associated with decreased male fertility EXCEPT:

  1. A Bladder neck, V-Y plasty
  2. B Unilateral orchidectomy
  3. C Retroperitoneal lymph node dissection
  4. D Ureteral reimplantation
  5. E Transurethral resection of prostate
💡 查看答案
D

Ureteral reimplantation does NOT commonly affect male fertility. Bladder neck V-Y plasty (retrograde ejaculation), orchidectomy, RPLND (retrograde ejaculation), and TURP (retrograde ejaculation) all decrease fertility.

URO·MCQ028 2005 MCQ17 MCQ The optimal time for visualizing the renal parenchyma during urography is at:

The optimal time for visualizing the renal parenchyma during urography is at:

  1. A 5 minutes postinjection
  2. B 10 minutes postinjection
  3. C 8 minutes postinjection
  4. D Immediately following completion of contrast injection
  5. E 30 minutes postinjection
💡 查看答案
D

Nephrogram phase (immediately after contrast injection) best shows renal parenchyma on urography.

URO·MCQ029 2005 MCQ47 MCQ The FIRST sign of TUR syndrome is:

The FIRST sign of TUR syndrome is:

  1. A Elevated CVP
  2. B Changes in sensorium
  3. C Bradycardia
  4. D Tachypnoea
  5. E Drop in blood pressure
💡 查看答案
B

Changes in sensorium (confusion, restlessness) is the FIRST sign of TUR syndrome (cerebral oedema from hyponatraemia).

URO·MCQ030 2005 MCQ55 MCQ A 68 years old man presents with microscopic haematuria. The MOST LIKELY etiology of hematuria in the patient is

A 68 years old man presents with microscopic haematuria. The MOST LIKELY etiology of hematuria in the patient is

  1. A Kidney stones
  2. B BPH
  3. C UTI
  4. D Bladder tumour
  5. E Glomerulonephritis
💡 查看答案
B

68/M with microscopic haematuria → most likely BPH (most common cause in elderly male). However, must rule out bladder tumour.

URO·MCQ031 2005 MCQ83 MCQ Which of the following statements pertaining to varicoceles is FALSE?

Which of the following statements pertaining to varicoceles is FALSE?

  1. A Varicoceles occur in 15 to 20% of normal males
  2. B Approximately 40% of subfertile men have varicoceles
  3. C Not all clinically identifiable varicoceles should be repaired
  4. D The right internal spermatic vein is 8 to 10 cm longer than the left
  5. E Varicocele repair is associated with improved semen quality in some patients
💡 查看答案
D

RIGHT internal spermatic vein is 8-10cm SHORTER than left (not longer). Left is longer, draining into left renal vein (right drains directly into IVC). All other statements about varicoceles are correct.

URO·MCQ032 2006 MCQ18 MCQ A 40 years old male complains of red ejaculation for 2 months. The most likely diagnosis is:

A 40 years old male complains of red ejaculation for 2 months. The most likely diagnosis is:

  1. A Idiopathic
  2. B CA prostate
  3. C Prostate cyst
  4. D TB seminal vesicles
  5. E UTI
💡 查看答案
A

40/M, red (bloody) ejaculation ×2 months → haematospermia, most commonly idiopathic (~70% of cases, especially in men <40).

URO·MCQ033 2006 MCQ19 MCQ Regarding bilateral maldescended testes, the following are true except:

Regarding bilateral maldescended testes, the following are true except:

  1. A Impaired testicular function
  2. B Infertility
  3. C Priapism
  4. D Increased risk of malignancy
  5. E Prone to torsion
💡 查看答案
C

Priapism is NOT associated with bilateral maldescended testes. Impaired testicular function, infertility, increased malignancy risk, and torsion are associated.

URO·MCQ034 2006 MCQ41 MCQ Which of the following is incorrect regarding testicular torsion?

Which of the following is incorrect regarding testicular torsion?

  1. A Lifting and supporting testis will not relieve pain and may worsen
  2. B Epididymis is palpable separately
  3. C Affected testis high lying
  4. D Opposite testis may have abnormal lie
💡 查看答案
B

In testicular torsion, epididymis is NOT palpable separately (testis and epididymis form a single tender mass due to twisting). Lifting doesn't relieve pain (A true). High-riding (C true). Opposite abnormal lie (D true — bell-clapper).

URO·MCQ035 2006 MCQ59 MCQ Which of the following is not a protective factor for prostate cancer?

Which of the following is not a protective factor for prostate cancer?

  1. A Liver cirrhosis
  2. B Enough vitamin D intake
  3. C Low yellow/green leaves intake
  4. D Rural dweller
  5. E Increased vitamin E intake
💡 查看答案
C

Low yellow/green leaf intake is NOT protective — it's a RISK factor (green vegetables are protective through phytoestrogens). Liver cirrhosis (low testosterone), vitamin D, rural dwelling, and vitamin E are protective/associated with lower risk.

URO·MCQ036 2007 MCQ5 MCQ 30/M. History of primary infertility. Semen analysis: azoospermia. P/E: normal sized testes, bilateral vas deferens were not palpable. The associated inherited …

30/M. History of primary infertility. Semen analysis: azoospermia. P/E: normal sized testes, bilateral vas deferens were not palpable. The associated inherited disorder is:

  1. A Adult polycystic kidney
  2. B Cystic fibrosis
  3. C Down’s syndrome
  4. D Tuberous sclerosis
  5. E Turner’s syndrome
💡 查看答案
B

30/M, azoospermia, normal testes, bilateral absent vas deferens = cystic fibrosis (CF causes congenital bilateral absence of vas deferens — CBAVD).

URO·MCQ037 2007 MCQ19 MCQ Which is not an indication for transrectal prostate biopsy?

Which is not an indication for transrectal prostate biopsy?

  1. A PSA 5 ng/ml
  2. B Prostate nodule
  3. C Previous biopsy shows atypical hyperplasia
  4. D Previous biopsy shows prostate intraepithelial neoplasia
  5. E TURP specimen found 5% adenocarcinoma
💡 查看答案
E

TURP specimen showing 5% adenocarcinoma is NOT an indication for REPEAT transrectal biopsy (already have diagnosis — need staging and treatment planning, not more biopsies). PSA 5 (A), prostate nodule (B), atypical hyperplasia (C), and PIN (D) are all indications for biopsy.

URO·MCQ038 2009 MCQ19 MCQ A 20 years old gentleman presented with acute onset of severe left scrotal pain starting 4 hours ago. What would be the MOST APPROPRIATE next step management?

A 20 years old gentleman presented with acute onset of severe left scrotal pain starting 4 hours ago. What would be the MOST APPROPRIATE next step management?

  1. A Analgesics and ice pack to scrotum
  2. B Antibiotics therapy
  3. C Emergency scrotal exploration
  4. D Urgent radionuclide scan of testis
  5. E Urgent USG of scrotum
💡 查看答案
C

20/M, acute scrotal pain ×4h → emergency scrotal exploration (suspected testicular torsion — don't delay with imaging, explore within 6h to save testis).

URO·MCQ039 2009 MCQ20 MCQ A 30 years old man presents to the clinical complaining of primary infertility. On examination, there was tall stature, bilateral gynecomastia, and small testes…

A 30 years old man presents to the clinical complaining of primary infertility. On examination, there was tall stature, bilateral gynecomastia, and small testes. Initial blood investigations showed low FSH. What is the MOST LIKELY diagnosis?

  1. A Bilateral congenital absence of vas deferens
  2. B Kallmann syndrome
  3. C Kartagener syndrome
  4. D Klinefelter syndrome
  5. E Sertoli cell only syndrome
💡 查看答案
D

Tall stature + bilateral gynaecomastia + small testes + low testosterone + raised FSH/LH = Klinefelter syndrome (47,XXY).

URO·MCQ040 2009 MCQ21 MCQ A 50 years old lady suffered from recurrent left renal stone. Her urine culture repeatedly grew Proteus mirabilis. The stone is MOST LIKELY composed of:

A 50 years old lady suffered from recurrent left renal stone. Her urine culture repeatedly grew Proteus mirabilis. The stone is MOST LIKELY composed of:

  1. A Ammonium acid urate
  2. B Calcium oxalate
  3. C Calcium phosphate
  4. D Cystine
  5. E Magnesium ammonium phosphate
💡 查看答案
E

Recurrent stones + Proteus mirabilis (urease-producer) → magnesium ammonium phosphate (struvite) stone.

URO·MCQ041 2009 MCQ22, 2010 MCQ7 MCQ A 30 years old man sustained a straddle injury. On examination, there was gross perineal bruising with a palpable bladder. He was haemodynamically stable. What …

A 30 years old man sustained a straddle injury. On examination, there was gross perineal bruising with a palpable bladder. He was haemodynamically stable. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Alpha blocker
  2. B Analgesics and observe
  3. C Antibiotics
  4. D Suprapubic catheterization
  5. E Urethral catheterization with care
💡 查看答案
D

Straddle injury + perineal bruising + palpable bladder + stable = urethral injury with retention → suprapubic catheterization (do NOT attempt urethral catheterization with suspected urethral disruption).

URO·MCQ042 2010 MCQ77, 2017 MCQ70, 2019 MCQ??, 2020 MCQ13 MCQ A 40 years old gentleman presented with one day’s history of fever and left loin pain. Urgent imaging showed a left upper ureteric stone causing moderate left h…

A 40 years old gentleman presented with one day’s history of fever and left loin pain. Urgent imaging showed a left upper ureteric stone causing moderate left hydronephrosis. Fever did not subside with antibiotics. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE treatment?

  1. A Change antibiotics
  2. B Observation
  3. C Percutaneous nephrolithotomy
  4. D Retrograde JJ stent insertion
  5. E Ureteroscopic lithotripsy
💡 查看答案
D

Obstructing ureteric stone + fever not responding to antibiotics = infected obstructed kidney → urgent decompression with retrograde JJ stent (or percutaneous nephrostomy).

URO·MCQ043 2010 MCQ78 MCQ 38/F with staghorn renal stone. What is most likely organism?

38/F with staghorn renal stone. What is most likely organism?

  1. A Clostridium difficile
  2. B Mycobacterium avium
  3. C Neisseria gonorrhoeae
  4. D Enterococcus spp.
  5. E Proteus mirabilis
💡 查看答案
E

38/F with staghorn stone → Proteus mirabilis (urease-producing organism → alkaline urine → struvite/staghorn stone formation).

URO·MCQ044 2010 MCQ79 MCQ A 26 years old motorcyclist presented to the AED due to road traffic accident. He had a fracture neck of pelvis and acute urinary retention. On physical examina…

A 26 years old motorcyclist presented to the AED due to road traffic accident. He had a fracture neck of pelvis and acute urinary retention. On physical examination, there was blood at the external meatus. Bladder was distended up to the umbilicus level. The prostate was not palpable on digital rectal examination. He was haemodynamically stable after initial resuscitation. Which of the following is the most appropriate urological intervention at this moment?

  1. A Cystoscopy and urethral realignment
  2. B Suprapubic catheterization
  3. C Surgical exploration
  4. D Urethral bouginage
  5. E Urethral catheterization
💡 查看答案
B

Pelvic fracture + urinary retention + blood at meatus → suspected posterior urethral injury → suprapubic catheterization (DO NOT attempt urethral catheterization).

URO·MCQ045 2010 MCQ80 MCQ A man was diagnosed with urothelial carcinoma of the bladder. On cystoscopy, 2 cm papillary tumour was identified on lateral wall of the bladder. After the TURB…

A man was diagnosed with urothelial carcinoma of the bladder. On cystoscopy, 2 cm papillary tumour was identified on lateral wall of the bladder. After the TURBT, the specimen showed high-grade non muscle invasive papillary tumour, and carcinoma in situ was also found. What is the most appropriate management now?

  1. A Observe / conservative
  2. B Intravesical chemotherapy
  3. C Intravesical interferon
  4. D Intravesical BCG
  5. E Radical cystoprostatectomy
💡 查看答案
D

High-grade non-muscle-invasive TCC (pT1 high grade) after TURBT → intravesical BCG (gold standard for high-grade NMIBC — reduces recurrence and progression).

URO·MCQ046 2010 MCQ82, 2017 MCQ70 MCQ A 48 years old lady with bilateral renal angiomyolipoma. She presented with acute abdominal pain, CT scan showed retroperitoneal haematoma. Which of the followi…

A 48 years old lady with bilateral renal angiomyolipoma. She presented with acute abdominal pain, CT scan showed retroperitoneal haematoma. Which of the following is the most appropriate?

  1. A Observation
  2. B Partial nephrectomy
  3. C Renal angiogram and embolization
  4. D Renal transplant
  5. E Total nephrectomy
💡 查看答案
C

48/F, bilateral renal AML + acute pain + retroperitoneal haematoma = ruptured AML → renal angiogram and embolization (selective embolization to control bleeding while preserving renal function).

URO·MCQ047 2011 MCQ58, 2012 MCQ26, 2016 MCQ43 MCQ A 40 years old lady complained of anorexia and fatigue. Her serum creatinine was 300 µmol/L. CT showed severe bilateral hydronephrosis and retroperitoneal soft …

A 40 years old lady complained of anorexia and fatigue. Her serum creatinine was 300 µmol/L. CT showed severe bilateral hydronephrosis and retroperitoneal soft tissue mass encasing the great vessels and ureters. Which of the following are the most appropriate acute management?

  1. A Corticosteroid
  2. B CT-guided biopsy of mass
  3. C Open surgical biopsy of mass
  4. D Placement of bilateral ureteric shunt
  5. E Ureterolysis
💡 查看答案
D

Bilateral hydronephrosis + retroperitoneal mass encasing great vessels = retroperitoneal fibrosis → placement of bilateral ureteric stents first (relieve obstruction, protect kidneys). Then investigate/treat the cause.

URO·MCQ048 2011 MCQ59, 2019 MCQ16 MCQ What is the most common cancer after kidney transplantation?

What is the most common cancer after kidney transplantation?

  1. A Breast
  2. B Cervix
  3. C Kaposi sarcoma
  4. D Skin
  5. E Thyroid
💡 查看答案
D

Skin cancer is the most common malignancy after kidney transplantation (SCC > BCC, due to chronic immunosuppression).

URO·MCQ049 2011 MCQ60 MCQ 70/M. History of stroke, otherwise good past health. He presented with straining, intermittency, frequency and urgency. What is the most appropriate next step m…

70/M. History of stroke, otherwise good past health. He presented with straining, intermittency, frequency and urgency. What is the most appropriate next step management?

  1. A Cholinergic blocker
  2. B Clean intermittent catheterization
  3. C Full uroflowmetry investigation
  4. D Transurethral incision of bladder and prostate
  5. E TURP
💡 查看答案
C

70/M, stroke history + LUTS (straining, intermittency, frequency, urgency) → full uroflowmetry investigation first (need to differentiate BPH obstruction from neurogenic bladder from stroke before treatment).

URO·MCQ050 2011 MCQ62, 2012 MCQ27, 2015 MCQ75, 2020 MCQ50 MCQ Which of the following is an ABSOLUTE indication for transurethral resection of prostate (TURP)?

Which of the following is an ABSOLUTE indication for transurethral resection of prostate (TURP)?

  1. A International Prostatism Symptom Score of 22 or above
  2. B Obstructive uropathy
  3. C Patient developed hypotension with Terazosin
  4. D Post-void residual urine volume of 200 ml
  5. E Severe disturbing nocturia affecting the quality of life
💡 查看答案
B

Obstructive uropathy (bilateral hydronephrosis/renal impairment from BPH) is an ABSOLUTE indication for TURP.

URO·MCQ051 2011 MCQ?? MCQ What is the most accurate indicator of CA prostate control?

What is the most accurate indicator of CA prostate control?

  1. A Long PSA doubling time
  2. B No nodule on DRE
  3. C No nodule on TRUS
  4. D No histology change on TRUS biopsy
💡 查看答案
A

Long PSA doubling time indicates slow disease progression = good control of prostate cancer.

URO·MCQ052 2012 MCQ23 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a cause of obstructive azoospermia?

Which of the following is NOT a cause of obstructive azoospermia?

  1. A Congenital absence of vas deferens
  2. B Ejaculatory duct obstruction
  3. C Epididymitis
  4. D Radiotherapy
  5. E Vasectomy
💡 查看答案
D

Radiotherapy causes NON-obstructive azoospermia (damages spermatogenesis directly). CBAVD, ejaculatory duct obstruction, epididymitis, and vasectomy are all obstructive causes.

URO·MCQ053 2012 MCQ24, 2017 MCQ?? MCQ Pregnant woman 18th week of gestation suffered renal colic. Which one is the most appropriate investigation?

Pregnant woman 18th week of gestation suffered renal colic. Which one is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A KUB
  2. B Non contrast CT
  3. C CT urogram
  4. D IVU
  5. E MR urogram
💡 查看答案
E

Pregnant woman ×18 weeks with renal colic → MR urogram (no ionizing radiation, safe in pregnancy). CT and XR expose fetus to radiation.

URO·MCQ054 2012 MCQ25, 2018 MCQ56, 2020 MCQ81 MCQ A 78 years old man have CA prostate with multiple bone metastasis. Which of the following is the best treatment?

A 78 years old man have CA prostate with multiple bone metastasis. Which of the following is the best treatment?

  1. A Abiraterone
  2. B LHRH analogue
  3. C Oral anti-androgen only
  4. D Systemic targeted therapy
  5. E Taxol based chemotherapy
💡 查看答案
B

78/M, prostate CA + multiple bone mets → LHRH analogue (medical castration — standard first-line hormonal therapy for metastatic prostate cancer).

URO·MCQ055 2012 MCQ28, 2018 MCQ55, 2020 MCQ65 MCQ A 25 years old lady with multiple renal angiomyolipoma. This is associated with:

A 25 years old lady with multiple renal angiomyolipoma. This is associated with:

  1. A Von Hippel Lindau syndrome
  2. B Prune belly syndrome
  3. C Down syndrome
  4. D Von Recklinghausen syndrome
  5. E Tuberous sclerosis
💡 查看答案
E

25/F, multiple bilateral renal angiomyolipoma = tuberous sclerosis (TSC — bilateral AMLs pathognomonic). VHL is associated with RCC/haemangioblastoma.

URO·MCQ056 2013 MCQ7 MCQ What is the most common subtype of renal cell carcinoma?

What is the most common subtype of renal cell carcinoma?

  1. A Clear cell
💡 查看答案
A

Clear cell carcinoma is the most common subtype of RCC (~75-80%).

URO·MCQ057 2013 MCQ?? MCQ What is the most common type of testicular tumour?

What is the most common type of testicular tumour?

  1. A Choriocarcinoma
  2. B Teratoma
  3. C Seminoma
  4. D Yolk sac tumour
💡 查看答案
C

Seminoma is the most common type of testicular tumour (~40-50%).

URO·MCQ058 2014 MCQ68, 2015 MCQ76 MCQ An 80 years old gentleman with prostate cancer with multiple bone secondaries. He has multiple co-morbidities. What is the most suitable treatment?

An 80 years old gentleman with prostate cancer with multiple bone secondaries. He has multiple co-morbidities. What is the most suitable treatment?

  1. A Bilateral orchidectomy
  2. B Ketoconazole
  3. C LHRH analogue injection
  4. D Oral antiandrogen
  5. E Taxol-based chemotherapy
💡 查看答案
C

80/M, prostate CA + bone mets + multiple comorbidities → LHRH analogue injection (less invasive than bilateral orchidectomy for elderly comorbid patient). Monthly injection vs surgery.

URO·MCQ059 2014 MCQ70, 2015 MCQ77, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ A 60 years old male with LUTS is worried about CA prostate. PSA is 10ng/ml. DRE is normal. What is your next step of management?

A 60 years old male with LUTS is worried about CA prostate. PSA is 10ng/ml. DRE is normal. What is your next step of management?

  1. A Contrast CT of pelvis
  2. B MRI prostate with endorectal coil
  3. C PET whole body
  4. D Transrectal ultrasound of prostate
  5. E Transrectal ultrasound of prostate and random biopsy
💡 查看答案
E

PSA 10 + normal DRE → TRUS-guided systematic prostate biopsy (to confirm/exclude cancer). MRI increasingly used first (B), but TRUS biopsy is the traditional answer.

URO·MCQ060 2014 MCQ72, 2016 MCQ42 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman with a history of chronic smoking presents with gross painless haematuria. What is the most common cause of the disease?

A 60 years old gentleman with a history of chronic smoking presents with gross painless haematuria. What is the most common cause of the disease?

  1. A Benign prostatic hyperplasia
  2. B Bladder cancer
  3. C Infection
  4. D Renal calculus
  5. E Trauma
💡 查看答案
B

60/M smoker + painless gross haematuria → most common cause is bladder cancer (TCC — strong association with smoking).

URO·MCQ061 2014 MCQ74 MCQ A man with history of kidney stones now presented with haematuria and left sided loin pain. Initial investigation with plain radiograph did not reveal any abnor…

A man with history of kidney stones now presented with haematuria and left sided loin pain. Initial investigation with plain radiograph did not reveal any abnormality. Which of the following is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A Contrast CT
  2. B NCCT
  3. C DMSA
💡 查看答案
B

Kidney stone history + haematuria + loin pain + normal plain XR → non-contrast CT (NCCT) is the gold standard investigation for urinary stones (detects radiolucent stones that plain XR misses).

URO·MCQ062 2015 MCQ78, 2017 MCQ73 MCQ A 40 years old male, good past health, presented with colicky left loin pain. KUB showed 3.5 cm left partial staghorn stone. Confirmed with non-contrast CT. DTP…

A 40 years old male, good past health, presented with colicky left loin pain. KUB showed 3.5 cm left partial staghorn stone. Confirmed with non-contrast CT. DTPA left:right = 45:55. What is the best treatment?

  1. A ESWL
  2. B Nephrectomy
  3. C Left percutaneous nephrolithotomy
  4. D Left ureteroscopy
  5. E Observation with analgesics
💡 查看答案
C

3.5cm partial staghorn stone + normal split function (45:55) → left percutaneous nephrolithotomy (PCNL — gold standard for stones >2cm or staghorn).

URO·MCQ063 2015 MCQ86 MCQ A boy presenting with acute right testicular pain. Right scrotum is red, tender and swollen and is higher in the right side than the left. Initial blood investi…

A boy presenting with acute right testicular pain. Right scrotum is red, tender and swollen and is higher in the right side than the left. Initial blood investigations were all normal. Which of the following is the most appropriate action?

  1. A Emergency surgery to explore and repair
  2. B Angiography
💡 查看答案
A

Acute testicular pain + erythematous swollen tender high-riding testis → emergency surgical exploration and repair (testicular torsion until proven otherwise).

URO·MCQ064 2015 MCQ89 MCQ A 13 years old boy presented with acute right scrotal swelling and pain after jogging. Physical examination showed an erythematous, swollen and very tender righ…

A 13 years old boy presented with acute right scrotal swelling and pain after jogging. Physical examination showed an erythematous, swollen and very tender right testis. What is the most likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute epididymo-orchitis
  2. B Testicular torsion
  3. C Scrotal haematoma
  4. D Incarcerated inguinal hernia
  5. E Infected hydrocele
💡 查看答案
B

13/M, acute right scrotal swelling/pain after jogging + erythematous swollen tender testis = testicular torsion.

URO·MCQ065 2016 MCQ37, 2020 MCQ61 MCQ An 8 years old boy developed left scrotal pain for several hours after playing football game. On examination, the left scrotum was grossly swollen and the testi…

An 8 years old boy developed left scrotal pain for several hours after playing football game. On examination, the left scrotum was grossly swollen and the testis was tender. There were no urinary symptoms. Which would be the most appropriate management?

  1. A Antibiotics
  2. B Doppler ultrasound testis
  3. C Immediate surgical exploration
  4. D Analgesics
  5. E Observation
💡 查看答案
C

8/M, acute scrotal pain after football + grossly swollen tender testis → immediate surgical exploration (testicular torsion — don't waste time with imaging).

URO·MCQ066 2017 MCQ71 MCQ Chronic schistosomiasis of the bladder is predominantly associated with which type of bladder cancer?

Chronic schistosomiasis of the bladder is predominantly associated with which type of bladder cancer?

  1. A Adenocarcinoma
  2. B Giant cell carcinoma
  3. C Papillary carcinoma
  4. D Small cell carcinoma
  5. E Squamous cell carcinoma
💡 查看答案
E

Chronic schistosomiasis of bladder → squamous cell carcinoma (SCC). (Regular TCC/urothelial is more common overall, but schistosomiasis specifically causes SCC.)

URO·MCQ067 2017 MCQ?? MCQ A 69-year-old gentleman with good past health presents to the Accident and Emergency Department for the third episode acute retention of urine. He is all along …

A 69-year-old gentleman with good past health presents to the Accident and Emergency Department for the third episode acute retention of urine. He is all along on high-dose alpha blocker. What is the BEST treatment option for him?

  1. A Increase dose of alpha blocker and try wean off Foley
  2. B Long-term Foley
  3. C Prostatic stent
  4. D Self intermittent catheterization
  5. E Transurethral resection of prostate
💡 查看答案
E

69/M, 3rd episode of AROU on high-dose alpha-blocker → TURP (failed medical therapy with recurrent retention = indication for surgical management).

URO·MCQ068 2018 MCQ52 MCQ Which of the following is not a complication of transurethral resection of the prostate?

Which of the following is not a complication of transurethral resection of the prostate?

  1. A Bleeding
  2. B Dilutional hyponatremia
  3. C Retrograde ejaculation
  4. D Sepsis
  5. E Tumor seeding
💡 查看答案
E

Tumor seeding is NOT a recognized complication of TURP (TURP chips can show incidental cancer but it doesn't 'seed'). Bleeding, dilutional hyponatraemia (TUR syndrome), retrograde ejaculation, and sepsis are complications.

URO·MCQ069 2018 MCQ53 MCQ Which of the following features of renal trauma is an indication for surgical exploration?

Which of the following features of renal trauma is an indication for surgical exploration?

  1. A Gross hematuria
  2. B Perinephric hematoma
  3. C Renal laceration > 1cm
  4. D Renal pedicle avulsion
  5. E Severe loin pain
💡 查看答案
D

Renal pedicle avulsion (Grade V injury) is an absolute indication for surgical exploration. Other findings can often be managed conservatively.

URO·MCQ070 2018 MCQ54 MCQ A patient present with gross hematuria. Cystoscopy with transurethral resection (TURBT) showed a 3cm pT1 tumor without invasion to muscularis propria. What is t…

A patient present with gross hematuria. Cystoscopy with transurethral resection (TURBT) showed a 3cm pT1 tumor without invasion to muscularis propria. What is the most appropriate next step?

  1. A Neoadjuvant chemotherapy + radical cystectomy
  2. B Neoadjuvant radiotherapy + radical cystectomy
  3. C Radical cystectomy
  4. D Second look TURBT
  5. E Systemic chemotherapy
💡 查看答案
D

3cm pT1 TCC after TURBT → second-look TURBT (re-staging TURBT within 2-6 weeks — detects residual tumour and confirms staging accuracy, especially for T1 disease).

URO·MCQ071 2018 MCQ57 MCQ Doctor suspects a patient has a testicular tumor. After a complete history and thorough physical examination, what is the next most appropriate investigation?

Doctor suspects a patient has a testicular tumor. After a complete history and thorough physical examination, what is the next most appropriate investigation?

  1. A Computed tomography scan of thorax, abdomen and pelvis
  2. B Magnetic resonance imaging scan of scrotum
  3. C Ultrasound scan of scrotum
  4. D Abdominal radiograph
💡 查看答案
C

Suspected testicular tumour → ultrasound scan of scrotum is the first investigation (non-invasive, highly sensitive, differentiates testicular from paratesticular pathology).

URO·MCQ072 2019 MCQ40 MCQ 30 years old man who was a backseat passenger got a sustained rapid deceleration blunt injury to left part of the abdomen in a road traffic accident. There was …

30 years old man who was a backseat passenger got a sustained rapid deceleration blunt injury to left part of the abdomen in a road traffic accident. There was no gross hematuria and he was hemodynamically stable. What is the best management to his urological trauma?

  1. A Anterograde pyelogram
  2. B CT abdomen and pelvis with delayed phase
  3. C Magnetic resonance urogram
  4. D Cystoscopy with retrograde pyelogram
  5. E Ultrasound
💡 查看答案
B

30/M, blunt left abdominal trauma, no gross haematuria, stable → CT abdomen and pelvis with delayed phase (to assess renal/ureteric injury — delayed phase for collecting system/ureteric leak).

URO·MCQ073 2019 MCQ48 MCQ 45 years old lady, asymptomatic, incidental finding of staghorn stone, urine culture done, what is the most likely organism?

45 years old lady, asymptomatic, incidental finding of staghorn stone, urine culture done, what is the most likely organism?

  1. A Klebsiella spp.
  2. B Pseudomonas aeruginosa
  3. C Enterococcus faecalis
  4. D Clostridium difficile
  5. E Mycoplasma
💡 查看答案
A

45/F, asymptomatic staghorn stone → Klebsiella (urease-producing organism associated with struvite/staghorn stones). Proteus is most classic but Klebsiella is also urease-positive.

URO·MCQ074 2019 MCQ49 MCQ Which of the following may occur in male patient DURING urinary catheterisation?

Which of the following may occur in male patient DURING urinary catheterisation?

  1. A Prostatitis
  2. B Pyelonephritis
  3. C Stone formation
  4. D Urethral bleeding
  5. E Urethral stricture
💡 查看答案
D

Urethral bleeding can occur DURING catheterization (traumatic insertion). Prostatitis, pyelonephritis, stone formation, and stricture are late complications.

URO·MCQ075 2020 MCQ71 MCQ 3cm staghorn stone in middle and lower pole of kidney. What is the most appropriate management?

3cm staghorn stone in middle and lower pole of kidney. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Open nephrolithotomy
  2. B Percutaneous nephrolithotomy
  3. C ESWL
  4. D Observation
  5. E Nephrectomy
💡 查看答案
B

3cm staghorn stone in kidney → PCNL (percutaneous nephrolithotomy — gold standard for large renal stones/staghorn).

URO·MCQ076 2021 MCQ48 MCQ Erectile dysfunction after prostatectomy. But with ischemic heart disease on nitrates. Which of the following is the best drug?

Erectile dysfunction after prostatectomy. But with ischemic heart disease on nitrates. Which of the following is the best drug?

  1. A Alprostadil
  2. B Sildenafil
  3. C Tadalafil
  4. D Vardenafil
💡 查看答案
A

ED post-prostatectomy + on nitrates for IHD → alprostadil (intracavernosal/intraurethral PGE1). PDE5 inhibitors (sildenafil, tadalafil, vardenafil) are CONTRAINDICATED with nitrates.

URO·MCQ077 2021 MCQ57 MCQ AROU due to 1 week of constipation, Cr 300. After catheterisation and laxative the creatinine normalized to 100, constipation resolved. Now still on catheter. H…

AROU due to 1 week of constipation, Cr 300. After catheterisation and laxative the creatinine normalized to 100, constipation resolved. Now still on catheter. How to manage?

  1. A Trial without catheter
  2. B keep the catheter and discuss surgical treatment for BPH
  3. C Give 5α reductase 2 days, then trial without catheter
  4. D Give α blocker 2 days, then trial without catheter
💡 查看答案
D

AROU from constipation resolved, creatinine normalized → give alpha-blocker for a few days, then trial without catheter (TWOC). Alpha-blocker relaxes bladder neck/prostate smooth muscle to facilitate voiding.

URO·MCQ078 2021 MCQ83 MCQ Asymptomatic DM lady. Incidental finding of 105 CFU of E. Coli per 100mL MSU. No pyuria. No proteinuria or hematuria. What is the management?

Asymptomatic DM lady. Incidental finding of 105 CFU of E. Coli per 100mL MSU. No pyuria. No proteinuria or hematuria. What is the management?

  1. A Observe
  2. B 7 days nitrofurantoin
  3. C 3 day trimethoprim
  4. D 3 day oral quinolones
💡 查看答案
A

Asymptomatic bacteriuria in diabetic female with no pyuria/symptoms → observe (no treatment needed for asymptomatic bacteriuria in most non-pregnant adults, even with DM).

URO·MCQ079 2021 MCQ84 MCQ 50-year-old lady, painful gross hematuria 2 months ago. Resolved on its own. USG abdomen and pelvis normal, 1 week ago done in health check up in private is nor…

50-year-old lady, painful gross hematuria 2 months ago. Resolved on its own. USG abdomen and pelvis normal, 1 week ago done in health check up in private is normal.

  1. A Contrast CTU
  2. B Cystoscopy
  3. C MSU then antibiotics if bacteriuria
  4. D Observe for further hematuria episodes
💡 查看答案
B

50/F, painful gross haematuria resolved, normal USG → cystoscopy (must rule out bladder cancer — any episode of gross haematuria in adult >40 requires cystoscopy).

URO·MCQ080 2021 MCQ86 MCQ Middle aged male, history of mitral valve repair and on warfarin; now presenting with a 1cm left upper calyceal renal stone at upper pole. HU of 800. Keen for m…

Middle aged male, history of mitral valve repair and on warfarin; now presenting with a 1cm left upper calyceal renal stone at upper pole. HU of 800. Keen for management.

  1. A PCNL
  2. B RIRS
  3. C ESWL
  4. D Medical expulsive therapy by CCB
💡 查看答案
C

1cm upper calyceal stone, HU 800, on warfarin → ESWL (non-invasive, suitable for small upper pole stones, anticoagulation can be managed). RIRS also possible but ESWL is most appropriate for this size/location.

URO·MCQ081 2021 MCQ?? MCQ PSA 9, prostate exam all normal, median groove preserved , 50g. Wife report some symptoms of BPH. He had an episode of AROU 2 weeks ago. What would you do?

PSA 9, prostate exam all normal, median groove preserved , 50g. Wife report some symptoms of BPH. He had an episode of AROU 2 weeks ago. What would you do?

  1. A Check the prostate health index and free PSA
  2. B Recheck PSA 4 weeks later
  3. C Do multiparametric prostate MRI
  4. D Do prostate biopsy
💡 查看答案
C

PSA 9 + normal DRE + recent AROU → multiparametric prostate MRI (mpMRI — PI-RADS scoring, guides decision for targeted biopsy if suspicious).

URO·MCQ082 2022 MCQ8 MCQ 50 years old woman, recurrent loin pain, found staghorn stone in left kidney, thin cortex. Radionuclide scan shows left kidney split function 4%. What is the ap…

50 years old woman, recurrent loin pain, found staghorn stone in left kidney, thin cortex. Radionuclide scan shows left kidney split function 4%. What is the appropriate treatment for her stone?

  1. A Left nephrectomy
  2. B RIRS
  3. C ESWL
  4. D PCNL
💡 查看答案
A

Staghorn stone + thin cortex + split function 4% → left nephrectomy (non-functioning kidney with stone burden — remove the source of infection/stones).

URO·MCQ083 2022 MCQ20 MCQ Erectile dysfunction with insidious onset, progressively worsened, fail to penetrate during coitus now, most preferable treatment given he has no contraindicati…

Erectile dysfunction with insidious onset, progressively worsened, fail to penetrate during coitus now, most preferable treatment given he has no contraindications to it is:

  1. A Intracavernosal alprostadil
  2. B Oral PDE5 inhibitors
  3. C Psychosexual counselling
  4. D Vacuum penile device
💡 查看答案
B

Insidious onset progressive ED + no contraindications → oral PDE5 inhibitors (sildenafil/tadalafil) are the preferred first-line treatment for organic ED.

URO·MCQ084 2022 MCQ39 MCQ 40/M with 1cm left lower pole stone did ESWL uneventfully. Now 1 hour after procedure has severe left loin pain and progressively increasing in pain. Hypotensio…

40/M with 1cm left lower pole stone did ESWL uneventfully. Now 1 hour after procedure has severe left loin pain and progressively increasing in pain. Hypotension and tachycardia.

  1. A Inadequate post op analgesia
  2. B Perinephric haematoma
  3. C Renal colic due to shattered fragment from ESWL
  4. D UTI
💡 查看答案
B

Post-ESWL 1h, severe progressive loin pain + hypotension + tachycardia = perinephric haematoma (subcapsular/perirenal bleeding — serious complication of ESWL).

URO·MCQ085 2022 MCQ50 MCQ Elderly man present with AROU. Found high creatinine. Now on foley. Creatinine normalized.

Elderly man present with AROU. Found high creatinine. Now on foley. Creatinine normalized.

  1. A Prescribe alpha antagonist and wean off foley
  2. B Prescribe alpha antagonist together with 5a reductive inhibitor and discharge
  3. C Clean intermittent catheterization to aid bladder recovery
  4. D Directly proceed to surgery for BPH
💡 查看答案
A

Elderly + AROU + high creatinine normalized after catheter → prescribe alpha antagonist and attempt trial without catheter (TWOC with alpha-blocker support).

URO·MCQ086 2022 MCQ51 MCQ 3cm RCC with thrombus in renal vein.

3cm RCC with thrombus in renal vein.

  1. A Open
  2. B Retroperitoneoscopic
  3. C Robot
  4. D Transabdominal lap
💡 查看答案
A

3cm RCC with renal vein thrombus → open radical nephrectomy (need to extract the tumour thrombus from renal vein — requires open approach for vascular control).

URO·MCQ087 2022 MCQ52 MCQ Girl with external genitalia but no uterus. XY.

Girl with external genitalia but no uterus. XY.

  1. A Androgen insensitivity
  2. B CAH
  3. C Tuner
  4. D Mixed gonadal dysgenesis
💡 查看答案
A

Female external genitalia + no uterus + XY karyotype = androgen insensitivity syndrome.

URO·MCQ088 2022 MCQ60 MCQ Most appropriate investigation to confirm underlying bladder outlet obstruction in 60/M with lower urinary tract symptoms:

Most appropriate investigation to confirm underlying bladder outlet obstruction in 60/M with lower urinary tract symptoms:

  1. A Serum urea and creatinine
  2. B USG urinary tract
  3. C Urodynamic study
  4. D Uroflowmetry
💡 查看答案
C

Most appropriate investigation to confirm bladder outlet obstruction = urodynamic study (pressure-flow study — measures detrusor pressure vs flow to diagnose obstruction objectively).

URO·EMQ001 2002 EMQ2 EMQ TREATMENT OF URINARY TRACT CALCULI

Theme: TREATMENT OF URINARY TRACT CALCULI

Options
  1. A ESWL
  2. B Pyelolithotomy
  3. C Cystolithotomy
  4. D Ureterorenoscopic lithotripsy (URSL)
  5. E Double-J catheter stenting
  6. F Ureterolithotomy
  7. G Percutaneous nephrolithotripsy (PCNL)
  8. H Nephrectomy
  9. I Observation
  10. J Cystolitholapaxy

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST appropriate treatment option from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 40 years old gentleman, who enjoyed good past health, presented with on and off left loin pain for 3 months. Intravenous pyelogram showed a left renal stone measuring 4 cm in size.
  2. Q2. A 56 years old lady with a history of recurrent right acute pyelonephritis. Investigations show a staghorn stone in the right kidney. A DTPA renal scan showed the right kidney to be contributing to 5% of the total renal function.
  3. Q3. A 60 years old gentleman presented with acute onset of left loin pain, fever, chills and rigors. Ultrasonogram of the kidneys revealed one obstructing left upper ureteric stone with left hydronephrosis. The temperature is still swinging despite 24 hours of intravenous broad-spectrum antibiotics.

  5. E Renal cell carcinoma
  6. F Renal stone

  8. H Urethral rupture
  9. I Renal trauma
  10. J Anticoagulation therapy

For each of the patients who have blood found in urine below, choose the single MOST likely cause of bleeding from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 19 years old gentleman suffered from left superior and inferior pubic rami fracture after being knocked down by a car. Drops of fresh blood were found at external urethral meatus even without voiding.
  2. Q2. A 45 years old gentleman intermittently passes whole stream haematuria with tubular- shaped clot. There is neither dysuria nor loin pain.
  3. Q3. A 24 years old lady, who was recently married, presents with haematuria associated with dysuria, frequency and urgency.

  5. E Carcinoma of the bladder
  6. F Carcinoma of prostate
  7. G Renal stone
  8. H Renal cell carcinoma
  9. I Ruptured kidney
  10. J Urinary tract tuberculosis

For each of the following patients who presented with haematuria, select the MOST likely diagnosis from the list above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 30 years old gentleman who is a non-smoker presented with sudden onset of dysuria and voiding difficulty. He passed a few drops of blood at the end of stream.
  2. Q2. A 40 years old lady with on and off haematuria for weeks associated with dysuria. Urine microscopy showed numerous white cells but urine culture was negative.
  3. Q3. A 50 years old gentleman presented with on and off gross haematuria for three months. Physical examination showed left varicocele. Ultrasound of kidneys revealed a heterogenous left renal mass.

  8. H Pyelonephrotomy

  10. J JJ catheter
Questions
  1. Q1. A 23 years old female, sexually active, presented with acute onset of right loin pain, fever, chills and rigor. Also has dysuria and frequency. USG discovered right renal calyceal stone. No hydronephrosis.
  2. Q2. A 65 years old man presented with loin pain. Right ureteric stone, non-functioning kidney.
  3. Q3. A 45 years old man presented with pyelonephritis and shock. Ultrasound: pus in ureter, hydronephrosis.
URO·EMQ005 2007 EMQ10 EMQ TREATMENT OF PROSTATE CANCER

Options
  1. A Bilateral orchidectomy
  2. B Brachytherapy
