  5. E LHRH analogue injection
  6. F Oral antiandrogen
  7. G Radical prostatectomy
  8. H Transrectal ultrasound-guided prostate biopsy
  9. I Transurethral resection of prostate
  10. J Watchful waiting
Questions
  1. Q1. 65/M Newly diagnosed cancer of prostate. Digital rectal examination is normal. Gleason score is 2+3. PSA is 5. Good past health. Keen on curation. Worry about the irradiation side effects.
  2. Q2. 85/M newly diagnosed cancer of prostate. Digital rectal examination is normal. PSA is 4. Gleason score is 2+2. Asymptomatic.
  3. Q3. 80/M. Multiple bone pain. Digital rectal examination show hard prostate gland with frozen pelvis. PSA 300. X-ray of lumbar spine show multiple sclerotic lesion. Not keen of surgery.

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST SUITABLE management option from the list above. Each option may be used once, more than once, or not at all.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 50-year-old gentleman presents with gross haematuria for 3 months. Physical examination shows that the left kidney is ballotable. The renal function test is normal. Contrast computed tomography (CT) abdomen shows an 18-cm left renal tumour with contrast enhancement. There is left renal vein thrombus without inferior vena cava involvement.
  2. Q2. A 25-year-old lady has a known history of Von Hippel Lindau syndrome. She has a history of right renal cell carcinoma and had right radical nephrectomy 2 years ago. Recent contrast CT abdomen shows that there is a 3-cm endophytic left renal tumour in the upper pole.
  3. Q3. A 60-year-old gentleman presents with gross haematuria and right loin pain for 6 months. Contrast CT abdomen and thorax shows that there is a 6-cm right renal tumour and multiple lung secondaries. His performance status is otherwise good. Cytoreductive nephrectomy has been done. Pathology report shows clear cell carcinoma.

  7. G Renal cell carcinoma
  8. H Urothelial cancer of renal pelvis
  9. I Urothelial cancer of bladder

CT urogram showed that she had a 10 mm obstructive left upper ureteric stone and hydronephrosis with minimal cortical substance. DTPA scan show non-functioning L kidney.

SECTION 16

Vascular Surgery

94 MCQ · 6 EMQ
VAS·MCQ001 2001 MCQ13 MCQ A 60 years old lady with hypertension presents with acute onset of severe chest pain radiating to the back. Chest x-ray shows widening of the superior mediastin…

A 60 years old lady with hypertension presents with acute onset of severe chest pain radiating to the back. Chest x-ray shows widening of the superior mediastinum. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Acute myocardial infarction
  2. B Acute aortic dissection
  3. C Acute haemorrhage in retrosternal goitre
  4. D Acute perforated peptic ulcer
  5. E Acute pericarditis
💡 查看答案
B

60/F, HTN + acute chest pain radiating to back + widened mediastinum on CXR = acute aortic dissection.

VAS·MCQ002 2001 MCQ15, 2002 MCQ34 MCQ In general, operative treatment should be offered to a patient with 6 cm diameter abdominal aortic aneurysm UNLESS:

In general, operative treatment should be offered to a patient with 6 cm diameter abdominal aortic aneurysm UNLESS:

  1. A The patient is older than 80 years old
  2. B The patient has advanced malignancy
  3. C The aneurysm involves the renal arteries
  4. D The patient is asymptomatic
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
B

Advanced malignancy is the only absolute contraindication for 6cm AAA repair (limited life expectancy makes repair futile). Age >80 is relative. Suprarenal involvement needs repair but different approach.

VAS·MCQ003 2001 MCQ23 MCQ Which of the following is an ACCEPTED indication for carotid endarterectomy?

Which of the following is an ACCEPTED indication for carotid endarterectomy?

  1. A Symptomatic patient with ipsilateral internal carotid artery occlusion
  2. B Symptomatic patient with 30% ipsilateral internal carotid artery stenosis, with contralateral internal carotid artery occlusion
  3. C Asymptomatic patient with 80% stenosis over the ipsilateral internal carotid artery
  4. D Asymptomatic patient with tortuous carotid artery
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
C

Asymptomatic patient with ≥80% ICA stenosis → carotid endarterectomy is accepted indication (ACST/ACAS trials). Symptomatic with occlusion = no benefit. 30% stenosis = no benefit.

VAS·MCQ004 2001 MCQ26 MCQ The following are indications for performing preoperative aortogram in the investigation of a patient with an abdominal aortic aneurysm EXCEPT:

The following are indications for performing preoperative aortogram in the investigation of a patient with an abdominal aortic aneurysm EXCEPT:

  1. A Endoluminal stent graft is considered
  2. B The aneurysm involves the visceral branches
  3. C There is concomitant lower limb atherosclerotic occlusive disease
  4. D The aneurysm involves the iliac bifurcation
  5. E The relation between the renal artery origin and the neck of the aneurysm is not well defined on ultrasound or computed tomography
💡 查看答案
E

Relationship of renal artery to aneurysm neck is assessed on CT (not aortogram). Aortogram for EVAR planning (A), visceral branch involvement (B), concomitant PAD (C), and iliac involvement (D) are all indications.

VAS·MCQ005 2001 MCQ32 MCQ A 70 years old gentleman complained of right calf claudication for 2 years. In recent one month, he noted spontaneous onset of pain and gangrene over his right …

A 70 years old gentleman complained of right calf claudication for 2 years. In recent one month, he noted spontaneous onset of pain and gangrene over his right 2nd toe. The following statements concerning the management of this patient are correct EXCEPT:

  1. A Revascularization is indicated
  2. B He should quit smoking
  3. C If left untreated, the chance of limb loss is high
  4. D Heparin infusion should be started
  5. E Angiogram should be performed
💡 查看答案
D

Heparin infusion is NOT the primary concern — question asks about TRUE statements. All (A-E) could be true, but the 'EXCEPT' format is testing. Let me reconsider: revascularization (A true), quit smoking (B true), high limb loss risk (C true), angiogram needed (E true). Heparin (D) — yes, anticoagulation with heparin is indicated for critical limb ischaemia. All are true. If the question is straightforward about critical limb ischaemia, all statements are appropriate.

VAS·MCQ006 2001 MCQ36 MCQ Which of the following is the conduit of FIRST choice in performing a femoro-popliteal bypass?

Which of the following is the conduit of FIRST choice in performing a femoro-popliteal bypass?

  1. A Stretch Goretex
  2. B Ringed Goretex
  3. C Woven Dacron
  4. D Cryopreserved arterial graft
  5. E Autologous vein graft
💡 查看答案
E

Autologous vein graft (reversed great saphenous vein) is the conduit of first choice for femoro-popliteal bypass — best long-term patency.

VAS·MCQ007 2001 MCQ43 MCQ The followings are the steps in the management of an infected femoral pseudoaneurysm in drug addicts EXCEPT:

The followings are the steps in the management of an infected femoral pseudoaneurysm in drug addicts EXCEPT:

  1. A Debridement
  2. B Systemic antibiotics
  3. C Ligation of the arteries
  4. D In-situ bypass
  5. E Drainage of abscess
💡 查看答案
D

In-situ bypass is NOT performed for infected femoral pseudoaneurysm in drug addicts (high infection risk → graft will become infected). Debridement, antibiotics, ligation, and drainage are appropriate steps.

VAS·MCQ008 2001 MCQ53 MCQ Which of the following is the MOST commonly encountered organism in femoral pseudoaneurysm associated with drug abuse:

Which of the following is the MOST commonly encountered organism in femoral pseudoaneurysm associated with drug abuse:

  1. A Methicillin sensitive Staphylococcus aureus (MSSA)
  2. B Methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA)
  3. C Salmonella enteriditis
  4. D Pseudomonas aeruginosa
  5. E Enterococcus
💡 查看答案
B

MRSA is the most commonly encountered organism in femoral pseudoaneurysm from IV drug abuse in Hong Kong.

VAS·MCQ009 2001 MCQ59 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman with ischaemic heart disease underwent cardiac catheterization 2 days ago. He complained of sudden onset of pain and cyanosis of the to…

A 60 years old gentleman with ischaemic heart disease underwent cardiac catheterization 2 days ago. He complained of sudden onset of pain and cyanosis of the toes. All the peripheral pulses of the lower limbs were palpable. What is the MOST likely diagnosis?

  1. A Vasculitis
  2. B Buerger’s disease
  3. C Atherosclerotic occlusive disease
  4. D Blue toe syndrome
  5. E Acute femoral emboli
💡 查看答案
D

Post-cardiac catheterization + sudden toe cyanosis + intact pulses = blue toe syndrome (cholesterol crystal embolization/atheroembolism from catheter manipulation).

VAS·MCQ010 2002 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the following is NOT considered as a contraindication for endoluminal stent graft for abdominal aortic aneurysm?

Which of the following is NOT considered as a contraindication for endoluminal stent graft for abdominal aortic aneurysm?

  1. A A tortuous aneurysm neck of > 90°
  2. B An aneurysm neck of < 1 cm in length
  3. C Aneurysm involving the aortic bifurcation
  4. D Severe atherosclerotic occlusive disease of the iliac arteries
  5. E An aneurysm involving the renal artery
💡 查看答案
C

Aneurysm involving aortic bifurcation is NOT a contraindication for EVAR (bifurcated stent grafts address this). Tortuous neck >90° (A), short neck <1cm (B), severe iliac disease (D), and renal artery involvement (E) are relative/absolute contraindications.

VAS·MCQ011 2002 MCQ40 MCQ A 30 years old intravenous drug addict presented with a 3-day history of right groin swelling associated with fever and on and off bleeding. What should be the …

A 30 years old intravenous drug addict presented with a 3-day history of right groin swelling associated with fever and on and off bleeding. What should be the working DIAGNOSIS?

  1. A Femoral pseudoaneurysm
  2. B Groin abscess
  3. C Lymphadenitis
  4. D Femoral hernia
  5. E Saphenous varix
💡 查看答案
A

30/M IVDU + right groin swelling + fever + bleeding = femoral pseudoaneurysm (from injecting into femoral artery/vein).

VAS·MCQ012 2002 MCQ65, 2002# MCQ58, 2004 MCQ23, 2014 MCQ74 MCQ A 70-year-old gentleman with a history of chronic venous insufficiency has a non-healing right ankle ulcer for 3 years. He noted that the ulcer has increased in…

A 70-year-old gentleman with a history of chronic venous insufficiency has a non-healing right ankle ulcer for 3 years. He noted that the ulcer has increased in size rapidly in the past 2 weeks. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE management for this patient?

  1. A Debridement and split thickness skin graft
  2. B Debridement with free flap reconstruction
  3. C Superficial vein surgery followed by debridement
  4. D Ulcer biopsy
  5. E Venogram followed by deep vein reconstruction
💡 查看答案
D

70/M, chronic venous ulcer rapidly enlarging → ulcer biopsy (rule out Marjolin's ulcer — malignant transformation of chronic ulcer). Must exclude malignancy before treatment.

VAS·MCQ013 2002 MCQ77, 2003 MCQ68 MCQ True statements concerning aortic dissection include all of the following EXCEPT:

True statements concerning aortic dissection include all of the following EXCEPT:

  1. A Hypertension is the most important predisposing factor
  2. B A normal chest X-ray does not rule out aortic dissection
  3. C A murmur of aortic regurgitation can be present
  4. D Emergency surgery is indicated in aortic dissection involving the ascending aorta
  5. E It occurs with equal incidence during the three trimesters of pregnancy
💡 查看答案
E

Aortic dissection does NOT occur with equal incidence in all trimesters — it's more common in the 3rd trimester and peripartum. All other statements are true.

VAS·MCQ014 2002# MCQ12 MCQ A 70 years old gentleman presented to you with an incidental finding of a pulsatile central abdominal mass. What should be the FIRST line of investigation?

A 70 years old gentleman presented to you with an incidental finding of a pulsatile central abdominal mass. What should be the FIRST line of investigation?

  1. A Ultrasound
  2. B Computed tomography
  3. C Magnetic resonance imaging
  4. D Angiography
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
A

Incidental pulsatile abdominal mass → ultrasound (first-line investigation — non-invasive, widely available, accurate for AAA diameter measurement).

VAS·MCQ015 2002# MCQ13 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman presented with bilateral lower limb claudication with rest pain. Physical examination showed absent femoral and distal pulses over both…

A 60 years old gentleman presented with bilateral lower limb claudication with rest pain. Physical examination showed absent femoral and distal pulses over both lower limbs. Angioplasty was deemed unsuitable from angiographic findings. What would be the BEST surgical option for him?

  1. A Aorto-bifemoral bypass
  2. B Axillo-bifemoral bypass
  3. C Cross-femoral bypass
  4. D Femoro-popliteal bypass
  5. E Lumbar sympathectomy
💡 查看答案
A

Bilateral lower limb claudication + rest pain + absent femoral pulses = aorto-iliac occlusive disease → aorto-bifemoral bypass (optimal for aorto-iliac disease in fit patient).

VAS·MCQ016 2002# MCQ22 MCQ What is the MOST COMMON cause for post-operative mortality after aneurysmorrhaphy for abdominal aortic aneurysm?

What is the MOST COMMON cause for post-operative mortality after aneurysmorrhaphy for abdominal aortic aneurysm?

  1. A Myocardial infarction
  2. B Chest infection
  3. C Renal failure
  4. D Bleeding
  5. E Acute graft thrombosis
💡 查看答案
A

Myocardial infarction is the most common cause of post-operative mortality after AAA repair.

VAS·MCQ017 2002# MCQ57 MCQ Which of the following is NOT a treatment option for deep vein thrombosis?

Which of the following is NOT a treatment option for deep vein thrombosis?

  1. A Systemic heparin
  2. B Low molecular weight heparin
  3. C Warfarin
  4. D Aspirin
  5. E Thrombolytic agent
💡 查看答案
D

Aspirin is NOT a standard treatment for acute DVT (antiplatelet, not anticoagulant). Heparin, LMWH, warfarin, and thrombolytics are all treatment options.

VAS·MCQ018 2002# MCQ59, 2004 MCQ14, 2010 MCQ73 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman was admitted to the Accident and Emergency Department because of abdominal pain. The blood pressure was 80/40 mmHg with a pulse of 120/…

A 60 years old gentleman was admitted to the Accident and Emergency Department because of abdominal pain. The blood pressure was 80/40 mmHg with a pulse of 120/min. Physical examination revealed a pulsatile abdominal mass. What is the IMMEDIATE step of management?

  1. A Urgent CT scan
  2. B Urgent Angiogram
  3. C Urgent MRI
  4. D Laparotomy
  5. E Admission to ICU
💡 查看答案
D

Ruptured AAA (hypotension + pulsatile mass + abdominal pain) → emergency laparotomy (no time for CT/MRI in haemodynamically unstable patient).

VAS·MCQ019 2002# MCQ84 MCQ In a patient with a history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia, which of the following is the BEST treatment option when he develops acute deep vein thrombosis…

In a patient with a history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia, which of the following is the BEST treatment option when he develops acute deep vein thrombosis of the lower limb?

  1. A Heparin
  2. B Low molecular weight heparin
  3. C Hirudin
  4. D Aspirin
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
C

History of HIT + acute DVT → hirudin (direct thrombin inhibitor) or other non-heparin anticoagulant (argatroban, fondaparinux). LMWH is contraindicated in HIT (cross-reactivity).

VAS·MCQ020 2003 MCQ11 MCQ Which of the following concerning popliteal artery aneurysm is NOT true?

Which of the following concerning popliteal artery aneurysm is NOT true?

  1. A It is the most common form of peripheral artery aneurysm
  2. B It is associated with abdominal aortic aneurysm
  3. C It is bilateral in less than 10% of cases
  4. D Thrombosis is a significant complication
  5. E Rupture is a rare complication
💡 查看答案
C

Popliteal artery aneurysm is bilateral in ~50% of cases (not <10%). It IS the most common peripheral aneurysm (A true), associated with AAA (B true). Thrombosis (D true) is more common than rupture (E true).

VAS·MCQ021 2003 MCQ27, 2004 MCQ71, 2007 MCQ32 MCQ Which of the following vascular grafts is frequently used for open aneurysm repair?

Which of the following vascular grafts is frequently used for open aneurysm repair?

  1. A Superficial femoral vein
  2. B Long saphenous vein
  3. C Human umbilical vein
  4. D Dacron
  5. E None of the above
💡 查看答案
D

Dacron (polyester) graft is the most frequently used for open AAA repair.

VAS·MCQ022 2003 MCQ42 MCQ A 78 years old lady with unstable angina recently underwent right transfemoral coronary angioplasty. She noticed a persistent lump over her right groin after th…

A 78 years old lady with unstable angina recently underwent right transfemoral coronary angioplasty. She noticed a persistent lump over her right groin after the procedure. Clinical examination revealed a 5 cm expansile mass over the right groin with surrounding bruises. What is the MOST likely underlying pathology?

  1. A Right femoral artery aneurysm
  2. B Right femoral artery pseudoaneurysm
  3. C Right femoral haematoma
  4. D Right femoral arterio-venous fistula
  5. E Right groin foreign body
💡 查看答案
B

Post-femoral catheterization + persistent pulsatile groin lump + expansile + bruit = femoral artery pseudoaneurysm.

VAS·MCQ023 2003 MCQ57 MCQ During carotid endarterectomy, which of the following procedures is always NECESSARY?

During carotid endarterectomy, which of the following procedures is always NECESSARY?

  1. A Insertion of a carotid shunt
  2. B Measurement of the stump pressure
  3. C Systemic heparinization
  4. D Vein patch closure of the arteriotomy
  5. E Intraoperative Duplex scan
💡 查看答案
C

Systemic heparinization is ALWAYS necessary during CEA (prevents thrombosis during arterial clamping).

VAS·MCQ024 2003 MCQ59 MCQ The COMMONEST organism causing infection of a thoracic aortic aneurysm is:

The COMMONEST organism causing infection of a thoracic aortic aneurysm is:

  1. A Shigella
  2. B Salmonella
  3. C Escherichia coli
  4. D Staphylococcus aureus
  5. E Streptococcus viridans
💡 查看答案
B

Salmonella is the most common organism causing mycotic (infected) thoracic aortic aneurysm.

VAS·MCQ025 2004 MCQ11 MCQ A 60 years old gentleman with brain tumour underwent craniotomy two days ago. He developed shortness of breath and was diagnosed to have pulmonary embolism. He …

A 60 years old gentleman with brain tumour underwent craniotomy two days ago. He developed shortness of breath and was diagnosed to have pulmonary embolism. He was mildly dyspnoeic and the haemodynamic status was stable. What should be the MOST appropriate treatment?

  1. A Systemic heparin
  2. B Subcutaneous low molecular weight heparin
  3. C Thrombolytic therapy
  4. D Insertion of Caval filter
  5. E Pulmonary embolectomy
💡 查看答案
D

Post-craniotomy 2 days + PE → can't anticoagulate (bleeding risk too high) → IVC filter insertion (prevents recurrent PE while contraindicated for anticoagulation).

VAS·MCQ026 2004 MCQ54 MCQ Which of the following is a contraindication to carotid endarterectomy?

Which of the following is a contraindication to carotid endarterectomy?

  1. A Symptomatic 90% internal carotid artery stenosis
  2. B Asymptomatic 90% internal carotid artery stenosis
  3. C Ipsilateral internal carotid artery occlusion
  4. D Contralateral internal carotid artery occlusion
  5. E Post-radiotherapy carotid stenosis
💡 查看答案
C

Ipsilateral ICA OCCLUSION → CEA cannot be performed (no flow to restore). Symptomatic 90% stenosis (A — indication). Asymptomatic 90% (B — relative indication). Contralateral occlusion (D — not contraindication). Post-RT stenosis (E — relative, may prefer stenting).

VAS·MCQ027 2004 MCQ72 MCQ Regarding abdominal aortic aneurysms, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

Regarding abdominal aortic aneurysms, which of the following statements is INCORRECT?

  1. A The 30 day mortality of elective repair should be less than 5%
  2. B Most arise in the infrarenal aorta
  3. C May present with an acutely ischaemic leg
  4. D The 30 day mortality after emergency repair for rupture is less than 25%
  5. E Surgery is usually considered when the diameter of the aneurysm is greater than 5.5 cm
💡 查看答案
D

30-day mortality after emergency ruptured AAA repair is ~40-50% (not <20%). Elective mortality <5% (A true). Most infrarenal (B true). Can present with acute leg ischaemia (C true). Surgery at >5.5cm (E true).

VAS·MCQ028 2005 MCQ5, 2015 MCQ58 MCQ Which one the following statements concerning ankle-brachial index (ABI) is CORRECT?

Which one the following statements concerning ankle-brachial index (ABI) is CORRECT?

  1. A An ABI>0.95 is abnormal
  2. B The ABI is a ratio of the lower to upper extremity diastolic blood pressure
  3. C An ABI values of 0.5 is an accurate cut off point between patients with intermittent claudication and those with tissue loss
  4. D Continuous wave Doppler is required to measure the ABI
  5. E If the patient has exercised, the ABI value will increase in patient with significant occlusive disease
💡 查看答案
D

Continuous wave Doppler is required to measure ABI (handheld Doppler + sphygmomanometer). ABI >0.95 is NORMAL (A wrong). Ratio of systolic (not diastolic) pressures (B wrong). 0.4 (not 0.5) is critical ischaemia threshold (C wrong). After exercise, ABI DROPS in PVD patients (E wrong).

VAS·MCQ029 2005 MCQ19 MCQ An asymptomatic infrarenal abdominal aneurysm measuring 8 cm is found in an otherwise healthy 70 years old man. The PRIMARY management consists of:

An asymptomatic infrarenal abdominal aneurysm measuring 8 cm is found in an otherwise healthy 70 years old man. The PRIMARY management consists of:

  1. A Operative repair only if symptoms develop
  2. B Elective aneurysm repair
  3. C Reassurance and observation
  4. D Strict bed rest
  5. E Anti-hypertensive therapy
💡 查看答案
B

Asymptomatic 8cm AAA in healthy 70/M → elective aneurysm repair (high rupture risk at this size).

VAS·MCQ030 2005 MCQ42 MCQ The MOST effective method to prevent cerebral ischaemia during arterial clamping when performing carotid endarterectomy is:

The MOST effective method to prevent cerebral ischaemia during arterial clamping when performing carotid endarterectomy is:

  1. A Placement of a well function shunt
  2. B To perform the procedure under local anesthesia
  3. C Induction of hyperthermia
  4. D Use of vasopressors
  5. E Create hypercapnia
💡 查看答案
A

Well-functioning carotid shunt is the most effective method to prevent cerebral ischaemia during arterial clamping in CEA.

VAS·MCQ031 2005 MCQ56 MCQ The MOST effective strategy to prevent limb loss in diabetic patients is:

The MOST effective strategy to prevent limb loss in diabetic patients is:

  1. A Early revascularization
  2. B Strict regulation of blood glucose level
  3. C Use of insulin to control blood glucose level
  4. D Careful foot care
  5. E Broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage
💡 查看答案
D

Careful foot care is the most effective strategy to prevent limb loss in diabetic patients (preventive podiatry, daily inspection, proper footwear). Prevention > treatment.

VAS·MCQ032 2005 MCQ59 MCQ Which of the following concerning endovascular stent graft repair for abdominal aortic aneurysm is CORRECT?

Which of the following concerning endovascular stent graft repair for abdominal aortic aneurysm is CORRECT?

  1. A Rupture of the aneurysm may still occur despite endovascular repair
  2. B Secondary intervention is rarely required
  3. C Follow-up angiography every 6 monthly is the usual practice
  4. D The presence of a common iliac aneurysm on one side is a contraindication for the procedure
  5. E Old age is a contraindication for the procedure
💡 查看答案
A

Rupture may still occur despite EVAR (endoleak → continued aneurysm pressurization). Secondary intervention often needed (B wrong). Follow-up CT not angiography (C wrong). Common iliac aneurysm manageable with extension (D wrong). Age not a contraindication (E wrong).

VAS·MCQ033 2005 MCQ78, 2009 MCQ35, 2019 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following is the MOST COMMON cause for chronic ulceration over the lower limbs?

Which of the following is the MOST COMMON cause for chronic ulceration over the lower limbs?

  1. A Arterial occlusive disease
  2. B Chronic venous insufficiency
  3. C Malignancy
  4. D Tuberculosis
  5. E Peripheral neuropathy
💡 查看答案
B

Chronic venous insufficiency is the most common cause of lower limb chronic ulceration (~70% of leg ulcers).

VAS·MCQ034 2006 MCQ22 MCQ The followings are indications for surgical repair of abdominal aneurysm except:

The followings are indications for surgical repair of abdominal aneurysm except:

  1. A Aneurysm > 5.5cm
  2. B Symptomatic aneurysm
  3. C Suprarenal aneurysm
  4. D Rapid growing aneurysm
  5. E Aneurysm related blue toe syndrome
💡 查看答案
C

Suprarenal aneurysm IS an indication for surgical repair (not an exception). AAA >5.5cm, symptomatic, rapid growth, and blue toe syndrome are all indications.

VAS·MCQ035 2006 MCQ23 MCQ Which of the following about AAA is correct?

Which of the following about AAA is correct?

  1. A It is more common in elderly male
  2. B About 60% is infrarenal
  3. C Epigastric pain is a common presentation
  4. D Asymptomatic AAA is safe to observe
  5. E Benefits of operation for small AAA (4-5 cm) is well proven
💡 查看答案
A

AAA is more common in elderly males. ~95% are infrarenal not 60% (B wrong). Often asymptomatic (C wrong — epigastric pain not common). Asymptomatic AAA not always safe to observe if >5.5cm (D wrong). Small AAA benefit not proven (E wrong).

VAS·MCQ036 2006 MCQ32 MCQ Which of the following most commonly cause of infection of pseudoaneurysm?

Which of the following most commonly cause of infection of pseudoaneurysm?

  1. A Staphylococcus aureus
  2. B E. coli
💡 查看答案
A

Staphylococcus aureus is the most common cause of pseudoaneurysm infection.

VAS·MCQ037 2006 MCQ65 MCQ Management of pseudoaneurysm include following except:

Management of pseudoaneurysm include following except:

  1. A Thrombolytic therapy
  2. B USG guided compression
  3. C Injection of thrombin
  4. D Open surgery
  5. E Observation
💡 查看答案
A

Thrombolytic therapy is NOT a standard management for pseudoaneurysm (would worsen bleeding). USG compression, thrombin injection, open surgery, and observation (for small ones) are management options.

VAS·MCQ038 2006 MCQ76 MCQ Which of the followings is always needed in CEA?

Which of the followings is always needed in CEA?

  1. A Carotid shunt
  2. B Duplex USG
  3. C Systemic heparinization
  4. D Maintaining blood pressure ≥ 180 mmHg
  5. E General anaesthesia
💡 查看答案
C

Systemic heparinization is always needed in CEA (prevents thrombosis during arterial clamping).

VAS·MCQ039 2007 MCQ27 MCQ Diabetic foot ulcer

Diabetic foot ulcer

  1. A Often occurs on the dorsum of the foot
  2. B Is usually painful
  3. C May occur in a well perfused limb with palpable pedal pulse
  4. D Only occurs in insulin-dependent diabetic patients
  5. E Is rarely septic
💡 查看答案
C

Diabetic foot ulcer may occur in a well-perfused limb with palpable pulses (neuropathic ulcer — the primary mechanism is sensory neuropathy, not ischaemia).

VAS·MCQ040 2007 MCQ39 MCQ Which is true regarding Buerger’s Disease?

Which is true regarding Buerger’s Disease?

  1. A More in Caucasians
  2. B Cessation of smoking is most important step in management
  3. C More in elderly women
  4. D Usually in aorto-iliac
  5. E Due to atherosclerosis
💡 查看答案
B

Buerger's disease (thromboangiitis obliterans): cessation of smoking is the single most important step in management. More common in young Asian males. Involves distal small/medium vessels. Not atherosclerosis.

VAS·MCQ041 2009 MCQ30, 2014 MCQ73, 2017 MCQ44 MCQ Which of the following atherosclerotic lesion of lower limb is most amendable by percutaneous transluminal angioplasty?

Which of the following atherosclerotic lesion of lower limb is most amendable by percutaneous transluminal angioplasty?

  1. A A focal stenosis of 80% of the distal superficial femoral artery
  2. B 80% stenosis of proximal external iliac artery
  3. C Total occlusion of distal superficial femoral artery
  4. D Total occlusion of proximal external iliac artery
  5. E Diffuse occlusion of superficial femoral artery
💡 查看答案
B

80% stenosis of proximal external iliac artery (focal, proximal, large-calibre vessel) is most amenable to PTA. Short, focal, iliac lesions have best angioplasty results.

VAS·MCQ042 2009 MCQ31, 2015 MCQ59, 2017 MCQ?? MCQ Which of the following drugs may be useful in relieving the symptoms of claudication?

Which of the following drugs may be useful in relieving the symptoms of claudication?

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Cilostazol
  3. C Clopidogrel
  4. D Dipyridamole
  5. E Warfarin
💡 查看答案
B

Cilostazol (phosphodiesterase III inhibitor) is specifically indicated for relieving claudication symptoms (increases walking distance). Aspirin and clopidogrel are antiplatelet, not symptom-relieving.

VAS·MCQ043 2009 MCQ32 MCQ In peripheral vascular disease, which of the following is an indication for intervention?

In peripheral vascular disease, which of the following is an indication for intervention?

  1. A Claudication distance <200 m
  2. B Age < 60
  3. C Tissue loss
  4. D All of the above
💡 查看答案
C

Tissue loss (gangrene, non-healing ulcer) is a definite indication for intervention in PVD. Claudication alone (A) is relative. Age (B) is not an indication per se.

VAS·MCQ044 2009 MCQ33 MCQ M/80 with sudden right hemiplegia (left-sided stroke). Doppler found carotid stenosis. Which of the following is an indication for surgery?

M/80 with sudden right hemiplegia (left-sided stroke). Doppler found carotid stenosis. Which of the following is an indication for surgery?

  1. A 70% stenosis of right carotid artery
  2. B 70% stenosis of left carotid artery
  3. C Occlusion of both carotid artery
  4. D 30% stenosis of left carotid artery
💡 查看答案
B

Right hemiplegia = left-sided stroke → 70% stenosis of LEFT carotid artery (ipsilateral to the symptomatic hemisphere) is an indication for CEA.

VAS·MCQ045 2009 MCQ34 MCQ Which of the following is not a specific complication of thoracic aortic stent grafting?

Which of the following is not a specific complication of thoracic aortic stent grafting?

  1. A Access vessel injury
  2. B Contrast nephropathy
  3. C DVT
  4. D Stroke
  5. E Type A aortic dissection
💡 查看答案
C

DVT is NOT a specific complication of thoracic aortic stent grafting (it's a general surgical complication). Access vessel injury, contrast nephropathy, stroke (wire manipulation in arch), and Type A dissection are specific complications.

VAS·MCQ046 2010 MCQ72 MCQ 65/M with history of angina, admitted for coronary angiogram via right groin. Some time after catheterization, an expansile tender mass is noticed at the right …

65/M with history of angina, admitted for coronary angiogram via right groin. Some time after catheterization, an expansile tender mass is noticed at the right groin. PE: 10 × 10 cm right groin mass, exquisitely tender. Duplex USG showed a pseudoaneurysm at right femoral bifurcation. Best treatment approach is:

  1. A Conservative since most pseudoaneurysms resolves spontaneously
  2. B Surgical femoral exploration + repair
  3. C Repair by endovascular stent
  4. D USG guided alcohol injection into the pseudoaneurysm
  5. E USG guided compression
💡 查看答案
E

Post-catheterization + 10cm pulsatile groin mass = femoral pseudoaneurysm → USG-guided compression (first-line non-invasive treatment for small-medium pseudoaneurysms).

VAS·MCQ047 2010 MCQ76, 2014 MCQ77, 2018 MCQ41 MCQ A 63 years old lady presents with discomfort in left calf after walking 200 metres. The pedal pulses were palpable. There were no tissue loss or gangrene. The A…

A 63 years old lady presents with discomfort in left calf after walking 200 metres. The pedal pulses were palpable. There were no tissue loss or gangrene. The ABI is 1.02. Which of the following is most appropriate to do?

  1. A Observe
  2. B Duplex ultrasound of leg
  3. C Angiogram of leg
  4. D Bypass
  5. E Treat as intermittent claudication and prescribe aspirin and other medication
💡 查看答案
A

63/F, calf discomfort 200m + palpable pedal pulses + ABI 1.02 (normal) → likely NOT vascular claudication. Observe (may be musculoskeletal/neurogenic). Normal ABI essentially rules out significant PAD.

VAS·MCQ048 2010 MCQ89, 2011 MCQ70, 2019 MCQ66 MCQ A 55 years old diabetic lady developed an ulcer on plantar surface of the first metatarsal head of her left foot. The ulcer bed is covered with pink granulation…

A 55 years old diabetic lady developed an ulcer on plantar surface of the first metatarsal head of her left foot. The ulcer bed is covered with pink granulation tissue. What is the MOST LIKELY underlying pathology?

  1. A Gouty tophus ulcer
  2. B Infected ulcer
  3. C Ischemic ulcer
  4. D Malignant ulcer
  5. E Neuropathic ulcer
💡 查看答案
E

55/F diabetic + plantar ulcer under 1st metatarsal head + pink granulation = neuropathic ulcer (classic pressure point, painless, in well-perfused diabetic foot).

VAS·MCQ049 2011 MCQ30 MCQ 65/M, admitted for angina. Coronary angiogram done through puncturing femoral artery, next day with bruit over puncture site with swelling and minimal tendernes…

65/M, admitted for angina. Coronary angiogram done through puncturing femoral artery, next day with bruit over puncture site with swelling and minimal tenderness. There is a 1 cm pseudoaneurysm. What is the best treatment?

  1. A Cross femoral bypass
  2. B Open femoral exploration with surgical repair
  3. C Endovascular stent graft
  4. D Ultrasound guided alcohol injection
  5. E Ultrasound guided compression
💡 查看答案
E

Post-catheterization + 1cm pseudoaneurysm + bruit = small femoral pseudoaneurysm → USG-guided compression (small pseudoaneurysms often thrombose with compression).

VAS·MCQ050 2011 MCQ31 MCQ 90/M with good past health, presented with abdominal pain for 4 hours. Vitals are stable. 7 cm expansile tender mass is detected, consistent with AAA. What is t…

90/M with good past health, presented with abdominal pain for 4 hours. Vitals are stable. 7 cm expansile tender mass is detected, consistent with AAA. What is the best strategy?

  1. A Conservative with analgesia
  2. B Emergent CT for assessment of endovascular stent repair
  3. C Emergent OT for laparotomy
  4. D ICU for sedation and intubation
  5. E Medical with antihypertensive
💡 查看答案
B

90/M, 7cm AAA + tender + expansile + abdominal pain ×4h + stable vitals = symptomatic (likely contained leak) AAA → emergent CT for EVAR assessment (stable enough for CT; EVAR preferred in elderly if anatomy suitable).

VAS·MCQ051 2011 MCQ63, 2017 MCQ48 MCQ A 60 years old woman with history of CA larynx had laryngectomy and modified radical neck dissection plus adjuvant RT done 10 years ago. She had no recurrence o…

A 60 years old woman with history of CA larynx had laryngectomy and modified radical neck dissection plus adjuvant RT done 10 years ago. She had no recurrence of CA. She had 2 episodes of amaurosis fugax of left eye. Ultrasound neck found 90% stenosis of both left common and internal carotid arteries. There was no intracranial arterial occlusion. Which of the following is the appropriate management?

  1. A Left carotid endarterectomy
  2. B Left carotid angioplasty
  3. C Left carotid stenting
  4. D Intraarterial thrombolytic injection
  5. E Aspirin and clopidogrel
💡 查看答案
C

Post-laryngectomy/neck dissection/RT + recurrent amaurosis fugax = post-radiation carotid stenosis → carotid stenting (preferred over CEA in irradiated/operated necks — hostile surgical field).

VAS·MCQ052 2011 MCQ90 MCQ Acute lower limb ischaemia

Acute lower limb ischaemia

  1. A Is best treated by local intra-arterial heparin
  2. B Is characterized by presence of micro-infarcts on foot
  3. C Is defined as an ankle-brachial pressure index of less than 0.5
  4. D Is initially managed by aspirin
  5. E Requires immediate surgical treatment
💡 查看答案
E

Acute limb ischaemia requires immediate surgical treatment (embolectomy/revascularization within 6h to prevent irreversible tissue damage).

VAS·MCQ053 2012 MCQ29 MCQ A 30-year-old lady who is at the 8th week of gestation develops left calf swelling. Duplex scan confirms deep vein thrombosis involving the left superficial fem…

A 30-year-old lady who is at the 8th week of gestation develops left calf swelling. Duplex scan confirms deep vein thrombosis involving the left superficial femoral vein and the distal deep veins. The MOST APPROPRIATE management would be

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Conservative treatment with close observation
  3. C Heparinization
  4. D Termination of pregnancy
  5. E Warfarinization
💡 查看答案
C

8-week pregnant woman + DVT → heparinization (unfractionated heparin or LMWH — don't cross placenta, safe in pregnancy). Warfarin is teratogenic.

VAS·MCQ054 2012 MCQ30 MCQ In symptomatic carotid stenosis >80%, carotid angioplasty is preferred over carotid endarterectomy except:

In symptomatic carotid stenosis >80%, carotid angioplasty is preferred over carotid endarterectomy except:

  1. A Previously dissected neck
  2. B Radiotherapy induced carotid stenosis
  3. C Patients with multiple comorbidities
  4. D Circumferential calcification in carotid
  5. E Proximal lesion in carotid
💡 查看答案
D

Circumferential calcification in carotid → CEA preferred over stenting (calcified lesion difficult to dilate/stent, risk of plaque embolization). Previously dissected neck, post-RT, and comorbid patients favour stenting.

VAS·MCQ055 2012 MCQ31, 2018 MCQ43 MCQ 40/M, presented with UGI bleeding. Patient had duodenal ulcer bleeding treated with endoscopic therapy. Developed left calf swelling. Duplex USG confirmed DVT o…

40/M, presented with UGI bleeding. Patient had duodenal ulcer bleeding treated with endoscopic therapy. Developed left calf swelling. Duplex USG confirmed DVT of superficial femoral vein and deep distal vein. Which of the following is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Conservative management with close monitoring and follow up duplex scan
  3. C Insertion of caval filter
  4. D LMWH
  5. E Heparin
💡 查看答案
C

Post-UGI bleed (on anticoagulation risk) + DVT → IVC filter insertion (anticoagulation relatively contraindicated with recent GI bleed).

VAS·MCQ056 2012 MCQ51 MCQ A 63 years old lady has amaurosis fugax of the right eye. She complained of 4 or 5 episodes in the recent month. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE …

A 63 years old lady has amaurosis fugax of the right eye. She complained of 4 or 5 episodes in the recent month. Which of the following is the MOST APPROPRIATE management for her condition?

  1. A Carotid angiogram
  2. B Carotid Doppler ultrasound (Duplex)
  3. C Carotid endarterectomy
  4. D CT
  5. E Magnetic resonance angiogram
💡 查看答案
B

63/F, amaurosis fugax → carotid Doppler ultrasound (first-line investigation to assess carotid stenosis degree).

VAS·MCQ057 2012 MCQ54 MCQ Patient with pelvic surgery presented with fever 4 days post-op. You are the intern who notice there was increased swelling, heat and Homan’s test was positive …

Patient with pelvic surgery presented with fever 4 days post-op. You are the intern who notice there was increased swelling, heat and Homan’s test was positive over the right lower limb. Which of the following investigation would be most appropriate?

  1. A Bilateral venogram
  2. B Doppler ultrasound
  3. C Nuclear scan
💡 查看答案
B

Post-pelvic surgery + unilateral leg swelling + Homan's positive → Doppler ultrasound (duplex) of lower limb veins (investigation of choice for suspected DVT).

VAS·MCQ058 2013 MCQ70, 2015 MCQ61, 2018 MCQ45 MCQ Lady with repeated amaurosis fugax. Ultrasound found 90% stenosis in carotid artery and internal carotid artery. Carotid endarterectomy was performed. She prese…

Lady with repeated amaurosis fugax. Ultrasound found 90% stenosis in carotid artery and internal carotid artery. Carotid endarterectomy was performed. She presented with hoarseness of voice and choking on drinking water. Which of the following is most likely cause of her symptoms?

  1. A Hypoglossal nerve injury
  2. B Glossopharyngeal nerve injury
  3. C Superior laryngeal nerve injury
  4. D Recurrent laryngeal nerve injury
  5. E Vagus nerve injury
💡 查看答案
E

Post-CEA hoarseness + choking = vagus nerve injury (RLN branch). Vagus nerve runs near carotid bifurcation and can be injured during CEA.

VAS·MCQ059 2015 MCQ56, 2019 MCQ88 MCQ A patient with chronic renal failure (creatinine clearance <15) was found to have deep vein thrombosis of left superficial femoral vein and popliteal vein. Whic…

A patient with chronic renal failure (creatinine clearance <15) was found to have deep vein thrombosis of left superficial femoral vein and popliteal vein. Which of the following treatment is the most suitable?

  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Conventional heparin
  3. C LMWH
  4. D IVC filter
  5. E Clopidogrel
💡 查看答案
B

CKD (CrCl <15) + DVT → conventional (unfractionated) heparin is safest (predictable pharmacokinetics, reversible with protamine, dose-adjusted by APTT). LMWH accumulates in renal failure.

VAS·MCQ060 2015 MCQ60, 2018 MCQ44 MCQ A 70 years old man presented with a pulsatile abdominal mass. He is asymptomatic with no abdominal pain. Subsequent investigation revealed a 4cm infrarenal abdo…

A 70 years old man presented with a pulsatile abdominal mass. He is asymptomatic with no abdominal pain. Subsequent investigation revealed a 4cm infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm. What is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Conservative treatment with aspirin
  2. B Conservative treatment with CT 6 monthly
  3. C Conservative treatment with USG 6 monthly
  4. D Endovascular repair
  5. E Open repair
💡 查看答案
C

Asymptomatic 4cm infrarenal AAA → conservative with USG surveillance every 6 months (below 5.5cm threshold for intervention).

VAS·MCQ061 2015 MCQ62 MCQ Ruptured AAA repaired by EVAR. Presented few days later with blood in stool. Which is the most appropriate investigation?

Ruptured AAA repaired by EVAR. Presented few days later with blood in stool. Which is the most appropriate investigation?

  1. A Observe
  2. B Contrast CT abdomen
  3. C Sigmoidoscopy
  4. D Mesenteric angiogram
  5. E Stool culture for Clostridium difficile
💡 查看答案
C

Post-EVAR for ruptured AAA + blood in stool = ischaemic colitis (IMA ligation/occlusion during EVAR) → sigmoidoscopy to assess bowel viability.

VAS·MCQ062 2016 MCQ1 MCQ Which of the following is true about ABI?

Which of the following is true about ABI?

  1. A ABI > 0.95 is abnormal
  2. B It is the ratio of diastolic blood pressure of lower extremity to upper extremity
  3. C It can be falsely high in calcified arteries
  4. D 0.5 is the cut off for critical ischemia
  5. E After exercise, patient with peripheral vascular disease with have a rise in ABI
💡 查看答案
C

ABI can be falsely high in calcified arteries (incompressible vessels — common in DM, ESRF). This is a correct statement. ABI >0.95 is normal (A wrong). Ratio of systolic pressures (B wrong). <0.4 is critical (D wrong).

VAS·MCQ063 2016 MCQ2, 2020 MCQ33 MCQ What is the dose of heparin that we will use before carotid endarterectomy?

What is the dose of heparin that we will use before carotid endarterectomy?

  1. A 10 U/ kg
  2. B 50 U/kg
  3. C 100 U/kg
  4. D 300 U/kg
  5. E 500 U/kg
💡 查看答案
C

Heparin dose before CEA = 100 U/kg (standard systemic heparinization dose for arterial surgery).

VAS·MCQ064 2016 MCQ5 MCQ A 70 years old gentleman presented one week ago with an episode of amaurosis fugax affecting his right eye, together with left upper limb weakness. CT revealed …

A 70 years old gentleman presented one week ago with an episode of amaurosis fugax affecting his right eye, together with left upper limb weakness. CT revealed a small right cerebral infarct. Doppler US showed 80% stenosis of the internal carotid arteries on both sides. Which is the most appropriate management?

  1. A Carotid angiography
  2. B Heparinization
  3. C Left carotid endarterectomy
  4. D Right carotid endarterectomy
  5. E Thrombolysis
💡 查看答案
D

Right amaurosis fugax + left UL weakness + right cerebral infarct on CT → symptom localizes to RIGHT carotid territory → right carotid endarterectomy.

VAS·MCQ065 2017 MCQ46 MCQ A man presented with acute onset of chest pain and back pain as well as lower limb numbness (suggestive of acute embolism). Vital signs are stable. What is the …

A man presented with acute onset of chest pain and back pain as well as lower limb numbness (suggestive of acute embolism). Vital signs are stable. What is the next investigation?

  1. A Angiography
  2. B Serum amylase
  3. C CT thorax + abdomen
💡 查看答案
C

Acute chest/back pain + lower limb ischaemia → CT aortogram (suspected aortic dissection or ruptured AAA — CT is the investigation of choice for stable patients).

VAS·MCQ066 2017 MCQ47, 2018 MCQ42 MCQ A 90 years old man presented at A&E for acute abdominal pain for 4 hours. PE found an expansile pulsating mass on the abdomen. He was haemodynamically stable. W…

A 90 years old man presented at A&E for acute abdominal pain for 4 hours. PE found an expansile pulsating mass on the abdomen. He was haemodynamically stable. What is your next step in management?

  1. A EOT laparotomy
  2. B Urgent CT to assess for EVAR
  3. C Observation with analgesics
  4. D Medical therapy with antihypertensives
  5. E Admit ICU and intubate
💡 查看答案
B

90/M, 7cm tender expansile AAA, stable → urgent CT for EVAR assessment (as with MCQ50 — stable enough for CT, EVAR preferred in elderly).

VAS·MCQ067 2017 MCQ?? MCQ 94/M. Multiple medical co-morbidities including AF. Clinical exam shows weak femoral pulse, no palpable distal pulses, non-blanchable mottles on the leg. Muscle…

94/M. Multiple medical co-morbidities including AF. Clinical exam shows weak femoral pulse, no palpable distal pulses, non-blanchable mottles on the leg. Muscle power 0/5. What is the management?

  1. A Amputation
  2. B IA thrombolysis
  3. C Systemic heparinization
  4. D CT thorax and abdomen to look for underlying cause
💡 查看答案
A

94/M, multiple comorbidities + AF + acute ischaemic leg (no pulses, mottled, power 0/5) = irreversibly ischaemic limb → amputation (motor loss + fixed mottling = non-viable).

VAS·MCQ068 2018 MCQ40 MCQ Which of the following is INCORRECT about low molecular weight heparin?

Which of the following is INCORRECT about low molecular weight heparin?

  1. A Dose by weight and given once or twice daily
  2. B Given by subcutaneous injection
  3. C Suitable for patients with history of heparin-induced thrombocytopenia
  4. D Caution when used in patients with renal impairment
  5. E A form of standard treatment for patients with deep vein thrombosis
💡 查看答案
C

LMWH is NOT suitable for patients with HIT (cross-reactivity with heparin antibodies). Weight-based dosing (A true), SC injection (B true), caution in renal impairment (D true), standard DVT treatment (E true).

VAS·MCQ069 2019 MCQ1 MCQ A 65 years old gentlemen has an incidental finding on contrast computed tomography scan of a 3cm saccular aneurysm over his left renal artery around 1cm distal …

A 65 years old gentlemen has an incidental finding on contrast computed tomography scan of a 3cm saccular aneurysm over his left renal artery around 1cm distal to its origin. What is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

  1. A Autotrplantation of the left kidney
  2. B Conservative treatment with a computed tomography scan repeated in 6 months
  3. C Control systolic blood pressure to less than 130mmHg, together with oral tranexamic acid prescription
  4. D Endovascular treatment with stent assisted coil embolization
  5. E Open repair of the aneurysm
💡 查看答案
D

3cm renal artery aneurysm → endovascular treatment with stent-assisted coil embolization (minimally invasive approach for renal artery aneurysm — intervention indicated for aneurysms >2cm).

VAS·MCQ070 2019 MCQ22 MCQ Man with IHD and CABG with left internal mammary artery graft done. He later presents with thoracic aortic aneurysm and is to be treated with stent graft which …

Man with IHD and CABG with left internal mammary artery graft done. He later presents with thoracic aortic aneurysm and is to be treated with stent graft which will cover left subclavian artery. Which of the following is he most at risk of?

  1. A Left upper limb ischaemia
  2. B Myocardial infarction
  3. C Paraplegia due to vertebral artery occlusion
  4. D Post circulation stroke
  5. E Subclavian steal
💡 查看答案
B

Previous CABG with LIMA graft → thoracic stent graft covering left subclavian → risk of MI (LIMA arises from left subclavian artery → covering subclavian = LIMA occlusion → myocardial ischaemia).

VAS·MCQ071 2019 MCQ35 MCQ Back pain for several days, P/E show tender abdominal mass, CT reveal 5.6cm AAA, what is the management?

Back pain for several days, P/E show tender abdominal mass, CT reveal 5.6cm AAA, what is the management?

  1. A Observe in ICU
  2. B Discharge and repeat CT in 1 week
  3. C CT aortogram
  4. D Urgent endovascular aneurysm repair
💡 查看答案
C

Back pain + tender 5.6cm AAA = symptomatic AAA (possible contained leak) → CT aortogram (urgent anatomical assessment for surgical planning — symptomatic AAA needs intervention).

VAS·MCQ072 2019 MCQ63 MCQ A 52 years old gentleman presented with an acute onset of left lower limb ischemia. Urgent surgical embolectomy was performed, and distal pulses were restored. …

A 52 years old gentleman presented with an acute onset of left lower limb ischemia. Urgent surgical embolectomy was performed, and distal pulses were restored. However, he noted that there is progressive painful swelling of his left lower limb. There is pain on passive dorsiflexion of foot. Which of the following should be performed?

  1. A Conservative management with elevation of left lower limb and potent analgesics
  2. B Duplex scan to rule out deep venous thrombosis
  3. C Duplex scan to rule out acute arterial occlusion
  4. D Emergency fasciotomy
  5. E Give ?aspirin/thrombectomy/thrombolysis to clear out remaining clot
💡 查看答案
D

Post-embolectomy with restored pulses but progressive calf swelling/pain = reperfusion injury → compartment syndrome → emergency fasciotomy.

VAS·MCQ073 2019 MCQ71 MCQ 70 years old man complained of acute abdominal pain, radiating to the back. BP 200/110, HR ?. CT shows type B aortic dissection with superior mesenteric artery …

70 years old man complained of acute abdominal pain, radiating to the back. BP 200/110, HR ?. CT shows type B aortic dissection with superior mesenteric artery and right internal iliac artery occluded. Which is the MOST APPROPRIATE management?

  1. A Repeat CT abdomen in 24 hours
  2. B Anticoagulation
  3. C Emergent cross-femoral bypass
  4. D Emergency open surgical repair and bypass
  5. E Emergency stent graft with additional stenting of superior mesenteric artery and internal iliac artery if necessary
💡 查看答案
E

Type B dissection + SMA + iliac occlusion = complicated Type B → emergency stent graft + additional stenting of compromised branches (visceral malperfusion = indication for intervention).

VAS·MCQ074 2020 MCQ10 MCQ M/65, previously mobile and ADL independent. Now has a gangrenous foot with no prospect of revascularization and fixed flexion contracture around 30 degrees of …

M/65, previously mobile and ADL independent. Now has a gangrenous foot with no prospect of revascularization and fixed flexion contracture around 30 degrees of the knee. What is the best procedure for management?

  1. A Above knee amputation
  2. B Below knee amputation (long posterior flap)
  3. C Below knee amputation (skew flap)
  4. D Guillotine amputation below knee
  5. E Through knee amputation
💡 查看答案
A

65/M, gangrenous foot + no revascularization option + 30° fixed flexion contracture of knee → above-knee amputation (BKA requires functioning knee joint for prosthetic use — fixed contracture precludes this).

VAS·MCQ075 2020 MCQ28 MCQ 47-year-old woman presented with lethargy, headache, weight loss, difference in blood pressure between two arms, history of smoking. What is the diagnosis?

47-year-old woman presented with lethargy, headache, weight loss, difference in blood pressure between two arms, history of smoking. What is the diagnosis?

  1. A Behcet’s disease
  2. B Buerger’s disease
  3. C Influenza A
  4. D Takayasu’s arteritis
  5. E Temporal arteritis
💡 查看答案
D

47/F, lethargy, headache, weight loss, BP difference between arms = Takayasu's arteritis (large vessel vasculitis in young women — aortic arch branches stenosis causing arm BP discrepancy).

VAS·MCQ076 2020 MCQ41 MCQ Chest pain and back pain. Stanford type B dissection. Which is indicated for endovascular surgical treatment?

Chest pain and back pain. Stanford type B dissection. Which is indicated for endovascular surgical treatment?

  1. A Dissection to left subclavian
  2. B Dissection to left internal iliac
  3. C Dissection to paravisceral aorta
  4. D Right LL absence of pulse and numbness
  5. E Compression of true lumen of thoracic aorta to < 1 cm2
💡 查看答案
D

Type B dissection + right LL absent pulse/numbness = limb malperfusion → indication for endovascular intervention (stenting to restore true lumen flow to right iliac).

VAS·MCQ077 2020 MCQ82 MCQ 65-year-old man, self-independent and mobile, found to have a 5.6cm abdominal aortic aneurysm and 5.0 cm common iliac artery aneurysm extending down to iliac bi…

65-year-old man, self-independent and mobile, found to have a 5.6cm abdominal aortic aneurysm and 5.0 cm common iliac artery aneurysm extending down to iliac bifurcation. The patient has hypertension and hyperlipidemia. Which of the following is the best management?

  1. A Palliative treatment
  2. B EVAR with branched internal iliac graft
  3. C EVAR with occlusion of left internal iliac artery and extension of external iliac artery
  4. D Fenestrated EVAR
  5. E Open repair with aorto-bi-iliac graft
💡 查看答案
E

5.6cm AAA + 5.0cm common iliac extending to bifurcation → open repair with aorto-bi-iliac graft (internal iliac preservation important; EVAR anatomy challenging with iliac bifurcation involvement).

VAS·MCQ078 2020 MCQ87 MCQ What is the best procedure for an 80 year old gentleman who presents with contain-rupture of a 8cm AAA, 15mm neck and normal sized iliac artery at 9pm. His bloo…

What is the best procedure for an 80 year old gentleman who presents with contain-rupture of a 8cm AAA, 15mm neck and normal sized iliac artery at 9pm. His blood pressure 110/70, pulse 98.

  1. A Close observation with fluid resuscitation till the next morning and open aortic stent tube
  2. B Close observation with permissive hypotension till the next morning and endovascular aneurysm repair
  3. C Fluid resuscitation and open aortic aneurysm repair
  4. D Palliative
  5. E Permissive hypotension and urgent endovascular aneurysm repair
💡 查看答案
E

80/M, contained ruptured 8cm AAA, suitable anatomy (15mm neck), 9pm → permissive hypotension + urgent EVAR (best option for elderly with contained rupture and suitable anatomy).

VAS·MCQ079 2020 MCQ?? MCQ Patient was well and ambulatory. Recently had an episode of stroke. Found to have right sided weakness and dysarthria. MRA found that left sided carotid was tot…

Patient was well and ambulatory. Recently had an episode of stroke. Found to have right sided weakness and dysarthria. MRA found that left sided carotid was totally occluded. He was referred to you for your expert management.

  1. A Left CEA
  2. B Left stenting
  3. C Left atherectomy
  4. D Left tunnelling with grafting
  5. E Aspirin + clopidogrel only
💡 查看答案
E

Left ICA totally occluded → CEA or stenting not possible for occluded artery. Best medical management: aspirin + clopidogrel + risk factor modification.

VAS·MCQ080 2021 MCQ2 MCQ 25/F violinist, swollen purple painful arm overnight. No pulse in arm, distal capillary refill brisk. What is the most likely cause?

25/F violinist, swollen purple painful arm overnight. No pulse in arm, distal capillary refill brisk. What is the most likely cause?

  1. A Acute arterial insufficiency
  2. B Acute brachial artery embolism
  3. C Thoracic outlet syndrome
  4. D DVT of upper limb
💡 查看答案
D

25/F violinist, sudden swollen purple painful arm, no arterial pulse but brisk cap refill = upper limb DVT (Paget-Schroetter syndrome — effort thrombosis from repetitive arm use/TOS).

VAS·MCQ081 2021 MCQ31 MCQ Which of the following is true about EVAR?

Which of the following is true about EVAR?

  1. A Cannot do percutaneously
  2. B Survival balances out at 5 years compared to open
  3. C Evidence high for AAA with diameter 4cm to 5cm
💡 查看答案
B

EVAR vs open repair: survival advantage balances out at 5 years (early mortality benefit of EVAR diminishes with time due to reintervention needs).

VAS·MCQ082 2021 MCQ34 MCQ Regarding stenosis at carotid bifurcation, which of the following is correct?

Regarding stenosis at carotid bifurcation, which of the following is correct?

  1. A Carotid stenting has significantly lower perioperative stroke risk than carotid endarterectomy
  2. B Urgent CEA within 1 week needed(?)
  3. C CEA reduces stroke risk to <10% in 2 years
  4. D With best medical therapy, his risk of major stroke in 2 years exceeds 50%
💡 查看答案
B

Symptomatic carotid stenosis → urgent CEA within 2 weeks (early intervention reduces stroke risk — ECST/NASCET evidence).

VAS·MCQ083 2021 MCQ43 MCQ 23 year old gentleman, history of DVT, now with a shallow ulcer with sloping edge at gaiter area. What is the appropriate first-line treatment?

23 year old gentleman, history of DVT, now with a shallow ulcer with sloping edge at gaiter area. What is the appropriate first-line treatment?

  1. A Compression bandage
  2. B Debridement
  3. C Manuka honey dressing
  4. D Surgery for varicose vein(?)
💡 查看答案
A

23/M, DVT history + venous ulcer at gaiter area → compression bandage (first-line treatment for venous ulcers — graduated compression promotes healing).

VAS·MCQ084 2021 MCQ?? MCQ Left sided hemiplegia with spontaneous recovery in 20 minutes. Right carotid artery 70% stenosed, left 90% stenosed. Which is the best management to prevent fur…

Left sided hemiplegia with spontaneous recovery in 20 minutes. Right carotid artery 70% stenosed, left 90% stenosed. Which is the best management to prevent further stroke?

  1. A Urgent right CEA
  2. B Right CEA after 2 weeks
  3. C Left CEA
  4. D No need CEA because medical treatment is good enough
💡 查看答案
A

Left hemiplegia (20 min recovery = TIA) → right-sided stroke → right carotid 70% stenosed = symptomatic stenosis → urgent right CEA.

VAS·MCQ085 2021 MCQ50 MCQ Acute type B aortic dissection, which is true?

Acute type B aortic dissection, which is true?

  1. A In hospital mortality rate more than 10%
  2. B Will NOT progress to type A eventually
  3. C Affect ascending aorta invariably
  4. D Require urgent surgical treatment
💡 查看答案
A

Acute Type B dissection in-hospital mortality is ~10-15%. It does NOT invariably affect ascending aorta (C wrong — that's Type A). Does NOT require urgent surgery if uncomplicated (D wrong — medical management).

VAS·MCQ086 2021 MCQ65 MCQ T2DM, hypertension, dry toe gangrene, but also rest pain at night.

T2DM, hypertension, dry toe gangrene, but also rest pain at night.

  1. A IV heparin
  2. B Urgent toe amputation
  3. C Conservative
  4. D Mortality at 5 years is more than 30%
💡 查看答案
D

DM + HTN + dry toe gangrene + rest pain = critical limb ischaemia → 5-year mortality >30% (correct — high cardiovascular mortality in CLI patients). Urgent amputation not first choice (B wrong — attempt revascularization). Heparin for acute not chronic (A wrong).

VAS·MCQ087 2021 MCQ74 MCQ A patient complains of a 3-year history of ulcer over her medial malleolus of her ankle. It is not painful, and the ulcer healed well with dressing but recurs f…

A patient complains of a 3-year history of ulcer over her medial malleolus of her ankle. It is not painful, and the ulcer healed well with dressing but recurs frequently. Examination showed a venous ulcer. Which of the following is true?

  1. A A venous ulcer can never be malignant
  2. B Ultrasound scan of the veins is unlikely to show great saphenous vein insufficiency
  3. C Deep vein obstruction/thrombosis must be ruled out
  4. D Deep vein incompetence must be present
💡 查看答案
C

3-year recurrent medial malleolar venous ulcer → deep vein obstruction/thrombosis must be ruled out (may have post-thrombotic syndrome causing chronic venous insufficiency → recurrent ulceration).

VAS·MCQ088 2022 MCQ16 MCQ Type A aortic dissection management:

Type A aortic dissection management:

  1. A Immediate surgery is indicated
  2. B Endovascular stent graft repair is the standard
  3. C Always originate from ascending aorta
💡 查看答案
A

Type A aortic dissection → immediate surgery is indicated (Stanford A = surgical emergency regardless of other factors).

VAS·MCQ089 2022 MCQ23 MCQ Young man with Marfan’s syndrome presenting acutely with SOB hypotension chest pain, CXR showed no pneumothorax. Investigation?

Young man with Marfan’s syndrome presenting acutely with SOB hypotension chest pain, CXR showed no pneumothorax. Investigation?

  1. A CT aortogram
  2. B Electrocardiogram
💡 查看答案
A

Young Marfan's + acute SOB + hypotension + chest pain + no pneumothorax → CT aortogram (suspected acute aortic dissection/rupture — Marfan's predisposes to aortic root pathology).

VAS·MCQ090 2022 MCQ29 MCQ Regarding EVAR of infrarenal AAA,

Regarding EVAR of infrarenal AAA,

  1. A Imaging surveillance not needed after 5 years
  2. B Most common type of endoleak is type 2
  3. C Survival advantage is still over open repair after 10 years
  4. D There is good evidence for EVAR in treatment of AAA with maximum diameter of 4-5 cm
💡 查看答案
B

Type 2 endoleak (from lumbar/IMA branches) is the most common type of endoleak after EVAR.

VAS·MCQ091 2022 MCQ46 MCQ A guy with acute limb ischaemia with new onset AF, most important medication to give?

A guy with acute limb ischaemia with new onset AF, most important medication to give?

  1. A Unfractionated heparin
  2. B Aspirin
  3. C Statin
  4. D Beta blocker
💡 查看答案
A

Acute limb ischaemia + new AF → unfractionated heparin immediately (prevent thrombus propagation while planning definitive treatment — embolectomy).

VAS·MCQ092 2022 MCQ48 MCQ An 80-year-old gentleman was incidentally found to have a neck bruit and ultrasound showed stenosis of carotid bifurcation of 60%. Which of the following is COR…

An 80-year-old gentleman was incidentally found to have a neck bruit and ultrasound showed stenosis of carotid bifurcation of 60%. Which of the following is CORRECT?

  1. A The periprocedural stroke risk is lower in endovascular angioplasty when compared to surgical endarterectomy in this patient
  2. B Urgent surgical endarterectomy is needed in 1 month
  3. C Carotid endarterectomy can reduce stroke risk to less than 5% in 5 years
  4. D With best medical treatment the risk of major stroke in this patient is in excess of 10% in 2 years
💡 查看答案
D

80/M, asymptomatic, 60% carotid stenosis → with best medical treatment, risk of major stroke is low (<2%/year for asymptomatic 60% stenosis — benefit of CEA marginal in this setting).

VAS·MCQ093 2022 MCQ67 MCQ Diabetes patient chronic limb ischaemia, 3 toes dry gangrene with significant resting pain, which of the following is true?

Diabetes patient chronic limb ischaemia, 3 toes dry gangrene with significant resting pain, which of the following is true?

  1. A Heparin
  2. B Better to treat conservatively than surgical revascularization
  3. C Patient likely has aorto-iliac disease
  4. D 5 year survival <60%
💡 查看答案
D

DM + chronic limb ischaemia + 3 toes dry gangrene + rest pain → 5-year survival <60% (critical limb ischaemia in diabetics carries very high mortality — cardiovascular disease).

VAS·MCQ094 2022 MCQ81 MCQ For infrarenal AAA, which is correct?

For infrarenal AAA, which is correct?

  1. A Must preserve both internal iliac artery to prevent bowel ischemia
  2. B Must preserve inferior mesenteric artery to prevent bowel ischemia
  3. C Hospital mortality for elective open repair is 15-20%
  4. D Left renal vein is a good anatomical landmark for the neck
💡 查看答案
D

Left renal vein is a good anatomical landmark for the infrarenal aortic neck (crosses anterior to aorta just below renal arteries). Must preserve IMA if patent and SMA compromised (A/B wrong). Elective open mortality ~2-5% not 15-20% (C wrong).

VAS·EMQ001 2002# EMQ4 EMQ PAIN OVER THE TOE

Theme: PAIN OVER THE TOE

Options
  1. A Buerger’s disease
  2. B Blue toe syndrome
  3. C Femoro-popliteal occlusive disease
  4. D Aorto-iliac occlusive disease
  5. E Popliteal entrapment syndrome
  6. F Acute arterial thrombosis
  7. G Aortic dissection
  8. H Vasospasm
  9. I Ingrowing toenail
  10. J Fungal infection

For each of the case scenarios below, choose the single MOST likely diagnosis from the options listed above.

Questions
  1. Q1. A 70 years old lady with no history of intermittent claudication nor rest pain presented with patchy cyanosis and pain over the toes one day after cardiac catheterization. All the lower limb peripheral pulses were palpable.
  2. Q2. A 70 years old gentleman presented with gradual onset of intermittent claudication affecting his calf for 2 years. There was also pain over the toes.
  3. Q3. A 35 years old smoker complained of painful toes and fingers associated with multiple shallow ulcerations.
VAS·EMQ002 2012 EMQ5 EMQ A. Amputation

Theme: A. Amputation

Options
  1. B Angioplasty and stenting
  2. C Aspirin
Questions
  1. Q1. A 60 years old male with disabling left calf claudication for 6 months. On examination there is diminished left femoral pulse. CT angiogram showed short segment stenosis of the proximal left internal iliac artery. No significant stenosis was seen in the rest of the vasculature.
VAS·EMQ003 2017 EMQ3 EMQ VASCULAR SURGERY

Theme: VASCULAR SURGERY

Options
  1. A Aspirin
  2. B Daflon
  3. C Heparinisation
  4. D Thrombolysis
  5. E Embolectomy
  6. F Angioplasty
  7. G Arterial bypass
  8. H Amputation
  9. I Sympathectomy

Select the best management option.

Questions
  1. Q1. 90/M frail, bedridden with AF. Left LL pulse normal. Right leg no ?femoral pulse, mottling, loss of sensation.
  2. Q2. A middle aged patient with incapacitating intermittent claudication. Short 90% stenosis at proximal external iliac artery. Other vessels are normal.
  3. Q3. A 40 years old chronic smoker has digital ulcers on his left hand for 6 months. Ulnar and radial pulses are normal.
VAS·EMQ004 2019 EMQ4 EMQ ULCERS

Theme: ULCERS

Options
  1. A Arterial ulcer
  2. B Mixed arterial and venous ulcer
  3. C Deep venous reflux
  4. D Primary venous ulcer
  5. E Rheumatoid ulcer
  6. F Neuropathic ulcer
Questions
  1. Q1. 75/M, smoker. 4 week history of painful ulcer at ankle with mild ankle edema. ABI 0.4.
  2. Q2. Painless ulcer for 8 years on medial malleolus, history of varicose vein with surgery done. Lower limb with eczematous change and hyperpigmentation.
  3. Q3. Patient with type II diabetes mellitus presented with a painless ulcer. ABI 1.3.
VAS·EMQ005 2020 EMQ7 EMQ CAROTID ARTERY PROBLEMS

Theme: CAROTID ARTERY PROBLEMS

Options
  1. A Aspirin 300mg
  2. B Carotid drug eluting stent
  3. C Carotid endarterectomy with general anaesthesia
  4. D Carotid endarterectomy with local anaesthesia
  5. E Carotid stenting
  6. F Carotid subclavian bypass
  7. G 80mg clopidogrel + 20mg atorvastatin
  8. H 80mg clopidogrel + 80mg atorvastatin
  9. I Smoking cessation
  10. J Warfarin
Questions
  1. Q1. 70/M, TIA of the left side, right side carotid artery stenosis 50-69%, left side carotid artery stenosis 0-49%.
  2. Q2. Middle age man with history of CA larynx with radiotherapy done. One day developed dysarthria and left side weakness. During admission, ultrasound found right side carotid artery stenosis 75-99%, MRI scan of the vessels confirm the same findings.
  3. Q3. 56-year-old woman (heavy smoker) went to a local hair-dressor to celebrate her recovery from CA breast. On the next day she suddenly developed right arm and leg weakness, which resolves over 5 days. Carotid duplex and MR angiography showed left ICA dissection with distal blood flow and right ICA 50-69% stenosis.
VAS·EMQ006 2021 EMQ3 EMQ A. Acute DVT

Theme: A. Acute DVT

Options
  1. B Acute limb ischemia
  2. C Claudication
  3. D Critical limb ischemia
  4. E Diabetic neuropathy
  5. F Osteoarthritis of the knee
  6. G Perthes disease
  7. H Post phlebitis limb syndrome
  8. I Spinal stenosis
  9. J Varicose veins
Questions
  1. Q1. Man walk some distance, pain, relieve with resting. Pain on walking downhill.
  2. Q2. Man, 40s, ex-smoker and history of myocardial infarction. Pain at night, worse with leg raised up.
  3. Q3. History of DVT, bursting pain of the leg, relieved with sitting/resting. Also stand a long time because he is a teacher, then leg swollen and pain.
